gmw_00010371.name: NGC 2805
gmw_00010371.overview: NGC 2805 is a highly disturbed SABd disk galaxy [arXiv:1304.0447, arXiv:astro-ph/0502036] located in the LGG 173 group [arXiv:astro-ph/0407014]. It features an asymmetric neutral hydrogen distribution [arXiv:astro-ph/0502036] and a population of X-ray point sources associated with areas of recent star formation [arXiv:1304.0447].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 2805 as the sample galaxy with the largest X-ray point-source population, including likely ULX candidates despite substantial expected background contamination.

quotes.quote: NGC 2805 is an SABd galaxy at a distance of 28.0 Mpc. This galaxy has the largest population of X-ray point sources in our sample, with fourteen detections inside D25 and five consistent with ULXs. It also has the largest projected area in our sample, and M03 predict twelve background sources above our detection limit, including five consistent with ULXs. Due to the large expected CXB contribution, it is difficult to draw conclusions about the X-ray population of this galaxy.

However, we note that ten of the fourteen sources appear consistent with areas of recent star formation, a fraction that would be difficult to produce with randomly oriented background sources. Additionally, four of the sources lie within the central 15 '' of the galaxy, another unlikely orientation for background sources. Finally, the H measurements of W11, the H /SFR relation of Kennicutt (1998), and the SFR/N( L > 2 10^ 38 ) relation of Grimm, Gilfanov, and Sunyaev (2003, G03 hereafter) predict four X-ray sources above our flux limit within the D25 ellipse of NGC 2805.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 2805 as a massive, disturbed Ho 124 galaxy with asymmetric HI, weak radio continuum, an intriguingly radio-faint center, and ambiguous ram-pressure motion.

quotes.quote: The HI distribution in NGC 2805 is asymmetric with enhanced column densities in clumps towards the north (see Fig fig5 (a)). The HI is extended east-west along the highly-disturbed northern optical spiral arm and is confined to the optical disk. The radio continuum emission in NGC 2805 is fairly weak (see Fig fig5 (b)), with localised peaks in the south and northeast, and bearing little resemblance either to the optical or to the HI distribution. The central region of the galaxy is bright in the JHK near-IR bands and in the optical band but faint in radio continuum (Fig fig5 (b)) which is intriguing. In short, this massive galaxy appears to be highly disturbed, the reason for which is not clear from existing observations.

sources.summary: The paper lacks a direct reliable distance for NGC2805, identifies it as an LGG173 member, and adopts 9.77 Mpc from another group member.

quotes.quote: NGC2805. -- There is no reliable distance determination for NGC2805. This galaxy is a member of the LGG173 group (Garcia 1993). The distance to another member (NGC2880) of this group has been determined with the SBF method and is equal to 9.77 Mpc (Tonry et al. 2001). This value is adopted as distance to NGC2805.

gmw_00010372.name: ESO 501-23
gmw_00010372.overview: ESO 501-23 is a bulgeless SBdm disk galaxy located at an estimated distance of 7.01 megaparsecs [arXiv:1304.0447]. The galaxy hosts a bright off-center ultraluminous X-ray source, as well as an uncertain X-ray source located near its galactic center [arXiv:1304.0447].

sources.summary: The paper describes ESO 501-23 as a bulgeless SBdm galaxy with the survey’s brightest X-ray source, a likely ULX, plus an uncertain near-center source not claimed as an AGN.

quotes.quote: ESO 501-23 is an SBdm galaxy at an adopted distance of 7.01 Mpc, although this distance is uncertain by a factor of two (see 4.3). We find a total of four objects within this galaxy, including the brightest X-ray source discovered in this survey, ESO 501-23 X-1. This object is discussed further in 4.3. The source X-2 lies only 2 '' from the galactic center listed in Table 2. However, because of the peculiar shape of this galaxy, there is significant disagreement about the location of the galactic center, and several independent determinations of the central coordinates exist, as shown in Figure 2.

We discovered a bright X-ray source in the galaxy ESO 501-23, and argue that it is a bona \ fide ULX based on its flux, spectrum, off-center location, and coincidence with H . Its spectral index implies a luminosity 10^ 40 \ which, if correct, argues the distance to ESO 501-23 is larger than some previous published estimates.

gmw_00010373.name: SDSS J160705+533558
gmw_00010373.overview: SDSS J160705+533558 is a high-redshift quasar at z=3.653 that is part of a gas-rich merger system [arXiv:2202.07368]. The interaction between the quasar and its companion galaxy has created a dusty, star-forming overlap region [arXiv:2202.07368].

sources.summary: The paper detects CO in SDSS J160705+533558 and a nearby companion, interpreting the quasar as a gas-rich merger whose interaction creates a dusty star-forming overlap region.

quotes.quote: We present CO(1--0) observations of the high-redshift quasar SDSS J160705+533558 ( z=3.653 ) using the Karl G. Jansky Very Large Array (VLA). We detect CO emission associated with the quasar and at 16.8 kpc projected distance from it, separated by 800 km s^ 1 in velocity. The total molecular gas mass of this system is 5 10^ 10 M_ . By comparing our CO detections with previous submillimetre (submm) observations of the source, an offset between the different emission components is revealed: the peak of the submm emission is offset from the quasar and from the CO companion detected in our VLA data. To explain our findings, we propose a scenario similar to that for the Antennae galaxies: SDSS J160705+533558 might be a merger system in which the quasar and the CO companion are the merging galaxies, whose interaction resulted in the formation of a dusty, star-forming overlap region between the galaxies that is dominant at the submm wavelengths.

gmw_00010374.name: NGC 1532
gmw_00010374.overview: NGC 1532 is an edge-on barred spiral galaxy [arXiv:1711.03940] that is interacting with its neighbor NGC 1531 [arXiv:2403.05709]. This interaction has resulted in a highly distorted disk with large extraplanar emission plumes and a radio halo with an average scale height of 1.5 kpc [arXiv:2403.05709].

sources.summary: The paper analyzes NGC 1532 as the edge-on barred spiral host of DLT16am, giving its coordinates, distance, dust lanes, local metallicity assessment, and local star-formation rate.

quotes.quote: NGC 1532, the host of DLT16am, is a SB(s)b edge-on galaxy 1991rc3..book.....D , located at RA = 04:12:04.3, Dec =- 32:52:27 [ J2000 ] , with an apparent total magnitude of B=10.65 0.09 mag trove.nla.gov.au/work/8318286 , showing prominent dust lanes close to the position of DLT16am. From our X-shooter spectrum obtained on 2016 December 22, we infer an heliocentric velocity of 1367 ( z =0.00456 , see Section sec:opt_spectra ), estimated from the average positions of the Balmer emission lines. Throughout the paper we will adopt a distance of 20.0 1.8 Mpc 2013AJ....146...86T to NGC 1532 (leading to a projected distance of 8.4 kpc from the center of the host for DLT16am), although a wide range of values are reported in the literature, suggesting a larger uncertainty.

Metallicity and star formation rate After correcting our X-shooter UVB and VIS spectra for the foreground Galactic extinction, redshift (using the values reported above) and host galaxy extinction, we estimated the local metallicity and star formation rate (SFR) of NGC 1532 at the position of DLT16am.
Accounting for the derived distance of NGC 1532, we obtain a local SFR of 1.64 10^ 1 M_ yr^ 1 .

sources.summary: The paper says NGC 1532 is interacting with NGC 1531, has a distorted disk and large extraplanar plumes, and hosts one of the sample's most prominent radio halos.

quotes.quote: NGC 1532: Because this galaxy is part of an interacting pair with NGC 1531, its z profile was particularly difficult to fit. Its disk is highly distorted, and large plumes of extraplanar emission can be seen from the radio contour map. After removing the emission from NGC 1531, the best fit was found for a two-component function with a Gaussian disk. This galaxy has one of the most prominent radio halos in our sample, with an average scale height of 1.5 kpc.

gmw_00010375.name: ES509
gmw_00010375.overview: ES509 is a strong, compact radio source at 1.4 GHz that exhibits a well-defined power-law spectrum [arXiv:1011.2391]. It is classified as an active galactic nucleus and is polarized at 1.4 GHz and higher frequencies [arXiv:1011.2391].

sources.summary: The paper describes ES509 as a compact IFRS with a power-law radio spectrum, extreme radio-to-infrared ratio, significant polarization, and evidence supporting an AGN classification.

quotes.quote: ES509 is a strong, compact radio source at 1.4 GHz and is detected at all other frequencies. Its spectrum is a well-defined power-law with an index of 1.02 , and its S20/S3.6 ratio is 9240, clearly in the realm of the HzRG sample by . It is significantly polarised at 1.4 GHz (12 ) and also at higher frequencies. We conclude from this evidence that this is an AGN.

gmw_00010376.name: ES973
gmw_00010376.overview: ES973 is an extended radio source at 1.4 GHz classified as an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1011.2391]. It exhibits a steep radio spectrum and is polarized at 1.4 GHz, though it remains undetected at 8.6 GHz [arXiv:1011.2391].

sources.summary: The paper identifies ES973 as an extended, steep-spectrum, polarized IFRS whose morphology, polarization, and high radio-to-infrared ratio support an AGN classification.

quotes.quote: ES973 is an extended radio source at 1.4 GHz which has been classified as AGN based on this finding alone, but its S20/S3.6 ratio of 4710 supports this. Its spectrum is steep with an index of 1.15 , but it is not detected at 8.6 GHz, where, according to an extrapolation from the lower frequencies, a 13.3 detection would have been expected. It is polarised at 1.4 GHz (7 ) and unpolarised at 4.8 GHz. Therefore, based on morphology and polarisation, this is an AGN.

gmw_00010377.name: ES798
gmw_00010377.overview: ES798 is an extended radio source at 1.4 GHz that is classified as an active galactic nucleus based on its radio morphology [arXiv:1011.2391]. It exhibits a high radio-to-infrared flux ratio and is unpolarized at 1.4 GHz [arXiv:1011.2391].

sources.summary: The paper treats ES798 as an extended IFRS with a high radio-to-infrared ratio, uncertain spectrum, non-detection at 8.6 GHz, and enough morphology-based evidence for an AGN classification.

quotes.quote: ES798 is a somewhat extended radio source at 1.4 GHz, and the detection at 4.8 GHz pinpoints a position which suggests that the nearby IR source is not associated, as had already been suggested by . Its S20/S3.6 ratio is 4760 which is high, and its spectral index of 0.81 is not well-defined. The non-detection at 8.6 GHz is odd since a 14.6 detection would have been expected. It is unpolarised at 1.4 GHz ( < 5 ), but still we deem the evidence based on radio morphology and S20/S3.6 sufficient to classify this source as an AGN.

gmw_00010378.name: ES775
gmw_00010378.overview: ES775 is an extended radio source driven by an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1011.2391]. It exhibits a steep radio spectrum, and a faint bridge of emission extends from the source toward the nearby ES780 [arXiv:1011.2391].

sources.summary: The paper describes ES775 as an extended, steep-spectrum IFRS with a radio bridge toward ES780 and concludes from arcsecond morphology and spectrum that it is AGN-driven.

quotes.quote: ES775 is another extended (at 1.4 GHz) radio source with a S20/S3.6 ratio of 1360 and a steep spectrum with index 1.35 . A faint bridge of emission extends towards the nearby source ES780, and the nature of this bridge is unclear. ES775 is not detected at 8.6 GHz due to sensitivity limits, and is unpolarised ( < 6 ). found that in VLBI observations the compact emission from this source was less than 0.26 mJy at 1.6 GHz, however, the arcsec-scale morphology and steep spectrum lead us to conclude that this source is AGN-driven.

gmw_00010379.name: ES419
gmw_00010379.overview: ES419 is a compact 1.4 GHz radio source with a steep low-frequency spectral index of 1.35 [arXiv:1011.2391]. Due to its high radio-to-infrared flux ratio and lack of association with nearby infrared objects, it is tentatively identified as an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1011.2391].

sources.summary: The paper tentatively identifies ES419 as an AGN candidate based on its compact radio emission, lack of association with nearby infrared objects, steep low-frequency spectrum, and high radio-to-infrared ratio.

quotes.quote: ES419 is a compact 1.4 GHz radio source with a moderately high S20/S3.6 ratio of 1800. This source was deemed by not to be associated with the nearby IR objects visible in Figure fig:images . Its lower-frequency radio spectral index is a rather steep 1.35 , and it has not been detected at the higher frequencies because of its faintness. It is unpolarised ( < 5 ), and so the only clues to its classification are S20/S3.6 and , and we tentatively identify this source as an AGN.

gmw_00010380.name: CS114
gmw_00010380.overview: CS114 is a compact radio source across all frequencies that exhibits a steep spectrum [arXiv:1011.2391]. It is an active galactic nucleus with a compact core containing most of its total emission [arXiv:1011.2391].

sources.summary: The paper describes CS114 as a compact, steep-spectrum IFRS with a high radio-to-infrared ratio and a VLBI-detected compact core containing most of its emission, supporting an AGN-driven interpretation.

quotes.quote: CS114 is a compact radio source at all frequencies with a steep spectrum ( =-1.34 ) and a high S20/S3.6 of 2560. It is known to be an AGN because of its VLBI-detected compact core ( Norris2007b ) which contains 5 mJy and thus most of the total emission. It is unpolarised ( < 3 ), but we suggest it is AGN-driven.

gmw_00010381.name: NGC6361
gmw_00010381.overview: NGC 6361 is an edge-on spiral galaxy that lacks a bar-like structure [arXiv:1705.01665]. Its rotation curve suggests that the galaxy is dominated by a dark matter halo [arXiv:1705.01665].

sources.summary: GalRotpy uses NGC6361 as a spiral-galaxy test case, deriving its rotation curve from CALIFA data and finding dark matter halo dominance outside the inner disk.

quotes.quote: Then, we select the NGC6361 galaxy which is a spiral galaxy type (SAb edge-on) that does not present a bar-like structure in it. After that, we obtain from CALIFA survey the data product of NGC6361, one derived using PIPE3D, a technique implemented by sanchez2016pipe3d . Based on the datacube of NGC6361 and the velocity map for H emission line provided by CALIFA collaboration, we get the Fig. fig:Fi3 . In this example, the rotation curve of NGC6361 is obtained from the points over the kinematic center, taken over the radial coordinate on the gas velocity field, along the given major axis (see Fig. fig:Fi3 ).

It means that this profile is capable of reproducing the rotation curve by itself, which suggests that within the corresponding uncertainties, NGC6361 is a galaxy dominated by dark matter, for a halo with a mass M_h 10^ 12 M_ concentrated within a radius r_c 10^2kpc .

gmw_00010382.name: source 0
gmw_00010382.overview: Source 0 is a massive, quiescent central cluster galaxy located at a redshift of z=1.322 [arXiv:2201.01304]. It is detected as a radio source and is blended with a neighboring source in 24-micron observations [arXiv:2201.01304].

sources.summary: Source 0 is the central quiescent cluster galaxy targeted by ALMA, blended with source 1 in broad catalogs, and detected as a radio and 24 micron source.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: ALMA ID 0, UltraVISTA ID 210589, RA 150.131380, Dec 2.523800

quotes.quote: The X-ray center is very close to a massive Log _ 10 M ^* /M _ =11.2 quiescent galaxy targeted with ALMA spectroscopy (hereafter referred to as source 0) in Williams2021 at z=1.322.

Unfortunately, given the proximity of source 0 (the central quiescent galaxy) and source 1, we are unable to use any published SFR for these two sources, as they are blended in the ULTRAVISTA, 3DHST, and VLA 3 GHz radio catalogs Muzzin2013, Skelton2014, Smolcic2017, Algera2020 .
The quiescent galaxy (source 0) is both a radio source, deblended by our radio photometry, and a blended 24 m source.

gmw_00010383.name: source 1
gmw_00010383.overview: Source 1 is a high-redshift, star-forming cluster member galaxy [arXiv:2201.01304]. It is located near a central quiescent galaxy and lies above the star-forming main sequence [arXiv:2201.01304].

sources.summary: Source 1 is a CO-detected cluster member near source 0, blended in broad catalogs, lying above the main sequence, and absent from COSMOS optical/infrared and radio catalogs.

quotes.quote: Unfortunately, given the proximity of source 0 (the central quiescent galaxy) and source 1, we are unable to use any published SFR for these two sources, as they are blended in the ULTRAVISTA, 3DHST, and VLA 3 GHz radio catalogs Muzzin2013, Skelton2014, Smolcic2017, Algera2020 .
Source 0 is well below the main sequence and its companion source 1 lies above the main sequence.
As source 1 was absent from optical/infrared and radio catalogs in COSMOS, it was not identified as a cluster member in the Goz2019 catalog.

gmw_00010384.name: source 7
gmw_00010384.overview: Source 7 is an optical spectroscopically confirmed galaxy located within a high-redshift cluster overdensity [arXiv:2201.01304]. It has a redshift of z=1.319 and remains undetected in CO emission [arXiv:2201.01304].

sources.summary: Source 7 supplied the original cluster redshift, is an existing optical spectroscopic member undetected in CO, and agrees with the recalculated systemic cluster redshift.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: ALMA ID 7, UltraVISTA ID 210442, RA 150.132310, Dec 2.523304

quotes.quote: A cluster redshift of z=1.319 was assigned based on the sole spectroscopic redshift among candidate cluster members referred to herein as source 7 Hasinger2018 .

Two existing optical spectroscopically-confirmed galaxies (undetected in CO) are associated with this overdensity: the galaxy associated with the group by Goz2019 measured by source 7 Hasinger2018 and our target quiescent galaxy source 0; Belli2014a,Williams2021 , in addition to our 6 new CO(2-1) sources.
We find a systemic redshift of z =1.3188 (with a dispersion of dz = 0.00245 or 317 km/s) essentially the same as that in the published catalog (based only on the source 7 redshift of z=1.319 ).

gmw_00010385.name: B3 1343+451
gmw_00010385.overview: B3 1343+451 is a high-redshift blazar and active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2008.09675]. It is a highly variable source that exhibits sub-day flares, hard-spectrum episodes, and peak luminosities reaching up to 1.5e50 erg/s [arXiv:2008.09675].

sources.summary: The paper finds B3 1343+451 is the sample's brightest and most variable blazar, with high detection significance, sub-day flares, hard-spectrum episodes, and luminosity rising to 1.5e50 erg/s.

quotes.quote: B3 1343+451 is detected with the highest significance of TS=34652.79 (186.15 ) , allowing to perform a detailed spectral and variability analysis.

B3 1343+451 is the brightest and most variable source in the sample, and in its adaptively binned light curve (Fig. lightcurve a) several bright emission periods can be identified: around MJD 55100, MJD 55890, MJD 56170, MJD 56640 and MJD 57060. During the bright flaring periods, the flux changes in sub-day scales; there are 200 adaptive bins with a high flux and a width shorter than a day; the minimum adaptively binned time width is 6.33 hours observed on MJD 56176.34 when the source flux was (7.52 1.85) 10^ 7 photon cm^ 2 s^ 1 (above 163.9 MeV).

B3 1343+451 is among the top 30 bright blazars observed by , showing multiple periods of enhanced emission when the average luminosity of (2-4) 10^ 48 erg s^ 1 increased up to 1.5 10^ 50 erg s^ 1 . The peak flux of (1.82 0.45) 10^ 6 photon cm^ 2 s^ 1 above 100 MeV was observed on MJD 55891.7 which is 36.4 times higher than the flux in quiescent state.

gmw_00010386.name: PKS 0451-28
gmw_00010386.overview: PKS 0451-28 is a luminous, bright X-ray blazar and active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2008.09675]. The galaxy exhibits gamma-ray flux increases on day-scale intervals and experiences variations in its photon index [arXiv:2008.09675].

sources.summary: The paper identifies PKS 0451-28 as a luminous, bright X-ray blazar whose gamma-ray flux rises on day timescales and whose photon index varies significantly.

quotes.quote: The adaptively binned light curves of PKS 0347-211 and PKS 0451-28 (Fig. lightcurve and d) show several periods of brightening, when a flux increase within day scales is observed. For example, the shortest time interval when the flux increases is 2.65 days for PKS 0347-211, and it is 1.56 days for PKS 0451-28.
In the case of PKS 0451-28, the peak flux of (2.20 0.50) 10^ 7 photon cm^ 2 s^ 1 (above 163.2 MeV) was observed on MJD 56968.60 0.79 with 9.64 .

The spectrum of PKS 0451-28 is usually soft but in the 30-day binned light curve the periods when _ =2.06 0.07 and _ =2.17 0.15 are observed on MJD 56977.66 15 and 58297.66 15 , respectively. The first period overlaps with the large flare evident in the adaptively binned light curve (see Fig. lightcurve a).

gmw_00010387.name: PKS 0347-211
gmw_00010387.overview: PKS 0347-211 is a high-redshift blazar and active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2008.09675]. Its gamma-ray light curve exhibits several flaring periods, including day-scale brightening events where the flux increases over intervals as short as 2.65 days [arXiv:2008.09675].

sources.summary: The paper reports PKS 0347-211 among the highest-luminosity blazars, with an adaptively binned light curve showing day-scale brightening and a measured peak gamma-ray flux.

quotes.quote: The adaptively binned light curves of PKS 0347-211 and PKS 0451-28 (Fig. lightcurve and d) show several periods of brightening, when a flux increase within day scales is observed. For example, the shortest time interval when the flux increases is 2.65 days for PKS 0347-211, and it is 1.56 days for PKS 0451-28. The light curves of both sources reveal several flaring periods when the flux substantially increased. For example, on MJD 54757.04 2.71 the flux of PKS 0347-211 above 187.4 MeV was (1.57 0.41) 10^ 7 photon cm^ 2 s^ 1 , which corresponds to a flux of (3.57 0.93) 10^ 7 photon cm^ 2 s^ 1 above 100 MeV.

gmw_00010388.name: PKS 0438-43
gmw_00010388.overview: PKS 0438-43 is a quasar that exhibits variable X-ray emission, a high X-ray flux, and week-scale gamma-ray variability [arXiv:astro-ph/0507418, arXiv:2008.09675]. The source also displays excess X-ray absorption above the Galactic contribution [arXiv:astro-ph/0507418].

sources.summary: The paper says B0438 436 shows significant excess X-ray absorption; an XMM model is consistent with a solar-metallicity absorber at the quasar redshift, making associated reddening plausible despite exceeding observed E(B-K).

quotes.quote: B0438 436 has long been known to exhibit significant excess (i.e. above the Galactic contribution) absorption. Evidence for this excess absorption has come from the analysis of ROSAT, ASCA and XMM observations (Serlemitsos et al. 1994; Elvis et al. 1994; Cappi et al. 1997; Brocksopp et al 2004). The best quality data so far are those obtained with XMM and the best-fit model (Brocksopp et al 2004) is consistent with a solar metallicity absorber with N(H) = 1.3 10^ 22 cm ^ 2 , which Brocksopp et al. assumed to be at the redshift of the QSO With sufficiently high quality data, it is possible to determine the absorption redshift.

If we assume that ionized and molecular gas is negligible, this column density translates into a E(B-K) 18.5 and 5 , for a Galactic and SMC reddening-to-dust ratio respectively and extinction curves from Pei (1992). These values are considerably higher than the E(B-K) observed for this source (see Figure norm_z ). While there are a number of explanations for this discrepancy (e.g. dustless gas near the QSO or AGN extinction curves that are significantly different from Local Group galaxies, e.g. Gaskell et al. 2004) a large reddening due to assoaciated absorption is very plausible.

sources.summary: The paper finds PKS 0438-43 has variable X-ray emission, a bright X-ray state, week-scale gamma-ray variability, and high X-ray flux relative to most sources.

quotes.quote: Instead, the 2 test shows that the X-ray emission of PKS 0438-43 and B2 0743+25 is variable with P( 2)<5.1 10^ 6 . PKS 0438-43 was in a bright X-ray state on 15 December 2016 with a flux of (1.09 0.16) 10^ 11 erg cm^ 2 s^ 1 as compared with the flux of (1.30 0.31) 10^ 11 erg cm^ 2 s^ 1 in the quiescent state.

For example, during MJD 57729.16 3.5 and MJD 57736.16 3.5 , the flux of PKS 0438-43 above 100 MeV increased 15.2-29.3 times as compared to its average flux and was (3.40 0.59) 10^ 7 photon cm^ 2 s^ 1 and (6.57 0.86) 10^ 7 photon cm^ 2 s^ 1 , respectively.

The 2 test showed that the emission of B3 0908+416B, TXS 0800+618, PKS 0438-43, OD 166 and TXS 0907+230 is variable in week scales while that of MG3 J163554+3629, GB6 J0733+0456, B2 0743+25, PMN J1441-1523 and TXS 1616+517 in month scales.

gmw_00010389.name: B2 0743+25
gmw_00010389.overview: B2 0743+25 is a high-redshift flat-spectrum radio quasar and MeV blazar [arXiv:1203.6071]. The galaxy exhibits substantial spectral curvature [arXiv:2008.09675], broad ultraviolet emission lines [arXiv:1203.6071], a bright X-ray flux [arXiv:2008.09675], and gamma-ray variability on monthly timescales [arXiv:2008.09675].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSSJ074625.86 + 254902.1 as a high-redshift flat-spectrum radio quasar and MeV blazar with broad UV emission lines unusual for a blazar.

quotes.quote: SDSSJ074625.86 + 254902.1 . This is a high redshift FSRQ mas09 with z=2.979 and an MeV blazar discovered by \ sam06,tue08,jol09 and thus in the BAT-selected AGN catalog by win09 . It is also observed by Suzaku wat09 . The UV spectrum contains broad emission lines such as , \ and , which is unusual for a blazar.

sources.summary: The paper reports B2 0743+25 has substantial spectral curvature, bright X-ray flux, a high X-ray state, and month-scale gamma-ray variability.

quotes.quote: The curvature of the spectra of PKS 1351-018, B2 0743+25, PKS 0438-43, S4 2015+65, PKS 0834-20, and OD 166 is substantial ( =0.24-0.63 ), so their spectrum quickly declines.
Similarly, the X-ray flux of B2 0743+25 in the high state on 01 January 2006 was (1.06 0.56) 10^ 11 erg cm^ 2 s^ 1 .

The 2 test showed that the emission of B3 0908+416B, TXS 0800+618, PKS 0438-43, OD 166 and TXS 0907+230 is variable in week scales while that of MG3 J163554+3629, GB6 J0733+0456, B2 0743+25, PMN J1441-1523 and TXS 1616+517 in month scales.

gmw_00010390.name: El Anzuelo
gmw_00010390.overview: El Anzuelo is a submillimeter galaxy that is heavily distorted by cluster-galaxy lensing into a fishhook shape [arXiv:2210.06514]. Its red nucleus is lensed into at least three images that coincide with ALMA sources, though the galaxy still lacks spectroscopic confirmation [arXiv:2210.06514].

sources.summary: The paper identifies El Anzuelo as a fishhook-shaped, cluster-lensed submillimeter galaxy whose red nucleus is split into multiple JWST and ALMA images, though it lacks spectroscopic confirmation.

quotes.quote: Figure Fig_AnzueloI shows one such galaxy, which is heavily distorted by cluster-galaxy lensing into a fishhook shape. We will refer to this galaxy as "El Anzuelo" (Spanish for fishhook). Pairs of multiply lensed features are easily identified in the image. Image pairs constrain the position of critical curves, which are expected to pass between the images.

The red nucleus of the extended background galaxy is lensed into at least three images clearly visible in the reddest JWST bands. The three images of the nucleus coincide with the position of ALMA sources EG-SMG 2 and EG-SMG 4 Cheng2022 , the latter containing two images barely resolved by ALMA . Previous HST data did not show El Anzuelo as multiply lensed because this galaxy is red and therefore faint at wavelengths shorter than 1.6 m.

Finally, ``El Anzuelo'' is the image of a submillimeter galaxy that can be studied in great detail because of the lensing but it is not modelled in detail here because it still lacks spectroscopic confirmation.

gmw_00010391.name: La Flaca
gmw_00010391.overview: La Flaca is a gravitationally lensed galaxy arc that spans 22 arcseconds in length [arXiv:2210.06514]. It features a multiply-lensed nucleus and knots that are split into additional images by small perturbers [arXiv:2210.06514].

sources.summary: The paper describes La Flaca as a 22-arcsecond lensed galaxy arc, visible before JWST but newly resolved into detailed multiple features, with perturbers producing extra images of its nucleus and knots.

quotes.quote: We refer to this galaxy as "La Flaca" ("the thin one" in Spanish). Unlike El Anzuelo, La Flaca is easily visible in HST data. The new JWST data show with greater detail the multiply-lensed features within this arc.

In particular, the nucleus of the arc appears three times (2.1a, 2.2a, and 2.4a) instead of the expected two times. The extra image (2.4a, already seen in HST data) is in fact a double image (unresolved) that appears as a consequence of a small perturber marked with a white arrow in the figure. Another perturber splits counterimage 2.2b into three images (2.2b, 2.4b, and 2.5b).

Another object of interest is the smallest substructure measured to date at z= 0.87 with an estimated mass of 3.8 10^9 , and that creates additional images in portions of ``La Flaca'', a lensed galaxy 22 \ long.

gmw_00010392.name: SDSS J1609+1756
gmw_00010392.overview: SDSS J1609+1756 is a quasar located at a redshift of z=0.347 [arXiv:2309.08078]. It exhibits robust optical quasi-periodic oscillations with a periodicity of approximately 340 days, which strongly indicates the presence of a central sub-parsec binary black hole system [arXiv:2309.08078].

sources.summary: The paper reports robust 340-day optical QPOs in quasar SDSS J1609+1756, arguing they are not intrinsic AGN noise and indicate a central sub-pc binary black hole.

quotes.quote: In this manuscript, robust optical QPOs are reported in quasar SDSS J1609+1756 at z=0.347 . In order to detect reliable optical QPOs, four different methods are applied to analyze the 4.45 years-long ZTF g//i-band light curves of , direct fitting results by sine function, Generalized Lomb-Scargle periodogram, Auto-Cross Correlation Function and Weighted Wavelet Z-transform method. The Four different methods can lead to well determined reliable optical QPOs with periodicities 340 days with confidence levels higher than 5 , to guarantee the robustness of the optical QPOs in . Meanwhile, based on simulated light curves through CAR process to trace intrinsic AGN activities, confidence level higher than 3 can be confirmed that the optical QPOs are not mis-detected in intrinsic AGN activities, re-confirming the robust optical QPOs and strongly indicating a central sub-pc BBH system in . Furthermore, based on apparent red-shifted shoulders in broad Balmer emission lines in , space separation of the expected central BBH system can be estimated to be smaller than 107 60 light-days, accepted upper limit of total BH mass (1.03 0.22) 10^8 M_ .

gmw_00010393.name: NGC 6032
gmw_00010393.overview: NGC 6032 is a barred spiral galaxy classified as SBb [arXiv:1706.00766]. Its morphological structures include a bulge, a disc, and an old bar that formed approximately 10 Gyr ago [arXiv:1706.00766].

sources.summary: The paper uses CALIFA spectroscopy to study NGC 6032, an SBb barred spiral, finding distinct stellar age distributions and an old bar formed about 10 Gyr ago with later buckling.

quotes.quote: In this letter we present the analysis of the stellar age distributions (SADs) of different regions of the galaxy NGC 6032 for which we have integral field spectroscopic CALIFA data 2012A&A...538A...8S . NGC 6032 is a spiral galaxy classified as SBb with a total stellar mass of 2.6 10^ 10 M_ as derived from the SDSS colours 2013A&A...554A..58S . The analysed morphological structures comprise the bulge, the bar and the disc, focusing particularly on the fine-structures of the bar. The bar in this galaxy shows a barlens structure, as described in 2011MNRAS.418.1452L , and some light enhancements at the ends of it (see Fig. fig:light ), and therefore it is a perfect candidate to explore bar evolution.

We establish that the bar of NGC 6032 is old, formed around 10 Gyr ago while the buckling phase possibly happened around 8 Gyr ago. All these results point towards bars being long-lasting even in the presence of gas.

gmw_00010394.name: SDSS J033322.65 000907.5
gmw_00010394.overview: SDSS J033322.65 000907.5 is classified as a passive spiral galaxy [arXiv:0801.1108]. Its inner and outer regions lack [O II] emission lines, while the outer region shows weak H-alpha emission and the inner region contains a higher proportion of older stars [arXiv:0801.1108].

sources.summary: The paper describes SDSS J033322.65 000907.5 as a passive spiral whose inner and outer spectra lack [O II], show weak H emission, and indicate older inner stellar populations.

quotes.quote: As shown in Fig. 3, Balmer absorption lines, Ca and Fe absorption lines and deep absorption in G4300 bands are observed for both AP _ out and AP _ in in SDSSJ0333 0009. The [ O II ] emission line was not detected for either AP _ out or AP _ in , which ensures the passive nature of this galaxy. The H emission line, which is also an indicator of current star formation was, however, weakly detected for AP _ out with an equivalent width of 3 .

H _ A absorption is stronger for AP _ out than that for AP _ in by 1.6 (Table 2). By contrast, the D _ 4000 break is larger for AP _ in than it is for AP _ out by 0.07 (see Fig. 5). These results suggest that there is a higher proportion of old stars in the inner than there is in the outer regions for these galaxies.

gmw_00010395.name: SDSS J074452.52 + 373852.7
gmw_00010395.overview: SDSS J074452.52 + 373852.7 is a passive spiral galaxy with a spectrum dominated by strong Balmer absorption [arXiv:0801.1108]. The galaxy's outer regions host a younger stellar population than its inner regions, while weak hydrogen-alpha emission indicates a lack of prominent star formation activity throughout [arXiv:0801.1108].

sources.summary: The paper describes SDSS J074452.52 + 373852.7 as a passive spiral with strong Balmer absorption, younger outer stellar populations, and weak H emission in both inner and outer regions.

quotes.quote: As shown in Fig. 4, the spectrum of SDSSJ0744 + 3738 is dominated by the Balmar absorptions. The metal absorptions and the deep absorption in the G4300 band look similar to those of SDSSJ0333 0009, but the difference in the equivalent widths of observed features between AP _ in and AP _ out is larger: H _ F absorption is stronger for AP _ out than for AP _ in by 4.0 as shown in Table 2, and the H absorption is stronger for AP _ out by 3.5 0.5 . D _ 4000 is larger for AP _ in than for AP _ out by 0.5 (Table 2).

These results indicate that the stellar population is younger in the outer regions, similar to the situation for the previous galaxy. The equivalent width of the H emission line is found to be weak: 2.3 0.5 for AP _ in and 3.4 0.6 for AP _ out . The weakness of the H emission lines suggests the lack of prominent star formation activity in both the inner and the outer regions of this galaxy.

gmw_00010396.name: object at z=6.629
aliases_external: z=6.629 Ly-alpha arclet in MACS J0416
gmw_00010396.overview: The object at z=6.629 is a strongly lensed high-redshift source crossing the caustic of the galaxy cluster MACS J0416 [arXiv:2001.03619]. It exhibits a very high Lyman-alpha equivalent width and low luminosity, which may indicate the presence of a Population III star complex [arXiv:2001.03619].

sources.summary: The paper presents a strongly lensed z=6.629 source crossing a cluster caustic, with faint continuum, very high equivalent width, and properties consistent with a Population III star complex.

quotes.quote: In this letter we present an object at z=6.629 showing (1) the faintest \ emission ever detected at z>6 crossing the caustic of the Hubble Frontier Field (HFF) galaxy cluster MACS J0416 lotz17 and (2) a large \ EW _ 0 , potentially implying that extreme stellar populations are present.

We discovered a strongly lensed ( 40 ) \ emission at z=6.629 (S/N 18 ) in the MUSE Deep Lensed Field (MDLF) targeting the Hubble Frontier Field galaxy cluster MACS J0416. Dedicated lensing simulations imply that the \ emitting region necessarily crosses the caustic. The arc-like shape of the \ extends 3 '' on the observed plane and is the result of two merged multiple images, each one with a de-lensed \ luminosity L 2.8 10^ 40 \ arising from a confined region ( 150 pc effective radius). A spatially unresolved HST counterpart is barely detected at S/N 2 after stacking the near-infrared bands, corresponding to an observed(intrinsic) magnitude m_ 1500 30.8( 35.0) . The inferred rest-frame \ equivalent width is EW _ 0 > 1120 if the IGM transmission is T _ IGM <0.5 .
The low luminosities and the extremely large \ EW _ 0 match the case of a Population III star complex made of several dozens stars ( 10^ 4 ) which irradiate a \ region crossing the caustic.

gmw_00010397.name: J0811+4730
gmw_00010397.overview: J0811+4730 is a low-redshift star-forming dwarf galaxy [arXiv:2108.03850, arXiv:1902.01775]. It is characterized by an extremely low metallicity, exhibiting the lowest luminosity-weighted oxygen abundance known among low-redshift systems [arXiv:1902.01775], alongside a high iron-to-oxygen ratio [arXiv:2108.03850].

sources.summary: The paper discusses J0811+4730 as an EMPG with about 2 percent solar oxygen abundance and high Fe/O, motivating hypernova or pair-instability enrichment models.

quotes.quote: Almeida2016 have found 196 EMPGs from the SDSS spectroscopic data, and Izotov2018 have identified J0811+4730 with a metallicity down to 2 (O/H) _ . While J1631+4426 (Paper I) and J0811+4730 Izotov2018 have extremely low-metallicities of 2 (O/H) _ , Kojima2021 ( Kojima2021 ; hereafter Paper II) have reported that the 2 EMPGs show high Fe/O ratios of ( Fe/O)_ . Paper II concludes that the 2 EMPGs are too young to be affected by chemical enrichment of low- and intermediate-mass stars because the EMPGs have low N/O ratios.

If the z 0 IGM also has a Fe/O value of [ Fe/O ] 0.4 , we need some events such as SN explosions that enhance Fe/O ratios by 0.4 dex to explain high Fe/O ratios of J1631+4426 and J0811+4730. In this section, we revisit the origin of the high Fe/O ratio of J1631+4426 and J0811+4730 with 2 (O/H) _ , investigating possible contributors of HNe and PISNe that have not been discussed in Paper II (Section sec:intro ).

sources.summary: J0811 + 4730 is highlighted as an SDSS star-forming galaxy with the lowest known luminosity-weighted oxygen abundance among low-redshift systems.

quotes.quote: These are J0811 + 4730 with 12 + logO/H = 6.98 0.02 I18 , A198691 with 12 + logO/H = 7.02 0.03 H16 and SBS 0335 052W with 12 + logO/H ranging from 6.86 to 7.22 in different star-forming regions I09 .
It is also worth to mention SFG J0811 + 4730 because of showing the lowest luminosity-weighted oxygen abundance 12 + logO/H = 6.98 0.02 known so far filled star in Fig. fig1 a; I18 .

Two of these galaxies, J0811 + 4730 (see also Sect. sec:method ) and J1234 + 3901 are very close to or below the luminosity-weighted value 12 + logO/H = 7.0, representing the lowest-metallicity galaxies known so far. In fact, using Large Binocular Telescope observations that have high signal-to-noise ratio I18 have confirmed that J0811 + 4730 has the lowest luminosity-weighted metallicity among all low-redshift SFGs with z 0.1 I18 .

gmw_00010398.name: SECCO 1
gmw_00010398.overview: SECCO 1 is an extremely low-mass, gas-rich, and metal-rich stellar system located in the Virgo cluster [arXiv:2205.01695]. Despite its active star formation, the system is extraordinarily isolated, leaving its exact origin and parent object uncertain [arXiv:2205.01695].

sources.summary: The paper uses SECCO 1 as the prototype blue stellar system: a young, metal-rich, gas-rich Virgo object likely formed from stripped gas but with uncertain parentage.

quotes.quote: However, attempts to distinguish these objects have led to surprising discoveries, most notably SECCO 1 also called AGC 226067; Adams+2013,Bellazzini+2015,Sand+2015,Adams+2015,Beccari+2017,Sand+2017,Bellazzini+2018 and AGC 226178 Cannon+2015,Junais+2021,Jones+2022 . These are two young, blue, extremely low-mass ( M_ 10^5 ), gas-rich, metal-rich, actively star-forming stellar systems in the Virgo cluster. AGC 226178 has a gas-to-stellar mass ratio (1.4M_ HI / M_ ) 1000 , while SECCO 1 has a ratio of 150.

In the case of SECCO 1, despite it being relatively close to the Virgo cluster center, it is still sufficiently isolated that its origin is uncertain, and multiple possible parent objects have been suggested Sand+2017,Bellazzini+2018 . SECCO 1 and AGC 226178 were originally identified through their \ line emission, thereby guaranteeing gas richness. Finally, we consider SECCO 1. Figure fig:SECCO1_NN shows the neighbors of SECCO 1 within a 4 deg ^2 field and 500 , and demonstrates the extraordinary isolation of this system given that it is within the virial radius of a cluster.

gmw_00010399.name: BC1
gmw_00010399.overview: BC1 is a young, blue stellar system located in the Virgo cluster [arXiv:2205.01695]. It exhibits hydrogen-alpha emission across multiple distinct clumps, though it lacks detected neutral gas and has no clear parent galaxy [arXiv:2205.01695].

sources.summary: The paper finds BC1 is a young, blue Virgo stellar system with H emission, no detected neutral gas, and no viable nearby parent galaxy.

quotes.quote: BC1 is the closest, with a projected separation of approximately 600 kpc. MUSE detected H emission in all BCs, identifying between 4 and 18 distinct clumps of emission in each object Bellazzini+2022 . All the BCs are extremely high metallicity given their very low stellar masses ( sec:stellarpops ), which suggests that they formed from gas pre-enriched in more massive objects.

BC1's CMD (Figure fig:BC1_HST_GLX , bottom) is similar, but the brightest RHeB stars are more numerous and fainter than in BC3. These slight differences likely indicate that BC1 is somewhat older than BC3 as RHeB peak brightness is a function of age, e.g. McQuinn+2011 , which would be consistent with its non-detection in , if sufficient time has passed for its neutral gas to have been evaporated or stripped. Thus we are left with no strong candidate that matches the properties of BC1.

gmw_00010400.name: BC3
gmw_00010400.overview: BC3 is a dwarf system that contains a reservoir of neutral hydrogen [arXiv:2205.01695]. This gas reservoir appears to connect to the galaxy VCC 2034, which is almost certainly the source of the gas that formed BC3 [arXiv:2205.01695].

sources.summary: The paper treats BC3, also AGC 226178, as the SECCO-1-like system with a significant H I reservoir likely connected to stripped gas from VCC 2034.

quotes.quote: The object we refer to as BC3 is an independent re-identification (based on optical appearance) of the selected object AGC 226178 from the ALFALFA survey Haynes+2011,Cannon+2015 . This object has already been studied in detail Cannon+2015,Junais+2021,Jones+2022 and is the BC most similar to SECCO 1. Like SECCO 1, BC3 was known a priori to contain a significant \ reservoir as it was originally identified in the ALFALFA survey Haynes+2011 .

Based on the VLA spectrum (Figure fig:BC3_VLA_specs , extracted using the SoFiA source mask), and an assumed distance of 16.5 Mpc, BC3 has an \ mass of M_ HI / M_ = 7.3 . Jones+2022 show that when viewed in the ALFALFA data cube (which has better column density sensitivity for extended emission than the VLA observations) the \ emission coincident with BC3 appears to connect to the galaxy VCC 2034, approximately 70 kpc to the SW. This galaxy is almost certainly the source of the gas that formed BC3 discussed further in sec:discuss , and Jones+2022 .

gmw_00010401.name: BC4
gmw_00010401.overview: BC4 is a very low-mass, clumpy Virgo system composed of six components spread over 1.5 arcminutes [arXiv:2205.01695]. It has a marginally super-solar metallicity and likely shares a common origin with the nearby object BC5 [arXiv:2205.01695].

sources.summary: The paper describes BC4 as a very low-mass, clumpy, metal-rich Virgo system with little detected gas and a likely shared stripped-gas origin with BC5.

quotes.quote: The six components of BC4 are spread over 1.5 \ ( 7 kpc) and may indicate that this is either a very young collection of objects formed in a gas-rich stream, or a somewhat older object that has become gravitationally unbound. Of particular note are BC4 and 5, both of which are found to be marginally super-solar in metallicity 12 + ( O/H )_ = 8.69 , Asplund+2009 . For BC4 and BC5 the deepest data are those from the VLA, which have rms noise values of 0.9 and 1.0 mJy/beam (at 8.8 \ resolution), respectively, at the velocities of the H emission.

BC4 and BC5 likely formed from the same parent object as they are only separated by 45 \ on the sky, are at almost the same velocity (Table tab:metallicity ), have nearly identical metallicity measurements (Table tab:metallicity ), and have similar age estimates ( sec:stellarmasses ). However, as ram pressure tails only extend in one general direction (in the wake of a galaxy's motion through the ICM) and BC4 is to the south of NGC 4419 and BC5 is to the north, it is extremely unlikely that this is the point of origin of these BCs.

gmw_00010402.name: BC5
gmw_00010402.overview: BC5 is an extremely low-mass, clumpy dwarf galaxy located in the Virgo system [arXiv:2205.01695]. It has a marginally super-solar metallicity and likely shares a common origin with the nearby galaxy BC4 [arXiv:2205.01695].

sources.summary: The paper describes BC5 as an extremely low-mass, clumpy, metal-rich Virgo system with weak or absent H I and a likely common origin with BC4.

quotes.quote: The smallest components (e.g. BC3c, BC5b, BC5c) are less than 5 \ ( 400 pc) across and may only consist of a single cluster of stars. Of particular note are BC4 and 5, both of which are found to be marginally super-solar in metallicity 12 + ( O/H )_ = 8.69 , Asplund+2009 . The spectrum of BC5 (Figure fig:BC45_VLA_specs , bottom) also appears to have a peak coincident with the H velocity of BC5.

However, this peak is below 3 and extremely narrow, and is likely a noise spike. BC4 and BC5 likely formed from the same parent object as they are only separated by 45 \ on the sky, are at almost the same velocity (Table tab:metallicity ), have nearly identical metallicity measurements (Table tab:metallicity ), and have similar age estimates ( sec:stellarmasses ).

gmw_00010403.name: VCC 2034
gmw_00010403.overview: VCC 2034 is a galaxy located approximately 70 kiloparsecs southwest of the object BC3 [arXiv:2205.01695]. It is identified as the likely source of BC3's neutral hydrogen gas, which is connected via a possible gas bridge [arXiv:2205.01695].

sources.summary: The paper identifies VCC 2034 as the likely source of BC3’s gas through an H I bridge, while leaving the stripping mechanism unresolved.

quotes.quote: \ observations with the VLA Cannon+2015 and Arecibo Giovanelli+2005,Haynes+2011,Minchin+2019 indicate a possible bridge between BC3 and a pair of galaxies, VCC 2034 and 2037. However, the closer (in projection) of these, VCC 2037, is actually another foreground object at approximately 10 Mpc Karachentsev+2014 . Thus, VCC 2034 ( cz_ = 1507 ), 70 kpc to the SW, is the likely source of BC3's \ gas. However, Jones+2022 were unable to determine whether ram pressure or tidal stripping was responsible for removing the gas from VCC 2034.

gmw_00010404.name: GN 42437
gmw_00010404.overview: GN 42437 is a compact, low-mass extreme starburst galaxy located at a redshift of z=5.59 [arXiv:2402.18643]. Its complete nebular structure requires both massive stars and accretion onto a black hole, though it lacks broad-line active galactic nucleus signatures [arXiv:2402.18643].

sources.summary: The paper reports GN 42437 as a compact z=5.59 extreme starburst whose [Ne V] emission requires stars plus black-hole accretion without broad-line AGN signatures.

quotes.quote: Here we present high spectral resolution JWST NIRSpec observations of GN 42437, a low-mass (log(M _ /M_ )=7.9 ), compact ( r_e < 500 pc), extreme starburst galaxy at z=5.59 with 13 emission line detections. GN 42437 has a low-metallicity (5-10 Z _ ) and its rest-frame H equivalent width suggests nearly all of the observed stellar mass formed within the last 3 Myr. GN 42437 has an extraordinary 7 significant [ Ne V ] 3427 \ detection.

Ionization from massive stars, shocks, or high-mass X-ray binaries cannot simultaneously produce these [ Ne v ] and other low-ionization line ratios. Reproducing the complete nebular structure requires both massive stars and accretion onto a black hole. We do not detect broad lines nor do the traditional diagnostics indicate that GN42437 has an accreting black hole.

gmw_00010405.name: SSA22a-C16
gmw_00010405.overview: SSA22a-C16 is a Lyman-break galaxy at a redshift of z=3.065 [arXiv:1010.2534]. It features a rotationally supported disk with symmetric ionized-gas kinematics and contains an offset region of lower metallicity that coincides with active star formation [arXiv:1010.2534].

sources.summary: SSA22a-C16 is one of three z 3 Lyman-break galaxies with symmetric ionized-gas kinematics, a rotationally supported disk, and a lower-metallicity region offset from its center.

quotes.quote: We selected three Lyman-break galaxies among the AMAZE and LSD sample, SSA22a-C16 ( z=3.065 ), CDFa-C9 ( z=3.219 ) and SSA22a-M38 ( z=3.288 ), to test the presence of metallicity gradients in galaxies beyond z=3 , when the Universe was only 14-16 of its actual age, and only a small fraction ( 5-10 ) of the total stellar mass in the Universe had been assembled. This epoch is particularly interesting, as it precedes the peak of the cosmic star formation density . We selected these three galaxies because they show a remarkably symmetric velocity field in the [ OIII ] emission line, which traces the ionized gas kinematics (see Fig. figmet ). Such kinematics indicates that these are rotationally supported disks (Gnerucci et al., in preparation), with no evidence for more complex merger-induced dynamics.

The derived metallicity maps are shown in Fig. figmet . An unresolved region with lower metallicity is evident in each map, surrounded by a more uniform disk of higher metal content. In CDFa-C9 the lower metallicity region is coincident with the galaxy center, as traced by the continuum peak, while it is offset by 0.60'' (4.6 kpc) in SS22a-C16 and 0.45'' (3.4 kpc) in SS22a-M38. On the other hand, in all the galaxies the area of lower metallicity is coincident or closer than 0.25'' (1.9 kpc, which is half the point spread function full-width at half-maximum) to the regions of enhanced line emission, which trace the more active star forming regions.

gmw_00010406.name: CDFa-C9
gmw_00010406.overview: CDFa-C9 is a Lyman-break galaxy at a redshift of z = 3.219 [arXiv:1010.2534]. It features a rotationally supported disk with symmetric ionized-gas kinematics and a central low-metallicity region that coincides with enhanced star formation [arXiv:1010.2534].

sources.summary: CDFa-C9 is a z 3.219 Lyman-break galaxy with symmetric ionized-gas kinematics, a rotationally supported disk, and a central low-metallicity region coincident with enhanced star formation.

quotes.quote: We selected three Lyman-break galaxies among the AMAZE and LSD sample, SSA22a-C16 ( z=3.065 ), CDFa-C9 ( z=3.219 ) and SSA22a-M38 ( z=3.288 ), to test the presence of metallicity gradients in galaxies beyond z=3 , when the Universe was only 14-16 of its actual age, and only a small fraction ( 5-10 ) of the total stellar mass in the Universe had been assembled. This epoch is particularly interesting, as it precedes the peak of the cosmic star formation density . The derived metallicity maps are shown in Fig. figmet . An unresolved region with lower metallicity is evident in each map, surrounded by a more uniform disk of higher metal content. In CDFa-C9 the lower metallicity region is coincident with the galaxy center, as traced by the continuum peak, while it is offset by 0.60'' (4.6 kpc) in SS22a-C16 and 0.45'' (3.4 kpc) in SS22a-M38. gmw_00010407.name: SSA22a-M38
gmw_00010407.overview: SSA22a-M38 is a Lyman-break galaxy at a redshift of z=3.288 [arXiv:1010.2534]. It features a rotationally supported disk with symmetric ionized-gas kinematics and contains an offset, lower-metallicity region associated with active star formation [arXiv:1010.2534].

sources.summary: SSA22a-M38 is one of three z 3 Lyman-break galaxies with symmetric ionized-gas kinematics, a rotationally supported disk, and a lower-metallicity region offset from its center.

quotes.quote: We selected three Lyman-break galaxies among the AMAZE and LSD sample, SSA22a-C16 ( z=3.065 ), CDFa-C9 ( z=3.219 ) and SSA22a-M38 ( z=3.288 ), to test the presence of metallicity gradients in galaxies beyond z=3 , when the Universe was only 14-16 of its actual age, and only a small fraction ( 5-10 ) of the total stellar mass in the Universe had been assembled. This epoch is particularly interesting, as it precedes the peak of the cosmic star formation density . We selected these three galaxies because they show a remarkably symmetric velocity field in the [ OIII ] emission line, which traces the ionized gas kinematics (see Fig. figmet ). Such kinematics indicates that these are rotationally supported disks (Gnerucci et al., in preparation), with no evidence for more complex merger-induced dynamics.

The derived metallicity maps are shown in Fig. figmet . An unresolved region with lower metallicity is evident in each map, surrounded by a more uniform disk of higher metal content. gmw_00010408.name: VVV-J144321.06-611753.9
gmw_00010408.overview: VVV-J144321.06-611753.9 is an early-type galaxy with an estimated redshift of z = 0.234 [arXiv:1901.11097]. It is the brightest galaxy in its cluster and lies on the cluster red sequence [arXiv:1901.11097].

sources.summary: VVV-J144321.06-611753.9 is the brightest early-type cluster galaxy observed with FLAMINGOS-2; its spectrum gives z = 0.234 and places it on the cluster red sequence.

quotes.quote: Follow-up near-IR spectroscopy with FLAMINGOS-2 at the Gemini-South telescope revealed that the two brighter cluster galaxies have typical spectra of early-type galaxies and the estimated redshift for the brightest galaxy VVV-J144321.06-611753.9 is z = 0.234 0.022 and for VVV-J144319.02-611746.1 is z = 0.232 0.019. Finally, these galaxies clearly follow the cluster Red Sequence in the rest-frame near-IR color--magnitude diagram with the slope similar to galaxy cluster at redshift of 0.2.

The brighter galaxies VVV-J144321.06-611753.9 and VVV-J144319.02-611746.1 were observed in a single pointing. The spectra were flux calibrated and then normalized to the photometry aperture fluxes, in order to be able to perform the dust reddening correction. The final spectra had S/N 9.5 enough to obtain a precise redshift estimation. Figure spec shows the spectra of the two galaxies. The solid lines represent the observed spectra with the continuum highly affected by the MW disk dust extinction, making the identification of the spectral features difficult.

gmw_00010409.name: VVV-J144319.02-611746.1
gmw_00010409.overview: VVV-J144319.02-611746.1 is an early-type galaxy located at a redshift of approximately z = 0.232 [arXiv:1901.11097]. It is one of the brighter members of its host galaxy cluster and follows the cluster red sequence [arXiv:1901.11097].

sources.summary: VVV-J144319.02-611746.1 is one of the two brighter early-type cluster galaxies observed with FLAMINGOS-2; its spectrum gives z = 0.232 and it follows the cluster red sequence.

quotes.quote: Follow-up near-IR spectroscopy with FLAMINGOS-2 at the Gemini-South telescope revealed that the two brighter cluster galaxies have typical spectra of early-type galaxies and the estimated redshift for the brightest galaxy VVV-J144321.06-611753.9 is z = 0.234 0.022 and for VVV-J144319.02-611746.1 is z = 0.232 0.019. Finally, these galaxies clearly follow the cluster Red Sequence in the rest-frame near-IR color--magnitude diagram with the slope similar to galaxy cluster at redshift of 0.2.

The brighter galaxies VVV-J144321.06-611753.9 and VVV-J144319.02-611746.1 were observed in a single pointing. The spectra were flux calibrated and then normalized to the photometry aperture fluxes, in order to be able to perform the dust reddening correction. The final spectra had S/N 9.5 enough to obtain a precise redshift estimation. Figure spec shows the spectra of the two galaxies. The solid lines represent the observed spectra with the continuum highly affected by the MW disk dust extinction, making the identification of the spectral features difficult.

gmw_00010410.name: RGB J0152+017
gmw_00010410.overview: RGB J0152+017 is an active galaxy classified as a high-frequency-peaked BL Lacertae object located at a redshift of z=0.080 [arXiv:0802.4021]. It is a source of very high-energy gamma rays, and its optical emission is dominated by a thermal host galaxy component [arXiv:0802.4021].

sources.summary: The paper reports H.E.S.S. VHE gamma-ray detection of RGB J0152+017, modeling its SED as a high-frequency-peaked BL Lac with an elliptical host component.

quotes.quote: Nedbal mailto:jean-philippe.lenain@obspm.fr jean-philippe.lenain@obspm.fr , mailto:dalibor.nedbal@mpi-hd.mpg.de dalibor.nedbal@mpi-hd.mpg.de Received 19 February 2008 / Accepted 26 February 2008 The BL Lac object \ ( z=0.080 ) was predicted to be a very high-energy (VHE; > 100 GeV) ray source, due to its high X-ray and radio fluxes. Our aim is to understand the radiative processes by investigating the observed emission and its production mechanism using the High Energy Stereoscopic System (H.E.S.S.) experiment. We report recent observations of the BL Lac source \ made in late October and November 2007 with the H.E.S.S. array consisting of four imaging atmospheric Cherenkov telescopes.

The source spectral energy distribution (SED) can be described using a two-component non-thermal synchrotron self-Compton (SSC) leptonic model, except in the optical band, which is dominated by a thermal host galaxy component. The parameters that are found are very close to those found in similar SSC studies in TeV blazars. \ is discovered as a source of VHE rays by H.E.S.S. The location of its synchrotron peak, as derived from the SED in Swift data, allows clear classification as a high-frequency-peaked BL Lac (HBL). galaxies: BL Lacertae objects: individual: RGB J0152+017 - gamma rays: observations -- galaxies: BL Lacertae objects: general -- galaxies: active First detected as a radio source 1991ApJS...75....1B by the NRAO Green Bank Telescope and in the Parkes-MIT-NRAO surveys 1995ApJS...97..347G , \ was later identified as a BL Lac object by 1998ApJS..118..127L , who located it at z=0.080 , and was claimed as an intermediate-frequency-peaked BL Lac object by 1999ApJ...525..127L .

gmw_00010411.name: NGC 4753
gmw_00010411.overview: NGC 4753 is a bright lenticular galaxy located in a low-density environment [arXiv:1508.07653]. Its complex structure features dust filaments, isophote twisting, and remnants of spiral arms, which suggest a merger occurred 1 to 3 billion years ago [arXiv:1508.07653].

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 4753 as a bright lenticular galaxy with a poor, peculiar globular-cluster system, substructure, dust lanes, SNe Ia, and merger evidence.

quotes.quote: NGC 4753 is a bright ( M_V −22.3 ) lenticular galaxy. It is a very interesting target to test different theories of formation of lenticular galaxies, due to its low-density environment and complex structure. We perform the first comprehensive study of NGC4753 globular cluster system (GCS), using Gemini/GMOS and CTIO/MosaicII images.

The GC specific frequency S_N = 1.3 0.15 is surprisingly low for a galaxy of its class. The GC luminosity function (GCLF) is also peculiar, with an excess of bright GCs compared to the expected gaussian distribution. The underlying galaxy body has significant substructure, with remnants of spiral arms, dust filaments, and isophote twisting. This, and the fact that NGC4753 hosted two type Ia SNe, support the possibility that the intermediate GC subpopulation may have originated during a recent merger, 1−3 Gyr ago. galaxies: star clusters: individual: NGC 4753 - galaxies: elliptical and lenticular, cD Despite that the fraction of lenticular galaxies (S0s) seems to increase with the number of neighbours wil12b , the existence of S0s in low density environments poses questions about their origin.

gmw_00010412.name: Car2
gmw_00010412.overview: Car2 is a dwarf companion galaxy in the Local Group [arXiv:2001.01746]. It is classified as a long-term Magellanic satellite that is gravitationally bound to the Large Magellanic Cloud, having completed an average of two bound orbits around it over the last 2.5 to 3 billion years [arXiv:2001.01746].

sources.summary: The paper identifies Car2 as a fiducial long-term Magellanic satellite, bound to the LMC and completing two bound orbits on average in the last 2.5-3 Gyr.

quotes.quote: Criterion 3 : Of the satellites that remain (Car2, Car3, Hor1, Hyi1, Phx2, Ret2), all six are to the left of the dashed blue line and below the solid blue line, indicating that they are bound to the LMC. Each satellite completes a recent passage around the LMC in the last 0.5 Gyr. To further separate these satellites into those that only recently passed around the LMC once versus those that may have completed multiple tightly bound orbits around the LMC, the right side of Fig. fig:orbparams illustrates the same quantities for the second to last pericentric passage. By applying Criterion 1 and Criterion 2 to the orbital properties at the second to last pericentric passage, Car2, Car3, Hor1, and Hyi1 remain. These satellites are therefore designated long-term Magellanic satellites since they complete two bound orbits on average around the LMC in the last 2.5-3 Gyr.

By evaluating the statistical significance of orbital properties calculated relative to the LMC in a combined LMC+SMC+MW potential, we separate galaxies into the following classes: 1) long-term Magellanic satellites, 2) recently captured Magellanic satellites, 3) MW satellites that have recently interacted with the MCs, and 4) MW satellites. For the fiducial LMC model (LMC2), Car2, Car3, Hor1, and Hyi1 are identified as long-term satellites, while Ret2 and Phx2 are recently captured Magellanic satellites (see Table tab:summary_sats_frac ).

gmw_00010413.overview: Car3 is a dwarf companion galaxy located in the Local Group [arXiv:2001.01746]. It is classified as a long-term Magellanic satellite that is gravitationally bound to the Large Magellanic Cloud [arXiv:2001.01746].

sources.summary: The paper identifies Car3 as a long-term Magellanic satellite, bound to the LMC and robustly retained among early-bound satellites across its criteria.

gmw_00010414.overview: Hor1 is classified as a dwarf companion galaxy in the Local Group [arXiv:2001.01746]. It is identified as a long-term Magellanic satellite that is bound to the Large Magellanic Cloud [arXiv:2001.01746].

sources.summary: The paper identifies Hor1 as a long-term Magellanic satellite, with LMC-perturbed direct orbits and two bound LMC passages in the favored classification.

gmw_00010415.overview: Hyi1 is a dwarf companion galaxy in the Local Group [arXiv:2001.01746]. It is classified as a long-term Magellanic satellite that is bound to the Large Magellanic Cloud [arXiv:2001.01746].

sources.summary: The paper identifies Hyi1 as a long-term Magellanic satellite, strongly perturbed by the LMC and consistently classified as early-bound in the combined-potential model.

gmw_00010416.name: Ret2
gmw_00010416.overview: Ret2 is classified as a dwarf companion galaxy in the Local Group [arXiv:2001.01746]. Under the fiducial model, it is identified as a recently captured Magellanic satellite that is bound to the Large Magellanic Cloud [arXiv:2001.01746].

sources.summary: The paper classifies Ret2 as a recently captured Magellanic satellite in the fiducial model and notes it remains recently captured when proper-motion errors are reduced.

quotes.quote: Criterion 3 : Of the satellites that remain (Car2, Car3, Hor1, Hyi1, Phx2, Ret2), all six are to the left of the dashed blue line and below the solid blue line, indicating that they are bound to the LMC. Each satellite completes a recent passage around the LMC in the last 0.5 Gyr.
Ret2 and Phx2 are classified as recently captured Magellanic satellites since they only complete one bound orbit around the MCs in the last 1 Gyr.

By evaluating the statistical significance of orbital properties calculated relative to the LMC in a combined LMC+SMC+MW potential, we separate galaxies into the following classes: 1) long-term Magellanic satellites, 2) recently captured Magellanic satellites, 3) MW satellites that have recently interacted with the MCs, and 4) MW satellites. For the fiducial LMC model (LMC2), Car2, Car3, Hor1, and Hyi1 are identified as long-term satellites, while Ret2 and Phx2 are recently captured Magellanic satellites (see Table tab:summary_sats_frac ).

gmw_00010417.name: Phx2
gmw_00010417.overview: Phx2 is a dwarf companion galaxy in the Local Group [arXiv:2001.01746]. Under the fiducial Large Magellanic Cloud model, it is classified as a recently captured Magellanic satellite that completed a bound orbit around the Magellanic Clouds within the last 1 Gyr [arXiv:2001.01746].

sources.summary: The paper classifies Phx2 as a recently captured Magellanic satellite in the fiducial model, while smaller proper-motion uncertainties could make it long-term.

quotes.quote: Criterion 3 : Of the satellites that remain (Car2, Car3, Hor1, Hyi1, Phx2, Ret2), all six are to the left of the dashed blue line and below the solid blue line, indicating that they are bound to the LMC. Each satellite completes a recent passage around the LMC in the last 0.5 Gyr.
Ret2 and Phx2 are classified as recently captured Magellanic satellites since they only complete one bound orbit around the MCs in the last 1 Gyr.

By evaluating the statistical significance of orbital properties calculated relative to the LMC in a combined LMC+SMC+MW potential, we separate galaxies into the following classes: 1) long-term Magellanic satellites, 2) recently captured Magellanic satellites, 3) MW satellites that have recently interacted with the MCs, and 4) MW satellites. For the fiducial LMC model (LMC2), Car2, Car3, Hor1, and Hyi1 are identified as long-term satellites, while Ret2 and Phx2 are recently captured Magellanic satellites (see Table tab:summary_sats_frac ).

gmw_00010418.name: Tuc3
gmw_00010418.overview: Tuc3 is a Milky Way satellite galaxy that orbits within 50 kpc of the Galactic Center [arXiv:2001.01746]. It has recently interacted with the Magellanic Clouds, which is consistent with models of its stellar stream formation [arXiv:2001.01746].

sources.summary: The paper classifies Tuc3 as a Milky Way satellite that recently interacted with the Magellanic Clouds, consistent with models of its stellar stream formation.

quotes.quote: By doing so, the following galaxies remain: Seg1, Tuc3, Scu1, Car2, Car3, Hor1, Hyi1, Ret2, and Phx2. Criterion 2 : Next, we examine which of the remaining candidate satellites have velocities that are comparable to or less than the escape velocity of the LMC. All candidate satellites whose velocities at r_ peri fall below the blue solid curve ( v_ esc of the LMC) in Fig. fig:orbparams remain. Seg1, Tuc3, and Scu1 have significantly higher velocities than the LMC's escape speed. These galaxies are likely MW satellites that orbit within 50 kpc of the Galactic Center and consequently pass nearby the LMC.

We will refer to these galaxies as MW satellites that recently interacted with the MCs . Our results for Tuc3's orbit are well-aligned with recent literature wherein models of the formation of Tuc3's stellar stream require a recent, close encounter with the LMC erkal18, li18b, simon18 .

gmw_00010419.name: Segue 1
gmw_00010419.overview: Segue 1 is a Milky Way satellite galaxy that orbits within 50 kpc of the Galactic Center [arXiv:2001.01746]. It recently interacted with the Magellanic Clouds and may possess faint tidal debris resulting from a close passage with the Large Magellanic Cloud [arXiv:2001.01746].

sources.summary: The paper classifies Segue 1 as a Milky Way satellite with a recent Magellanic-Cloud encounter and possible faint tidal debris from close LMC passage.

quotes.quote: By doing so, the following galaxies remain: Seg1, Tuc3, Scu1, Car2, Car3, Hor1, Hyi1, Ret2, and Phx2. Criterion 2 : Next, we examine which of the remaining candidate satellites have velocities that are comparable to or less than the escape velocity of the LMC. All candidate satellites whose velocities at r_ peri fall below the blue solid curve ( v_ esc of the LMC) in Fig. fig:orbparams remain. Seg1, Tuc3, and Scu1 have significantly higher velocities than the LMC's escape speed. These galaxies are likely MW satellites that orbit within 50 kpc of the Galactic Center and consequently pass nearby the LMC.

We will refer to these galaxies as MW satellites that recently interacted with the MCs . Our results for Tuc3's orbit are well-aligned with recent literature wherein models of the formation of Tuc3's stellar stream require a recent, close encounter with the LMC erkal18, li18b, simon18 . This further suggests that Segue 1 may also have faint tidal debris resulting from a close passage with the LMC.

gmw_00010420.name: Fornax
gmw_00010420.overview: Fornax is a dwarf spheroidal galaxy located in the Local Group [arXiv:2001.01746]. Although previously posited as a satellite of the Magellanic Clouds, its orbital history and kinematics calculated from Gaia DR2 data reject this dynamical association [arXiv:2001.01746].

sources.summary: The paper finds Fornax has Gaia DR2 orbital differences and rejects its dynamical association with the Magellanic Clouds despite earlier LMC-satellite claims.

quotes.quote: In the cases of Carina, Fornax, and Ursa Minor the Gaia DR2 and pre-existing PMs are consistent with each other at the 2 level of the old measurement, although the latter have large error bars (100-200 as/yr). The Gaia DR2 PMs for these galaxies reach much higher precision (3-15 as/yr). Fornax shows the most significant differences between the previously measured and Gaia DR2 PMs. In the MW+LMC+SMC potential, Fornax's Gaia DR2 orbit indicates that it has completed nearly two orbital passages in the last 6 Gyr with the most recent passage around the MW occurring at 1.5 Gyr ago at a distance of 90-120 kpc, which is much closer than the orbit calculated using the piatek07 PMs. Carina and Fornax have recently been posited as satellites of the MCs pardy19, jahn19 .

The SMC counts towards this prediction and they additionally claimed that Carina and Fornax are also satellites of the LMC given the coherence between their orbital poles on the sky compared to that of the LMC. However, we do not find that Carina and Fornax are associated to the MCs using our satellite criteria, which accounts for the orbital histories of the galaxies in addition to their current kinematics and dynamics. jahn19 used the subset of simulations from the FIRE suite hosting an LMC analog to calculate the expected abundance of LMC satellite galaxies and found that it can host 5-10 satellites with M_* 10^4 M_ within its virial radius.

gmw_00010421.name: Q1307--BM1163
gmw_00010421.overview: Q1307--BM1163 is a bright galaxy located at a redshift of z = 1.411 [arXiv:astro-ph/0407296]. Its nebular oxygen abundance and stellar indices indicate a near-solar to moderately subsolar metallicity [arXiv:astro-ph/0407296].

sources.summary: The paper presents Q1307--BM1163 as a bright z=1.411 galaxy whose nebular oxygen abundance, wind-line profile, and UV stellar indices indicate near-solar to moderately subsolar metallicity.

quotes.quote: The combination of high luminosity and relatively low redshift makes the R = 21.67 , z = 1.411 galaxy Q1307--BM1163 one of the brightest in the BM/BX sample of galaxies in the `redshift desert' ( z 1.4 - 2.5 ) recently assembled by . These authors reported an oxygen abundance 12 + (O/H) = 8.53 0.25 for the H 2 gas from the observed [ N 2 ] 6583 /H emission line ratio the N2 index of pp04 ; this value corresponds to a metallicity Z = ( 0.65^ +0.5 _ 0.3 ) when compared with our reference 12 + (O/H) = 8.72 . The profile of the C 4 wind line is also consistent with near-solar metallicity see Fig. 6 of steidel04 .

Inspection of Figure fig:lbg_models_BM1163 indicates that the synthetic spectrum with Z = 0.4 is the closest match (lowest rms difference) to Q1307--BM1163 in the 1415--1435 \ region, while the solar metallicity model reproduces best the Fe 3 blend near 1978 . The latter may be more reliable, given the higher S/N of the data at these wavelengths.

gmw_00010422.name: AM 0529-565
gmw_00010422.overview: AM 0529-565 is a Magellanic-irregular-like merger galaxy characterized by a double nucleus and diffuse tidal tails [arXiv:astro-ph/0004405]. Its western nucleus is currently undergoing a starburst phase, and the system is associated with a nearby blue dwarf galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0004405].

sources.summary: AM 0529-565 is described as a Magellanic-irregular-like merger with a double nucleus, tidal tails, a starburst nucleus, an associated blue dwarf, and diffuse tidal knots.

quotes.quote: AM 0529-565 The image of this system at a redshift of V_ A = 4420 km s ^ 1 is shown in Fig. AM0529-565 . The principal galaxy, ``A'', has an integrated absolute blue magnitude typical of a Magellanic Irregular galaxy. However the presence of a double nucleus and of diffuse tidal tails are characteristics of mergers. The eastern nucleus ``Ae'' has redder colors than the western one ``Aw'' which is currently in a starburst phase (see Fig. AM0529-565_plot ).

``D'' is a blue dwarf galaxy associated with ``A'' at a relative velocity of V_ rel = 220 km s ^ 1 . Several diffuse knots can be seen around ``A''; they are designated with ``a'' to ``l''. ``a'' seems to be detached from the eastern tidal tail. From preliminary spectroscopic test observations of AM 0529-565 (Weilbacher et al. 2000, in prep.), we could estimate an oxygen abundance of 12+ (O/H) = 8.1 (equivalent to Z = 0.003 ) for ``e'' and ``h'' and a very low 12+ (O/H) = 7.6 (equiv. to Z = 0.0008 ) for object ``D''.

gmw_00010423.name: AM 0537-292
gmw_00010423.overview: AM 0537-292 is a strongly disturbed galaxy system featuring a central bar and tidal tails [arXiv:astro-ph/0004405]. These tails contain several large blue knots that are likely star-forming regions with burst ages of approximately 3 million years [arXiv:astro-ph/0004405].

sources.summary: AM 0537-292 is identified with MCG-5-14-9 and described as a disturbed barred tidal-tail system whose tail knots match young burst models.

quotes.quote: AM 0537-292 The image of this system also known as MCG-5-14-9 is shown in Fig. AM0537-292 . ``A'' is a strongly disturbed galaxy with tidal tails and a central bar. ``B'' has the morphology of an E5 galaxy in the central part, but the contours get distorted further out. The overall color of both galaxies is strongly dominated by the light of the nuclei.

Several large blue knots probably associated with star-forming regions can be seen in the tails. They are labeled ``a'' to ``i''. Fig. AM0537-292_plot shows that all knots are well described by the Z_3 model with a burst strength of the order of 10 . They have similar burst ages of around 3 Myrs.

gmw_00010424.name: AM 0547-244
gmw_00010424.overview: AM 0547-244 is an interacting system containing a peculiar galaxy with three tidal tails and a companion galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0004405]. The nuclei of both galaxies are very red, and the system features two luminous tidal dwarf galaxy candidates at the ends of the tails [arXiv:astro-ph/0004405].

sources.summary: AM 0547-244 contains a peculiar galaxy with three tidal tails, a companion, red nuclei, and luminous tidal-dwarf candidates whose colors fit burst models.

quotes.quote: AM 0547-244 This system (Fig. AM0547-244 ) consists of a peculiar galaxy ``A'' ( V_ A = 13164 km s ^ 1 ), from which three tidal tails emanate, and a companion ``B'', the redshift of which is unknown. The colors of the nuclei of ``A'' and ``B'' are very red (see Fig. AM0547-244_plot ). This could indicate an `old' burst age or strong reddening due to large amounts of dust. At the ends of the tails of galaxy ``A'' two large condensations are visible, denoted by ``a'' and ``b''. With absolute magnitudes of M_ B_ a = -16.94 and M_ B_ b = -17.05 , ``a'' and ``b'' are good candidates for luminous TDGs.

Around ``A'' four more knots with unknown redshift can be seen (``'' to ``f''). All other objects have very red colors and are therefore most probably background objects. Fig. AM0547-244_plot shows that the objects ``a'' to ``d'' agree well with the models with metallicity Z_1 and intermediate to high burst strengths while ``f'' is not matched by any model.

gmw_00010425.name: AM 0607-444
gmw_00010425.overview: AM 0607-444 is a spiral galaxy with severely disturbed outer regions [arXiv:astro-ph/0004405]. Its northern tidal tail contains a very blue, star-forming knot and a redder object that may be a background galaxy or the remnant core of an interacting companion [arXiv:astro-ph/0004405].

sources.summary: AM 0607-444 is described as a spiral with severely disturbed outer parts, a northern-tail star-forming knot, and a redder possible companion remnant or background object.

quotes.quote: AM 0607-444 This system consists of a spiral galaxy (``A'') which is severely disturbed in its outer parts (Fig. AM0607-444 ). The object labeled ``B'' has red colors and may be a background galaxy. Two objects are seen towards the northern tail. The very blue knot at its tip, ``a'', hosts a star-forming region. In contrast, ``b'' is much redder, has elliptical contours in its center and may either be the remnant core of the interacting companion of ``A'' or a background galaxy superimposed on the tail.
The colors of the TDG candidate ``a'' are best matched with a Z_1 model with a weak burst (Fig. AM0607-444_plot ), note, however, the large systematic errors of this field.

gmw_00010426.name: AM 0642-645
gmw_00010426.overview: AM 0642-645 is a peculiar galaxy system and a likely merger that exhibits two red nuclei and a bluer central knot [arXiv:astro-ph/0004405].
