The system also features a plume extending to the southeast and several surrounding objects that are speculative tidal dwarf galaxy candidates [arXiv:astro-ph/0004405].

sources.summary: AM 0642-645 is presented as a likely merger with two red nuclei, a blue central knot, a southeast plume, and speculative tidal-dwarf candidates.

quotes.quote: AM 0642-645 The peculiar system ``A'' (Fig.. AM0642-645 ) exhibits two red nuclei (``a'',``b'') and a bluer central knot,``'', and may well be a merger. A plume to the southeast is designated with ``d'' and nine other extended objects in the field of view that have colors in the range of our models have been labeled. Fig. AM0642-645_plot shows that all of these objects would be consistent with TDG models having weak to intermediate burst strengths with the exception of object ``f'', which has colors similar to an elliptical galaxy at z 1.3 (M oller et al. in prep.). However, without a spectroscopic redshift and in the absence of tidal structures linking them with the parent galaxy, the tidal origin of the objects surrounding ``A'' is very speculative.

gmw_00010427.name: AM 0748-665
gmw_00010427.overview: AM 0748-665 is an interacting system composed of two galaxies connected by a long, narrow bridge [arXiv:astro-ph/0004405]. The morphology of these galaxies indicates that their progenitors may have been a spiral and an elliptical galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0004405].

sources.summary: AM 0748-665 is described as two connected interacting galaxies, likely spiral and elliptical progenitors, with condensations in tidal features modeled as young bursts.

quotes.quote: AM 0748-665 This interacting system is composed of two galaxies (``A'' and ``B'') which are connected by a long narrow bridge (Fig. AM0748-665 ). Their morphology indicates that the progenitors of ``A'' and ``B'' may have been a spiral and an elliptical, respectively. Several condensations labeled ``a'' to ``e'' can be seen in the associated tidal features. The brightest condensation ``d'' is blended with a bright star. The latter has been subtracted with a scaled PSF before carrying out the photometry.
Fig. AM0748-665_plot shows that all data points agree with Z_3 models that provide burst strengths up to 0.10.

gmw_00010428.name: AM 1054-325
gmw_00010428.overview: AM 1054-325 is an interacting system composed of an irregular H II galaxy and a redder, spiral-like object [arXiv:astro-ph/0004405]. The irregular galaxy contains two nuclei of different colors and features several large star-forming regions along its western tail [arXiv:astro-ph/0004405].

sources.summary: AM 1054-325 is treated as an interacting system with an irregular H II galaxy, redder spiral-like object, two differently colored nuclei, and western-tail star-forming regions.

quotes.quote: AM 1054-325 This presumably interacting system (Fig. AM1054-325 ) is composed of an irregular H ii galaxy, ``A'', at a redshift of V_ A = 3795 km s ^ 1 SW93 and a spiral-like object ``B'', which has however colors that are redder and more homogeneous than typical spiral galaxies. Its redshift is not known.

The enlarged image of ``A'' (Fig. AM1054-325A ) indicates that this galaxy seems to have two nuclei, one marked as ``A _ nucl '', the other as ``e'' (blended by a foreground star). Like in AM 0529-565 A the two nuclei have different colors. The former is redder ( B-V =0.52 ) while the latter has the blue colors of a strong starburst ( B-V =0.21 ).

In the western tail of ``A'' several large star-forming regions are visible (``g'' to ``m'') with luminosities of fainter dwarf galaxies. A few other features of special interest are marked as ``a'' to ``d'' and ``f''. Fig. AM1054-325_plot shows that their colors are well matched with Z_1 models with 0.02 < b < 0.09 .

gmw_00010429.name: AM 1208-273
gmw_00010429.overview: AM 1208-273 is a three-member system containing a disturbed Sc spiral galaxy with a small central bar [arXiv:astro-ph/0004405]. This main galaxy features extended spiral arms with several knots and is accompanied by a foreground starburst object [arXiv:astro-ph/0004405].

sources.summary: AM 1208-273 is identified with MCG-5-29-28 and described as a three-member system with a disturbed barred spiral, foreground starburst object, arm knots, and diffuse detached objects.

quotes.quote: AM 1208-273 This system, also known as MCG-5-29-28, has three apparent members (Fig. AM1208-273 ). The main galaxy, ``A'', is a disturbed Sc spiral with a small bar visible at the center ( V_ A = 14780 km s ^ 1 ). ``B'' is a foreground object with an active starburst V_ B = 12433 km s ^ 1 , DP97 . The redshift of the disk galaxy ``C'' is not known.

Along the extended spiral arms of ``A'' several knots are visible, marked as ``a'' to ``q''. Two detached diffuse objects ``'' and ``s'' are visible northeast and north of the main galaxy, but their association with the other galaxies is uncertain. Fig. AM1208-273_plot shows that all knots in the arms are matched by a Z_3 model with a high burst strength. They have roughly the same burst age.

gmw_00010430.name: AM 1325-292
gmw_00010430.overview: AM 1325-292 is a closely interacting system consisting of the spiral galaxy NGC 5152 and the elliptical galaxy NGC 5153 [arXiv:astro-ph/0004405]. The spiral galaxy features three visible spiral arms or tidal tails, which terminate in blue, dwarf-sized knots [arXiv:astro-ph/0004405].

sources.summary: AM 1325-292 is a close interaction between NGC 5152 and NGC 5153, with spiral arms or tidal tails ending in blue dwarf-sized knots matching burst models.

quotes.quote: AM 1325-292 AM 1325-292 (Fig. AM1325-292 ) is a close interacting system between a spiral, NGC 5152 (``A'') and an elliptical, NGC 5153 (``B''). Their redshifts are V_ A = 4598 km s ^ 1 and V_ B = 4431 km s ^ 1 , respectively. The spiral has three visible spiral arms/tidal tails, a long one to the north and two apparently shorter ones to the southwest, at the end of which two bigger knots of dwarf galaxy size are seen (``a'' and ``b''). These knots have the bluest colors of the entire system and do well agree with Z_1 models and small or intermediate burst strength shown in Fig. AM1325-292_plot . The small photometric errors preclude Z_3 models although due to the nature of the parent galaxy a higher metallicity would have been expected.

gmw_00010431.name: LV J1157+5638
gmw_00010431.overview: LV J1157+5638 is a dwarf irregular galaxy located at a distance of 9.22 Mpc [arXiv:1711.00696]. It has a linear Holmberg diameter of 1.36 kpc and is accompanied by a faint satellite dwarf irregular galaxy [arXiv:1711.00696].

sources.summary: The paper measures LV J1157+5638 as a dwarf irregular at 9.22 Mpc, with a nearby faint satellite, modest size, and ongoing star formation.

quotes.quote: The distance modulus of LV J1157+5638 using the tip of the red giant branch (TRGB) distance indicator is 29.82 0.09 mag ( D = 9.22 0.38 Mpc). The TRGB distance modulus of LV J1157+5638 sat is 29.76 0.11 mag ( D = 8.95 0.42 Mpc). The distances to the two galaxies are consistent within the uncertainties. The projected separation between them is only 3.9 kpc.

LV J1157+5638 has a total absolute V-magnitude of 13.26 0.10 and linear Holmberg diameter of 1.36 kpc, whereas its faint satellite LV J1157+5638 sat has M_V = -9.38 0.13 mag and Holmberg diameter of 0.37 kpc. Such a faint dwarf was discovered for the first time beyond the nearest 4 Mpc from us. The presence of main sequence stars in both galaxies unambiguously indicates the classification of the objects as dwarf irregulars (dIrrs) with recent or ongoing star formation events in both galaxies.

gmw_00010432.name: LV J1157+5638 sat
gmw_00010432.overview: LV J1157+5638 sat is a faint dwarf irregular galaxy located at a distance of approximately 8.95 Mpc [arXiv:1711.00696]. It is a physical satellite of the galaxy LV J1157+5638 and contains main sequence stars that indicate recent or ongoing star formation [arXiv:1711.00696].

sources.summary: The paper reports LV J1157+5638 sat as a faint dwarf irregular companion at 8.95 Mpc, resolved into stars and only 3.9 kpc from LV J1157+5638.

quotes.quote: A faint dwarf irregular galaxy has been discovered in the HST/ACS field of LV J1157+5638. The galaxy is resolved into individual stars, including the brightest magnitude of the red giant branch. The dwarf is very likely a physical satellite of LV J1157+5638. The distance modulus of LV J1157+5638 using the tip of the red giant branch (TRGB) distance indicator is 29.82 0.09 mag ( D = 9.22 0.38 Mpc). The TRGB distance modulus of LV J1157+5638 sat is 29.76 0.11 mag ( D = 8.95 0.42 Mpc).

LV J1157+5638 has a total absolute V-magnitude of 13.26 0.10 and linear Holmberg diameter of 1.36 kpc, whereas its faint satellite LV J1157+5638 sat has M_V = -9.38 0.13 mag and Holmberg diameter of 0.37 kpc. Such a faint dwarf was discovered for the first time beyond the nearest 4 Mpc from us. The presence of main sequence stars in both galaxies unambiguously indicates the classification of the objects as dwarf irregulars (dIrrs) with recent or ongoing star formation events in both galaxies.

gmw_00010433.name: SHiZELS-7
gmw_00010433.overview: SHiZELS-7 is a high-redshift galaxy featuring an extended, disk-like rotating structure in its hydrogen-alpha kinematics [arXiv:2011.00686]. It contains two hydrogen-alpha star-forming clumps, a diffuse far-infrared continuum, a compact stellar core, and an extended ultraviolet morphology [arXiv:2011.00686].

sources.summary: The paper describes SHiZELS-7 as an ALMA-detected high-redshift galaxy with extended disk-like H kinematics, two H clumps, diffuse FIR continuum, and compact stellar plus extended UV morphology.

quotes.quote: SHiZELS-7 : The H kinematics reveal the presence of an extended disc-like rotating structure. Two H star-forming clumps, separated by 4 kpc, are identified by Swinbank2012a , although the fainter clump is not seen in Gillman2019 because of the different S/N criteria. The FIR continuum is spread over a diffuse structure. The two H clumps are not likely to be affected by significant dust obscuration, so there might be no massive dust and gas clouds associated with the H features. The HST F140W and F606W images show that the least obscured regions in this galaxy show a compact stellar core and a extended UV morphology.

gmw_00010434.name: SHiZELS-9
gmw_00010434.overview: SHiZELS-9 is a high-redshift galaxy that displays an extended, rotation-dominated structure in H-alpha [arXiv:2011.00686]. This structure contains three bright clumps, while its central region features a V-shaped far-infrared continuum linking multiple stellar cores [arXiv:2011.00686].

sources.summary: The paper describes SHiZELS-9 as a rotation-dominated high-redshift galaxy with three H clumps, central V-shaped FIR continuum, and a third stellar core visible in UV and FIR.

quotes.quote: SHiZELS-9 : shows an extended rotation-dominated structure in H with three bright clumps separated by 3 kpc, Swinbank2012a . The FIR continuum shows a V-like structure in the central 3 kpc, linking a bright stellar core with a fainter one. A third stellar core shows both UV and FIR emission.

gmw_00010435.name: SHiZELS-11
gmw_00010435.overview: SHiZELS-11 is a disk galaxy with a compact hydrogen-alpha structure and a marginally extended morphology [arXiv:2011.00686]. It hosts an obscured active galactic nucleus detected in both X-ray and radio wavelengths [arXiv:2011.00686].

sources.summary: The paper describes SHiZELS-11 as a compact disk-like galaxy hosting an obscured AGN, with X-ray/radio detections, unclear rotation, high metallicity, and ALMA continuum offset from H emission.

quotes.quote: SHiZELS-11 : This source was classified as a disk galaxy with a compact H structure Swinbank2012a ; although a newer analysis shows a marginally extended morphology Gillman2019 . SHiZELS-11 has an active galactic nucleus (AGN) identified in the X-rays by XMM-Newton and in the radio by the Very Large Array VLA --- 2006MNRAS.372..741S, 2008ApJS..179..124U . SHiZELS-11 is also detected by Chandra in X-UDS survey Kocevski2018 , with X-ray luminosity is L_ X = 10^ 43.3 erg s^ 1 . The velocity dispersion of H is about 90 km s ^ 1 Gillman2019 , much less than the typical velocity dispersion of type-I AGN, implying that the AGN in SHiZELS-11 must be obscured. Rotation is not clear from the H dynamics, and the position of the maximum velocity dispersion is offset from the brightest H pixel Gillman2019 .

The source has an apparently high metallicity and a steep metallicity gradient Swinbank2012a , which might be due to contamination from an AGN. Both UV and FIR are bright in the galaxy centre, while the rest-frame optical is mainly coming from a clumpy structure surrounding the centre. The ALMA continuum shows compact and extended emission, which does not overlap with the H .

gmw_00010436.name: SHiZELS-21
gmw_00010436.overview: SHiZELS-21 is a compact rotating galaxy [arXiv:2011.00686]. Its Hubble Space Telescope F606W image reveals a double ultraviolet core structure, and the galaxy exhibits a double-peaked H-alpha velocity dispersion profile [arXiv:2011.00686].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SHiZELS-21/UDS-10 as a compact rotating galaxy whose H velocity dispersion is double-peaked and whose HST image shows a double UV core.

quotes.quote: Individual properties SHiZELS-21 , also known as UDS-10 in Molina2017 . This galaxy is a compact rotating galaxy. The H velocity dispersion profile is double peaked at about 1.5 kpc from the galaxy center. The HST F606W image shows a double UV core structure which is barely resolved in the F140W image.

gmw_00010437.name: SHiZELS-10
gmw_00010437.overview: SHiZELS-10 is a compact galaxy identified as a merger candidate [arXiv:2011.00686]. Although its continuum emission is undetected by ALMA, optical and infrared imaging reveals a structure characterized by aligned bright knots and a long tail [arXiv:2011.00686].

sources.summary: The paper describes SHiZELS-10 as a compact H merger candidate undetected in ALMA continuum, with HST and VLT/SINFONI images showing aligned bright knots and a long tail.

quotes.quote: SHiZELS-10 : This galaxy is compact in H ( 2.3 kpc) and identified as a merger Swinbank2012a, Gillman2019 . The ALMA continuum emission is undetected, probably due to limitations in the surface brightness. On the other hand, the HST F140W, F606W and the VLT/SINFONI images show a good spatial consistency with bright dots and a long tail.

gmw_00010438.name: SHiZELS-2
gmw_00010438.overview: SHiZELS-2 is a compact star-forming galaxy that exhibits a clear rotation curve in its H-alpha emission [arXiv:2011.00686]. Its center contains two clumps separated by about 1.5 kiloparsecs, with a bright rest-frame ultraviolet star-forming region located at the galactic core [arXiv:2011.00686].

sources.summary: The paper describes SHiZELS-2 as a compact star-forming galaxy with a clear H rotation curve, central clumps, aligned HST morphology, and bright rest-frame UV center.

quotes.quote: SHiZELS-2 : The H IFU observations evidence a clear rotation curve, including two clumps at the centre with a separation of 0 2 about 1.5 kpc Gillman2019 . The fainter clump locates in the rotation center. The orientation of the HST morphology is similar to that revealed by rotation in H . The HST images show a compact stellar and star formation distribution. The HST F606W image also shows that the star formation in the galaxy center is bright in rest-frame UV Gillman2019 .

gmw_00010439.name: J1023+1952
gmw_00010439.overview: J1023+1952 is a tidal dwarf galaxy located in the interacting system Arp 94 [arXiv:2011.01287]. It exists as a kinematically detached cloud positioned in front of the spiral galaxy NGC 3227 [arXiv:2011.01287].

sources.summary: The paper resolves CO emission in the tidal dwarf J1023+1952, finding abundant diffuse molecular gas, 111 GMCs with near-normal mass spectra, unusual large-scale molecular organization, and southern star formation.

quotes.quote: Here we present multi-configuration ALMA observations of J1023+1952, a TDG in the interacting system Arp 94, where we resolve CO(2-1) emission down to giant molecular clouds (GMCs) at 0.64'' 45 pc resolution. We find a remarkably high fraction of extended molecular emission ( 80 90 ), which is filtered out by the interferometer and likely traces diffuse gas. We detect 111 GMCs that give a similar mass spectrum as those in the Milky Way and other nearby galaxies (a truncated power law with slope of 1.76 0.13 ). We also study Larson's laws over the available dynamic range of GMC properties ( 2 dex in mass and 1 dex in size): GMCs follow the size-mass relation of the Milky Way, but their velocity dispersion is higher such that the size-linewidth and virial relations appear super-linear, deviating from the canonical values.

J1023 + 1952 is a kinematically detached cloud in front of the spiral galaxy NGC 3227, in the interacting system Arp 94 1995MNRAS.277..641M . Fig. 1 shows our target in context, highlighting the large size of the Arp 94 system and its complexity in terms of tidal tails as revealed by and deep optical emission. Specifically, the Arp 94 system is composed of a spiral galaxy hosting a Seyfert 2 nucleus NGC 3227; 1995MNRAS.275...67M,2001ApJ...549..254S,2014ApJ...794....2N interacting with a ``green valley'' elliptical NGC 3226; 2014ApJ...797..117A .

gmw_00010440.name: Arp 94
gmw_00010440.overview: Arp 94 is an interacting galaxy system composed of the spiral galaxy NGC 3227, which hosts a Seyfert 2 nucleus, and the elliptical galaxy NGC 3226 [arXiv:2011.01287]. The system features two sets of tidal tails, including giant, old neutral hydrogen lobes and younger, fast-moving inner tails [arXiv:2002.07312].

sources.summary: The review describes NGC 3226/7 as a merging system with old giant H I lobes, younger inner tidal tails, and faint optical counterparts to some gas structures.

quotes.quote: Two HI tails NGC 3226/7 (Arp 94) . --- The merging galaxy system NGC 3226/7 appears to have two sets of tidal tails, possibly one old and one young pair, offset by 45 degr on the sky (see Fig. 2). Discovered in HIPASS data, while investigating the nearby HCG 44/NGC 3162 system (Serra et al. 2013), these giant H i tails/lobes span 100 ' (ie. 730 kpc for a distance of 25 Mpc). The whole system contains at least 1.5 10^9 . M _ , at least half of which resides in the north-eastern lobe.

The giant H i lobes are likely quite old ( > 1.5 Gyr) and probably falling back towards the merger core as shown in disk-disk merger simulations by Di Matteo et al. (2005). The inner H i tidal tails, discovered by Mundell et al. (1995), span only 20 ' (oriented north to south) and are likely relatively young, recently formed and still fast moving. Deep optical images of this area reveal a very faint stellar counterpart to the northern H i tail (e.g., Appleton et al. 2014; Duc et al. 2015).

sources.summary: The paper describes Arp 94 as the interacting system hosting the tidal dwarf J1023+1952, composed of NGC 3227 and NGC 3226 with tidal tails and long dynamical timescales.

quotes.quote: J1023 + 1952 is a kinematically detached cloud in front of the spiral galaxy NGC 3227, in the interacting system Arp 94 1995MNRAS.277..641M . Fig. 1 shows our target in context, highlighting the large size of the Arp 94 system and its complexity in terms of tidal tails as revealed by and deep optical emission. Specifically, the Arp 94 system is composed of a spiral galaxy hosting a Seyfert 2 nucleus NGC 3227; 1995MNRAS.275...67M,2001ApJ...549..254S,2014ApJ...794....2N interacting with a ``green valley'' elliptical NGC 3226; 2014ApJ...797..117A .

In the interacting system Arp 94 where the TDG formed, the dynamical timescales are probably long, of the order of several 100 Myr or even Gyr (see Sect. Sec:dynamical below). Therefore, atomic gas in the tidal tail may have had enough time to form molecules on its own. This should be facilitated by the lack of strong UV emission from stars, unlike typical galaxy discs where radiation from young stars can easily photo-dissociate H _2 . In this framework, it seems plausible that molecular gas first appeared as a diffuse phase, and then started to locally condense into more compact structures like the GMCs. Spatial variations in the CO(2-1)/CO(1-0) ratio In Sect. Sec:R21 , we showed that the R_ 21 = CO(2-1)/CO(1-0) ratio varies across the TDG with a mean value of 0.5. Values of 0.5 1.0 have typically been found in the Milky Way and at kpc scales in the discs of nearby galaxies e.g. den Brok et al. \ submitted 1994PASP..106.1112S,1996ApJ...460..334O,2010PASJ...62.1277Y,2009AJ....137.4670L . Therefore, the R_ 21 ratio found in this newborn TDG is not abnormal compared to nearby galaxies, even though the value of 0.35 in the south lies at the lower end of what has been measured so far. The low R_ 21 measured might be connected to the nature of the interaction occurring in this system. Generally, low values of R_ 21 may indicate that the gas is not in thermal equilibrium due to low volumetric densities, consistent with the high fraction of diffuse molecular gas that we inferred. This agrees with the recent study by 2020MNRAS.495.3840M in NGC 1300, who found a very high fraction of diffuse molecular emission in its bar (74-91 ) mapping to a very low R_ 21 (0.17-0.34). We might be witnessing a similar effect in the TDG. The observed gradient in R_ 21 in the TDG points to different physical conditions between the quiescent north and the star-forming south. Specifically, variations in the R_ 21 line ratio are expected to be driven by differences in the optical depth (closely related to density) and/or the excitation temperature of the gas 1994PASP..106.1112S, 2009AJ....137.4670L . We find lower R_ 21 in the south, suggesting lower excitation temperature and/or lower density; these conditions are traditionally interpreted as associated with lower star formation efficiency. This might seem puzzling given that star formation is detected almost exclusively in the south However, the integrated R_ 21 value at 28'' resolution is dominated by emission from diffuse molecular gas that is presumably not taking part in star formation. Based on synthetic observations of a simulated GMC, 2017MNRAS.465.2277P found that R_ 21 follows a bimodal distribution, with 0.7 for cold and dense parts and 0.3 in warmer and more diffuse regions. This is consistent with the fact that the CO emission in the TDG is dominated by diffuse molecular gas with a low R_ 21 value. Dynamical state and survival of the TDG In Sect. Sec:kinematics , we showed that atomic and diffuse molecular gas as well as most GMCs in the TDG display a north-south velocity gradient of 100 km s ^ 1 (Fig. fig:pvdiagram ). To first order approximation, the diffuse CO component tracks atomic gas very well, suggesting that atomic and diffuse molecular gas are well mixed. The north-south gradient may be compatible with rotation, but given the uncertain geometry of the system it could also reflect stretching motions along the tidal tail. Moreover, there is some kinematic evidence for a gaseous ``bridge'' along the line of sight towards the North-East direction, which is mostly made up of compact structures (GMCs) and connects the TDG with the background galaxy NGC 3227. Overall, the velocity of the TDG along the line of sight is positive relative to the centre of NGC 3227, similar to the southern part of NGC 3227. Since extinction arguments confirm that the TDG is currently in front of the spiral galaxy 2004ApJ...614..648M , the TDG must be moving towards NGC 3227, as long as the tangential velocities (which we cannot measure) are not much larger than the radial velocity. That ``backward'' motion towards NGC 3227 would suggest that the TDG has been detached from its parent galaxy for a considerable amount of time; the timescales for such interactions are typically of the order of several hundred Myr or even 1 Gyr. It is unclear whether the TDG will survive much longer. Simulations show that condensations of matter at the tip of tidal tails have a larger probability of surviving long and eventually becoming detached from their parent system e.g. 2006A&A...456..481B .

gmw_00010441.name: NGC 7265
gmw_00010441.overview: NGC 7265 is an early-type galaxy [arXiv:1701.08772] that hosts a compact radio source and a soft nuclear X-ray source at its center [arXiv:1701.08772]. The galaxy exhibits asymmetric, redshifted warm-gas emission that may indicate an active galactic nucleus-driven outflow, an inflow, or an ongoing merger [arXiv:1701.08772].

sources.summary: The paper flags NGC 7265 as an ambiguous warm-gas case with asymmetric redshifted emission, possible AGN-driven outflow, and compact radio and soft nuclear X-ray sources.

quotes.quote: Another very interesting case is NGC 7265. It is unclear what the source of the gas is in NGC 7265 since it is redshifted with respect to the systemic velocity and has a very asymmetric spatial extent with no blueshifted component. The gas velocities are consistent with the systemic velocity near the center of the galaxy but extend up to 250 km s ^ 1 at a radius of 7.4 kpc (see fig:portraits ).

It is tempting to think we are observing an outflow driven by an AGN e.g., ciotti16 , in analogy with the ATLAS ^ 3D galaxy NGC 1266 alatalo11,davis12,nyland13,alatalo15 . However, with only redshifted emission, we cannot rule out an inflow, rotation, or simply an ongoing merger. We do detect a compact radio source (Nyland et al. in preparation) and a soft nuclear X-ray source goulding16 in the center. Since we do not detect H we cannot say much about the nuclear line ratios. If this is an outflow, however, it is difficult to understand why we observe only redshifted emission.

gmw_00010442.name: J120331.145+203818.69
gmw_00010442.overview: J120331.145+203818.69 is a quasar located at a redshift of z=3.475 [arXiv:1806.03374]. It is associated with GALEX near- and far-ultraviolet emission, which may be contaminated by a faint neighboring galaxy [arXiv:1806.03374].

sources.summary: The paper notes J120331.145+203818.69 as a z=3.475 quasar with GALEX near- and far-UV emission that may be contaminated by a nearby faint galaxy.

quotes.quote: J120331.145+203818.69 This quasar at z=3.475 was matched to a GALEX source with a distance of 0 7 with contributions in the near- and far- UV band. It has a near-UV magnitude of 21.34 0.40 and a far-UV magnitude of 22.07 0.52 . However, there is a faint galaxy, J120331.19+203823.65, close to the quasar at a separation of 5 , which could contaminate the near- and far- UV flux measured by GALEX .

gmw_00010443.name: J131608.97+254930.43
gmw_00010443.overview: J131608.97+254930.43 is a high-redshift active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1806.03374]. Although initially identified by an automated pipeline as a broad-line quasar at a redshift of 3.24, visual re-evaluation of its spectrum classified the object as a low-ionization broad-absorption line quasar at a redshift of 3.65 [arXiv:1806.03374].

sources.summary: The paper reclassifies J131608.97+254930.43 from a pipeline z=3.24 broad-line quasar to a z=3.65 low-ionization BAL quasar after visual spectrum review.

quotes.quote: J131608.97+254930.43 This object was observed as part of the Segue2 survey and was identified as an z 3.24 broad-line quasar by the pipeline. A visual re-evaluation of the pipeline spectrum resulted in the discovery that the object is a low ionization BAL quasar at z 3.65 . We show its spectrum along with a quasar template for comparison in Figure fig_sdss_spectrum .

gmw_00010444.name: mosaic 1520
gmw_00010444.overview: Mosaic 1520 is a high-redshift Lyman-alpha galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 5.3 [arXiv:2202.03883]. It is characterized by a low mass, a star-formation rate exceeding 100 solar masses per year, and a possible outflow with a velocity up to 60 kilometers per second [arXiv:2202.03883].

sources.summary: The paper models mosaic 1520 as a z~5.3 Ly source with low mass, moderate star formation, HI column up to 10^20 cm^-2, and possible modest outflow.

quotes.quote: The best fit zELDA models are consistent with galaxies with HI column densities up to 10 ^ 20 atoms cm ^ 2 and expansion velocities on the order of a few tens of km sec ^ 1 . This combination of parameters is consistent with the fact that these sources are characterized by low masses and moderate star-formation rates (see Table 2). In particular, the mosaic 1520 source is characterized by a star-formation rate of more than 100 M _ yr ^ 1 and it is plausible that this rate is accompanied by an outflow with a velocity up to 60 km sec ^ 1 .

gmw_00010445.name: mosaic 1548
gmw_00010445.overview: The high-redshift galaxy mosaic 1548 is located on the outskirts of a protocluster [arXiv:2202.03883]. Its spectrum exhibits two Lyman-alpha peaks, which may indicate a low-column-density gas inflow of a few tens of kilometers per second [arXiv:2202.03883].

sources.summary: The paper proposes an alternative zELDA model for mosaic 1548, interpreting its two Ly peaks as possible low-column-density inflow on the protocluster outskirts.

quotes.quote: Since the CubEx detection map shows a S/N larger than 5 from 7662 to 7675 , we propose an alternative zELDA solution for the spectrum of the mosaic 1548 source. In this solution, we found a combination of parameters that account for two peaks, a higher blue one at 7665 and a lower red one at 7672 . The best fit zELDA model suggests the presence of an inflow of a few tens of km sec ^ 1 and a gas with a low column density up to 2 10^ 19 atoms cm ^ 2 . If confirmed, this solution indicates the presence of an inflow at the outskirt of the protocluster.

gmw_00010446.name: mosaic 199
gmw_00010446.overview: The high-redshift galaxy mosaic 199 is located in the AzTEC-3 environment [arXiv:2202.03883]. It exhibits Lyman-alpha emission and shows signs of interaction with a nearby submillimeter galaxy, possibly involving neutral gas flows between the two systems [arXiv:2202.03883].

sources.summary: The paper links mosaic 199 to the AzTEC-3 environment as a possible interacting Ly source associated with southern SMG emission and neutral-gas flows.

quotes.quote: In addition, mosaic 1548, mosaic 1520, and mosaic 199 overlap with sources in the COSMOS2015 catalog, where their photometric redshifts agree with the Ly redshift.

The Ly emission is compact in wavelength (around 300 km sec ^ 1 ) also in the region below the peak of the Ly emission associated to the SMG. In this position, the gas kinematics could be associated with neutral gas flows between the SMG and the mosaic 199 source (Table NBcandidates ). Since Ly photons can be scattered by HI gas and so reveal its presence, the elongated shape of the Ly emission to the north of LBG-3 (N position in Fig. AzTEC3optimalNBonallHST ), in the bridge between SMG and LBG, and to the southeast of the SMG (SE position in Fig. AzTEC3optimalNBonallHST ) could be the result of the interaction between AzTEC-3, LBG-3, and possibly mosaic 199.
The structure seen in the region to the south of the SMG suggests interaction between the SMG and the mosaic 199 source, a third phenomenon of interaction (Fig. AzTEC3channels ) around AzTEC-3.

gmw_00010447.name: JKB 18
gmw_00010447.overview: JKB 18 is a nearby, low-metallicity blue diffuse dwarf galaxy [arXiv:2006.10768]. It features a rotating gas structure with chemically inhomogeneous ionized gas and clumpy, scattered sites of ongoing star formation [arXiv:2006.10768].

sources.summary: The paper presents VLT/MUSE observations of JKB 18, a nearby low-metallicity blue diffuse dwarf, finding chemically inhomogeneous ionized gas and clumpy, rotating star-forming structure.

quotes.quote: Here we present VLT/MUSE observations of one such system, JKB 18, a blue diffuse dwarf galaxy with a metallicity of only 12+log(O/H) =7.6 0.2 ( 0.08 ). With large-scale dispersions in O/H, N/H and N/O of 0.5--0.6 dex and regions harbouring chemical abundances outside this 1 distribution, we deem JKB 18 to be chemically inhomogeneous.

As presented in James:2017 , this is a nearby ( z=0.004 ), low metallicity (12+log(O/H) =7.56 0.20 ) system forming stars at an average rate of 0.20 0.09 10^ 2 /yr. As shown in Figure fig:color , JKB 18 has an extremely interesting morphology, with numerous sites of ongoing star-formation scattered in a somewhat random fashion amongst a diffuse body. The emission appears to be clumpy, with a multitude of \ regions ( 100--130 pc in size, as found by the \ region fitting algorithm described in Section sec:HIIregs and resolved at the resolution of our observations) distributed randomly across the galaxy. We can see from the radial velocity map that there is indeed some large-scale gas structure throughout JKB 18, in that the gas appears to be rotating as a large body.

gmw_00010448.name: manga-8249-6101
gmw_00010448.overview: The galaxy manga-8249-6101 is classified as a typical barred galaxy [arXiv:1810.03257]. It features an ultrafast bar structure, and its pattern speed measurement is considered highly reliable [arXiv:1810.03257].

sources.summary: The paper identifies manga-8249-6101 as one of two bars ultrafast under both PA measurements, describes it as a typical barred galaxy, and judges its pattern-speed measurement reliable.

quotes.quote: The two galaxies that are both ultrafast measured using the kinematic PA and the photometric PA are manga-8249-6101 and manga-8447-6101. They are both in our refined sample, i.e. the two red dots with lower dark matter fraction in the kinematic results of Fig. dm_frac_ref . Their PA differences between the kinematic PA and the photometric PA are 0.6 and 0.2 degree, respectively.

The galaxy manga-8249-6101 is a typical barred galaxy, and the slit length has covered the bright disc. The pattern speed measurement for this galaxy is reliable. Thus the probability that this galaxy has an ultrafast bar is high.

gmw_00010449.name: manga-8447-6101
gmw_00010449.overview: The galaxy manga-8447-6101 is identified as an ultrafast-bar galaxy [arXiv:1810.03257]. It features an 11.8-degree position angle difference between its disc and its bar, and it has a companion galaxy located close to the bar structure [arXiv:1810.03257].

sources.summary: The paper identifies manga-8447-6101 as one of two consistently ultrafast-bar galaxies, but notes an 11.8 degree disc-bar PA difference and a nearby companion that may affect the TW measurement.

quotes.quote: The two galaxies that are both ultrafast measured using the kinematic PA and the photometric PA are manga-8249-6101 and manga-8447-6101. They are both in our refined sample, i.e. the two red dots with lower dark matter fraction in the kinematic results of Fig. dm_frac_ref . Their PA differences between the kinematic PA and the photometric PA are 0.6 and 0.2 degree, respectively.

The galaxy manga-8447-6101 has an 11.8 degrees PA difference between the disc and the bar and a companion close to the bar. These may influence the performance of the TW method though it has good linear fitting in the V vs. X plot.

gmw_00010450.name: Andromeda I
gmw_00010450.overview: Andromeda I is a dwarf spheroidal galaxy located in the Local Group [arXiv:2004.05620]. A photometric survey of the galaxy identified 5,581 stars within two half-light radii of its center, including 59 long-period variable candidates [arXiv:2004.05620].

sources.summary: The survey uses And I to demonstrate its INT dwarf-galaxy monitoring, detecting LPV candidates, building a complete photometric catalogue, and deriving the galaxy's distance modulus and half-light radius.

quotes.quote: In this first of a series of papers, we present the methodology of the variability survey and describe the photometric catalogue of Andromeda I (And I) dwarf galaxy as an example of the survey, and discuss the identified long period variable (LPV) stars. We detected 5 581 stars and identified 59 LPV candidates within two half-light radii of the centre of And I.

A photometric catalogue was constructed containing 9 824 stars in the region of CCD 4 of WFC ( 11.26 22.55 arcmin ^2 ) with central coordinate 00^ h 45^ m 39 . ^ s 9 , +38^ 02^ 28^ among which 97 were identified as LPV candidates. The INT catalogue is complete both in photometric data and in terms of finding LPV stars, especially with amplitude more than 0.2 mag. We estimated a distance modulus of 24.41 mag for And I based on the tip of the RGB. Also, a half-light radius of 3.2 0.3 arcmin is calculated.

gmw_00010451.name: GRB 050416A host galaxy
gmw_00010451.overview: The host galaxy of GRB 050416A has a moderately red optical color [arXiv:0905.0001]. However, Hubble Space Telescope observations reveal a bluer color in the near-infrared, and emission-line ratios suggest the galaxy harbors internal extinction [arXiv:0905.0001].

sources.summary: The host of GRB 050416A has moderately red optical color, a bluer HST color, and emission-line evidence suggesting significant host-galaxy extinction.

quotes.quote: The host galaxy color is moderately red: g-R_ AB = 0.8 ; in part this is likely due to the presence of the 4000 \ break between the g and R bands at the emission redshift of z = 0.6535. detected the host in the HST F775W filter and estimate I = 22.7 0.1 , corresponding to a significantly bluer color of (R-I)_ AB 0.15 . Neither of these values constrain the host extinction strongly. However, on the basis of the observed emission line ratio of H_ / H_ = 0.3 0.1 , they conclude that the host galaxy does likely harbor significant extinction.

gmw_00010452.name: GRB 050713A host galaxy
gmw_00010452.overview: The GRB 050713A host galaxy is detected in the R band and marginally detected in the g band [arXiv:0905.0001]. Its blue color limits the galaxy's redshift to z < 3.6, which rules out Lyman absorption as the cause of the associated afterglow's faintness [arXiv:0905.0001].

sources.summary: The paper identifies a source coincident with the GRB 050713A optical position, measures a weak blue color constraint, and rules out Lyman absorption as the afterglow cause.

quotes.quote: After this step a source coincident with the optical position is clearly visible in R and marginally detected in g . The color of g-R_ AB = 0.4 0.3 does not constrain the nature of the galaxy given the unknown redshift. It does limit the redshift to z < 3.6 , ruling out any contribution of Lyman absorption to the observed afterglow faintness.

gmw_00010453.name: GRB 050915A host galaxy
gmw_00010453.overview: The GRB 050915A host galaxy is a faint, underluminous system [arXiv:0905.0001] that lacks an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2007.11874]. Its blue colors indicate a young stellar population with little global dust [arXiv:0905.0001], and its star formation rate has been constrained using ALMA photometry [arXiv:2007.11874].

sources.summary: The GRB 050915A host candidate is a faint low-redshift galaxy offset from the IR position; its blue colors imply little global dust despite afterglow extinction.

quotes.quote: A faint galaxy, previously discovered by Ovaldsen+2007 , is well-detected consistent with the XRT position in all filters in which it was observed ( g , V , R , and I ). It is somewhat offset (by about 1.1 ) from the IR position, although because of the relatively low-significance detection of the infrared afterglow the 90 confidence circle is large and its edge skirts that of the optical disk. While P_ chance is still low (0.06), we admit that this is one of the more tenuous associations in the sample. While the optical detection of the host alone rules out a high-redshift origin, VLT spectroscopy of this galaxy (P. Jakobsson et al. in preparation) has revealed a surprisingly low redshift of z 0.4 , indicating an extremely underluminous system ( M_ V(AB) 17.4 ) and requiring a significant (though not, in this case, particularly large) dust column to explain the redness of optical afterglow.

The blue colors of this galaxy indicate a young population free of widespread dust (global A_V 1.0 mag from our template modeling). This limit is not inconsistent with the relatively modest minimum extinction inferred from the afterglow.

sources.summary: The paper selects the closest GRB050915A host candidate, favors the no-AGN fit, and argues ALMA FIR photometry makes its revised SFR more reliable.

quotes.quote: In this host galaxy, we found several candidates for the host galaxy (Fig. fig1 f). We chose the closest one between the candidate and the host position. The separation between the two is 0.17 arcsec. We performed again the setting for two configurations: with and without AGN component. The results show an over-fit when we added the AGN component (reduced ^2 = 0.47). However, the reduced ^2 value is 0.97 when we removed the AGN component.

There are significant differences in stellar masses and SFRs between this work and Perley2013a . Also, the transformation by using Kennicutt1998b shows that SFR _ Kennicutt =148 7(M_ yr^ 1 ) . This value is slightly larger compared to all the previous results and our results. In this work, the new photometry of the host galaxy with ALMA and previous upper limits are consistent with the best-fit template. The derived SFR including the rest-frame FIR data is likely to be more reliable than that in previous studies.

gmw_00010454.name: GRB 060210 host galaxy
gmw_00010454.overview: The GRB 060210 host galaxy is a luminous, high-redshift galaxy detected in the R and I bands [arXiv:0905.0001]. It exhibits a blue rest-frame ultraviolet color, which suggests that the host galaxy has a low average dust extinction [arXiv:0905.0001].

sources.summary: The GRB 060210 host is a likely high-redshift, luminous galaxy detected in R and I, with blue rest-UV color and lower inferred extinction than the afterglow.

quotes.quote: However, a bright source is detected at the OT position in R and I . This is not the object mentioned in GCN4753 , which according to that note is 2-3 north of the XRT position. No source is detected at that position in our imaging. The offset between this object and the OT is less than 0.5 \ ( P_ chance < 0.01 ) and the association is further bolstered by the g band nondetection. This therefore likely represents among the highest-redshift GRB host galaxies detected to date, as well as among the most luminous ( M_R < -20.2 for a starburst template).

In spite of the optical extinction, redward of Lyman- the color of the object is quite blue, with (R-I)_ AB = 0.1 0.3 (the large uncertainty is dominated by the poor calibration of this field using USNO standards). Given that the R and I bands correspond to wavelengths well into the ultraviolet at this redshift (1300--1700 ) where dust absorption is extremely efficient, this suggests that the average observed extinction cannot be high, though given the lack of knowledge about the extinction law it is difficult to constrain this formally. For an assumed SMC-like extinction law, the host extinction is A_V = 0.25 0.25 , which is certainly much less than the inferred extinction from the afterglow.

gmw_00010455.name: GRB 080319A host galaxy
gmw_00010455.overview: The GRB 080319A host galaxy is a relatively bright galaxy that is coincident with an optical afterglow [arXiv:0905.0001]. It exhibits an ordinary optical color, and spectroscopy of the host limits its redshift to z < 2.2 [arXiv:0905.0001].

sources.summary: The GRB 080319A host is a relatively bright galaxy coincident with the optical afterglow, with ordinary optical color and spectroscopy limiting it to z < 2.2.

quotes.quote: A relatively bright galaxy is located coincident with the P60 optical afterglow position. As with other galaxies in our sample, the optical color is not unusual ( g-R_ AB = 0.60 0.06 ). While this single color does not strongly constrain host extinction, as with other bursts the relative brightness of the host combined with the lack of obvious redness does not give any reason to suspect its presence. Spectroscopy reveals no line features over our spectral range redward of the atmospheric cutoff, limiting the redshift to z<2.2 . At this redshift and assuming an intrinsic spectral slope < 1.2 , the lower limit on the extinction implied by the photometric SED is A_V 0.25 mag (SMC extinction). Any deviation from these assumptions (lower redshift, shallower intrinsic slope, or other standard extinction laws) would require additional extinction, implying a lower limit on the extinction of A_V > 0.25 mag.

gmw_00010456.name: GRB 080319C host galaxy
gmw_00010456.overview: The host galaxy of GRB 080319C is a blue galaxy with a remarkably bright apparent magnitude [arXiv:0905.0001]. However, this apparent brightness and the galaxy's estimated mass may be overestimated due to contamination from an intervening Mg II system along the line of sight [arXiv:1406.0568].

sources.summary: The GRB 080319C host candidate is remarkably bright and blue; if at the burst redshift it would be among the most luminous known GRB hosts.

quotes.quote: The host galaxy of this event is remarkably bright: R_ AB = 22.3 mag. In fact, this value is consistent with the reported P60 flux from observations taken the night after the GRB and was likely serendipitously detected even by this small-aperture telescope. The relatively high redshift makes this particularly remarkable: the absolute magnitude of this galaxy for a flat-spectrum k correction is M_R = -22.6 mag ( 4 L_ * at z 2 ; Reddy+2008 ), which would make it the second most luminous GRB host galaxy known (second to the even more remarkable host of GRB 081008, GCN8372 , if its reported luminosity is real.). The color is blue ( g-R_ AB = 0.33 0.05 mag).

An alternate hypothesis for the galaxy detected in our imaging associates it with the z=0.8104 Mg II absorber in Table 54 of , rather than the true host. The apparent brightness of the object would be much less remarkable at this redshift, especially if the flux were combined with that of a true background host along the line of sight. Spectroscopy or high-resolution imaging of this system will be needed to confirm or rule out this possibility.

sources.summary: The paper says the GRB 080319C host may have overestimated apparent brightness and mass because an intervening Mg II system contaminates the line of sight.

quotes.quote: For GRB 080319C, the apparent brightness of the host galaxy may be overestimated. This burst is a dark burst with large extinction ( A_V=0.65 mag) (Perley et al. 2009). There is an intervening Mg II system at z=0.81 along the line of sight of GRB 080319C (Fynbo et al. 2009). The apparent brightness of the host galaxy may be overestimated due to the contamination of Mg II system. So the mass of host galaxy should be overestimated.

gmw_00010457.name: IRAS F13308+5946
gmw_00010457.overview: IRAS F13308+5946 is a luminous infrared galaxy and a Seyfert 1.5 quasar [arXiv:1005.3909]. Optical imaging reveals characteristics of the later stages of a merger, and the system is likely transitioning from an ultraluminous infrared galaxy phase into a classical quasar [arXiv:1005.3909].

sources.summary: The paper presents IRAS F13308+5946 as a merging type I LIRG/Seyfert 1.5 quasar transitioning from a type I ULIRG or IR QSO into a classical QSO.

quotes.quote: We present a stellar population synthesis study of a type I luminous infrared galaxy (LIRG): IRAS F13308+5946. It is a quasar with absolute magnitude M_i=-22.56 and has a spectral feature of a Seyfert 1.5 galaxy. Optical images show characteristics of later stages of a merger.

With the help of the stellar synthesis code \ (Cid Fernandes et al. \ 2005) and both Calzetti et al. \ (2000) and Leitherer et al.'s (2002) extinction curves, we estimate the past infrared (IR) luminosities of the host galaxy and find it may have experienced an ultraluminous infrared galaxy (ULIRG) phase for nearly 300 Myr, so this galaxy has probably experienced a type I ULIRG phase. Both nuclear starburst and active galactic nuclei (AGN) contribute to the present IR luminosity budget, and starburst contributes 70 . The mass of supermassive black-hole (SMBH) is M_ BH =1.8 10^8 M_ and the Eddington ratio L_ bol /L_ Edd is 0.12, which both approximate to typical values of PG QSOs. These results indicate that IRAS F13308+5946 is probably at the transitional phase from a type I ULIRG to a classical QSO.

gmw_00010458.name: J221141.01+001118.92
gmw_00010458.overview: J221141.01+001118.92 is a faint quasar at a redshift of approximately 5.23 [arXiv:1708.00314]. It hosts a supermassive black hole with a lower mass but a higher Eddington ratio than luminous SDSS quasars at similar redshifts [arXiv:1708.00314].

sources.summary: This faint z about 5.23 quasar shows multiple UV emission lines and has an inferred lower black hole mass but higher Eddington ratio than luminous SDSS quasars.

quotes.quote: J221141.01+001118.92 . The redshift and M_ 1450 of this object are z 5.23 and 24.29 , respectively. This object shows Ly 1216 , N v 1240 , O i 1304 , Si iv 1400 , and C iv 1549 . As described in Sections 4.3 and 4.4, the inferred log M_ BH and log (L/L_ Edd ) are 8.53 0.20 and 0.42 0.22 , respectively. also identified this object without C iv emission lines, due to the wavelength coverage. The estimated redshift is consistent to that of .

Therefore M_ BH of J2211+0011 is lower than that of luminous SDSS quasars at similar redshift. On the other hand, M_ BH of J2222-0004 is similar to that of luminous SDSS quasars at similar redshift. As for the Eddington ratio, the Eddington ratio of J2211+0011 is higher than that of luminous SDSS quasars at similar redshift.

gmw_00010459.name: J221520.22-000908.39
gmw_00010459.overview: J221520.22-000908.39 is a newly discovered faint quasar at a high redshift of 5.28 [arXiv:1708.00314]. The object exhibits Lyman-alpha, Nitrogen V, and Carbon IV emission lines, along with absorption features of Lyman-alpha and Carbon IV [arXiv:1708.00314].

sources.summary: The paper reports J221520.22-000908.39 as a newly discovered faint z = 5.28 quasar with Ly-alpha, N V, C IV emission and absorption features.

quotes.quote: J221520.22-000908.39 . The redshift and M_ 1450 of this object are 5.28 and 24.25 , respectively. This object is the newly discovered faint quasars and the faintest quasars in our sample. Ly 1216 , N v 1240 , and C iv 1549 emission lines are detected. This object shows absorption lines of Ly and C iv 1549 .

gmw_00010460.name: J221941.90+001256.20
gmw_00010460.overview: J221941.90+001256.20 is a quasar at a redshift of 4.29 [arXiv:1708.00314]. Its spectrum exhibits numerous absorption lines, suggesting it may be a low-ionization broad absorption line quasar, and it is detected at both radio and mid-infrared wavelengths [arXiv:1708.00314].

sources.summary: The paper identifies J221941.90+001256.20 as a z = 4.29 quasar with many absorption lines, possible FeLoBAL nature, radio detection, and WISE counterpart.

quotes.quote: J221941.90+001256.20 . The redshift of this object is 4.29 . also identified this object. The estimated redshift is slightly lower than that of . A large number of absorption lines are present in the spectrum of this object, suggesting that this object could be one of the FeLoBAL quasar. Furthermore, this object is detected in the radio wavelength ( 1995ApJ...450..559B,2011AJ....142....3H ). The peak flux density at 1.4 GHz from the Faint Images of the Radio Sky at Twenty-Centimeters (FIRST) and Very Large Array imaging of Stripe 82 is 0.87 0.10 mJy beam^ 1 and 0.92 0.07 mJy beam^ 1 , respectively. This object also has a mid-infrared ( 3.4 m and 4.6 m with S/N>5 ) counterpart in the Wide-field Infrared Survey Explorer (WISE; 2010AJ....140.1868W ), with the magnitude are 19.22 at 3.4 m and 19.45 at 4.6 m , respectively ( 2014yCat.2328....0C ).

gmw_00010461.name: J222216.02-000405.66
gmw_00010461.overview: J222216.02-000405.66 is a faint quasar at a redshift of 4.94 [arXiv:1708.00314]. It hosts a supermassive black hole with a mass comparable to luminous SDSS quasars at similar redshifts, but exhibits a lower Eddington ratio [arXiv:1708.00314].

sources.summary: This z = 4.94 faint quasar has clearly detected Ly-alpha, N V and C IV lines, a black hole mass similar to luminous SDSS quasars, and a lower Eddington ratio.

quotes.quote: J222216.02-000405.66 . The redshift and M_ 1450 of this object are 4.94 and 24.27 , respectively. Ly 1216 , N v 1240 , and C iv 1549 emission lines are clearly detected. As described in Sections 4.3 and 4.4, the inferred log M_ BH and log (L/L_ Edd ) are 9.04 0.14 and 1.00 0.15 , respectively. also identified this object without C iv emission lines, due to the wavelength coverage. The estimated redshift is slightly lower than that of .

On the other hand, M_ BH of J2222-0004 is similar to that of luminous SDSS quasars at similar redshift. As for the Eddington ratio, the Eddington ratio of J2211+0011 is higher than that of luminous SDSS quasars at similar redshift. On the other hand, the Eddington ratio of J2222-0004 is lower than that of luminous SDSS quasars at similar redshift.

gmw_00010462.name: NGC 7556
gmw_00010462.overview: NGC 7556 is a massive, large elliptical galaxy that serves as the brightest cluster galaxy of a nearby fossil group [arXiv:1809.01251]. It hosts a centrally concentrated stellar population that is younger and more metal-rich than its outer regions, with shallow metallicity gradients suggesting a history of major mergers [arXiv:1809.01251].

sources.summary: NGC 7556 is a massive fossil-group BCG with centrally concentrated, older and metal-rich stars, shallow metallicity gradients, and properties consistent with major mergers.

quotes.quote: We present long-slit spectroscopic observations along the major, minor, and diagonal axes of NGC 6482 and NGC 7556, which are the BCGs of two nearby FGs. The measurements include spatially resolved stellar kinematics and radial profiles of line-strength indices, which we converted into stellar population parameters using single stellar-population models. NGC 6482 and NGC 7556 are very massive ( M_ 10^ 11.5 ) and large ( D_ 25 50 kpc) galaxies. They host a centrally concentrated stellar population, which is significantly younger and more metal rich than the rest of the galaxy.

The age gradients of both galaxies are somewhat larger than those of the other FG BCGs studied so far, whereas their metallicity gradients are similarly negative and shallow. Moreover, they have negligible gradients of element abundance ratio. The measured metallicity gradients are less steep than those predicted for massive galaxies that formed monolithically and evolved without experiencing any major merger. We conclude that the observed FGs formed through major mergers rather than being failed groups that lacked bright satellite galaxies from the beginning.

gmw_00010463.name: UGC 2124
gmw_00010463.overview: UGC 2124 is a spiral galaxy that contains a young stellar population [arXiv:1006.3831]. This stellar component contributes approximately 33 percent of the galaxy's V-band normalized light, with evidence of an enhanced stellar population in its bulge region [arXiv:1006.3831].

sources.summary: The paper identifies a young stellar component in UGC 2124 and notes that its color offsets may reflect TP-AGB treatment plus enhanced abundance ratios in the bulge.

quotes.quote: As we are interested in predictions from models that use different treatments of the TP-AGB phase, we focus on two galaxies from our spectroscopic sample (NGC 628 UGC 2124) with the highest quality data and whose derived SFHs indicate significant contribution (in V band light-weight) from a 1 Gyr SP, \ where the TP-AGB NIR signature is expected to be strong.

The contribution to the V band normalized light of the young population of interest is 67 and 33 for NGC 628 and UGC 2124, respectively. Thus, if our SFHs are reliable, according to the Mar05 models, these galaxies would be expected to show signs of the TP-AGB NIR excess Note that we did not perform the population synthesis fits with the Mar05 models as they have a lower resolution than BC03, thus reducing the predictive power of the spectra to disentangle the high frequency features in the fitting process.

For UGC 2124 in Figure fig:colorcolor2 , it appears that a steeper vector would be required to precisely match the data--model difference. In Mac09 we demonstrated by a comparison of absorption-line indices that there is evidence of an enhanced \ SP in the bulge region of UGC 2124 (see Figure 4 in Mac09), which could also affect the observed colors (only solar-scaled abundance ratio models are considered here).

gmw_00010464.name: NA.v1.489
gmw_00010464.overview: NA.v1.489 is a gravitationally lensed, massive sub-millimeter galaxy located at a redshift of z=2.685 [arXiv:1701.01121]. It hosts a large reservoir of molecular gas that is rapidly forming stars without a dominant active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1701.01121].

sources.summary: The paper presents NA.v1.489 as a gravitationally lensed z=2.685 dusty SMG with extreme star formation, a large molecular gas reservoir rapidly forming stars, low gas fraction, and likely evolution toward quiescence.

quotes.quote: We present the results of combined deep Keck/NIRC2, HST /WFC3 near-infrared and Herschel far infrared observations of an extremely star forming dusty lensed galaxy identified from the Herschel Astrophysical Terahertz Large Area Survey ( H ATLAS J133542.9+300401). The galaxy is gravitationally lensed by a massive WISE identified galaxy cluster at z 1 . The lensed galaxy is spectroscopically confirmed at z=2.685 from detection of CO (1 0) by GBT and from detection of CO (3 2) obtained with CARMA.

The combined physical properties derived from the SED fits and spectroscopic molecular CO line observations reveal that NA.v1.489 is a very massive sub-millimeter galaxy with SFR 2000 M_ yr^ 1 , putting it as one of the most extreme starbursts during peak epoch of star-formation, without a dominant AGN mode. The low- J molecular line luminosities revealed the existence of large reservoir of molecular gas that is being rapidly converted into stars at a much higher pace compared to that of local ULIRGs (see Figure fig:Fig10 ). This combined with the measured low gas fraction ( f_ g =11 ) and high stellar mass, shows that NA.v1.489 has already assembled most of its mass and is most likely on its way to becoming quiescent, forming a progenitor for the most massive early type galaxies found in the local Universe.

gmw_00010465.name: APPLES 1
gmw_00010465.overview: APPLES 1 is a field dwarf galaxy with an optical morphology that suggests it may be a dwarf spheroidal galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0508396]. However, its evidence of recent star formation and low neutral hydrogen content suggest it belongs to the class of mixed dwarf irregular/spheroidal transition-type galaxies [arXiv:astro-ph/0508396].

sources.summary: The paper searches for neutral hydrogen in APPLES 1, finding no significant line emission and arguing its low gas content, morphology, and recent star formation suggest a transition dwarf type.

quotes.quote: We present observations in a search for neutral hydrogen associated with the recently detected field dwarf galaxy APPLES 1 , performed with the Parkes radiotelescope. The observed radio spectrum shows no evident ( > 3 rms) line emission indicating an upper limit for the \ content of the galaxy of 10^ 6 and providing an upper value for the M _ HI /L _ B ratio equal to 2.4 / . The low value of the \ content suggested by the observations, together with the galaxy optical morphology, might indicate that APPLES 1 is a dwarf spheroidal. This indication is in contrast with the evidence of recent star formation, which is typical for dwarf irregular galaxies. This may suggest that APPLES 1 belongs to the class of mixed dwarf irregular/spheroidal transition-type galaxies.

gmw_00010466.name: FBQS J1151 + 3822
gmw_00010466.overview: FBQS J1151 + 3822 is an active galactic nucleus classified as an iron low-ionization broad absorption line quasar [arXiv:1401.0605]. It is the second-brightest and second-closest known example of this specific quasar class, hosting a dense main outflow with a kinetic luminosity up to 4.5 percent of its bolometric luminosity [arXiv:1401.0605].

sources.summary: The paper identifies FBQS J1151 + 3822 as a nearby bright FeLoBAL quasar and constrains its dense outflow radius, column density, covering fractions, and kinetic luminosity.

quotes.quote: We show for the first time that FBQS J1151 + 3822 is an iron low-ionization broad absorption line quasar (FeLoBAL QSO), the second-brightest and second-closest known example of this class. He 1 * and Fe 2 together act as an effective analytical tool, allowing us to obtain useful kinematic constraints from photoionization models of the outflow without needing to assume any particular acceleration model. The main outflow's log ionization parameter is 1.5 , the log hydrogen density ( cm^ 3 ) 5.5--8, the log hydrogen column density ( cm^ 2 ) 21.7--21.9, the absorption radius 7.2-- 127 pc , and the kinetic luminosity 0.16 -4.5 of the bolometric luminosity. We obtain line-of-sight covering fractions of 0.25 for strong Fe 2 , 0.5 for He 1 *, and 0.6 for Mg 2 . Narrower and shallower absorption lines from weaker Fe 2 and Mn 2 with outflow velocity 3400 km s^ 1 have appeared between 2005 and 2011, suggesting that dense cores may have condensed inside the main outflow.

gmw_00010467.name: AGC 749170
gmw_00010467.overview: AGC 749170 is a gas-rich tidal cloud located in the NGC 871/6/7 group that has detached from its parent galaxies [arXiv:1605.04386]. It features an extremely faint, young stellar counterpart and lacks a dark matter component [arXiv:1605.04386].

sources.summary: The paper describes AGC 749170 as a massive, optically dim tidal cloud with little dark matter, a young faint stellar counterpart, and properties unlike simulated TDGs.

quotes.quote: The other low-mass feature is AGC 749170, which has an H i mass M_ H_I 1.4 10^ 9 M_ but an extremely faint stellar counterpart near the detection limit of our deep CFHT data, implying a dynamical mass to stellar light ratio M_ dyn /L_ g >1000 M_ /L_ . The lack of dark matter ( M_ dyn /M_ H_I 1 ) associated with AGC 749170 and the apparently young age of the putative optical counterpart suggest tidal origins for this intriguing gas cloud; however, while it is massive enough to be long-lived (e.g. bou2006 ), its low stellar content does not resemble any of the tidal systems obtained in simulations. Note, the extremely optically dim optical feature that spatially coincides with AGC 749170 was detected, for the first time, by MegaCam and the Elixir/Elixir-LSB processing pipeline ( mag2004 , cui2011 ), which illustrates the utility of deep optical photometry for understanding the origin of H i features in nearby interacting galaxy groups.

AGC 749170 --- located in NGC 871/6/7 --- is a gas-rich tidal cloud that has clearly detached from its parent galaxies; however, the lack of a significant stellar component designates AGC 749170 into a possibly rare class of optically dark/dim tidal features that are not currently found in simulations.

gmw_00010468.name: NE-1
gmw_00010468.overview: NE-1 is a gas-rich tidal knot located approximately 15 kiloparsecs from the spiral galaxy NGC 4747 [arXiv:1605.04386]. It is a second-generation feature formed from material pulled out during an interaction event, though it lacks a clearly bound stellar counterpart [arXiv:1605.04386].

sources.summary: The paper identifies NE-1 as a gas-rich tidal knot near NGC 4747, lacking rotation or a bound stellar counterpart but potentially able to become a long-lived TDG.

quotes.quote: In addition, the interferometric maps distinguish an H i knot, NE-1, between NGC 4747 and AGC 229104 (see Fig. fig:GMRTn4747 ). Both NE-1 and AGC 229104 (hereafter NE-2) appear to be second-generation features formed from the gaseous material pulled out from NGC 4747 during an interaction event.

Nevertheless, NE-1 is located closer to NGC 4747 than NE-2 --- with separation distances of 4 arcmin (15 kpc) and 7 arcmin (25 kpc), respectively, from the centre of the host spiral --- causing the former to have a higher likelihood of succumbing to the gravity of its parent galaxy. We note that NE-1 and NE-2 are unlikely to be self-gravitating and therefore their values of M_ dyn (and M_ dyn /M_ gas ) may have little meaning. Fig. fig:NGC4725_cfht shows that neither NE-1 nor NE-2 has a clearly bound stellar counterpart, despite the fact that the gas-rich tail is situated along a prominent stellar tail.

If NE-1 and NE-2 continue to accumulate mass and move a sufficient distance away from NGC 4747 to avoid falling back into the latter, then optimistically, both of these objects could evolve into long-lived TDGs.

gmw_00010469.name: NE-2
gmw_00010469.overview: NE-2 is a second-generation tidal knot formed from gaseous material pulled out of the spiral galaxy NGC 4747 during an interaction event [arXiv:1605.04386]. It lacks a clearly bound stellar counterpart but could potentially evolve into a long-lived tidal dwarf galaxy [arXiv:1605.04386].

sources.summary: The paper identifies NE-2, also called AGC 229104, as a second-generation tidal knot from NGC 4747 material that lacks a bound stellar counterpart but may evolve into a TDG.

quotes.quote: Both NE-1 and AGC 229104 (hereafter NE-2) appear to be second-generation features formed from the gaseous material pulled out from NGC 4747 during an interaction event.

Nevertheless, NE-1 is located closer to NGC 4747 than NE-2 --- with separation distances of 4 arcmin (15 kpc) and 7 arcmin (25 kpc), respectively, from the centre of the host spiral --- causing the former to have a higher likelihood of succumbing to the gravity of its parent galaxy. We note that NE-1 and NE-2 are unlikely to be self-gravitating and therefore their values of M_ dyn (and M_ dyn /M_ gas ) may have little meaning. Fig. fig:NGC4725_cfht shows that neither NE-1 nor NE-2 has a clearly bound stellar counterpart, despite the fact that the gas-rich tail is situated along a prominent stellar tail.

If NE-1 and NE-2 continue to accumulate mass and move a sufficient distance away from NGC 4747 to avoid falling back into the latter, then optimistically, both of these objects could evolve into long-lived TDGs.

gmw_00010470.name: KK 167
gmw_00010470.overview: KK 167 is a dark-matter-dominated dwarf irregular galaxy with a low star formation rate [arXiv:1605.04386]. Although it is not in the immediate vicinity of another galaxy, it exhibits a truncated neutral hydrogen distribution within its faint stellar disk [arXiv:1605.04386].

sources.summary: The paper classifies KK 167 as a gas-rich, dark-matter-dominated dIrr with low star formation, a truncated H i distribution, and uncertain relation to group interactions.

quotes.quote: KK 167 is not in the immediate vicinity of another galaxy and appears to be a dark matter dominated dIrr with SFR = 0.010 0.001 M_ yr ^ 1 . Nevertheless, the H i distribution in KK 167 appears truncated within a faint stellar disk, which is unusual for undisturbed dIrrs (e.g. bro1997 , hun2012 ). The optical appearance of KK 167 is similar to other low-mass galaxies such as blue compact dwarfs (e.g. pus2005 ) but its SFR also precludes it from this classification (see cai2001 ). Although there are clear signs of tidal interactions occurring within the NGC 4725/47 group, it is unclear whether these same interactions are responsible for the stellar properties and truncated H i distribution of KK 167.

gmw_00010471.name: AGC 208537
gmw_00010471.overview: AGC 208537 is classified as a likely classical dwarf irregular galaxy [arXiv:1605.04386]. It is relatively isolated from tidal features and exhibits a velocity gradient across its major axis that suggests rotation [arXiv:1605.04386].

sources.summary: The paper classifies AGC 208537 as a likely classical dIrr, relatively isolated from tidal features, with H i rotation and a dark-matter-dominated mass ratio.

quotes.quote: Based on our measurements in that paper, AGC 208537, AGC 208443 and AGC 208444 are likely classical dIrrs; the latter two galaxies appear to be interacting with each other while falling in towards the central region of the group.

Our H i and optical observations reveal that AGC 208537 is relatively isolated (i.e. not located near any tidal features) and has a velocity gradient across its major axis that suggests rotation. Unfortunately, another foreground star prohibits the detection and measurement of an optical counterpart. The measured dynamical to gas mass ratio is 5 for AGC 208537 and since typical dIrrs have M_ H_I /M_ stellar 1 bra2015 , any likely associated optical feature would not change the fact that this galaxy is dark matter dominated.

gmw_00010472.name: DELVE J1500+0554
gmw_00010472.overview: DELVE J1500+0554, also known as Virgo II, is an ultra-faint system located in the constellation Virgo [arXiv:2209.12422]. Situated at a distance of approximately 72 kpc, its physical size and absolute magnitude suggest it is likely an ultra-faint dwarf galaxy [arXiv:2209.12422].

sources.summary: The paper identifies DELVE J1500+0554 as Virgo II, an ultra-faint system at 72 kpc whose size and luminosity make it more likely a dwarf galaxy.

quotes.quote: DELVE J1500+0554 (Virgo II) DELVE J1500+0554 ( virgoII_6panel ) was identified in all four iterations of our search with detection significances ranging from 5.6 to 7.9 . It was visually confirmed in the Legacy Surveys Sky Viewer as a concentration of faint blue stars spread across a spatial region that was at least 2 2 in size. This system is located in the constellation Virgo and falls 3 above the northern boundary of the Stripe 82 subsection covered by the second data release of HSC SSP (HSC SSP DR2); this explains why this candidate was not discovered in tandem with the Virgo I ultra-faint dwarf 2016ApJ...832...21H . With our GMOS-N imaging, we confidently resolve stars three magnitudes below the main sequence turnoff, revealing a large sample of possible member stars consistent with the best-fit old, metal-poor PARSEC isochrone. Our structural fit reveals that DELVE J1500+0554 is a relatively small, ultra-faint system ( r_ 1/2 = 16^ +3 _ 3 pc; M_V = -1.6^ +0.4 _ 0.6 ) at a distance of D_ = 72^ +8 _ 7 kpc.

This effectively precludes us from making a high-confidence classification for DELVE J1500+0554. However, given the close similarity of both the absolute magnitude and half-light radius between DELVE J1500+0554 and Triangulum II, we believe that DELVE J1500+0554 is more likely to be an ultra-faint dwarf galaxy. We therefore adopt the name Virgo II, but emphasize that spectroscopic follow-up is necessary to reveal its true nature.

gmw_00010473.name: DELVE J1415+3254
gmw_00010473.overview: DELVE J1415+3254, also named Bootes V, is a low-luminosity dwarf galaxy candidate located in the outer Milky Way halo [arXiv:2209.12422]. It features an intermediate-size, relatively circular structure and a highly metal-poor stellar population [arXiv:2209.12422].

sources.summary: The paper names DELVE J1415+3254 as Bo o tes V, favoring a dwarf-galaxy classification from its size, luminosity, metal-poor population, and proper motion.

quotes.quote: DELVE J1415+3254 (Bo o tes V) DELVE J1415+3254 ( bootesV_6panel ) was detected in both g, iterations of the search, but only barely surpassed our 5.5 detection threshold for the iteration that used a metal-rich isochrone. Despite its low detection significance, we flagged this candidate for further investigation due to the apparent presence of three blue horizontal branch stars in our diagnostic plots from . We also identified that multiple stars in the immediate vicinity displayed self-consistent proper motions, supporting the reality of the system. With the deeper GMOS-N data, we were able to robustly constrain DELVE J1415+3254's properties, finding that it is an intermediate-size ( r_ 1/2 = 20^ +2 _ 2 pc), relatively circular ( = 0.2^ +0.1 _ 0.1 ), and low-luminosity ( M_V = -3.2^ +0.3 _ 0.3 ) system in the outer Milky Way halo ( D_ = 102^ +7 _ 7 kpc). Its stellar population is well described by the most metal-poor isochrone in our PARSEC grid ( Fe/H = -2.2 dex).

Based on the likelihood that three of the five systems with similar morphological properties to DELVE J1415+3254 are dwarf galaxies, we conclude that DELVE J1415+3254 is also most likely a dwarf galaxy. Accordingly, we name the system Bo o tes V following the convention that dwarf galaxy candidates are named after the constellation within which they reside; this name matches that given by the contemporaneous work by Smith:2022 .

gmw_00010474.name: DELVE J1057+2852
gmw_00010474.overview: DELVE J1057+2852, also named Leo Minor I, is a faint candidate dwarf galaxy located at a distance of approximately 82 kpc [arXiv:2209.12422]. It has an absolute magnitude of -2.4 mag and an azimuthally-averaged half-light radius of 26 pc [arXiv:2209.12422].

sources.summary: The paper names DELVE J1057+2852 as Leo Minor I, a likely dwarf galaxy supported by its extended size, low luminosity, metal-poor stars, and preliminary kinematics.

quotes.quote: It was detected in all four iterations of our search at significances of 6.1 to 7.3 , despite the presence of CCD gaps in the surrounding field within DELVE DR2 ( mapfigure ). The system is visible in the Legacy Surveys Sky Viewer as a cloud of marginally-resolved blue stars extending at least two arcminutes in diameter (similar to Virgo II). Despite our additional imaging of this system with DECam, the data available for DELVE J1057+2852 are relatively shallow for characterizing the structure of such a faint system at this distance ( D_ = 82^ +4 _ 7 kpc). In particular, the small sample of available member stars, combined with the lack of blue horizontal branch stars or a fully-resolved main sequence turnoff, resulted in considerable uncertainties on the best-fit distance and size of this system.

Accepting the caveats above, DELVE J1057+2852 is the most extended and second most luminous system we report in this work (after Bo o tes V), with an azimuthally-averaged half-light radius of r_ 1/2 = 26^ +9 _ 9 pc and an absolute magnitude of M_V = -2.4^ +0.5 _ 0.4 mag.

These stars display hints of a small, but measurable, spread in velocities, suggestive of a dwarf galaxy classification for this system, although this result should be interpreted cautiously given its preliminary nature and the small number of observed stars. Deeper imaging, as well as a more complete spectroscopic dataset, will undoubtedly aid us in elucidating the nature of this system. Nonetheless, we believe that the totality of the evidence presented above favors a dwarf galaxy classification for this candidate. We therefore name this system Leo Minor I.

gmw_00010475.overview: The high-redshift galaxy 450.28 is located at a spectroscopic redshift of z=2.47 [arXiv:2104.02098]. It is associated with an extremely bright, unresolved infrared counterpart detected by SPIRE [arXiv:2104.02098].

sources.summary: The paper describes 450.28 as a z=2.47 selected galaxy in the first VLA pointing, associated with an extremely bright unresolved SPIRE counterpart before higher-resolution follow-up separated nearby sources.

quotes.quote: Pointing 1 is centered at = 10:00:57.30 = +02:20:13.3, selected to include a selected galaxy named 450.28 Casey2013a . Prior to high-resolution ALMA follow-up, 450.28 was identified at a redshift of z=2.47 via emission Casey2015a ; we now know that this spectroscopic redshift is only one of multiple sources at similar redshifts. 450.28 is also associated with an extremely bright (S _ 350 m 90 mJy), unresolved /SPIRE counterpart.

gmw_00010476.name: ID3
gmw_00010476.overview: ID3 is a high-redshift quiescent galaxy in a post-starburst phase [arXiv:2104.02098]. It is classified as a low-luminosity radio active galactic nucleus with broad CO kinematics that may indicate merger or AGN activity [arXiv:2104.02098].

sources.summary: The paper classifies ID3 as a low-luminosity radio AGN with significant radio excess, broad CO kinematics suggestive of AGN or merger activity, and post-starburst quiescent colors.

quotes.quote: ID3 is consistent with a significant radio excess considering the error and its unusually steep , as Delhaize2017a also predict q_ IR = 1.65 0.01 for radio AGN at z=2.5 . Further, ID3 has the largest CO line FWHM (1300 140 ) which is kinematically indicative of AGN or merger activity, even when compared to the typically broad ( 600--800 ) CO lines in high-redshift DSFGs e.g., Narayanan2009a . Therefore, we classify ID3 as a low-luminosity radio AGN but this is unlikely to affect our measurements of the stellar masses or star formation rates.

The only galaxy in the quiescent regime is ID3, which we claimed in Section sec:agn to be a low-luminosity radio AGN. This further supports the scenario that ID3 is a quiescent galaxy in a post-starburst phase.

gmw_00010477.name: ID5
gmw_00010477.overview: ID5 is a high-redshift infrared galaxy located near a core of spatially confused sources [arXiv:2104.02098]. It has an individual star formation rate of 400 solar masses per year and a short gas depletion timescale of 180 million years [arXiv:2104.02098].

sources.summary: The paper treats ID5 as an independent detection near the core, deriving its individual far-infrared luminosity, star formation rate, and a short 180 Myr gas depletion timescale.

quotes.quote: Because of the poor angular resolution of SPIRE (FWHM 36 at 500 ), six of the sources in Pointing 1 are spatially confused as one source, with an additional independent detection corresponding to ID5.

Thus, the star formation rate, far-infrared luminosity, and gas depletion timescales are calculated individually for ID5 and 450.58, and as aggregate quantities for the six core galaxies as well as any galaxies that may lie at the same redshift but are not detected in CO. After extracting the photometry, we fit a modified blackbody with a fixed slope ( = 1.8) where only _ peak is allowed to vary. This results in a total SFR for the six core galaxies of 2300 ^ +280 _ 250 , 400 ^ +80 _ 70 in ID5, and 370 ^ +110 _ 80 in 450.58.
The total depletion timescale for the six core galaxies is 340 40 Myr, 180 35 Myr for ID5, and885 250 Myr for 450.58.

gmw_00010478.overview: The galaxy 450.58 is a source located at a redshift of z=2.464 [arXiv:2104.02098]. It exhibits a high star formation rate of 380 solar masses per year and a double-peaked CO line that may indicate either a rotating disk or an unresolved merger [arXiv:2104.02098].

sources.summary: The paper describes 450.58 as an isolated z=2.464 CO-detected galaxy with an unresolved double-peaked line, possible disk or merger structure, high star formation, and normal gas fraction.

quotes.quote: Pointing 2 contains one CO-detected source, associated with the galaxy identified in Casey2015a as 450.58 at z=2.464 . As seen in Figure fig:45058 , the source is unresolved, but the spectrum shows a double-peaked CO line. This is consistent with a rotating disk, though at this spatial resolution it could also indicate two unresolved components of a merger. Thus, we cannot make a statement about the morphology of this galaxy, but we can place an upper limit on its size of 40 kpc.
