It has a high star formation rate of 380 but its gas fraction (50 ) is in line with other z 2 DSFGs of similar gas mass Scoville2016a , showing no evidence for enhanced star formation efficiency (see Figure fig:sfr_ms ).

gmw_00010479.name: G1a
gmw_00010479.overview: G1a is a moderately inclined galaxy located at a redshift of 0.04320 [arXiv:2405.12348]. It is part of a non-strongly-interacting pair, exhibiting symmetric kinematics, strong emission lines, and gas that rotates with its stellar component [arXiv:2405.12348].

sources.summary: The paper describes G1a as a moderately inclined member of a non-strongly-interacting pair, with symmetric kinematics, strong emission lines, and gas rotating with the stars.

quotes.quote: G1 has two galaxies: one moderately inclined (G1a, see Fig.  fig:face_on_maps (a), z_ G1a =0.04320 ) and another edge-on (G1b, see Fig.  fig:edge_on_maps (a), z_ G1b =0.04308 ). Both have reasonably symmetric morphologies and rotation fields, which is an indication that they are not strongly interacting. General properties of these galaxies are listed in Table  tab:morphology . They are at impact parameters of 40.4 kpc and 55.4 kpc from the quasar line-of-sight, respectively, while the two galaxies are separated from each other by 86.7 kpc (see Fig.  fig:system ).

G1a is moderately inclined at i=40^ , while G1b is edge-on with an inclination angle of i=89^ . Both galaxies have strong emission lines, as shown in Figs.  fig:face_on_maps (b) and fig:edge_on_maps (b). Rotation maps of the gas component are shown in Figs.  fig:face_on_maps (d) and fig:edge_on_maps (d), while those of the stellar component are shown in Figs.  fig:face_on_maps (e) and fig:edge_on_maps (d).

gmw_00010480.name: G1b
gmw_00010480.overview: G1b is an edge-on galaxy that forms a pair with the nearby galaxy G1a [arXiv:2405.12348]. It exhibits a symmetric morphology and rotation field, with a maximum gas rotation velocity of 245 km/s [arXiv:2405.12348].

sources.summary: The paper describes G1b as the edge-on member of the G1 pair, with symmetric rotation, strong emission lines, and a maximum gas rotation velocity similar to G1a.

quotes.quote: G1 has two galaxies: one moderately inclined (G1a, see Fig.  fig:face_on_maps (a), z_ G1a =0.04320 ) and another edge-on (G1b, see Fig.  fig:edge_on_maps (a), z_ G1b =0.04308 ). Both have reasonably symmetric morphologies and rotation fields, which is an indication that they are not strongly interacting. General properties of these galaxies are listed in Table  tab:morphology . They are at impact parameters of 40.4 kpc and 55.4 kpc from the quasar line-of-sight, respectively, while the two galaxies are separated from each other by 86.7 kpc (see Fig.  fig:system ).

The stars and gas in G1a are rotating in the same direction with similar rotation velocities, and this is also true for G1b. The coherence between the stars and gas in both galaxies, along with the symmetric and smooth rotation maps are further evidence that the two galaxies are not strongly interacting. G1a has a maximum gas rotation velocity of 237 km s ^ 1 and G1b of 245 km s ^ 1 .

gmw_00010481.name: G2a
gmw_00010481.overview: G2a is one of four merging galaxies within a group located at a mean redshift of approximately 0.098 [arXiv:2405.12348]. It is characterized by strong emission lines and exhibits the highest star formation rate in the group at 5.13 solar masses per year [arXiv:2405.12348].

sources.summary: The paper describes G2a as one of four merging galaxies, a strong-emission-line component dominating group emission, with median oxygen abundance 8.79 and the highest SFR in G2.

quotes.quote: This group (see Fig.  fig:system ) contains four merging galaxies that have a mean redshift of z_ G2 =0.09849 . While at first glance they may look like only two galaxies, further inspection of these system's redshifts (see Table  tab:morphology ) and kinematics (see Fig.  fig:G2_kin ) indicates that they are four distinct galaxies. This group has a velocity dispersion of 109 56 km s ^ 1 . Their distance to the QSO line-of-sight is 100 kpc. G2a, G2b and G2c have strong emission lines, as shown in Fig.  fig:G2_maps (b).
G2a and G2c dominate the emission from this group. For G2c, the are the only emission lines detected.
G2a has the highest SFR of all the galaxies in this Group at 5.13   0.70 M _  yr ^ 1 .

gmw_00010482.name: G2b
gmw_00010482.overview: G2b is a merging-system galaxy that exhibits the characteristics of a post-starburst state [arXiv:2405.12348]. It has older stellar ages than its companions G2a and G2c, and it displays a low star formation rate of 0.06 solar masses per year [arXiv:2405.12348].

sources.summary: The paper treats G2b as a distinct merging-system galaxy, identifying post-starburst characteristics, older stellar ages than G2a and G2c, and low measurable star formation.

quotes.quote: The galaxies in G2 were deblended by analysing the kinematics of the system. We found that G2c is rotating perpendicular to G2a, while G2d has a much lower redshift compared to G2b. Additionally, we looked into differences in the spectra of the different galaxies, with the difference between G2b and G2d being the most significant. G2b has the characteristics of being a post-starburst galaxy (its  equivalent width is 4.3 ), while G2d does not have any emission lines and only Ca  ii  H K absorption is present, indicating that we are looking at different galaxies.

However, in the case of G2b, the median age of its stars is 9.21 0.36  Gyr, meaning that G2b's stellar ages are higher than G2a and G2c. Interestingly, the stars are younger where G2a and G2b meet, having ages as low as 7.48 Gyr in that area, suggesting a recent star-formation event likely triggered by the merging. Further evidence of a recent star-formation event is provided by the fact that the  emission line in G2b has a rest-frame equivalent width of 4.3  , which is indicative of a post-starburst state Wu2018 .
In the case of G2b, we could only calculate its SFR _  =  0.06   0.03 M _  yr ^ 1 , as it does not contain  emission lines.

gmw_00010483.name: G2c
gmw_00010483.overview: G2c is a distinct galaxy located within a merging system of four galaxies [arXiv:2405.12348]. It exhibits strong emission lines, dominates the group's emission alongside G2a, and rotates perpendicular to G2a [arXiv:2405.12348].

sources.summary: The paper describes G2c as a distinct merging-system galaxy with strong emission lines that dominates the group emission with G2a and rotates perpendicular to G2a.

quotes.quote: This group (see Fig.  fig:system ) contains four merging galaxies that have a mean redshift of z_ G2 =0.09849 . While at first glance they may look like only two galaxies, further inspection of these system's redshifts (see Table  tab:morphology ) and kinematics (see Fig.  fig:G2_kin ) indicates that they are four distinct galaxies. This group has a velocity dispersion of 109 56 km s ^ 1 . Their distance to the QSO line-of-sight is 100 kpc. G2a, G2b and G2c have strong emission lines, as shown in Fig.  fig:G2_maps (b).
G2a and G2c dominate the emission from this group. For G2c, the are the only emission lines detected.
We found that G2c is rotating perpendicular to G2a, while G2d has a much lower redshift compared to G2b.

gmw_00010484.name: G2d
gmw_00010484.overview: G2d is a quiescent member of a merging galaxy system [arXiv:2405.12348]. The galaxy lacks emission lines entirely, showing only Ca II H and K absorption features in its spectrum [arXiv:2405.12348].

sources.summary: The paper describes G2d as a distinct quiescent member of the merging system, lacking emission lines, showing Ca II absorption, and requiring cautious stellar-kinematic modeling.

quotes.quote: G2d does not have any emission lines. The only lines present in its spectrum are Ca  ii  H K absorption.

It was not possible to calculate a gas velocity for G2d, as it does not feature any emission lines. In the case of the stellar kinematics, G2d appears quite blueshifted at 270 km s ^ 1 compared to the rest of the group.

It is important to point out that the SNR of the spectra in G2d is considerably lower than that of the other galaxies, so performing Voronoi binning was necessary to reach a SNR  3 . Unfortunately, this meant we only had eight spaxels to analyse the kinematics of this galaxy, and the model had to extrapolate a maximum velocity with less information than the other galaxies. Thus, the model in this galaxy should be interpreted with caution.

gmw_00010485.name: 4C+25.05
gmw_00010485.overview: 4C+25.05 is a luminous flat-spectrum radio quasar located at a high redshift of z = 2.368 [arXiv:1710.01748]. Its broad-band X-ray spectrum displays a hard X-ray power law alongside a soft excess component [arXiv:1710.01748].

sources.summary: The paper studies 4C+25.05 as a luminous high-redshift FSRQ whose broad-band X-ray spectrum shows a soft excess consistent with bulk Comptonization and a jet Lorentz factor near 11.7.

quotes.quote: Here we present the analysis of the simultaneous XMM-Newton and NuSTAR observation of the luminous FSRQ 4C+25.05 ( z=2.368 ). The observed 0.3--30 keV spectrum is best described by the sum of a hard X-ray power law ( = 1.38_ 0.03 ^ +0.05 ) and a soft component, approximated by a blackbody with kT_ BB = 0.66_ 0.04 ^ +0.05 keV (rest frame). If the spectrum of 4C+25.05 is interpreted in the context of bulk Comptonization by cold electrons of broad-line region photons emitted in the direction of the jet, such an unusual temperature implies a bulk Lorentz factor of the jet of _ bulk 11.7 .

In this letter, we study the X-ray spectrum provided by a coordinated XMM-Newton and NuSTAR observation of the FSRQ 4C+25.05 a.k.a. PKS 0123+257, z = 2.368 , (M_ BH /M_ )= 9.24 0.44 ; Kelly07 . The following cosmological parameters are assumed: _ M = 0.27 , _ = 0.73 , and H_0 = 70 km s^ 1 Mpc^ 1 . 4C+25.05 was observed simultaneously by XMM-Newton and NuSTAR , on 2017 January 15 (Obs. IDs 0790820101 and 60201047002, respectively).

gmw_00010486.name: G79635
gmw_00010486.overview: G79635 is a massive, well-resolved spiral galaxy [arXiv:1710.03241]. It has a stellar mass of approximately 10 to the power of 10.4 solar masses [arXiv:1710.03241].

sources.summary: The paper applies its dynamical modeling method to G79635, a massive well-resolved GAMA/SAMI spiral, deriving broadly consistent bulge-disk parameters and testing rotation-curve extrapolation.

quotes.quote: We apply the method to a GAMA spiral galaxy (G79635) with kinematics from the SAMI Galaxy Survey and deep g and band photometry from the VST-KiDS survey, comparing parameter constraints with those from traditional 2D bulge-disk decomposition. Our method returns broadly consistent results for shared parameters, while constraining the mass-to-light ratios of stellar components and reproducing the inferred circular velocity well beyond the limits of the SAMI data.

We choose a well-resolved, massive SAMI spiral galaxy (G79635), with M_ 10^ 10.4 TayHopBal11 from broadband spectral energy distribution fits. Stellar kinematics are derived using single-Gaussian, two-moment pPXF CapEms04 fits to data from the blue and red arms combined, degrading the red arm (FWHM=1.696 , covering the redder half of SDSS band) to match the blue arm's spectral resolution (2.717 , covering the SDSS g band); see vdSBlaFog17 and FogScoOwe15 for details. We create `` SAMIgr '' flux maps by collapsing the spectral cube, masking emission and sky lines as in vdSBlaFog17 .

gmw_00010487.name: NVSS J163547 + 362930
gmw_00010487.overview: NVSS J163547 + 362930 is a high-redshift blazar located at a redshift of 3.647 [arXiv:1710.02904]. The galaxy is confirmed as a gamma-ray source that exhibits gamma-ray variability, including a flare and a bluer-when-brighter spectral behavior [arXiv:1710.02904].

sources.summary: The paper confirms NVSS J163547 + 362930 as a z=3.647 high-redshift blazar with gamma-ray variability, associated flare localization, and bluer-when-brighter spectral behavior.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: The optimized ray location for this time bin is R.A.= 248.89 ^ and Dec.= 36.46 ^

quotes.quote: NVSS J163547 + 362930 was discovered by the MIT-Green Bank 5 GHz survey 1990ApJS...74..129G . Then it has been included in the DR10 SDSS quasar catalog, with a redshift estimation of 3.647 2014A&A...563A..54P . As shown in Figure fig:500mev , there is a flare in the monthly > 500 MeV ray light curve of NVSS J163547 + 362930 which is probably not from the neighbor.

Since the angular separation is 0.06 ^ while the 95 C.L. ray error radius of is 0.10 ^ , the association between NVSS J163547 + 362930 and the ray source is confirmed. Similar with former two sources, a sign of bluer-when-brighter spectral variability behavior has been found for NVSS J163547 + 362930 (i.e. _ flare,>500 MeV = 2.58 0.25 while _ 105 month,>500 MeV = 2.73 0.23).

gmw_00010488.name: G 1
gmw_00010488.overview: G 1 is an obscured, edge-on disk galaxy resembling an Sc or Sd type [arXiv:astro-ph/0611397]. It contains atomic hydrogen with a large velocity width and a lopsided gas profile that is extended toward the south-east [arXiv:astro-ph/0611397].

sources.summary: The paper identifies G 1 as an obscured edge-on disk galaxy in the Great Attractor region, with infrared star-formation signatures, H I detection, lopsided gas, and a large velocity width.

quotes.quote: The combined NIR+MIR images reveal G 1 to be an edge-on disk galaxy with a weak nucleus/bulge, akin to Sc/Sd types, while G 2 has a more face-on spiral orientation with the hint of a large-scale bar structure, possibly of SBbc-type. Atomic Hydrogen A close-up view of the ATCA \ distribution map that is centered on the GLIMPSE galaxies is presented in Fig. 3. Both galaxies are clearly detected in . After primary-beam correction we measure \ flux densities of \ = 1.1 Jy \ and 5.6 Jy \ for G 1 and G 2, respectively.

For the galaxy G 1 (top) we measure 140 . Its large velocity width, \ = 429 , is consistent with the edge-on orientation seen in the infrared images (Fig. 1). Interestingly, the \ profile is quite lopsided with the red-shifted horn being considerably stronger. This is also reflected in the \ distribution which shows an extension of red-shifted velocity gas towards the south-east. A higher resolution \ peak flux distribution (not shown) also shows the flux maximum shifted towards the south-east of the galaxy.

gmw_00010489.name: G 2
gmw_00010489.overview: G 2 is an obscured, relatively face-on spiral galaxy that exhibits hints of a large-scale bar structure [arXiv:astro-ph/0611397]. It is characterized by strong atomic hydrogen emission with an asymmetric gas distribution and a surprisingly large velocity width [arXiv:astro-ph/0611397].

sources.summary: The paper describes G 2 as an obscured, more face-on spiral with possible bar structure, strong H I emission, a large velocity width, and an asymmetric gas distribution.

quotes.quote: The combined NIR+MIR images reveal G 1 to be an edge-on disk galaxy with a weak nucleus/bulge, akin to Sc/Sd types, while G 2 has a more face-on spiral orientation with the hint of a large-scale bar structure, possibly of SBbc-type. Atomic Hydrogen A close-up view of the ATCA \ distribution map that is centered on the GLIMPSE galaxies is presented in Fig. 3. Both galaxies are clearly detected in . After primary-beam correction we measure \ flux densities of \ = 1.1 Jy \ and 5.6 Jy \ for G 1 and G 2, respectively.

The PA of the G 2 disk is approximately --70 , compared to --40 \ as viewed in the infrared. The large velocity width, \ = 418 , is surprising given its rather face-on morphology on the infrared images, thus implying that the inclination-corrected linewidth is very large indeed. Finally, like G 1, the \ spectrum of G 2 appears to be asymetric. In the higher resolution \ peak flux distribution, the \ maximum towards the southeastern part of the galaxy (blue-shifted velocities) is much stronger than that seen in the northwest (red-shifted velocities).

gmw_00010490.name: POWIR1
gmw_00010490.overview: POWIR1 is classified as a highly inclined disk galaxy that exhibits a regular rotational gradient and an ordered velocity field [arXiv:1305.0268]. It features two real continuum blobs, with elevated velocity dispersion near the northern blob potentially signaling a minor merger event [arXiv:1305.0268].

sources.summary: The paper classifies POWIR1 as a very inclined disk galaxy with compact emission, possible real continuum blobs, elevated velocity dispersion near one blob, and ordered rotation.

quotes.quote: However, we notice two blobs which seem to be real. They have an angular size comparable with the seeing of this observation, which reassures us of their detections. The fainter one, near the northern edge of the primary galaxy, may help us understand why this galaxy shows large velocity dispersion values close to the blob, as this may signal a minor merging event.

Conversely, the brightest spot on the south west of the continuum image of the galaxy is not associated with any emission, and it does not cause any significant distortion of the main galaxy. Kinematic models show a regular rotational gradient and a fairly high ( 70 km/s) 1/ error ^ 2 velocity dispersion. Although it is tentative to identify this galaxy as a merger, we prefer to classify it as very inclined disk galaxy, because of its large rotational velocity and ordered velocity field without any substantial disruption.

gmw_00010491.name: POWIR2
gmw_00010491.overview: POWIR2 is a relaxed and ordered galaxy that exhibits an asymmetrical southern region, which may indicate the accretion of a minor object [arXiv:1305.0268]. The system is interpreted as a disk galaxy with clear [N II] emission that suggests the possible presence of an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1305.0268].

sources.summary: The paper describes POWIR2 as asymmetric but relaxed and ordered, with possible minor accretion, clear [N II], possible AGN emission, and a disk-favored interpretation.

quotes.quote: The galaxy looks asymmetrical in its southern part, where we found an enhancement of the SNR as well. We are perhaps witnessing an accretion of a minor object. But what we can state is that this galaxy looks like a relaxed and ordered system.

The line lies in a spectral region far away from any sky line and thus the [ NII ] line is clearly identified, giving a ratio of [ NII ] / = 0.49 . This 1:2 ratio between and [ NII ] is preserved within the external spaxels, and may indicate the presence of an AGN. We consider this system as an early disk-like galaxy or spheroid with a disk, as its large velocity dispersion and low V_ max / ratio reveal. Nevertheless, the interpretation of a disk galaxy is favoured at the light of the strong velocity gradient.

gmw_00010492.name: POWIR3
gmw_00010492.overview: POWIR3 is classified as a disturbed or merging galaxy that exhibits a clear rotational field [arXiv:1305.0268]. Although it lacks any neighboring galaxy, its stretched shape provides evidence for an ongoing merger [arXiv:1305.0268].

sources.summary: The paper classifies POWIR3 as a disturbed or merging galaxy, noting a clear rotational field, stretched morphology, no neighboring galaxy, and limited integration time.

quotes.quote: This is a clear case of a disturbed object, but it is remarkable that even in this case, the rotational field is quite clear. The lack of any neighbouring galaxy and stretched shape are evidence for an ongoing merger. With the data we have we cannot add anything to this discussion. It is important to stress that this is one of the galaxies which was observed for half of the integration time, and for this reason it does not have a well-defined shape. Taking all the available probes into account we define it as disturbed/merging galaxy.

gmw_00010493.name: POWIR4
gmw_00010493.overview: POWIR4 is classified as a disturbed or merging galaxy [arXiv:1305.0268]. It exhibits a clumpy morphology with at least two visible clumps, a modest rotational velocity field, and a comparatively large velocity dispersion consistent with its interacting nature [arXiv:1305.0268].

sources.summary: The paper treats POWIR4 as a disturbed or merging system, with Palomar imaging favoring interaction, kinematics from a non-primary visible object, modest rotation, high dispersion, and clumpy morphology.

quotes.quote: The Palomar image also shows this feature, indicating that the merging interpretation is favoured. Our kinematics are thus inferred for the visible object which was not the primary target of our observations. We find a rotational velocity field, but it is not large (perhaps due to its non-massive nature) and a comparatively big velocity dispersion consistent with its interacting nature. Its morphology is clumpy, with at least two clumps being visible. We catalog it as disturbed/merging galaxy.

gmw_00010494.name: POWIR5
gmw_00010494.overview: POWIR5 is classified as a perturbed galaxy that appears as a blob with a tail [arXiv:1305.0268]. It exhibits extended emission, high rotational velocity, and a high velocity dispersion, with widespread nitrogen emission detected across its spaxels [arXiv:1305.0268].

sources.summary: The paper describes POWIR5 as a blob with a tail, possible companions, extended emission, high rotation and dispersion, puzzling widespread [N II], and a perturbed-galaxy classification.

quotes.quote: By looking at the flux images, this galaxy appears as a blob with a tail in its upper part. There might be companions according to the POWIR/DEEP2 imaging, but we cannot state this with certainty. Its emission extends over the image and the continuum image. The maximum rotational velocity is very high, as is the velocity dispersion, and the result is that the dynamical mass is the second largest in our sample.

We find [ NII ] / = 0.60 , which is puzzling as it is a large ratio and we note that the [ NII ] is detectable all over the galaxy spaxels, and not just concentrated in the center, as we would expect for AGN emission. The properties of this massive galaxy cannot be perfectly explained by a disk-like object and for this reason we classify it as perturbed galaxy.

gmw_00010495.name: POWIR6
gmw_00010495.overview: POWIR6 is a disk galaxy that is strongly rotationally supported [arXiv:1305.0268]. It is the brightest galaxy in its imaging sample and features a central H-alpha emission hole surrounded by four star-forming clumps [arXiv:1305.0268].

sources.summary: The paper identifies POWIR6 as the brightest POWIR-imaging galaxy, with a central H-alpha hole, surrounding clumps, strong rotational support, and a disk-galaxy classification.

quotes.quote: This is the brightest galaxy in our POWIR imaging. The appearance of the galaxy is different from either the continuum or the band image. Remarkably, we do not detect emission in the central area, appearing as a hole in the flux map and four knots or clumps surrounding it. This hole overlaps with the center of the and continuum images.
Another piece of evidence for a star-forming disk is that it is strongly rotationally supported ( V_ max / = 4.88 and V_ max = 214 km s ^ 1 ). Hence, we classify it as a disk galaxy.

gmw_00010496.name: POWIR7
gmw_00010496.overview: POWIR7 is classified as a perturbed galaxy characterized by a slightly distorted and clumpy shape [arXiv:1305.0268]. Although its low signal-to-noise ratio limits further analysis, rotation appears to play a role in supporting the galaxy [arXiv:1305.0268].

sources.summary: The paper classifies POWIR7 as perturbed: its detector-edge placement lowers SNR, but POWIR/DEEP and continuum maps align, showing a slightly distorted clumpy galaxy with rotational support.

quotes.quote: POWIR7 The maps belonging to this galaxy are shown in Figure fig:montage_powir7 . In this case, one of the images of the galaxy fell in the borders of the SINFONI detector. This is the reason for its low SNR in all its spaxels. However, POWIR/DEEP imaging, and continuum maps overlap well, and show a slightly distorted and clumpy galaxy. Rotation seems to play an important role in the support of this galaxy (with V_ max / = 6.88 ), but the low SNR prevents us from drawing any further conclusions. Because of its irregular shape, we consider this is a perturbed galaxy.

gmw_00010497.name: POWIR8
gmw_00010497.overview: POWIR8 is classified as a rotating disk galaxy with very strong rotational support [arXiv:1305.0268]. It features a large, reliably measured dynamical mass and may contain a central bulge component indicated by a bright central region with a high velocity dispersion [arXiv:1305.0268].

sources.summary: The paper finds POWIR8 is a clear rotating disk with possible central bulge, high central velocity dispersion, strong rotational support, and a large, reliably measured dynamical mass.

quotes.quote: POWIR8 The maps belonging to this galaxy are shown in Figure fig:montage_powir8 . Our best seeing (0.42 arcsec) observations are for this system where we retrieve a very clear disk. A bulge component in the center may be present, as the central region is very bright in both and imaging, and displays a large velocity dispersion of 130 km/s. This agrees with the renditions of the anisotropy plot (which will be explained in the Section subsec:rot_vs_sigma ), as this system is found near a locus close to the local fast rotators. However, an alternative explanation could be that our models were not able to resolve completely the rotation in the inner part of the galaxy, in which case we would have a ring distribution of gas as in POWIR6.

The rotational support is very strong ( V_ max / = 4.60 ). Its dynamical mass is also very large ( 6.63 10^ 11 M_ ) and reliably measured due to its high rotational velocity gradient, the identification of which was helped by the excellent seeing of this object's observation. We classify this galaxy as a rotating disk.

gmw_00010498.name: POWIR10
gmw_00010498.overview: POWIR10 is an interacting system consisting of two merging galaxies [arXiv:1305.0268]. The southern galaxy likely hosts an active galactic nucleus at its center, while the northern galaxy exhibits clear kinematics and a hint of continuum emission [arXiv:1305.0268].

sources.summary: The paper describes POWIR10 as a two-galaxy interacting or merging system: flux and emission arise from different objects, the southern object may host an AGN, and kinematics trace the northern galaxy.

quotes.quote: POWIR10 The maps belonging to this galaxy are shown in Figures fig:montage_powir10_agn and fig:montage_powir10_no_agn . We present two different set of figures for this galaxy. Our and continuum images show two galaxies. Most of the flux in these two maps comes from the object in the southern part of the image (Figure fig:montage_powir10_agn ), although emission is mainly found in the galaxy on the north (Figure fig:montage_powir10_no_agn ).

This system has some similarities with POWIR4, specifically in that most of the emission does not come from the position where we had expected to find the massive galaxy. For this reason, the kinematic parameters listed on the tables as POWIR10 are associated with the object identified as the non-massive galaxy, as kinematics could only be reliably retrieve from it. As a matter of fact, we found strong emission at the [ NII ] wavelength for the central spaxels of the southern object. This makes us suspect that, as it is nearly a point source, this system harbours an AGN on its center, and the emission at its sides might be related with shocked gas by outflows of material coming out from it. Regarding the non-massive galaxy within our imaging, we are able to detect a hint of continuum and very clear kinematics. Due to the low values in the kinematic velocities for this second galaxy we conclude that the photometric mass is only derived for the AGN. For the total system, due to its distortion/clumpiness, the velocity dispersions enhancement in between the two objects and of course since is detected at the same wavelength for the two subsystems, we classify this object as a merger.

gmw_00010499.name: POWIR11
gmw_00010499.overview: POWIR11 is a clearly detected, nearly face-on galaxy with an extended structure [arXiv:1305.0268]. It exhibits a disturbed discoidal morphology with central clumps and a detectable rotational velocity gradient, indicating a perturbed or turbulent disk rather than a merger [arXiv:1305.0268].

sources.summary: The paper describes POWIR11 as a clearly detected, nearly face-on galaxy with extended emission, a detectable rotational velocity gradient, disturbed discoidal morphology, central clumps, and a perturbed disk classification.

quotes.quote: POWIR11 The maps belonging to this galaxy are shown in Figure fig:montage_powir11 . This is a clearly detected galaxy which was only observed half of the time as the other systems. However, emission show an extended galaxy, in agreement with the and continuum images. It is most probably close to being face-on, as its inclination is the smallest (30 ^ ) in our sample.

The SNR decreases in its western side because of the presence of a sky line at the same side of the spectrum where the line is found. Nevertheless, the line is clearly distinguished in all the spaxels up to the faint outskirts, making the rotational velocity gradient easy to detect. This was the reason behind setting S/N =2 as a threshold for the galaxy maps. Overall, this galaxy presents a disturbed discoidal shape and a couple of bright spots or clumps in its central part. The absence of any merger signature makes us conclude that this is a disk, although rather perturbed or turbulent, as indicated by its fairly low V_ max / ratio.

gmw_00010500.name: NGC 4849
gmw_00010500.overview: NGC 4849 is a radio galaxy with a visible core and a hint of jet emission oriented in the North-South direction [arXiv:2203.01958]. It features a long, steep-spectrum tail that likely traces the motion of the galaxy through the intracluster medium [arXiv:2203.01958].

sources.summary: The paper detects NGC 4849 as a radio source with visible core, possible jet/lobe emission, a long steep-spectrum tail, and motion-tracing analogs in other cluster outskirts.

quotes.quote: From the 6 resolution image, the core of NGC 4849 is visible, and a hint of jet emission in the North-South direction is also present. A second bright component is located at the South of the core, which could either be an unrelated radio source seen in projection, or the lobes of NGC 4849. The maximum extension of this tail in the WSRT image is 7 ^ . Using the WSRT H and ``LOFAR as WSRT H" images (see Sec. sec:data and Tab. tab:images ) and taking into account the higher noise of the WSRT image in that region, we derive a 2- limit for the spectral index of the tail < -2 . Analysing the emission of this source is not the aim of this paper, we note that similar steep spectrum tails have been found in the outskirts of other clusters (e.g. Abell 1132 Wilber17 , Abell 2255 Botteon20 ) likely tracing the motion of the galaxy in the ICM.

gmw_00010501.name: SDSS J123142.17+381658.9
gmw_00010501.overview: SDSS J123142.17+381658.9 is classified as a blazar based on models of its X-ray data [arXiv:2203.09527]. The galaxy hosts a supermassive black hole with a mass below one billion solar masses [arXiv:2203.09527].

sources.summary: The paper models SDSS J123142.17+381658.9 with a sub-billion-solar-mass black hole and classifies it as a blazar because smaller viewing-angle models fit its X-ray data.

quotes.quote: SDSS J123142.17+381658.9 --- The BH mass is M=7 10^8 M_ with L_ d =20 L_ Edd . We show three different models with the same =11 but different _ v .

According to our definition, to be classified as blazar, _ v 1/ . Therefore, using =11 , the limiting angle is 5.2 degrees. We see that the model with _ v =8^ passes below the X--ray data, that are instead well accounted for with smaller _ v . We thus classify this source as a blazar, but with a mass below 10^9 M_ .

gmw_00010502.name: SDSS J123503.03--000331.7
gmw_00010502.overview: SDSS J123503.03--000331.7 is classified as a blazar with a small viewing angle [arXiv:2203.09527]. The system hosts a supermassive black hole of 2 billion solar masses accreting at seven percent of the Eddington limit, which produces a hard, highly luminous X-ray spectrum [arXiv:2203.09527].

sources.summary: The paper classifies SDSS J123503.03--000331.7 as a blazar, with a 2e9 solar-mass black hole, 7 percent Eddington accretion, hard luminous X-rays, and a small viewing angle.

quotes.quote: SDSS J123503.03--000331.7 --- The BH mass is M=2 10^9 M_ with L_ d = 7 Eddington. The X--ray spectrum is hard with a large luminosity, and is fitted well with =13 and _ v =3^ . We classify this source as a blazar.

gmw_00010503.name: SDSS J124230.58+542257.3
gmw_00010503.overview: SDSS J124230.58+542257.3 is an active galactic nucleus hosting a supermassive black hole with a mass of 3.5 billion solar masses [arXiv:2203.09527]. Although its X-ray spectrum is poorly constrained, the data disfavor a very hard spectrum, leading to its classification as a non-blazar [arXiv:2203.09527].

sources.summary: The paper reports SDSS J124230.58+542257.3 has a 3.5e9 solar-mass black hole, but its poorly constrained X-ray spectrum disfavors a very hard spectrum, so it is classified non-blazar.

quotes.quote: SDSS J124230.58+542257.3 --- The BH mass is M=3.5 10^9 M_ with L_ d = 8 Eddington. The X--ray spectrum is not well constrained, but data seem to exclude a very hard spectrum, therefore we classify the source as a non-blazar.

gmw_00010504.name: SDSS J231448.71+020151.1
gmw_00010504.overview: SDSS J231448.71+020151.1 is a luminous, radio-loud X-ray quasar [arXiv:2203.09527]. The system hosts a supermassive black hole with a mass of 1.5 billion solar masses and exhibits a hard X-ray spectrum [arXiv:2203.09527].

sources.summary: The paper models SDSS J231448.71+020151.1 as a hard, luminous X-ray quasar with a 1.5e9 solar-mass black hole and best-fit viewing angle within the blazar beaming criterion.

quotes.quote: SDSS J231448.71+020151.1 --- The BH mass is M=1.5 10^9 M_ with L_ d =15 Eddington. The X--ray spectrum is hard and luminous: the best model prefer _ v < 1/ , but note that in this case we find a relatively small value of (=9), so that the limiting angle becomes 6.3^ .

gmw_00010505.name: NGC 3837
gmw_00010505.overview: NGC 3837 is an elliptical galaxy that contains an unresolved X-ray source [arXiv:astro-ph/0202431]. Its X-ray spectrum exhibits a soft component, which may represent a thermal halo, and a hard component dominant above 2-3 keV [arXiv:astro-ph/0202431].

sources.summary: The paper reports NGC 3837 as an unresolved X-ray source with roughly 400 counts and a two-component spectrum including a possible thermal halo.

quotes.quote: NGC 3837 In this observation, it is 12.4 ' off-axis (the 50 EEAR of PSF is 13 '' there) in the S1 chip and unresolved. About 400 counts from it were collected. No significant variations in its \ lightcurve were found. Its X-ray spectrum clearly shows at least two components: a soft one and a hard one dominant above 2-3 keV (Fig. 4). A MEKAL model is used to represent the soft component, which may be a thermal halo.

gmw_00010506.name: CGCG 097-109
gmw_00010506.overview: CGCG 097-109 is a galaxy featuring a point-like X-ray source at its optical nucleus [arXiv:astro-ph/0202431]. This nuclear emission is most likely powered by a low-luminosity active galactic nucleus [arXiv:astro-ph/0202431].

sources.summary: The paper presents CGCG 097-109 as a first X-ray detection whose nuclear, point-like power-law emission is more likely a low-luminosity AGN than X-ray binaries.

quotes.quote: CGCG 097-109 This is the first X-ray detection of this source, while the previously claimed detections Be5 (B83), Gp11* and Ge14 (G95) are actually the nearby bright point source 15 (Fig. 2). In this observation, it is a point-like source and the position agrees with the optical nucleus of the galaxy within 0.5 '' . The superior spatial resolution of \ puts an upper limit 0.5h ^ 1 _ 0.5 kpc to the size of the X-ray emitting source.
The source found is point-like with an upper limit of only 1 '' . Thus, this X-ray source is more likely the LLAGN of CGCG 097-109.

gmw_00010507.name: CGCG 097-113
gmw_00010507.overview: CGCG 097-113 is a galaxy with an optical size of approximately 15 arcseconds [arXiv:astro-ph/0202431]. It hosts a compact nuclear X-ray source that is likely a low-luminosity active galactic nucleus [arXiv:astro-ph/0202431].

sources.summary: The paper reports CGCG 097-113 as a first X-ray detection with a compact nuclear source that is probably a low-luminosity AGN rather than an ultraluminous X-ray source.

quotes.quote: CGCG 097-113 This is the first X-ray detection of this source. Only 18 counts are collected from this source and the upper limit on the size of the X-ray emitter is 2 '' . The position agrees with the optical nucleus of the galaxy within 1 '' . Its 0.5 - 10 keV luminosity is estimated to be 4 - 9 10 ^ 39 ergs s ^ 1 assuming a photon index of 1.7 - 2.5.

However, the optical size of the galaxy ( 15 '' ) is much larger than the size of the X-ray emitter. Thus, the observed X-ray source is more likely a LLAGN, while the possibility of an ULX source is low as we mentioned above for CGCG 097-109.

gmw_00010508.name: NGC 3861B
gmw_00010508.overview: NGC 3861B is a detected member galaxy in its region [arXiv:astro-ph/0202431]. Its measured X-ray luminosity is 5e40 ergs/s, which exceeds the expected contribution from stellar binaries and suggests the presence of an additional X-ray emission component [arXiv:astro-ph/0202431].

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 3861B as a detected member galaxy whose X-ray luminosity may exceed the expected binary contribution, although the data are statistically limited.

quotes.quote: NGC 3861B In this observation, only 70 counts were collected. We simply used a thermal Bremsstrahlung with a temperature fixed at 8 keV (following S01) to fit the spectrum because of the limited statistics. The fit is acceptable and the derived L _ x is 5 10 ^ 40 ergs s ^ 1 (0.3 - 10 keV), which is 4 times the scaled value from its optical luminosity (based on X-ray-to-optical luminosity ratio of the bulge of M31). This might imply that there is another X-ray emission component besides the binary one.

gmw_00010509.name: UGC 3968
gmw_00010509.overview: UGC 3968 is a low surface brightness galaxy characterized by a faint disk and a bright oval bulge [arXiv:1108.0763, arXiv:1607.01889]. The galaxy features a long, thin, boxy bar with two faint spiral arms, and it may host a starburst-AGN composite nucleus [arXiv:1108.0763, arXiv:1607.01889].

sources.summary: The paper presents UGC 3968 as a little-studied LSB galaxy with a prominent bulge, faint disk, bar, faint spiral arms, and possible composite nucleus.

quotes.quote: UGC 3968: Not much is known about this LSB galaxy except that it has a prominent bulge with a faint disk. The 2MASS image reveals a bar associated with the bulge and two faint spiral arms. It is also classified as an LSB galaxy by but it is not clear whether the galaxy has an AGN.
UGC 3968 might also host a starburst-AGN composite nucleus.

sources.summary: The paper says UGC 3968 had interrupted H-band observations but shows a long thin bar and bright oval bulge, gave good ellipse-fitting results, and has a boxy bar shape.

quotes.quote: UGC 3968- The observations in the H band were interrupted by the clouds. The galaxy has a long thin bar and a bright oval bulge.
For galaxies that had good signal to noise, such as UGC 3968, UGC 9927, IC 2423, even without fixing the center gave good fitting results.
For example the b4 parameter of UGC 3968 indicates the bar has a boxy shape.

gmw_00010510.name: UGC 4219
gmw_00010510.overview: UGC 4219 is a giant low surface brightness galaxy that hosts a large central bulge and an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1108.0763]. Its low surface brightness disk is characterized by faint spiral arms [arXiv:1108.0763].

sources.summary: The paper describes UGC 4219 as a giant LSB galaxy with a large bulge, an AGN, and faint spiral arms in its disk.

quotes.quote: UGC 4219: Not much is known about this giant LSB galaxy either. According to , the galaxy has a large bulge and an AGN. The LSB disk shows faint spiral arms.

gmw_00010511.name: UGC 6968
gmw_00010511.overview: UGC 6968 is a low surface brightness galaxy characterized by a central bulge and two faint spiral arms extending into its disk [arXiv:1108.0763]. The galaxy hosts an active galactic nucleus powered by a black hole on the lower mass tail of low-mass black holes [arXiv:1108.0763].

sources.summary: The paper reports UGC 6968 as an LSB galaxy with a prominent bulge, AGN, faint spiral arms, clear AGN activity, and low black-hole mass.

quotes.quote: UGC 6968: Not much is known about this galaxy but it is described as an LSB galaxy having a prominent bulge and an AGN schombert98 . There are two faint spiral arms extending out into the disk.
Of the galaxies in our study, UGC 6614, UGC 1922, UGC 6968 and LSBC F568-6 (Malin 2) show clear signatures of AGN activity.
The masses are 0.3 10^6 \ M_ for the galaxies UGC 1922, UGC 6968 and F568-6, and lie on the lower mass tail of the low-mass blackhole sample of which has a median mass of 1.3 10^6 M _ .

gmw_00010512.name: UGC 7357
gmw_00010512.overview: UGC 7357 is a low surface brightness disk galaxy characterized by a bright bulge and a fairly featureless disk [arXiv:1108.0763]. The galaxy does not appear to host an active galactic nucleus and may have a metallicity slightly below solar [arXiv:1108.0763].

sources.summary: The paper summarizes UGC 7357 as an LSB disk galaxy with a bright bulge, no apparent AGN, featureless disk, and possibly subsolar metallicity.

quotes.quote: UGC 7357: This galaxy has an LSB disk macarthur.etal.2003 and a bright bulge. But does not appear to have an AGN schombert98 . The disk is fairly featureless.
The exception is the galaxy UGC 7357 which may have metallicity slightly less than solar.

gmw_00010513.name: 2MFGC 13581
gmw_00010513.overview: 2MFGC 13581 is an edge-on galaxy located at a distance of approximately 145 Mpc [arXiv:1310.5530]. It is classified as a probable Seyfert 2 active galaxy that hosts symmetric high-velocity water megamasers, which likely originate from a circumnuclear disk [arXiv:1310.5530].

sources.summary: The paper reports 2MFGC 13581 as a probable Seyfert 2 edge-on galaxy with symmetric high-velocity water megamaser groups likely arising from a circumnuclear disk.

quotes.quote: A new H _2 O disk-MM is found in the optically edge-on galaxy 2MFGC 13581 ( v_ LSR =10309 42 km s ^ 1 2000AAS..142..417H , D_ L =145 Mpc, z=0.034 ). It is the second-closest of six Seyfert candidates in the Hamburg/SAO survey for emission-line galaxies and is a probable Sy 2 2000AAS..142..417H . Masers are detected in two high-velocity groups, symmetrically offset from the systemic velocity by about 390 km s ^ 1 .
The symmetric spectrum suggests that emission most likely originates from a circumnuclear masing disk.

gmw_00010514.name: IRAS 17526+3253
gmw_00010514.overview: IRAS 17526+3253 is an infrared galaxy and a candidate major merger system [arXiv:1310.5530]. Near-infrared imaging reveals two components separated by approximately 30 kiloparsecs, and the galaxy hosts narrow water megamaser emission that may originate from a shocked region within the ongoing merger [arXiv:1310.5530].

sources.summary: The paper finds narrow systemic water megamasers in IRAS 17526+3253, describes it as an infrared merger candidate, and weighs star-formation, shocked-region, or disk origins.

quotes.quote: IRAS 17526+3253 Water megamasers in this IR galaxy ( UGC 11035 ; v_ LSR =7818 9 km s ^ 1 1991rc3..book.....D , D_ L =108 Mpc, z=0.026 (NED), L_ IR 7e11 L_ estimated from IRAS fluxes using the method by 1986AA...168..237W ) have a relatively high peak flux of 170 mJy. Emission appears in a narrow, slightly blueshifted 40 km s ^ 1 window around the systemic velocity. It has three features: two wider profiles at 7797 km s ^ 1 (47 mJy peak, 4.0 km s ^ 1 FWHM, 50 L_ ) and 7810 km s ^ 1 (65 mJy peak, 10.9 km s ^ 1 FWHM, 210 L_ ), and a narrow emission line at 7808 km s ^ 1 (170 mJy peak, 2.2 km s ^ 1 FWHM, 100 L_ ).
The 2MASS image shows two near-infrared components separated by 60 '' (about 30 kpc) with an angular size ratio of 5 . The image suggests UGC 11035 could be a major merger system.
Given limited data on the object we speculate that H _2 O emission might originate from a small shocked region in an ongoing merger similar to Arp 299.

gmw_00010515.name: FM1
gmw_00010515.overview: FM1, also known as F6D1, is a dwarf spheroidal galaxy located at a distance of approximately 3.4 Mpc [arXiv:2207.02208, arXiv:1003.0861]. It hosts a nearly uniformly old, metal-poor stellar population [arXiv:2207.02208] that exhibits distinct metallicity gradients [arXiv:1003.0861].

sources.summary: The paper uses FM1 as an observational test case, describing its old metal-poor population, surface brightness, distance, expected and observed resolved-star counts.

quotes.quote: We therefore choose the galaxy FM1 (a.k.a. F6D1) which has a nearly uniformly old ( 11 Gyr ) and metal-poor ( Fe/H 1.25 ) stellar population as inferred from its resolved CMD weisz2011a . dalcanton2009 report 19,390 stars jointly detected in the F606W and F814W filters of the Hubble Space Telescope's Advanced Camera for Surveys (ACS) with a 50 completeness limiting magnitude of 28.92 and 27.85 magnitudes, respectively (in the Vega system). Detection in both bands is necessary for removal of contamination from background galaxies e.g. muzzin2013 .

Specifically, it is reported that FM1 has an average surface brightness in the B band This is the actual Johnson-Cousins B band, not our generic labeling of an arbitrary band B used throughout the rest of the paper. of 25.8 mag arcsec ^ 2 and an angular diameter of 0.89' . We also use the distance to the FM1 reported by dalcanton2009 as d = 3.4 Mpc .

Taking the angular diameter reported by karachentsev2013 this implies a total number of detected stars of 10,795 . If we restrict detected stars reported in dalcanton2009 to this same assumed footprint we find N_ det = 5162 .

sources.summary: F6D1 is included in the first-depth M81 dwarf sample and shows metallicity gradients, a centrally concentrated metal-rich population, and an elevated total contaminant fraction.

quotes.quote: The first depth category contains KDG 61 , KDG 64 , DDO 71 , F6D1 and F12D1.

Population Gradients By examining the cumulative metallicity distributions in the middle panels in Fig. sl_figure7 and Fig. sl_figure8 , we conclude that the metallicity gradients are present in the case of DDO 71, DDO 78, DDO 44 and F6D1, while the metallicity gradients are less pronounced or not present in the remaining dSphs. We again separate the RGB stars in each dSph into two samples, where we choose to separate the distributions at the observed weighted mean metallicity. The probabilities from the two-sided Kolmogorov-Smirnov (K-S) test that the two components are drawn from the same parent distribution are listed in Table table3 , column (4). The K-S results are consistent with showing spatially separated populations in the case of DDO 71, DDO 78, DDO 44 and F6D1.
The fraction of the total contaminants is less than 1 in all cases apart from IKN, HS 117 and F6D1, where the fraction of the total contaminants is approximately 1.1 , 1.3 and 2.1 , respectively.

gmw_00010516.name: host of AT2022lri
gmw_00010516.overview: The host of AT2022lri is a nearby quiescent galaxy with a total stellar mass of $10^{9.6}\ M_{\odot}$ [arXiv:2405.11343]. It is modeled with a face-on stellar disk, a pseudo-bulge, and a low-mass black hole, showing negligible star formation [arXiv:2405.11343].

sources.summary: The paper models the host of AT2022lri as a nearby quiescent low-mass galaxy with a face-on disk, pseudo-bulge, low-mass black hole, and negligible star formation.

quotes.quote:
Morphology and Spectral Energy Distribution Fitting
We employed the multi-band imaging decomposition tool GALFITS (Li Ho, in prep) to analyze the pre-TDE images of AT2022lri. The dataset comprises UV images from the Galaxy Evolution Explorer (GALEX, Martin2005 ), griy images from Pan-STARRS1 Chambers2016 The Pan-STARRS1 z band image was excluded due to artifacts resulting from overexposure. , grz images from the DESI Legacy Imaging Survey (LS; Dey2019 ), as well as infrared images from the Two-Micron All Sky Survey Skrutskie2006 and the Wide-field Infrared Survey Explorer Wright2010 , totaling 16 images.

In Table  tab:host , the first component has a larger \ index ( n 1.93 ) but a smaller size ( R_ e 0.9 ), while the second component has a \ index of approximately 0.92 but a larger size ( R_ e 3.6 ). Consequently, we identify the first component as the stellar bulge and the second as the stellar disk. These components also exhibit notably different axis ratios; the disk component has q 0.96 , suggesting a face-on view.

The main results from our work are: At a redshift of z=0.03275 , the host of AT2022lri is a quiescent galaxy that can be modeled with a disk component viewed face-on and a pseudo bulge component. It has a total stellar mass of 10^ 9.6 M_ , a black hole mass of 10^ 5 M_ , and no historical X-ray detection at 2.5 10^ 41 erg s^ 1 ( sec:hostgalaxy ).

gmw_00010517.name: Leo IV
gmw_00010517.overview: Leo IV is a dwarf spheroidal galaxy that serves as a satellite of the Milky Way [arXiv:0912.3251]. It is a significantly elongated system with an ellipticity of approximately 0.5, and it may form a bound, tumbling pair with the nearby dwarf galaxy Leo V [arXiv:0912.3251].

sources.summary: Deep photometry finds Leo IV larger and elongated, searches for extra-tidal material with Leo V, and argues the pair may be a bound tumbling system rather than a shared-orbit remnant.

quotes.quote: We have obtained deep photometry in two 1 1 fields covering the close pair of dwarf spheroidal galaxies Leo IV and Leo V and part of the area in between. From the distribution of likely red giant branch and horizontal branch stars in the data set, we find that both Leo IV and Leo V are significantly larger than indicated by previous measurements based on shallower data. With a half-light radius of r_h =4 6 0 8 (206 36 pc) and r_h =2 6 0 6 (133 31 pc), respectively, both systems are now well within the physical size bracket of typical dwarf spheroidal Milky Way satellites. Both are also found to be significantly elongated with an ellipticity of 0.5 , a characteristic shared by many of the fainter ( M_V>-8 ) Milky Way dwarf spheroidals.

We investigate different scenarios to explain the close proximity of Leo IV and Leo V and the possible tidal bridge between them. Orbit calculations demonstrate that the two systems cannot share the exact same orbit, while a compromise orbit does not approach the Galactic center more than 160 kpc, rendering it unlikely that they are remnants of a single disrupted progenitor. A comparison with cosmological simulations shows that a chance collision between unrelated subhalos is negligibly small. Given their relative distance and velocity, Leo IV and Leo V could be a bound `tumbling pair', if their combined mass exceeds 8 4 10^9 M _ .

gmw_00010518.name: Leo V
gmw_00010518.overview: Leo V is a dwarf spheroidal galaxy and a satellite of the Milky Way [arXiv:0912.3251]. It is significantly elongated with an ellipticity of approximately 0.5, and it may form a bound tumbling pair with the nearby dwarf galaxy Leo IV [arXiv:0912.3251].

sources.summary: Deep photometry finds Leo V larger and elongated, searches for extra-tidal material with Leo IV, and argues the pair may be a bound tumbling system rather than a shared-orbit remnant.

quotes.quote: We have obtained deep photometry in two 1 1 fields covering the close pair of dwarf spheroidal galaxies Leo IV and Leo V and part of the area in between. From the distribution of likely red giant branch and horizontal branch stars in the data set, we find that both Leo IV and Leo V are significantly larger than indicated by previous measurements based on shallower data. With a half-light radius of r_h =4 6 0 8 (206 36 pc) and r_h =2 6 0 6 (133 31 pc), respectively, both systems are now well within the physical size bracket of typical dwarf spheroidal Milky Way satellites. Both are also found to be significantly elongated with an ellipticity of 0.5 , a characteristic shared by many of the fainter ( M_V>-8 ) Milky Way dwarf spheroidals.

We investigate different scenarios to explain the close proximity of Leo IV and Leo V and the possible tidal bridge between them. Orbit calculations demonstrate that the two systems cannot share the exact same orbit, while a compromise orbit does not approach the Galactic center more than 160 kpc, rendering it unlikely that they are remnants of a single disrupted progenitor. A comparison with cosmological simulations shows that a chance collision between unrelated subhalos is negligibly small. Given their relative distance and velocity, Leo IV and Leo V could be a bound `tumbling pair', if their combined mass exceeds 8 4 10^9 M _ .

gmw_00010519.name: NGC 582
gmw_00010519.overview: NGC 582 is a galaxy that hosted the Type II supernova SN 2015cz, which was discovered in October 2015 [arXiv:2103.09166]. Spectroscopic analysis of the galaxy's nucleus reveals a super-solar oxygen abundance of 8.81 dex [arXiv:2103.09166].

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 582 as SN 2015cz host, fits its LAMOST nuclear spectrum, subtracts the stellar continuum, and measures emission lines yielding a super-solar oxygen abundance.

quotes.quote: SN 2015cz (PNV J01315945+3328458) was discovered in the galaxy NGC 582 by Jiaming Liao, Guoyou Sun and Xing Gao on 2015 October 02.9 UT (2457298.4 JD) www.cbat.eps.harvard.edu/unconf/followups/J01315945+3328458.html . A classification spectrum of the event, obtained on 2015 October 3.7 UT with the 2.4 m telescope at LiJiang Gaomeigu Station of Yunnan Observatories, confirmed SN 2015cz to be a Type II SN 2015ATel.8120....1Z .

The spectrum of the host galaxy nucleus of SN 2015cz (NGC 582) was published in the second data release of LAMOST (Large Sky Area Multi-Object Fiber Spectroscopic Telescope, also known as Guo Shou Jing telescope). We used Penalized Pixel-Fitting method (pPXF, 2017MNRAS.466..798C ) and the MILES spectral library 2006MNRAS.371..703S for stellar continuum fitting in NGC 582. The pPXF fits (as shown in Figure fig:Ng1 ) were subtracted from the observed spectrum to accurately measure the emission line fluxes.

The dereddened fluxes of the emission lines from NGC 7742 are measured from the spectrum of H II region in NGC 7742 closest to SN site from and that from NGC 582 using the spectrum of the host galaxy centre. The oxygen abundance of NGC 337 (SN 2014cx) is 8.27 0.02 dex , NGC 7742 (SN 2014cy) is 8.90 0.02 dex and NGC 582 (SN 2015cz) is 8.81 0.02 dex .

gmw_00010520.name: GP J0213+0056
gmw_00010520.overview: GP J0213+0056 is a starburst Green Pea galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 0.04 [arXiv:2206.07726]. It is undergoing a merger with a companion galaxy 4.7 kpc away, which has disturbed its atomic hydrogen into an extended, broken-ring structure [arXiv:2206.07726].

sources.summary: The paper maps H I around GP J0213+0056, finding a broken-ring distribution, a close companion, and merger-driven disturbed gas that likely triggered the starburst and aided LyC escape.

quotes.quote: We report the first mapping of the spatial distribution of atomic hydrogen ( ) in and around a Green Pea, GP J0213+0056 at z=0.0399 , using the Giant Metrewave Radio Telescope (GMRT). Like many Green Peas, GP J0213+0056 shows strong \ emission in single-dish spectroscopy, strong \ emission, and a high [ O iii ] 5007 / [ O ii ] 3727 \ luminosity ratio, O32 8.8 , consistent with a high leakage of Lyman-continuum radiation. Our GMRT \ images show that the \ emission in the field of GP J0213+0056 arises from an extended broken-ring structure around the Green Pea, with the strongest emission coming from a region between GP J0213+0056 and a companion galaxy lying 4.7 kpc away, and little \ emission coming from the Green Pea itself. We find that the merger between GP J0213+0056 and its companion is likely to have triggered the starburst, and led to a disturbed \ spatial and velocity distribution, which in turn allowed \ (and, possibly, Lyman-continuum) emission to escape the Green Pea.

Our GMRT \ mapping of the Green Pea GP J0213+0056 has revealed that the \ emission from an extended region around the galaxy, with a broken-ring structure, and evidence of disturbed kinematics, clear indications of merger activity. Consistent with the merger hypothesis, we have identified a main-sequence galaxy G1, 4.7 kpc away from GP J0213+0056 and at almost exactly the same redshift, z 0.0400 , with a slightly higher stellar mass. GP J0213+0056 and G1 are identified as independent galaxies by the SDSS. However, the separation between the two is very small, 4.7 kpc.

gmw_00010521.name: J0940+2935
gmw_00010521.overview: J0940+2935 is a nearby tadpole-shaped dwarf galaxy [arXiv:1909.10574] that consists of a low-mass star-forming complex with an extended tail [arXiv:1904.01615]. The system harbors extremely metal-poor gas [arXiv:1909.10574] and exhibits weak nebular ultraviolet emission alongside P-Cygni absorption [arXiv:1904.01615].

sources.summary: The paper presents J0940+2935 as a nearby tadpole-shaped dwarf with extremely metal-poor gas, no detected nebular emission, and only an X-ray upper limit.

quotes.quote: J0940+2935 KUG 0937+298 in brorbyXrayBinaryFormation2014 is a tadpole-shaped dwarf galaxy at z=0.0024 (8 Mpc), the most nearby galaxy in our sample senchynaExtremelyMetalpoorGalaxies2019 . This system is two orders of magnitude closer and presents an SFR more than four orders of magnitude lower than SHOC 042 ( 10^ 3.6 M_ /year ), and harbors gas with an oxygen abundance clearly in the XMP regime ( 12+ O/H =7.63 0.14 ). The Keck/ESI spectrum of this source reveals no nebular ( /H <1.2 2 ), and the 5 ks exposure (ObsID: 11301, PI: Prestwich) reveals no significant detections within an arcminute of the galaxy ( 2.3 comoving kpc), in agreement with brorbyXrayBinaryFormation2014 . Our 95 confidence upper limit places this galaxy at an X-ray production efficiency of <1.3 41 erg/s per M_ /year .

sources.summary: The paper describes J0940+2935 as a low-mass star-forming complex with an extended tadpole-like tail, modest H equivalent width, weak nebular UV emission, and prominent P-Cygni absorption.

quotes.quote: J0940+2935: This system is a 10^ 4.9 0.2 M_ star-forming complex with an extended tadpole-like tail. The 26 1 equivalent width of H in this system is also substantially smaller than in HS1442+4250 and closer to that of J0405-3648, indicative of a much less dominant population of massive stars. The UV spectra of this object reveal weak nebular emission, with only ] 1666 detected ( W_0=0.7 ), but fairly prominent P-Cygni absorption ( W_0=-0.9 ).

gmw_00010522.name: J1119+5130
gmw_00010522.overview: J1119+5130 is a compact, extremely metal-poor galaxy located at a distance of 22 Mpc [arXiv:1909.10574]. It exhibits strong nebular emission, active star formation, and a highly efficient associated X-ray source [arXiv:1909.10574].

sources.summary: The paper identifies J1119+5130 as a compact extremely metal-poor galaxy with strong nebular emission and the sample highest X-ray production efficiency, though offset from the aperture.

quotes.quote: J1119+5130 [ RC2 ] A1116+51 in brorbyXrayBinaryFormation2014 is a compact XMP ( 12+ O/H =7.51 0.07 ) at 22 Mpc forming stars at SFR(H ) = 10^ 2.3 M_ /year senchynaExtremelyMetalpoorGalaxies2019 . This galaxy shows strong nebular in a Keck/ESI spectrum at /H =0.026 0.003 senchynaExtremelyMetalpoorGalaxies2019 . We detect an X-ray source in the 11.7 ks exposure covering this galaxy (ObsID: 11287, PI: Prestwich), with a luminosity of =3.9 0.4 39 erg/s in good agreement with that reported in after correcting for the small difference in adopted distances; 22 versus 20.8 Mpc brorbyXrayBinaryFormation2014 .

Thus, while it is unlikely that this X-ray source contributes to the nebular observed in the Keck/ESI spectrum, it does appear to be clearly associated with the galaxy. We consider this galaxy detected with , but note this caveat in Table tab:chandra and in Figure fig:heiixr . This detection yields an X-ray production efficiency of 8.0 1.2 41 erg/s for a star formation rate of 1 M_ /year , the highest of our sample.

gmw_00010523.name: SB191
gmw_00010523.overview: SB191 is a compact star-forming region embedded in the large spiral galaxy NGC 4204 [arXiv:1909.10574]. Also known as UGC 07261, the host galaxy is heavily barred and possesses a stellar disk that appears nearly face-on [arXiv:2206.15443].

sources.summary: The paper describes SB191 as a compact star-forming region in NGC 4204 with moderate metallicity, no narrow nebular detection, and no nearby X-ray target detection.

quotes.quote: SB191 senchynaUltravioletSpectraExtreme2017 is a compact star-forming region embedded in the large spiral galaxy NGC 4204 at 10 Mpc distance, and hosts gas at a moderate oxygen abundance of 12+ O/H =8.30 0.07 with star formation rate 10^ 2.2 M_ /year . This system is undetected in nebular , with a purely broad stellar component identified with Keck/ESI. The host galaxy NGC 4204 was observed with /ACIS-S in a single 2 ks pointing (ObsID: 7092, PI: Swartz) targeting a ULX candidate in another part of the galaxy swartzCompleteSampleUltraluminous2011 .

The star-forming region we have targeted is more than an arcminute away from the ULX candidate studied in this previous analysis, but still near enough to the center of the S3 chip for confident source detection. This short exposure reveals no X-ray target detections within 45'' ( >2 comoving kpc) of SB191, placing a 95 upper limit on the X-ray luminosity of <1.3 38 erg/s (corresponding to <2.1 40 erg/s per M_ /year ).

sources.summary: The paper identifies UGC 07261 as an inclination outlier: optical photometry overpredicts its kinematic inclination because a strong bar lowers b/a even though the stellar disk appears nearly face-on.

quotes.quote: The optical inclination of UGC 07261 severely overpredicts the kinematic inclination. On inspection, its SDSS image shows it to be heavily barred, causing it to have a far lower b/a that does not reflect the stellar disc itself, which appears to be nearly face on. This could be mitigated by calculating b/a using outer isophotes only.

gmw_00010524.name: SB111
gmw_00010524.overview: SB111 is a blue compact dwarf galaxy located at a distance of 16 Mpc [arXiv:1909.10574]. It features low-metallicity gas, a star formation rate of 10^-2.4 solar masses per year, and strong nebular emission lines [arXiv:1909.10574].

sources.summary: The paper describes SB111 as a blue compact dwarf with strong nebular emission but no relevant X-ray detection at the optical galaxy position.

quotes.quote: SB111 ( [ RC2 ] A1228+12 in brorbyXrayBinaryFormation2014 ) is a blue compact dwarf at 16 Mpc, with low-metallicity gas at 12+ O/H =7.81 0.08 just above the XMP cutoff and a star formation rate from the Balmer lines of 10^ 2.4 M_ /year senchynaUltravioletSpectraExtreme2017 . This system has one of the strongest nebular lines detected among local star-forming galaxies, with /H =0.039 0.001 measured with Keck/ESI. In addition, a deep MMT/BC spectrum was obtained for this target by izotovDetectionNeEmission2012 . This spectrum revealed both (with /H =0.038 0.002 in very good agreement with our later Keck/ESI measurement) and a 2 detection of 3426 , with /H =0.79 0.36 (Table tab:optspec ).

However, as noted in senchynaUltravioletSpectraExtreme2017 and confirmed by the reanalysis presented in this work, this X-ray source is offset from the optical galaxy by 11.1'' (or 0.9 kpc comoving kpc; Figure fig:sdsschandra ). While it is possible this is an ejected HMXB associated with star formation in this galaxy, this X-ray source cannot in any case contribute to excitation of the observed nebular . For the purposes of this work, then, SB111 is undetected in X-rays, with <0.6 38 erg/s ( <1.4 40 erg/s per M_ /year ).

gmw_00010525.name: HS1442+4250
gmw_00010525.overview: HS1442+4250 is an extended, extremely metal-poor galaxy [arXiv:1909.10574] that harbors a very young stellar population and highly ionized gas [arXiv:1904.01615]. It contains a compact star-forming region [arXiv:1909.10574] and exhibits strong ultraviolet nebular emission [arXiv:1904.01615].

sources.summary: The paper reports HS1442+4250 as an extended extremely metal-poor galaxy with very strong nebular emission and an offset X-ray source identified as a background quasar.

quotes.quote: HS1442+4250 is a somewhat more extended XMP ( 12+ O/H =7.65 0.04 ) which hosts a compact star-forming region with SFR(H ) =10^ 3.0 M_ /year that we observed with MMT and Keck spectroscopy in senchynaExtremelyMetalpoorGalaxies2019 . The Keck spectroscopy reveals at a similar intensity to that found in SB111, with /H =0.0358 0.0006 , among the highest observed.

Our reanalysis revealed that as in the case of SB111, this X-ray source was significantly offset from the optical galaxy, at a distance of 8.3'' (corresponding to 0.4 comoving kpc) where contribution to the optical nebular line flux is impossible. In this case, we noted that the observed X-ray source was coincident with a faint point source in the SDSS image (at 14h44m12.01s, +42d37m30.05s; g=25.1 , i=21.03 ). On the night of April 15 2018, we observed this target with MMT/BC for 10 minutes.

The extracted spectrum revealed strong broad asymmetric emission at 4130 and 5270 , which we interpret as Ly and 1548,1550 at redshift z=2.4 . At this redshift, the observed flux corresponds to a rest-frame 2 - 27 keV band luminosity of 1.3 45 erg/s, just above the knee of the observed AGN luminosity function at z 2 airdEvolutionXrayLuminosity2015 . Thus in this case, the X-ray emission previously identified as associated with this galaxy appears to originate entirely from a distant quasar in chance projection rather than an ejected HMXB.

sources.summary: The paper presents HS1442+4250 as the most optically intense object in its XMP sample, with young stellar populations, highly ionized gas, strong UV nebular emission, and detectable P-Cygni absorption.

quotes.quote: HS1442+4250: This system is the optically-dominant component of a `cigar-like' extended light distribution (Figure fig:cutouts ), and presents the most intense optical emission line properties in our sample. The intense H emission (equivalent width 113 5 ) and high ratio ( 10.3 0.6 ) indicate this system harbors a very young stellar population and highly-ionized gas. The UV spectra of this object are also extreme: we detect strong nebular emission in all targeted lines, with prominent emission in both ] at W_0=11.6 0.6 and at W_0=4.38 0.07 equivalent width. Narrow at nearly the flux of ] 1666 is also present.
The highest signal-to-noise detection of the P-Cygni feature is achieved in HS1442+4250, also the strongest nebular emitter in our new sample of targeted XMPs.

gmw_00010526.name: J2251+1327
gmw_00010526.overview: J2251+1327 is a distant Lyman Break Analogue galaxy located approximately 279 megaparsecs away [arXiv:1909.10574]. It has a star formation rate of 1.7 solar masses per year, detected nebular emission, and a coincident X-ray source [arXiv:1909.10574].

sources.summary: The paper describes J2251+1327 as a distant Lyman Break Analogue with moderate metallicity, detected nebular emission, and a coincident Chandra X-ray source.

quotes.quote: J2251+1327 is a relatively distant (279 Mpc) LBA described in brorbyEnhancedXrayEmission2016 with direct- T_e gas-phase metallicity 12+ O/H =8.29 0.06 and SFR(H ) =1.7 M_ /year . Our MMT/BC spectrum yields a detection of nebular , with modestly large /H =0.0070 0.0034 ; but no ( /H <0.62 ). We find a clear X-ray detection in the ACIS-S image of this galaxy (ObsID: 13013, PI: Basu-Zych) separated by 0.4 '' from the center of the optical target (0.5 comoving kpc, but within our spatial offset tolerance of 1.4 ), with luminosity =1.77 0.30 41 erg/s consistent with the measurement of brorbyEnhancedXrayEmission2016 . This results in an estimate of the X-ray production efficiency of 1.1 0.2 41 erg/s per M_ /year .

gmw_00010527.name: SHOC 595
gmw_00010527.overview: SHOC 595 is a distant Lyman Break Analogue galaxy that hosts high-metallicity gas [arXiv:1909.10574]. The galaxy contains a luminous X-ray source that exhibits possible variability [arXiv:1909.10574].

sources.summary: The paper describes SHOC 595 as a distant Lyman Break Analogue with high metallicity, no clear narrow nebular contribution, and a variable luminous X-ray source.

quotes.quote: SHOC 595 is another LBA at similarly large distance (589 Mpc) hosting the highest-metallicity gas in our sample along with SB191 ( 12+ O/H =8.30 0.14 ). The profile measured with MMT/BC for this galaxy is broader than the other nebular lines (Section sec:optspec ), and we fail to detect a clear narrow contribution at 2 ( /H <0.68 ) or ( /H <0.62 ). We detect a luminous X-ray source in the 13.6 ks observation (ObsID: 17418, PI: Kaaret), with luminosity 3.2 1.1 41 erg/s, 1 lower than the measurement presented by brorbyEnhancedXrayEmission2016 .

This galaxy was observed in 2 separate instances, but the system was not detected in the other 9.1 ks integration (ObsID: 16020), with a 90 confidence limiting flux just below that measured in 17418. We adopt the former flux measurement, yielding an X-ray production efficiency of 1.1 0.4 41 erg/s per M_ /year , and discuss the impact of this possible variability in Section sec:datajointres .

gmw_00010528.name: Source J125149.16-122217.1
gmw_00010528.overview: Source J125149.16-122217.1 is identified as a likely gravitational lens system [arXiv:2210.01937]. Composite Pan-STARRS images reveal an extended structure with a complex color distribution, while Gaia resolves two sources separated by 0.9 arcseconds [arXiv:2210.01937].

sources.summary: The paper identifies Source J125149.16-122217.1 as a likely new gravitational lens with extended multi-image Pan-STARRS structure and two Gaia sources.

quotes.quote: Source J125149.16-122217.1 at position (192 954872187, -12 37147381) is likely to be a previously undetected gravitational lens. Composite Pan-STARRS images reveal an extended image probably composed of at least three images with a complex color distribution. Gaia resolves only two sources separated by 0 9 , however, and the northern companion has a large measured proper motion of 8.4 . The ML-predicted redshift is 1.11.

gmw_00010529.name: Source J205144.29+252736.9
gmw_00010529.overview: Source J205144.29+252736.9 is an optically variable quasar with a predicted redshift of 2.58 [arXiv:2210.01937]. It is a candidate strong lens with three images resolved in Gaia, though color differences suggest one component may be an interloping star [arXiv:2210.01937].

sources.summary: The paper discusses Source J205144.29+252736.9 as a candidate strong lens, noting Gaia-resolved images, color evidence for possible contamination, and variability follow-up.

quotes.quote: Source J205144.29+252736.9 at position (312 93464628, 25 46031899) , with three images resolved within 5 in Gaia, has been selected as a candidate strong lens by 2019A&A...622A.165D . Pan-STARRS images show, however, that the two closely separated ( 0 703 ) components have much different colors, so that the blue one is the quasar and the red one at position angle 204 may be an interloping star. The astrometric measurements for the red companion are quite uncertain. The quasar with z_ pre =2.58 is known to be optically variable 2018JAD....24....3U , so follow up observations on the light curves of the components can confirm or rule out this suggested lens.

gmw_00010530.name: Source J054535.89+582805.8
gmw_00010530.overview: Source J054535.89+582805.8 is a likely new gravitational lens system [arXiv:2210.01937]. While Gaia resolves only two close components separated by 0.817 arcseconds, additional components appear to be present in available Pan-STARRS images [arXiv:2210.01937].

sources.summary: The paper presents Source J054535.89+582805.8 as a likely new gravitational lens whose Pan-STARRS images suggest more components than Gaia resolves.

quotes.quote: Source J054535.89+582805.8 at position (86 39963598, 58 468300681) is likely a new gravitational lens. It has not been selected by previous Gaia-based search engines because only two close images have been resolved at separation 0 817 --however, more seems to be present in the available images. The measured proper motion of the northern component is a high 9.06 \ but Gaia proper motions are known to be strongly perturbed beyond the error budget by close unresolved multiplicity 2017ApJ...840L...1M .

gmw_00010531.name: SDSS J2321 097
gmw_00010531.overview: SDSS J2321 097 is an early-type lens galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.0819 [arXiv:0711.0888]. It is a very slow rotator with mild stellar anisotropy and a dark-matter contribution of 30 percent inside its effective radius [arXiv:0711.0888].

sources.summary: The paper models SDSS J2321 097 as an early-type lens galaxy, deriving its density slope, slow rotation, mild anisotropy, and inner dark-matter fraction from lensing and kinematics.

quotes.quote: We present the first results of a combined VLT VIMOS integral-field unit and Hubble Space Telescope ( HST )/ACS study of the early-type lens galaxy SDSS J2321 097 at z=0.0819 , extending kinematic studies to a look-back time of 1 Gyr. This system, discovered in the Sloan Lens ACS Survey, has been observed as part of a VLT Large Programme with the goal of obtaining two-dimensional stellar kinematics of 17 early-type galaxies to z 0.35 and Keck spectroscopy of an additional dozen lens systems. Bayesian modelling of both the surface brightness distribution of the lensed source and the two-dimensional measurements of velocity and velocity dispersion has allowed us, under the only assumptions of axisymmetry and a two-integral stellar distribution function (DF) for the lens galaxy, to dissect this galaxy in three dimensions and break the classical mass--anisotropy, mass-sheet and inclination--oblateness degeneracies. Our main results are that the galaxy (i) has a total density profile well described by a single power-law ^ ' with ' = 2.06^ +0.03 _ 0.06 ; (ii) is a very slow rotator (specific stellar angular momentum parameter _ R = 0.075 ); (iii) shows only mild anisotropy ( 0.15 ); and (iv) has a dark-matter contribution of 30 per cent inside the effective radius.

gmw_00010532.name: UGC 6594
gmw_00010532.overview: UGC 6594 is a nearby group galaxy with a systemic velocity of 1037 km/s [arXiv:2102.00598]. It forms an interacting pair with the dwarf galaxy UDG1137+16, which exhibits tidal distortions and isophotal twisting in the direction of UGC 6594 [arXiv:2102.00598].

sources.summary: The paper uses UGC 6594 as the nearby group galaxy paired with UDG1137+16, citing similar velocities, projected separation, and tidal distortions toward UGC 6594.

quotes.quote: Based on the centroids of these Gaussian fits we measure a recession velocity of 1237 3 km \ s^ 1 and thus confirm the association of this object with the low density UGC 6594 group (UGC 6594, V_ sys = 1037 2 km \ s^ 1 ; vanDriel2016 ).

Visual inspection of the image reveals a clear distortion of the stellar envelope in the direction of UGC 6594 (labelled with a red arrow), indicative of a tidal interaction between the galaxies. Moreover, the extraction of our 1D surface brightness profile reveals a clear twisting of the elliptical isophotes to align with the direction of UGC 6594, a known effect of tidal interactions Carleton2018 . We argue the close projected distance between the galaxies ( 20 kpc between galaxy centres at the adopted distance of 21.1 Mpc) and their extremely similar recession velocities provides further evidence that they are likely an interacting pair.

gmw_00010533.name: HIZOA J0630+08
gmw_00010533.overview: HIZOA J0630+08 is a newly detected, likely nearby dwarf galaxy with a low velocity of 367 km/s [arXiv:astro-ph/0409570]. It experiences high foreground Galactic extinction and is a probable member of the Orion Group [arXiv:astro-ph/0409570].

sources.summary: The survey identifies HIZOA J0630+08 as a newly detected, likely nearby dwarf galaxy with low velocity, high foreground extinction, probable Orion Group membership, and an estimated HI mass at 6 Mpc.

quotes.quote: The newly detected galaxy HIZOA J0630+08 ( l,b = 203.1 ,--0.9 ) has a velocity of only 367 1 km s ^ 1 (see Table 1) and could be a high velocity cloud, though its isolation and relative compactness suggest it is more likely a nearby dwarf galaxy. The Galactic extinction towards this galaxy is A_ B 3 mag (Schlegel et al. 1998). HIZOA J0630+08 may be part of the Orion Group (Giovanelli Haynes 1981) whose known members are the Orion Dwarf ( l,b = 200.6 , --12.3 ), the irregular galaxy 0554+07 ( l,b = 200.0 , --8.4 ), the dwarf irregular galaxy CGCG 422--003 ( l,b = 198.8 , --12.2 ) and UGC 03303 ( l,b = 198.6, --16.9 ).
At a distance of 6 Mpc the HI mass of HIZOA J0630+08 would be 4 10^ 7 . Further investigations of the Orion Group and surroundings are under way.

gmw_00010534.name: HIZOA J1914+10
gmw_00010534.overview: HIZOA J1914+10 is a nearby, isolated neutral hydrogen galaxy with a systemic velocity of 654 km/s [arXiv:astro-ph/0409570]. Due to extremely high Galactic extinction in its direction, the galaxy has no detected optical or infrared counterparts [arXiv:astro-ph/0409570].

sources.summary: The paper reports HIZOA J1914+10 as a nearby, isolated HI galaxy with systemic velocity 654 km/s, high extinction, no optical or infrared counterpart, and prior Dwingeloo survey detection.

quotes.quote: In addition to HIZOA J0630+08, we detect two other nearby galaxies, HIZOA J1940+11 and HIZOA J1914+10, with systemic velocities of 580 km s ^ 1 and 654 km s ^ 1 , respectively. For HIZOA J1914+10 we derive an HI mass of M_ HI = 6.5 10^8 M _ . This galaxy was previously detected in the Dwingeloo Obscured Galaxies Survey and is known as Dw044.8--0.5 (Henning et al. 1998, Rivers 2000). The Galactic extinction in this direction is extremely high, preventing detection of optical or infrared counterparts.

gmw_00010535.name: HIZOA J1940+11
gmw_00010535.overview: HIZOA J1940+11 is a newly cataloged, isolated nearby galaxy with a systemic velocity of 580 km/s [arXiv:astro-ph/0409570]. It has a low neutral hydrogen mass of 4.1 million solar masses and lies in the outskirts of the Local Void [arXiv:astro-ph/0409570].

sources.summary: The survey describes HIZOA J1940+11 as a newly cataloged, isolated nearby HI galaxy with velocity 580 km/s, low inferred HI mass, foreground extinction, and a weak edge-field detection.

quotes.quote: In addition to HIZOA J0630+08, we detect two other nearby galaxies, HIZOA J1940+11 and HIZOA J1914+10, with systemic velocities of 580 km s ^ 1 and 654 km s ^ 1 , respectively.

For HIZOA J1940+11, a newly cataloged galaxy, we derive an HI mass of only M_ HI = 4.1 10^7 M _ .
