sources.summary: The paper identifies WISEA J034638.04+431348.3 as the faint, low-mass host of SN 2020wnt, deriving redshift, distance, negligible host reddening, low star formation, and low metallicity.

quotes.quote: The host galaxy of \ is WISEA J034638.04+431348.3, a faint galaxy with no published redshift or distance information. The redshift adopted in our analysis is derived from the narrow emission lines (H , [ O iii ] 5007 ) visible in the SN spectrum. These lines give us a mean redshift of 0.032. Given the lack of independent measurements of distance to this galaxy, we estimate the uncertainty in our measurements assuming a peculiar velocity of 200 km s ^ 1 Tully13 . With these values, we compute a distance of d=140.4 3.0 Mpc, which corresponds to a distance modulus of =35.74 0.05 mag.

To characterise the global properties of the \ host galaxy, we got griz photometry from the Pan-STARRS1 (PS1) public science archive https://catalogs.mast.stsci.edu/panstarrs/ . To obtain estimates of the stellar mass ( M_* ) and star formation rate (SFR), we use a custom galaxy spectral energy distribution (SED) fitting code, following the procedure detailed in Sullivan10 . The best-fitting templates correspond to M_* = M/M_ = 8.22_ 0.12 ^ +0.20 and SFR = -4.89_ 5.11 ^ +3.09 yr ^ 1 . Such low stellar mass and star formation obtained for the host galaxy of \ are consistent with the expected range measured for other SLSN hosts Neill11,Chen13,Lunnan14,Leloudas15,Perley16,Schulze18 .

gmw_00010562.name: Tol 0957-278
gmw_00010562.overview: Tol 0957-278 is an isolated, low-metallicity blue compact dwarf galaxy containing Wolf-Rayet stars [arXiv:1707.07025, arXiv:2405.12361]. It features a 1.5 Gyr old stellar disk, twelve H II regions, and massive young stellar cluster clumps that undergo stochastic star formation [arXiv:1707.07025].

sources.summary: The paper characterizes Tol 02 as an isolated low-metallicity H II/BCD Wolf-Rayet galaxy with a 1.5 Gyr disk, massive young cluster clumps, 12 H II regions, and stochastic recent star formation.

quotes.quote: We have obtained broad U, B, R, I, J, H, K, intermediate Str o mgren y and narrow H and OIII deep images of the Wolf-Rayet, Blue Compact Dwarf, H II galaxy Tol 02. We have analysed the low surface brightness component, the stellar cluster complexes and the H II regions. The stellar populations in the galaxy have been characterised by comparing the observed broad band colours with those of single stellar population models. The main results are consistent with Tol 02 being formed by a 1.5 Gyr old disk component at the centre of which a group of 8 massive ( > 10^4 \ M_ ) stellar cluster clumps is located.

Six of these clumps are 10 Myr old and their near infrared colours suggest that their light is dominated by Red Supergiant stars, the other two are young Wolf-Rayet cluster candidates of ages 3 and 5 Myr respectively. 12 H II regions in the star-forming region of the galaxy are also identified. These are immersed in a diffuse H and OIII emission that spreads towards the North and South covering the old stellar disk. Our spatial-temporal analysis shows that star formation is more likely stochastic and simultaneous within short time scales.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes Tol 0957-278 as a blue compact dwarf with Wolf-Rayet stars, low metallicity, clumpy H emission, no coherent velocity gradient, and localized high dispersion.

quotes.quote: Tol 0957-278 The galaxy Tol 0957-278, also known as Tol 02, ESO 435-IG 020, AM 0957-275, PGC 028863, was initially observed by Smith1976 and classified as a medium-strong emission line emitter. Kunth1985, Schaerer1999, Mendez2000 also report the presence of Wolf-Rayet (WR) stars, similar to Tol 1004-296. Broadband CCD imaging from Kunth1988 and Doublier1999 confirms the clumpy shape of the object. Campbell1988 's modeling confirms a low metallicity of 12 + log(O/H) = 8.1, consistent with what has been reported in more recent studies Vacca1992, Masegosa1994, Buckalew2005, Engelbracht2008 . Terlevich1991 and Masegosa1994 published emission line fluxes and ion abundances for different samples of \ galaxies, including both objects in our study.

Kinematics of Tol 0957-278 Figure fig:VrDispTol0957-278 shows the radial velocity and velocity dispersion maps of Tol 0957-278. Radial velocities range between 1016 4 km s ^ 1 and 1041 5 km s ^ 1 , between the different regions. Unlike Tol 1004-296, the velocity field doesn't exhibit a consistent velocity gradient. However, it's notable that Knot 2 has a lower radial velocity (approximately 1016 km s ^ 1 5 km s ^ 1 ) compared to Knot 1, which has a much higher velocity (approximately 1041 5 km s ^ 1 ). Additionally, a small area (located at -2 , +2.5 in Figure fig:VrDispTol0957-278 (Right)), not associated with an emission knot, shows the highest radial velocity (approximately 1050 5 km s ^ 1 ).

gmw_00010563.name: NGC 5054
gmw_00010563.overview: NGC 5054 is a three-armed spiral galaxy [arXiv:0812.2959] with a radial velocity of 1704 km/s corrected for local group infall [arXiv:1711.00628]. Its spiral pattern is suggested to extend to the outer Lindblad resonance [arXiv:0812.2959], and its interstellar medium causes substantial reddening near its nucleus [arXiv:1711.00628].

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 5054 as SN 2004ab's host, linking its nuclear sightline to substantial host reddening, redshift-based distance, and non-standard low-R_V extinction.

quotes.quote: SN 2004ab occurred very close to the nucleus of the host galaxy NGC 5054. Hence, a substantial amount of reddening due to ISM within the host galaxy is expected. The spectra of SN 2004ab show strong narrow Na i D absorption lines (refer Section sec04ab_spec_results ) at the rest frame of host galaxy with an average equivalent width (EW) of 3.3 .

NGC 5054, the host galaxy of SN 2004ab, has a radial velocity corrected for local group infall onto Virgo cluster of 1704 km s ^ 1 (LEDA database). Assuming H_ 0 = 72 km s ^ 1 Mpc ^ 1 free01 , we derived distance modulus of = 31.87 0.15 mag for NGC 5054. This leads to V band peak absolute magnitude of SN 2004ab, M_V^ max = 22.03 0.15 mag, making it brighter by more than 2 magnitudes than the normal SNe Ia. This indicates that R_V = 3.1 is not applicable to the host galaxy component of extinction for SN 2004ab.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 5054 as a three-armed spiral whose single major phase-shift crossing implies the pattern probably extends to the outer Lindblad resonance.

quotes.quote: Figure granddesign shows the phase-shift distribution of the three-armed spiral NGC 5054. Only a single major P/N crossing is found, and it corresponds to the circle shown on the image. This circle passes through the middle of the spiral, and suggests that the pattern most likely extends to the OLR, not the IUHR.

gmw_00010564.name: GN-z10-2
gmw_00010564.overview: GN-z10-2 is a candidate high-redshift galaxy at z ~ 9.9 [arXiv:1309.2280]. Its stellar mass estimate is highly uncertain due to a lack of clean infrared photometry, which is affected by residuals from a bright foreground neighbor [arXiv:1309.2280].

sources.summary: The paper treats GN-z10-2 as a z about 9.9 candidate but emphasizes contamination and uncertain IRAC photometry, making its stellar mass estimate highly uncertain.

quotes.quote: For source GN-z10-2, the residuals of the bright foreground neighbor are still visible, and its IRAC flux measurements are therefore highly uncertain.
For source GN-z10-2, the spectrum is partially contaminated by the trace of a nearby bright source.
The stellar mass estimate of source GN-z10-2 is highly uncertain, because of the lack of clean IRAC photometry.

gmw_00010565.name: J0030 1525
gmw_00010565.overview: J0030 1525 is a quad-like strong gravitational lensing system [arXiv:1803.07601]. High-resolution imaging resolves four objects in the system, including a faint blue point source next to an extended red object [arXiv:1803.07601].

sources.summary: The paper describes J0030 1525 as a quad-like system where VST imaging resolves four objects, including a faint blue PSF beside an extended red object.

quotes.quote: J0030 1525 The Pan-STARRS gri image for this object shows two bright PSFs and a galaxy significantly offset from where it should lie to create a double-image system. However a better seeing VST -band image, Figure fig:J0030_VST , resolves four objects including a faint blue PSF next to an extended red object. This is consistent with a flux-ratio anomaly fold-configuration quad with the faint PSF being the counterimage. We model the Pan-STARRS data for photometry of A+B, C and D+G, given in Table tab:astrophotometry .

gmw_00010566.name: J0140-1152
gmw_00010566.overview: J0140-1152 is a highly magnified double gravitational lens system [arXiv:1803.07601]. The lensing galaxy, which is located at a redshift of 0.277, has a relatively circular light profile but exhibits an elliptical mass model that suggests a strong external shear [arXiv:1803.07601].

sources.summary: The paper reports J0140-1152 as an independently identified, highly magnified double lens whose mass model implies strong external shear.

quotes.quote: J0140-1152 This lens has been independently identified in the VST-ATLAS survey by agnellovst2017 , who report a lens redshift of 0.277. It is a highly magnified ( 10 times) double. The elliptical mass model suggests a strong external shear given the relatively circular light profile of the galaxy.

gmw_00010567.name: J0235-2433
gmw_00010567.overview: J0235-2433 is a gravitationally lensed quasar system at a redshift of 1.44 [arXiv:1803.07601]. The system has exhibited a brightness change of approximately one magnitude, which is likely caused by a microlensing event [arXiv:1803.07601].

sources.summary: The paper confirms J0235-2433 as a z=1.44 quasar lens candidate and interprets changing image brightness as likely microlensing.

quotes.quote: J0235-2433 agnello2017des have independently selected this object as a candidate gravitational lens from the Dark Energy Survey (DES) footprint despaper . We confirm the PSFs to be quasars at a redshift of 1.44. The DES data displayed in agnello2017des show that the image closest to the lens galaxy is brighter than the other, while the opposite is true in the Pan-STARRS data.

This change by approximately one magnitude within less than a few years (mean epoch for the Pan-STARRS detection is 56475, and 57350 for DES desdatarelease ) is likely attributed to a microlensing event rather than quasar variability. Indeed in the data, the closest and furthest images have magnitudes of G=18.12 and G=18.85 respectively. Since both images are detected by Gaia , they will have well-sampled lightcurves over 5 years.

gmw_00010568.name: J0417+3325
gmw_00010568.overview: J0417+3325 is a double-image lensed quasar system featuring an elliptical lens galaxy [arXiv:1803.07601]. The system is detected at radio wavelengths, showing two point sources that correspond to the optical quasar images but exhibit discrepant optical and radio flux ratios [arXiv:1803.07601].

sources.summary: The paper describes J0417+3325 as a double with an elliptical lens, radio detections, discrepant optical and radio flux ratios, and useful radio structure.

quotes.quote: J0417+3325 This double has an elliptical lens galaxy ( q=0.48 ) and the SIE lens model is aligned along the same position angle but less elliptical ( q=0.65 ). It was originally detected by colla1970 at 408MHz with a flux of 290 mJy, and subsequently at 5GHz by davis1971 and altschuler1986 with detections of 60 mJy and 39 mJy respectively. It is also detected in NVSS condon1998 at 1.4GHz with a flux density of 109mJy.

Archival 8GHz VLA data show two point sources in the same positions as the optical quasar point sources with a hint of a third source south of image A. There is an extended source 3 \ west of the system, lacking any optical detection in Pan-STARRS. The A to B flux ratio at 8GHz is 1.2, while the g band ratio---the magnitudes least affected by the presence of the lens galaxy---is 2.0, a discrepancy that could be explained by a number of effects including microlensing or extinction. The presence of a radio source is promising for using resolved source structure and radio flux ratios to precisely constrain lens models.

gmw_00010569.name: J0949+4208
gmw_00010569.overview: J0949+4208 is a radio source detected in both NVSS and FIRST surveys [arXiv:1803.07601]. Its spectrum reveals a background quasar at a redshift of 1.27 and a foreground lens galaxy at a redshift of 0.507, which is likely a radio galaxy [arXiv:1803.07601].

sources.summary: The paper describes J0949+4208 as a radio source where FIRST associates emission with the likely radio lens galaxy and spectra show quasar and galaxy redshifts.

quotes.quote: J0949+4208 This is a radio source in NVSS and FIRST, however FIRST clearly associates it with the lensing galaxy. The lensing galaxy is likely a radio galaxy, however we cannot rule out the fainter optical image being much brighter in the radio. It was targetted for a BOSS spectrum which shows quasar emission lines at z=1.27 and a galaxy at z=0.507 , in agreement with the absorption lines seen in our WHT spectrum.

gmw_00010570.name: J1640+1045
gmw_00010570.overview: J1640+1045 is a double-image lensed quasar system that exhibits an Einstein ring of the quasar host galaxy [arXiv:1803.07601]. The lensing galaxy is embedded within a galaxy group, and its mass modeling favors strong external shear [arXiv:1803.07601].

sources.summary: The paper describes J1640+1045 as a flux-ratio anomaly double whose mass modeling favors strong external shear aligned with a nearby galaxy.

quotes.quote: J1640+1045 were concurrently discovered by williams2017 . Deep CFHT data reveals an Einstein ring of the quasar host galaxy in the u , g and bands, as shown in Figure fig:J0941_cfht . We calculate the velocity dispersion of our SIE model to be 365 km s ^ 1 and compare this to the measured velocity dispersion of the galaxy from an SDSS spectrum of 313 18 km s ^ 1 . This discrepancy can be accounted for by a shallower than isothermal density profile of 1.8 see Figure 4 of auger2010 .

However this lens is embedded within a galaxy group and so close companions would suggest a steeper profile dobke2007, auger2008 . Since a quasar emission line from the closer, fainter quasar image is present in the galaxy spectrum, the SDSS velocity dispersion might not be trustworthy. A deeper spectrum and deeper optical imaging of the Einstein ring will help constrain the mass model. We calculate the time delay for this system as B lagging A by 270 days.

gmw_00010571.name: J1721+8842
gmw_00010571.overview: J1721+8842 is a strongly lensed system containing a lensing galaxy [arXiv:1803.07601]. The system features a lensed quasar that is split into multiple images at the same redshift [arXiv:1803.07601].

sources.summary: The paper describes J1721+8842 as a polar quad with absorption in all images, possible second lens or host arc residuals, and favorable time-delay monitoring geometry.

quotes.quote: J1721+8842. After masking cosmic rays and subtracting the sky background, the spectra were visually inspected for broad emission lines in the separated peaks and 1-D spectra were extracted using Gaussian apertures with 0.5 \ width. Results Table tab:observations shows a summary of the observations with sky positions, candidate selection method, outcome of the observation and WISE and magnitudes. We have classified 24 objects as lensed quasars since the spectra reveal the presence of (at least) two quasars at the same redshift and the pixel modelling of the Pan-STARRS images reveals a lensing galaxy.

Figure fig:overlaid_gri shows Pan-STARRS gri cutouts of the confirmed lens systems with detections overlaid. Figure fig:spectra shows the component spectra for each lens. We are able to establish the lens galaxy redshift for 4 lenses. We further discover 10 systems consisting of pairs of quasars at the same redshift, shown in Figure fig:overlaid_gri_binary .

gmw_00010572.name: Arp 181
gmw_00010572.overview: Arp 181 is an interacting galaxy pair at z = 0.032 consisting of NGC 3212 and NGC 3215 [arXiv:1211.6923]. The interaction has stripped approximately 90 percent of the system's neutral hydrogen into western tidal debris, which contains a forming tidal dwarf galaxy and a small star-forming galaxy [arXiv:1211.6923].

sources.summary: The paper presents Arp 181 as an interacting spiral pair whose encounter displaced most H i into western tidal debris, with a bridge, tidal-dwarf formation, and a small star-forming galaxy.

quotes.quote: Received ; accepted firstpage - lastpage We present results from GMRT H i 21 cm line observations of the interacting galaxy pair Arp 181 (NGC 3212 and NGC 3215) at z =0.032. We find almost all of the detected H i (90 ) displaced well beyond the optical disks of the pair with the highest density H i located 70 kpc west of the pair. An H i bridge black extending between the optical pair and the bulk of the H i black together with their H i deficiencies provide strong evidence that black the interaction between the pair has removed most of their H i to THE current projected position. H i to the west of the pair has two approximately black equal intensity peaks.

So molecular gas observations are required for Arp 181 to get a complete picture of the gas distribution of this interesting system. black In summary, Arp 181 appears to be at a particularly interesting stage of its evolution following the interaction between at least two, possibly three, of its members during which most of the H i in the system has been stripped from the parent galaxies and 9.5 10 ^ 9 M _ of \ tidal debris is now observed 70 kpc west of the principal galaxy pair. We find this \ debris to be in in a state of rapid evolution including hosting at least one tidal dwarf in formation. We confirm the radial velocity of this \ in the region of this TDG is 9659 km s ^ 1 . \ debris also contains another small star forming galaxy, its optical and IR properties suggest it to be a pre-existing member of Arp 181.

gmw_00010573.name: NGC 3212
gmw_00010573.overview: NGC 3212 is a spiral galaxy that forms the interacting pair Arp 181 alongside NGC 3215 [arXiv:1211.6923]. This tidal interaction has left NGC 3212 highly deficient in neutral hydrogen, with most of the system's gas displaced into an external tidal mass and a low-density bridge [arXiv:1211.6923].

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 3212 as one Arp 181 spiral, links it to an H i bridge and tidal tail, and finds the pair H i deficient after the interaction.

quotes.quote: In this Letter we present one of the most spectacular cases of the impact on H i of a tidal interaction between a pair of spirals, NGC 3212 and NGC 3215, which make up the Arp 181 system and possibly a third small galaxy. NGC 3212 has been classified as an `S?' galaxy at a redshift of 0.0324, while NGC 3215 has been classified as an `SB?' galaxy at a redshift of 0.0316 rc3 .

Figure fig1 (upper panel), shows that almost the entire H i mass of the system is detected well beyond the optical disks of NGC 3212 and NGC 3215 and is connected to NGC 3212 by a low density H i bridge. The two galaxies, NGC 3212 and NGC 3215 contain black \ of 2.7 10 ^ 9 M _ and 7.9 10 ^ 8 M _ respectively black while the western H i mass along with the tidal bridge black is 9.5 10 ^ 9 M _ The uncertainty on black these estimates are 10 .

Tidal interaction between NGC 3212, NGC 3215 and SDSS J102726.32+794911.9 seems to be a likely black source of the western \ mass as there is no nearby neighbour of similar size in similar redshift range within 3Mpc radius. Since morphological sub-classifications are not available for NGC 3212 and NGC 3215, we compared these galaxies against Sb type spirals. The H i surface density values for NGC 3212 and NGC 3215 are black 5.90 and 5.66, respectively, indicating they are H i deficient by a factor of 10. black Because of the morphological type, diameter and H i mass uncertainties, the values for the H i deficiencies are themselves highly uncertain.

gmw_00010574.name: NGC 3215
gmw_00010574.overview: NGC 3215 is a barred spiral galaxy that forms the Arp 181 system alongside NGC 3212 [arXiv:1211.6923]. Due to a tidal interaction with its companion galaxies, NGC 3215 is highly deficient in neutral hydrogen [arXiv:1211.6923].

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 3215 as the barred spiral component of Arp 181 and finds, with NGC 3212, that it is H i deficient after tidal interaction.

quotes.quote: In this Letter we present one of the most spectacular cases of the impact on H i of a tidal interaction between a pair of spirals, NGC 3212 and NGC 3215, which make up the Arp 181 system and possibly a third small galaxy. NGC 3212 has been classified as an `S?' galaxy at a redshift of 0.0324, while NGC 3215 has been classified as an `SB?' galaxy at a redshift of 0.0316 rc3 .

The two galaxies, NGC 3212 and NGC 3215 contain black \ of 2.7 10 ^ 9 M _ and 7.9 10 ^ 8 M _ respectively black while the western H i mass along with the tidal bridge black is 9.5 10 ^ 9 M _ The uncertainty on black these estimates are 10 .

Tidal interaction between NGC 3212, NGC 3215 and SDSS J102726.32+794911.9 seems to be a likely black source of the western \ mass as there is no nearby neighbour of similar size in similar redshift range within 3Mpc radius. Since morphological sub-classifications are not available for NGC 3212 and NGC 3215, we compared these galaxies against Sb type spirals. The H i surface density values for NGC 3212 and NGC 3215 are black 5.90 and 5.66, respectively, indicating they are H i deficient by a factor of 10. black Because of the morphological type, diameter and H i mass uncertainties, the values for the H i deficiencies are themselves highly uncertain.

gmw_00010575.name: SDSS J102726.32+794911.9
gmw_00010575.overview: SDSS J102726.32+794911.9 is a small, blue spiral galaxy that is a companion member of the Arp 181 system [arXiv:1211.6923]. It exhibits strong star formation activity and is likely interacting with tidal H I debris from the Arp 181 pair [arXiv:1211.6923].

sources.summary: The paper describes SDSS J102726.32+794911.9 as a small blue spiral companion, likely a pre-existing Arp 181 member interacting with tidal H i debris and showing strong star formation.

quotes.quote: HP(S) approximately coincides with a small blue spiral SDSS J102726.32+794911.9 black which has no published redshift. however information in http://www.etsu.edu/physics/bsmith/research/sg/sara_sg.html confirms this galaxy is at similar redshift to the ARP 181 system.

HP(S) on the other hand, coincides with the small, very blue galaxy black SDSS J102726.32+794911.9, which suggested is either a dwarf galaxy or a recently detached TDG. Our high-resolution H i image (lower right of Figure fig1 ) shows an offset ( 5 ^ ) between the galaxy and the H i peak. From the GMRT spectrum, we find the H i black velocity of HP(S) to be 9575 kms ^ 1 , indicating it is either interacting with the tidal debris black from the Arp pair or formed from it. The latter option is unlikely as the galaxy is bright in SDSS z -- band suggesting presence of an old stellar population black and its faint spiral structure would be unusual for a recently formed TDG. Unlike the diffuse black optical structure associated with HP(N), black SDSS J102726.32+794911.9 is bright in the Spitzer images implying strong star formation activity, possibly triggered by interaction. black Additionally, the offset between HP(S) and SDSS J102726.32+794911.9 may be the result of an ongoing interaction with the \ debris from the Arp 181 pair and the \ disk of SDSS J102726.32+794911.9, which is itself part of the Arp 181 system.

gmw_00010576.name: second TDG candidate
gmw_00010576.overview: The second TDG candidate is a diffuse clump of ultraviolet emission located at the tip of the optical tidal tail emanating from NGC 3212 [arXiv:1211.6923]. This candidate is associated with an H I peak and exhibits H I mass and column density values that match those typically found in tidal dwarf galaxies [arXiv:1211.6923].

sources.summary: The paper treats the second TDG candidate as a diffuse UV clump at the tip of NGC 3212 tidal tail, associated with HP(N), H i, and possible tidal-dwarf formation.

quotes.quote: The object at position 10 ^ h 27 ^ m 40. ^ s 1, 79 ^ 49 ^ 45.3 ^ ,is their second TDG candidate, although its association with the Arp 181 system remains to be confirmed spectroscopically Smith2010b . The black GALEX observations at the position of this second candidate showed it to be a diffuse clump of UV emission at the tip of the optical tidal tail emanating from NGC 3212. Unlike SDSS J102726.32+794911.9, which is bright in Spitzer and SDSS images, this clump is prominent only in UV. In Figure fig1 (upper panel) this second TDG candidate is faintly visible at the tip of the tidal tail emanating from NGC 3212 and is near to HP(N).

The black \ column density maximum in the low-resolution ( 30 ^ ) map for this object is 6.6 10 ^ 20 cm ^ 2 . At a distance of 130 Mpc, a 30 ^ beam samples 18 kpc. The \ mass of the debris and the H i column density values are a good match with those found in TDGs braine,duc1997 . The high-resolution ( 10 ^ ) H i map further confirms the H i peak to be associated WITH the TDG as well as H i extension to the east towards the black optical tidal tail of NGC 3212.

gmw_00010577.name: SDSSJ1029+6510
gmw_00010577.overview: SDSSJ1029+6510 is a quasar host galaxy that exhibits compact, vigorous star formation within 2 kpc of the active nucleus [arXiv:1410.4229]. Beyond this central region, the rest of the galaxy appears to have low star formation rates [arXiv:1410.4229].

sources.summary: The paper resolves compact narrow-line emission around SDSSJ1029+6510, infers vigorous star formation within 2 kpc, and favors low star formation beyond that radius.

quotes.quote: OSIRIS: SDSSJ1029+6510 Figure osiris_results (panel I) shows the K-band image of the SDSSJ1029+6510 QSO from the collapsed data cube (1.99-2.4 ). Figure osiris_results (panel II) and Figure osiris_results (panel III) show the 2D kinematics of the extended narrow line emission relative to the broad emission and the spectra of the individual components. The PSF subtracted data cube reveals three extended narrow line emission regions, labeled A, B and C in Figure osiris_results (panel II). These emission-line regions have a blue-shifted velocity offset of 10-500 with respect to broad emission, and a maximum projected separation of 0.6 (4.2 kpc) from the QSO.

SDSSJ1029+6510 The host galaxy of this object shows compact vigorous star formation within 2 kpc from the QSO. The rest of the galaxy seems to show no narrow which we attribute to low star formation rates. The ratio of <-1.5 is located in the star formation portion of the diagram (Figure bpt ) for region B making it a strong candidate for star formation with a formation rate of 67.4 5.7 . This indicates rapid star formation within 2kpc of the QSO.

gmw_00010578.name: SDSSJ0925+0655
gmw_00010578.overview: SDSSJ0925+0655 is a quasar host galaxy that exhibits resolved narrow emission originating from three distinct, compact regions [arXiv:1410.4229]. This extended emission is a strong candidate for active star formation, with an estimated integrated star formation rate of 29 solar masses per year [arXiv:1410.4229].

sources.summary: The paper detects resolved narrow emission around SDSSJ0925+0655 in three compact clumps, interprets the emission as active star formation, and estimates a 29 solar-mass-per-year rate.

quotes.quote: NIFS: SDSSJ0925 + 0655 Figure nifs_results (panel I) is a K-Band image of the QSO constructed by summing the flux across the entire data cube (1.99-2.4 ). Figure nifs_results (panel II and III), show the 2D kinematics of the extended narrow line emission relative to the redshift of the broad emission and the spectra of the individual components, respectively. The post-PSF subtracted data cube reveals resolved narrow emission originating from three distinct regions (A, B, and C), that are both spatially offset (0.5 1 ) and redshifted (80-250 ) from the QSO, see Table tabnifs for extracted parameters on individual regions.

SDSSJ0925+0655 The extended emission surrounding SDSSJ0925+0655 is a strong candidate for active star formation. The ratio of for region A is within the star formation region on the diagram (Figure bpt ) while our limits on regions B and C place them near the ambiguous regions between star formation and AGN. The total flux from all these implies an integrated star formation rate of 29 2.4 . The detected narrow emission regions are compact ( 2kpc) and we only detect narrow in these three regions.

gmw_00010579.name: J1615-061
gmw_00010579.overview: J1615-061 is a radio galaxy located behind a shock in the ACO2163 cluster [arXiv:2101.12163]. It is positioned near a turbulent region, where its fossil radio plasma could potentially influence the spectral index of the nearby radio halo [arXiv:2101.12163].

sources.summary: The paper notes radio galaxy J1615-061 behind the ACO2163 shock and tests whether its fossil plasma could affect the nearby halo spectral index and shock connection.

quotes.quote: Furthermore, we note the presence of a radio galaxy (J1615-061) behind the shock (Figure fig:spinx-vla-prof ) at RA=16h15m41.3s, DEC= -06d09m08s, which was first reported by (the source is labelled T2 in their Figure 2). If the radio galaxy is in the volume of the halo, the fossil radio plasma would affect the spectral index in the region of the halo, leading to a steep spectrum Kan15, Kan16 , or a flat spectrum if shock re-acceleration does not stop after the shock crosses the fossil plasma cloud, or there is ongoing merging activity in the region. To investigate if the flatter spectra in the S-W region could be due to the activity of the shock, and if there is any possible connection between the radio galaxy fossil electrons and the shock, we produced spectral index and flux radial profiles along the sector represented by the white dashed line in the spectral index image (Figure fig:spinx-vla-prof ), emerging from the turbulent region near the cluster centre, passing through the radio galaxy location, to the radio halo edge.

gmw_00010580.name: BCG2
gmw_00010580.overview: BCG2 is the second-brightest cluster galaxy in the galaxy cluster Abell 1351 [arXiv:1405.4707]. It is located along a north-south axis with the brightest cluster galaxy, BCG1, and is associated with one of the cluster's two northern X-ray subclusters [arXiv:1405.4707].

sources.summary: The paper identifies BCG2 as the second-brightest cluster galaxy in Abell 1351, placing it with BCG1 along the N-S axis and the northern X-ray subclusters.

quotes.quote: The observational scenario agrees with the presence of two main subclusters in the northern region, each with its brightest galaxy (BCG1 and BCG2), detected as the two most important X-ray substructures with a rest-frame LOS velocity difference of V_ rf 2500 and probably being in large part aligned with the LOS.
The second-brightest galaxy is ID. 66 with ^ =17.73 (hereafter BCG2), and then there are several other galaxies at similar brightness ( ^ <0.5 ).

BCG2 and BCG1 bright galaxies are placed along the N--S direction. In addition, a few other quite luminous members (IDs. 55, 56, 76, 79) are aligned following the same axis. Moreover, we detected a velocity gradient in the galaxy velocity field roughly in S--N direction and the presence of 3D substructure at a very significant level. The above results indicate that the cluster structure extends along the LOS but is not completely aligned with it. In particular, the two main subclusters, corresponding to BCG1 and BCG2 and detected as two northern X-ray clumps, cause the bimodality of the velocity distribution, low and high velocity peaks respectively (see Fig. figgauss ).

gmw_00010581.name: host galaxy of GRB 090423
gmw_00010581.overview: The host galaxy of GRB 090423 is a high-redshift galaxy located at z=8.23 [arXiv:1408.2520, arXiv:1507.03909]. It has an exceptionally low star-formation rate [arXiv:1507.03909] and is predicted to have a metallicity of a few percent solar [arXiv:1003.3873].

sources.summary: The paper says Spitzer observations of the host galaxy of GRB 090423 found a weak afterglow-contaminated source, yielding a host magnitude limit, and predicts a few-percent-solar metallicity.

quotes.quote: Chary et al. (2009) observed the field of GRB 090423 for 72 hours using Spitzer /IRAC at 3.6 m, looking for its host galaxy. The observation was performed 46 days after the GRB trigger, (corresponding to about 5 days in the burst rest-frame). A weak source was detected at the location of the GRB afterglow with L_ AB =27.2 0.3 . The observed flux is consistent with the power-law decay of the GRB afterglow, suggesting that the source is still contaminated by the GRB emission and implying a limit on the absolute magnitude of the host of M_ 3900 >-19.96 .

From Fig. 3, we expect that the host galaxy of GRB 090423 should be enriched at a level of a few percent solar. This relatively high metallicity may explain the high equivalent neutral hydrogen column density measured in the X-ray afterglow, although a wide range of systematic effects or the contamination by low- z intervening absorption systems may be a more valuable, alternative explanation for the observed absorption (Chandra et al. 2010).

sources.summary: The paper uses ALMA and Spitzer non-detections to set stringent limits on the z=8.23 GRB 090423 host's FIR flux, obscured and unobscured star formation, and stellar mass.

quotes.quote: We present rest-frame far-infrared (FIR) and optical observations of the host galaxy of GRB 090423 at z=8.23 from the Atacama Large Millimeter Array (ALMA) and the Spitzer Space Telescope , respectively. The host remains undetected to 3 limits of F_ (222 GHz ) 33 Jy and F_ (3.6 m ) 81 nJy. The FIR limit is about 20 times fainter than the luminosity of the local ULIRG Arp 220, and comparable to the local starburst M 82. Comparing to model spectral energy distributions we place a limit on the IR luminosity of L_ IR (8-1000 m ) 3 10^ 10 L _ , corresponding to a limit on the obscured star formation rate of SFR_ IR 5 M _ yr ^ 1 ; for comparison, the limit on the unobscured star formation rate from Hubble Space Telescope rest-frame UV observations is SFR_ UV 1 M _ yr ^ 1 . We also place a limit on the host galaxy stellar mass of M_* 5 10^7 M _ (for a stellar population age of 100 Myr and constant star formation rate).

sources.summary: The paper reports non-detections of the host of GRB 090423, using deep limits to infer a very low star-formation rate and possible stellar mass below 7 million solar masses.

quotes.quote: The deepest search obtained with HST/NIR (Tanvir et al. 2012), Spitzer medium IR, and ALMA radio observations (Berger et al. 2014) for the host of GRB 090423 at z=8.23 gave negative results (Fig. 7). The limiting magnitude obtained with HST in the NIR is m_ AB > 30.29 , which corresponds to a very low upper limit for the UV luminosity, L_ 1500 < 3.7 10^ 38 erg s ^ 1 , and an exceptionally low SFR < 0.07 M _ yr ^ 1 .

The SFR limit for the host of GRB 090423 indicates that a very low stellar mass, M_ 10^6 M _ , is possible if s SFR <70 Gyr ^ 1 . Vice versa, if we assume s SFR 10 Gyr ^ 1 , the limit SFR < 0.07 M _ yr ^ 1 gives M_ < 7 10^6 M _ . We conclude that in the past, GRB hosts must have been very small.

sources.summary: The paper constrains CO(3-2), continuum, molecular gas, and star-formation properties of the GRB 090423 host, arguing it was not an extreme starburst.

quotes.quote: Using observations at 37.5 GHz, we place constraints on molecular gas emission in the CO(3-2) line from its host galaxy and immediate environs. The source was not detected either in line emission or in the rest-frame 850 m continuum, yielding an upper limit of S_ =9.3 Jy and M(H _2 ) <4.3 10^ 9 M _ (3 ), applying standard conversions. This implies that the host galaxy of GRB 090423 did not possess a large reservoir of warm molecular gas but was rather modest either in star formation rate or in mass. It suggests that this was not an extreme starburst, and hence that gamma ray bursts at high redshift trace relatively modest star formation rates, in keeping with the behaviour seen at lower redshifts.

sources.summary: The paper treats the host of GRB 090423 as a z=8.23 high-redshift GRB host with deep HST non-detections, a very low star-formation limit, and inferred stellar mass below a few million solar masses.

quotes.quote: Remarkable are the deep NIR observations with HST of the host of GRB 090423 ( z=8.23 ), which lead to m_ AB > 30.29 (Tanvir et al. \ 2012), or L_ 1500 < 3.7 10^ 38 erg s ^ 1 and SFR < 0.06 M _ yr ^ 1 .

The SFR limit of the host of GRB 090423 indicates that a very low stellar mass, M_ 10^6 M _ , is possible if s SFR <60 Gyr ^ 1 . Vice versa, if we assume s SFR 10 Gyr ^ 1 , the limit SFR < 0.06 M _ yr ^ 1 would give M_ < 6 10^6 M _ .

gmw_00010582.name: 09 ^ h 55 ^ m 35 96 +18 08 24 7
gmw_00010582.overview: The source 09 ^ h 55 ^ m 35 96 +18 08 24 7 is a high-redshift emission line galaxy candidate [arXiv:1008.3383]. It is detected via a single strong carbon monoxide emission line, but it remains undetected in continuum emission [arXiv:1008.3383].

sources.summary: The paper reports a single CO-line galaxy candidate at the stated coordinates, undetected in continuum, with plausible redshifts 2.10, 5.20, or 8.31 and uncertain counterpart identification.

quotes.quote: We identify a single strong emission line source in our spectral data cube, centered at 37.164 GHz as shown in figure fig:spectra . The best fitting Gaussian line profile has a peak flux of 0.58 0.12 mJy and a velocity full width at half-maximum of 96 km s ^ 1 , yielding an integrated line flux S_ CO v=0.059 0.012 Jy km s ^ 1 , accounting for primary beam attenuation which reduces the measured flux at this position to 42.5 of its true value . The line flux is not significantly polarised (although we note that at this low signal to noise there is large flux uncertainty in each polarisation). The source, located at 09 ^ h 55 ^ m 35 96 +18 08 24 7, is not detected in continuum emission.

As discussed above, three plausible redshifts exist for this source: z=2.10 , 5.20 and 8.31, corresponding to the first three rotational transitions of the carbon monoxide molecule. Due to the offset between redshift, luminosity distance and intrinsic line flux for these three transitions, our observations reach comparable mass sensitivities in all three lines: 1.0, 1.1 and 0.98 10^ 10 M _ in molecular gas respectively (assuming M _ gas / L'_ CO =0.8 as discussed in section sec:grbhost ). Since the galaxy almost certainly does not fill our 7.8 6.3 beam, a detailed calculation of the dynamical mass is not possible with the current data, and, in fact, it is not clear whether the line width is actually diagnostic of ordered rotation in sources of this type.

gmw_00010583.name: P5169
gmw_00010583.overview: P5169 is a passive disk galaxy located at the edge of the virial radius of its group [arXiv:1809.02668]. The galaxy has no ionized gas, exhibits regular stellar kinematics, and completely quenched its star formation only a few 10^7 years ago [arXiv:1809.02668].

sources.summary: The paper presents P5169 as a passive GASP group galaxy with regular stellar kinematics, declining disk-wide star formation, and quenching consistent with strangulation.

quotes.quote: P5169 is the only passive galaxy in this group that has been observed in the context of the GASP survey. It is at the edge of the virial radius of the group and its position on projected phase space suggests it has been part of the structure for a long time. Its integrated color and the analysis of the SFHs suggest that its star formation started declining > 10^9 yr ago and that the galaxy completely quenched only a few 10 ^7 yr ago.

P5169 has no ionised gas, and has a spectrum typical of a passive galaxy (both its total integrated spectrum and that obtained integrating over a radius of 5 arcsec; see Fig. fig:P5169_extra ), which supports the scenario that it has been in the group for a long time. The stellar velocity field is regular, spanning a range of -190 < v ( ) < 190, with an uncertainty of 15 .

gmw_00010584.name: P5215
gmw_00010584.overview: P5215 is a rotating spiral galaxy located at the edge of a galaxy group [arXiv:1809.02668]. While its main body exhibits regular stellar and ionized gas kinematics, several detached ionized-gas clouds are visible to its southeast and north [arXiv:1809.02668].

sources.summary: The paper describes P5215 as a rotating spiral at the group edge whose detached ionized-gas clouds may trace cosmic-web enhancement or ram-pressure effects.

quotes.quote: P5215 Moving our attention to the next galaxy, P5215 is the farthest galaxy and it occupies approximately the region where recently infalling galaxies might be found. P5215 has a regular stellar kinematics, indicative of a regular rotation, ranging from v=-150 to v=110 , with a median error of 29 . The median velocity dispersion is 30 . The velocity field of the ionised gas unveils few interesting features. The rotation in the main galaxy body follows the stellar rotation, with a velocity of -170 < v ( ) < 170 and a median error of 4 , and its extent is slightly larger in the south-west and north-east parts, and in the south-eastern direction several detached clouds are visible.

Another peculiarity of the ionised gas velocity field of P5215 is the presence of a large cloud at 27.5 ^ north of the galaxy. This object has a diameter of 5^ , a velocity of 50 , ranging from -75 <v ( ) < -40, a gas velocity dispersion of 25 and has no significant stellar counterpart. Indeed pPXF has not been able to detect any signal in correspondence of the position of the object.

gmw_00010585.name: P5055
gmw_00010585.overview: P5055 is a dusty, edge-on spiral galaxy classified as a Seyfert 2 active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1809.02668]. It features a truncated ionized gas disk with disturbed kinematics, likely caused by ram-pressure stripping as it moves through its host group [arXiv:1809.02668].

sources.summary: The paper identifies P5055 as a dusty edge-on group spiral with a truncated ionized gas disk, disturbed gas kinematics, and likely ram-pressure stripping.

quotes.quote: P5055 P5055 is not only the closest galaxy to the group center, but also the galaxy with the highest velocity difference with respect to the group's mean velocity. This galaxy is in the region where ram pressure is expected to be highest ( Gunn1972 , Jaffe2018 ). Its stellar velocity ranges between -180 < v ( ) < 190, with a median error of 19 . The North-East side is receding and the South West side approaching with respect to the observer.

The ionised gas qualitatively follows the stellar velocity field, but it is much less extended, suggestive of a truncated disc. It is also slightly bended, in the direction opposite to the group center, and spans a relatively larger velocity range: -190 < v ( ) < 230.
Note that , using the ratio of and [ OIII ] , classified P5055 (identified as J101808.6-000503) as Seyfert 2.

gmw_00010586.name: P4946
gmw_00010586.overview: P4946 is a lenticular galaxy composed of two counter-rotating stellar disks [arXiv:1809.02668]. It exhibits chaotic stellar kinematics and is surrounded by an extended ionized gas disk that has formed stars within the last 570 million years [arXiv:1809.02668].

sources.summary: The paper portrays P4946 as an S0 with chaotic kinematics, counter-rotating stellar components, extended ionized gas, and evidence favoring gas accretion.

quotes.quote: P4946 Finally, P4946 is located in the phase-space region where recently infallen galaxies might be found, similar to P5215. Its stellar kinematics ranges from -230 to 190 , with a median error of 4 . The kinematics are chaotic, a receding and approaching side of the galaxy are hardly identifiable. The left panel of Figure fig:P4946_extra shows the stellar velocity dispersion map, along with the profile extracted within a rectangular slit covering the central 3^ 10^ of P4946. Two off-centered maxima are evident, and are indication of two counter-rotating stellar components e.g. Katkov2013 .

P4946 is probably the most peculiar object of the group. It is an S0 galaxy composed by two counter rotating stellar disks. The galaxy is surrounded by an extended ( 20 kpc) ionised gas disk that formed stars only in the last t<5.7 10^8 yr.

gmw_00010587.name: IRAS 06035-7102
gmw_00010587.overview: IRAS 06035-7102 is an interacting double ultraluminous infrared galaxy system with a separation of 10 arcseconds [arXiv:2311.02230]. Ground-based observations have detected its 752 GHz water line [arXiv:2311.02230], while its higher-J carbon monoxide transitions exhibit line broadening and increasingly visible line wings [arXiv:2402.17851].

sources.summary: The paper reports APEX detection of the 752 GHz water line in IRAS 06035-7102, compares its line profile and flux, and describes it as an interacting double ULIRG.

quotes.quote: We report the first ground-based detection of the water line p- 211202 at 752.033 GHz in three z<0.08 ultra-luminous infrared galaxies (ULIRGs): IRAS 06035-7102, IRAS 17207-0014 and IRAS 09022-3615. Using the Atacama Pathfinder EXperiment (APEX), with its Swedish-ESO PI Instrument for APEX (SEPIA) band-9 receiver, we detect this \ line with overall signal-to-noise ratios of 8--10 in all three galaxies. Notably, this is the first detection of this line in IRAS 06035-7102.

The spectra show that p- 211202 is detected with a peak flux density of 0.45 0.09 Jy beam ^ 1 in IRAS 09022-3615, 1.28 0.34 Jy beam ^ 1 in IRAS 17208-0014 and 0.33 0.08 Jy beam ^ 1 in IRAS 06035-7102.
We can see that IRAS 06035-7102 is an extended interacting double system, separated by a distance of 10 '' (in WFC2 image; 2008Arribas ).

sources.summary: The paper reports a CO(4--3) nondetection for IRAS 06035-7102, no observed CO turnover, increasingly prominent line wings, and notable high-J line broadening.

quotes.quote: Our final data coverage for the mid- J CO lines is as follows: CO(4--3) line observations for 16 sources, among which there is a non-detection due to a nearby atmospheric line (IRAS 06035-7102); CO(5--4) line detections in two sources, CO(6--5) line detections in 11 sources, and lastly, three sources with CO(7--6) line measurements.

While there are galaxies for which we observe line wings becoming more prominent with increasing J_ up , as can be seen, for instance, in IRAS 06035-7102 in Figure fig:spec06035 , in those cases the appearance of more prominent wings does not significantly affect the centroid of the line.

The sources showing the most remarkable line broadening in higher- J CO transitions are: IRAS 06035-7102 (Figure fig:spec06035 ), IRAS 06206-6315 (Figure fig:spec06206 ), IRAS 08311-2459 (Figure fig:spec08311 ) and IRAS 16090-0139 (Figure fig:spec16090 ).

gmw_00010588.name: IC 3370
gmw_00010588.overview: IC 3370 is a bright galaxy classified as either an elliptical or a peculiar lenticular system [arXiv:astro-ph/0508263]. It exhibits a large isophotal twist and minor-axis velocity suggesting a triaxial structure, with no dark matter required to fit the velocity dispersion within three effective radii [arXiv:astro-ph/0508263].

sources.summary: The paper presents IC 3370 as a bright, unusual elliptical or S0pec system with strong isophotal twisting, likely triaxiality, and no dynamically important dark matter within 3 effective radii.

quotes.quote: IC 3370 is a bright galaxy, classified as E2--E3 (elliptical) galaxy, absolute blue magnitude --21.4, heliocentric radial velocity 2930 \ (taken from NED database and in agreement with our observations). It covers 2.9 2.3 arcmin on the sky (RC3). However, it is a rather unusual elliptical galaxy and according to Jarvis (1987, hereafter referred to as J87) it should be classified as S0pec (see below).

(ii) IC 3370 may be even a more complex galaxy to model with the 2I approach. This galaxy shows a large isophotal twist and it has a significant non--zero velocity on the minor axis which strongly suggests its triaxiality. Therefore, although 2I modelling necessarily provides a poor fit for some quantities (for example, velocity and h_3 parameter for the minor axis) it nevertheless can give some insight into the dark matter content based on the study of the velocity dispersion. Again, as in the case of IC 1459 we can conclude that the dark matter within 3 R_e ( 100 arcsec 20.3 kpc) is not needed (or it is not dynamically important) because we can obtain a good fit to the velocity dispersion using a constant mass--to--light ratio: 5 < M/L_B < 13 (in solar units, for the Hubble constant of 70 km \ s^ 1 Mpc^ 1 ).

gmw_00010589.name: J0122+0100
gmw_00010589.overview: J0122+0100 is a luminous infrared galaxy merger candidate [arXiv:2306.16437]. It is classified as a mid-infrared dual active galactic nucleus system that is heavily obscured and undetected in hard X-rays [arXiv:2306.16437].

sources.summary: The paper treats J0122+0100 as a mid-IR dual-AGN merger candidate undetected by NuSTAR, with flux limits implying heavy obscuration while leaving possible star-formation contamination unresolved.

quotes.quote: The non-detections in J0122+0100, J1221+1137, and J1306+0735 imply there are no hard X-ray emitting AGNs in the 10-24 keV band in excess of 1.19 10^ 13 , 2.36 10^ 13 , and 1.23 10^ 13 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 , respectively, in these systems. The upper limits on the fluxes for J0122+0100, J1221+1137, and J1306+0735 imply column densities in excess of log( )= 24.9, 24.8, and 24.6 (assuming C = 0.99), if we attribute the non-detections to obscuration alone. The 2-10 keV flux upper limits from are consistent with the fluxes derived from pfeifle2019a .

The observed X-ray deficits relative to the mid-IR luminosities observed for J0122+0100, J1221+1137, and J1306+0735 (and other mergers in our sample) may not come as a surprise, given the fact that these mergers are all classified as LIRGs based on their infrared luminosities, i.e., log( L_ 8-1000 m /L_ )>11 ; LIRGs are in fact known to be underluminous in X-rays relative to the infrared luminosity iwasawa2011,torres-alba2018 , regardless of whether they host an AGN. Obscuration is often implicated as the source for the observed X-ray deficits in LIRGs that host AGNs; the presence of heavily obscured AGNs in these mergers is therefore consistent with the general picture that LIRGs are associated with high concentrations of gas and dust and that AGNs identified in late-stage LIRG mergers are often heavily obscured ricci2017MNRAS,ricci2021 , some of which have also lacked detections in hard X-ray NuSTAR imaging ricci2017MNRAS,ricci2021 .

gmw_00010590.name: J1221+1137
gmw_00010590.overview: J1221+1137 is a galaxy merger candidate classified as a luminous infrared galaxy [arXiv:2306.16437]. It is considered a mid-infrared dual active galactic nucleus candidate, though it remains undetected in hard X-rays due to potential heavy obscuration [arXiv:2306.16437].

sources.summary: The paper treats J1221+1137 as a mid-IR dual-AGN merger candidate undetected by NuSTAR, with flux limits implying heavy obscuration while leaving possible star-formation contamination unresolved.

quotes.quote: The non-detections in J0122+0100, J1221+1137, and J1306+0735 imply there are no hard X-ray emitting AGNs in the 10-24 keV band in excess of 1.19 10^ 13 , 2.36 10^ 13 , and 1.23 10^ 13 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 , respectively, in these systems. gmw_00010591.name: J1306+0735
gmw_00010591.overview: J1306+0735 is a luminous infrared galaxy classified as a mid-infrared dual active galactic nucleus merger candidate [arXiv:2306.16437]. It is undetected in hard X-rays, which suggests the presence of heavily obscured active galactic nuclei [arXiv:2306.16437].

sources.summary: The paper treats J1306+0735 as a mid-IR dual-AGN merger candidate undetected by NuSTAR, with flux limits implying heavy obscuration while leaving possible star-formation contamination unresolved.

quotes.quote: The non-detections in J0122+0100, J1221+1137, and J1306+0735 imply there are no hard X-ray emitting AGNs in the 10-24 keV band in excess of 1.19 10^ 13 , 2.36 10^ 13 , and 1.23 10^ 13 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 , respectively, in these systems. The upper limits on the fluxes for J0122+0100, J1221+1137, and J1306+0735 imply column densities in excess of log( )= 24.9, 24.8, and 24.6 (assuming C = 0.99), if we attribute the non-detections to obscuration alone. The 2-10 keV flux upper limits from are consistent with the fluxes derived from pfeifle2019a .

The observed X-ray deficits relative to the mid-IR luminosities observed for J0122+0100, J1221+1137, and J1306+0735 (and other mergers in our sample) may not come as a surprise, given the fact that these mergers are all classified as LIRGs based on their infrared luminosities, i.e., log( L_ 8-1000 m /L_ )>11 ; LIRGs are in fact known to be underluminous in X-rays relative to the infrared luminosity iwasawa2011,torres-alba2018 , regardless of whether they host an AGN. Obscuration is often implicated as the source for the observed X-ray deficits in LIRGs that host AGNs; the presence of heavily obscured AGNs in these mergers is therefore consistent with the general picture that LIRGs are associated with high concentrations of gas and dust and that AGNs identified in late-stage LIRG mergers are often heavily obscured ricci2017MNRAS,ricci2021 , some of which have also lacked detections in hard X-ray NuSTAR imaging ricci2017MNRAS,ricci2021 .

gmw_00010592.name: UGC4052
gmw_00010592.overview: UGC4052 is a central pair of large elliptical galaxies [arXiv:1407.5791] surrounded by a 40-arcsecond infrared and near-ultraviolet ring [arXiv:1407.5791]. The eastern galaxy, UGC4052 E, exhibits star formation [arXiv:1407.5791], while the western galaxy, UGC4052 W, hosts a ram-pressure-stripped X-ray corona [arXiv:1407.5791].

sources.summary: The paper describes UGC4052 as a central elliptical galaxy pair in the NE group, surrounded by a 40 arcsec IR/near-UV ring possibly formed by stripping or a close encounter.

quotes.quote: The northern group core also hosts a galaxy pair, UGC4052, with a surrounding IR and near-UV ring 40 in diameter. The ring may have been produced by tidal stripping of a smaller galaxy by UGC4052 or it may be a collisional ring generated by a close encounter between the two large galaxies.

The striking IR and near-UV ring surrounding the galaxy pair UGC4052 is 40 across and the major axis appears aligned with the central galaxies (Fig. fig:zoomin ). This alignment suggests that the formation of the ring could be linked to these galaxies. The group merger may have caused collisions between the constituent galaxies and the ring could correspond to the orbit of a smaller galaxy being stripped of its gas in the interaction. However, there is not a clear progenitor galaxy and the apparent spiralling of the orbit to the W complicates this interpretation (Fig. fig:zoomin ). The ring may have been produced by the stripping of multiple galaxies around UGC4052.

sources.summary: The paper identifies UGC4052 E as the eastern member at the X-ray peak, with luminous H emission, excess IR from likely star formation, and possible gas-stellar velocity offset.

quotes.quote: The NE group core hosts a pair of large elliptical galaxies (UGC4052) separated by a projected distance of only 15 ( Crawford99 ). The eastern galaxy of the pair, UGC4052 E, is coincident with the X-ray surface brightness peak in the core and hosts luminous H line emission ( L_ H 10^ 40 , Crawford99 ).

UGC4052 was included in the Quillen08 Spitzer study of the mid-IR emission from the central galaxies in groups and clusters with optical emission lines. Quillen08 found that UGC4052 E has excess IR emission above that expected for a quiescent elliptical galaxy, which is likely due to hot dust powered by star formation. ODea08 estimated a star formation rate for this galaxy from the IR luminosity of 0.7 .

UGC4052 E was targeted in the SDSS ( Abazajian09 ) and the spectrum shows a possible velocity offset between the gas emission lines and absorption lines in the stellar continuum. There could therefore also be a difference in the gas and stellar dynamics in this complex system.

sources.summary: The paper describes UGC4052 W as the western member ahead of the NE group core, associated with an X-ray nose and interpreted as hosting a ram-pressure-stripped corona.

quotes.quote: UGC4052 W is located ahead of the NE group core and appears associated with a small spur of X-ray emission or `nose' extending 7 ( 15 ; Fig. fig:zoomin upper left).
The `nose' of the NE group core is likely to be a ram pressure stripped X-ray corona hosted by the large elliptical galaxy UGC4052 W.
The W galaxy is positioned ahead of the group core and hosts a ram pressure stripped X-ray corona.

gmw_00010593.name: A2033 BCG
gmw_00010593.overview: A2033 BCG is a brightest cluster galaxy with a very large velocity dispersion of 357 +/− 6 km/s [arXiv:1809.01137]. Its stellar mass is estimated using SDSS DR7 cModel magnitudes [arXiv:1809.01137].

sources.summary: The paper corrects the A2033 BCG photometry with SDSS DR7 magnitudes for stellar mass and notes that the brightest galaxy of A2033 has a very large velocity dispersion.

quotes.quote: The stellar masses of the brightest galaxies of A2029 and A2033 we initially derive from SDSS DR12 are underestimated. Their SDSS DR12 cModel magnitudes are significantly overestimated: = 17.29 for the A2029 BCG and = 17.44 for the A2033 BCG. Their DR7 cModel magnitudes are more reasonable: = 13.59 for the A2029 BCG and = 14.20 for the A2033 BCG, similar to R-band magnitudes of these galaxies listed in NED. Therefore, we use the SDSS DR7 cModel magnitudes to estimate the stellar masses for these BCGs.

The brightest cluster galaxy (BCG) of A2029 (IC 1101) has the largest velocity dispersion, = 430 17 among the cluster members. The velocity dispersions of the brightest galaxies of SIG ( = 307 6 ) and A2033 ( = 357 6 ) are also very large. Interestingly, the velocity dispersions of the brightest galaxies in A2029 and its subsystems are roughly correlated with the masses of the systems. Sohn18b estimate the velocity dispersions of the A2029, A2033 and SIG systems: _ A2029 = 967 25 , _ A2033 = 701 74 , and _ SIG = 745 62 . The relation between the central galaxies and the cluster velocity dispersions suggests a connection between the brightest galaxy properties and the properties of the host system.

gmw_00010594.name: ADF22-QG1
gmw_00010594.overview: ADF22-QG1 is a massive quiescent galaxy located at the core of the SSA22 protocluster [arXiv:2309.12730]. Situated at a redshift of z = 3.092, it exhibits a low star formation rate and has a compact stellar structure [arXiv:2309.12730].

sources.summary: The paper treats ADF22-QG1 as a massive z=3.092 quiescent galaxy in the SSA22 protocluster, with constrained structure, low star formation, and no detected ALMA spectrum.

quotes.quote: ADF22-QG1 is a massive QG at z_ spec =3.092^ +0.008 _ 0.004 Kubo21 , located at the core of the SSA22 protocluster Umehata15 . Its stellar mass, size, and Sersic index have been constrained to M _ = 1.66-3.72 10^ 11 M _ , _ e =1.01 0.04 kpc (0.13"), and n _ Sersic =2.5 0.2 , respectively Kubo17, Kubo21 . No measurement on its stellar velocity dispersion is currently available. The integration on this source is 4.5 min, thus likely insufficient for our purposes.
ADF22-QG1 has an SFR _ IR < 9-20 M _ /yr 19 times below the coeval MS, Schreiber15 , based on its flux Kubo21 .
No detection was present in the spectra of our three galaxies (Fig. fig:spectra ).

gmw_00010595.name: GS-9209
gmw_00010595.overview: GS-9209 is a highly compact quiescent galaxy at a redshift of z = 4.658 [arXiv:2301.11413, arXiv:2309.12730]. It hosts an active galactic nucleus with a supermassive black hole, which likely played a role in quenching its star formation [arXiv:2301.11413].

sources.summary: The paper confirms GS-9209 as a compact massive quiescent galaxy at z=4.658, reconstructs its early star formation and quenching, and links its AGN to possible feedback.

quotes.quote: We report the spectroscopic confirmation of a massive quiescent galaxy, GS-9209 at a new redshift record of z=4.658 , just 1.25 Gyr after the Big Bang, using new deep continuum observations from JWST NIRSpec. From our full-spectral-fitting analysis, we find that this galaxy formed its stellar population over a 200 Myr period, approximately 600-800 Myr after the Big Bang ( z_ form =7.3 0.2 ), before quenching at z_ quench =6.7 0.3 . GS-9209 demonstrates unambiguously that massive galaxy formation was already well underway within the first billion years of cosmic history, with this object having reached a stellar mass of log _ 10 (M_*/ M _ ) > 10.3 by z=7 . This galaxy also clearly demonstrates that the earliest onset of galaxy quenching was no later than 800 Myr after the Big Bang.

Whilst its spectrum is dominated by stellar emission, GS-9209 also exhibits broad H emission, indicating that it hosts an active galactic nucleus (AGN), for which we measure a black-hole mass of log _ 10 (M_ / M _ ) = 8.7 0.1 . Although large-scale star formation in GS-9209 has been quenched for almost half a billion years, the significant integrated quantity of accretion implied by this large black-hole mass suggests AGN feedback plausibly played a significant role in quenching star formation in this galaxy. GS-9209 is also extremely compact, with an effective radius of just 215 20 parsecs.

sources.summary: The paper treats GS-9209 as the most distant massive quiescent galaxy in its sample, with compact structure, low H-alpha star formation, and incomplete spectral coverage.

quotes.quote: GS-9209 is located at z_ spec =4.6582^ +0.0002 _ 0.0002 and its stellar mass, size, Sersic index and stellar velocity dispersion are M _ =4.1^ +0.2 _ 0.2 10^ 10 M _ , _ e =215 20 pc (0.033"), n _ Sersic =2.3 0.3, _ =247 16 km s ^ 1 , respectively Carnall23 . The integration on this source is 52 s. The QGs considered here have been spectroscopically confirmed with an accuracy of z/(1+z) 0.001 , 0.0004, and 0.00004, respectively. This translates into an uncertainty on the position of the [ CII ] line of 440 km s ^ 1 , 159 km s ^ 1 , and 11 km s ^ 1 , respectively.

No SFR _ IR is currently available for GS-9209. The most stringent constraint available comes from the narrow component of its H emission line Carnall23 , which yields SFR _ H_ = 1.9 0.1 M_ /yr ( 50 times below the MS at its redshift).
Lastly, we note that the the spectral coverage at our disposal for ADF22-QG1 and GS-9209 is incomplete.

gmw_00010596.name: AGC115713
gmw_00010596.overview: AGC115713 is an infrared galaxy that has been confirmed as an OH megamaser host [arXiv:2403.03815]. Optical imaging reveals two nuclei in the southeast and northwest, with the northwest nucleus spatially coinciding with both compact OH line emission and radio continuum emission [arXiv:2403.03815].

sources.summary: The paper confirms AGC115713 as an OH megamaser host whose NW nucleus coincides with compact OH and radio continuum emission and meets infrared selection criteria.

quotes.quote: Among these candidates, we have identified two sources, AGC115713 and AGC249507, which display compact OH line emission that are spatially associated with radio continuum emission. These characteristics align with the typical properties of OHM galaxies. Furthermore, the infrared (IR) properties of these two galaxies are consistent with those of known OHM galaxies. Of the two sources, AGC 249507 has been confirmed through optical redshift, whereas AGC 115713 meets a WISE color selection criterion in the literature, providing additional support for this source being an OHM galaxy rather than a nearby galaxy.

AGC115713 The SDSS image of this source reveals the presence of two nuclei located in the southeast (SE) and northwest (NW) regions. Our GMRT observations have confirmed that the OH line emission and radio continuum emission spatially coincide with the NW nucleus, as illustrated in Figure contour . This confirms the optical counterpart identification of SDSS 014135.2+165731, a selection made by . The OH line profile displays two distinct peaks corresponding to the OH 1667 MHz line.

gmw_00010597.name: AGC249507
gmw_00010597.overview: AGC249507 is an infrared galaxy with an optical redshift of z=0.178139 [arXiv:2403.03815]. It is confirmed as an OH megamaser host, displaying multiple OH line components and compact radio continuum emission [arXiv:2403.03815].

sources.summary: The paper confirms AGC249507 as an OH megamaser host with matching optical redshift, compact spatially correlated OH and radio continuum emission, and multiple OH components.

quotes.quote: Among these candidates, we have identified two sources, AGC115713 and AGC249507, which display compact OH line emission that are spatially associated with radio continuum emission. These characteristics align with the typical properties of OHM galaxies. Furthermore, the infrared (IR) properties of these two galaxies are consistent with those of known OHM galaxies. Of the two sources, AGC 249507 has been confirmed through optical redshift, whereas AGC 115713 meets a WISE color selection criterion in the literature, providing additional support for this source being an OHM galaxy rather than a nearby galaxy.

AGC249507 This source has an available optical redshift of z=0.178139, obtained from LAMOST survey data release 8. Importantly, this redshift matches the OH line frequency, as shown in Figure OH . Furthermore, both the radio continuum emission and OH line emission from this source exhibit spatial correlation, with the fitted component size being smaller than the beam size used in our GMRT observations (see Tables table1 and table2 ). The OH line profile reveals the presence of multiple components with a resolution of approximately 6 km/s.

gmw_00010598.name: BCG
gmw_00010598.overview: The brightest cluster galaxy BCG, also known as M1932, is an ultraluminous infrared galaxy [arXiv:1511.03089, arXiv:2004.01786] that hosts a type II active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1511.03089]. It exhibits a high star formation rate [arXiv:1511.03089, arXiv:2004.01786] and contains a substantial reservoir of molecular gas [arXiv:2004.01786].

sources.summary: The paper identifies the BCG as a nearby ULIRG hosting a type II AGN, decomposes its FIR emission into AGN and starburst components, and finds an SFR exceeding the ICM cooling-rate limit.

quotes.quote: The BCG, with L _ IR =1.4 10 ^ 12 L _ is an Ultra Luminous Infrared Galaxy and hosts a type II AGN. We decompose its FIR SED into AGN and starburst components and find equal contributions from AGN and starburst. We also recompute the SFR of the BCG finding SFR=150 15 M _ yr ^ 1 . We search for an isobaric cooling flow in the cool core using Chandra X-ray data, and find no evidence for gas colder than 1.8 keV in the inner 30 kpc, for an upper limit to the istantaneous mass-deposition rate of 58 M _ yr ^ 1 at 95 .l. This value is 3 lower than the SFR in the BCG, suggesting that the on-going SF episode lasts longer than the ICM cooling events.

sources.summary: The paper describes M1932 as a highly star-forming BCG with previously detected molecular gas, included despite low declination because of strong ancillary SFR and stellar-mass information.

quotes.quote: More recently, Fogarty2019 detected a similar amount of molecular gas M_ H_2 10^ 10 M_ in the star-forming BCG of the cool-core cluster MACS 1931.8-2635 (abbreviated as M1932 in this work).

While we should formally discard the CLASH BCG M1932, at Dec.=-26:34:34.0, we included it in our BCG sample since it was recently detected in CO by Fogarty2019 and has a number of ancillary information from the literature, including a high star formation rate SFR (100-300) M_ /yr Fogarty2015 and a stellar mass estimate M_ 7 10^ 11 M_ Cooke2016 .

RX1532 and M1932 are more star-forming, with sSFR 5 sSFR _ MS . To allow for a homogeneous comparison, we assumed the same Galactic CO-to-H _2 conversion factor also for these two sources. Fogarty2019 adopted instead a lower _ CO =0.9 M_ ( K km s ^ 1 pc ^2)^ 1 for M1932, which is, however, typical of ultra-luminous infrared galaxies e.g., for a review Bolatto2013 .

gmw_00010599.name: GRB 051006
gmw_00010599.overview: The host galaxy of GRB 051006 is a distant, luminous star-forming galaxy located at a redshift of z=1.059 [arXiv:1407.4456]. It exhibits a moderately red rest-frame ultraviolet continuum with moderate dust extinction and a star-formation rate of approximately 100 solar masses per year [arXiv:1407.4456].

sources.summary: The paper identifies the GRB 051006 host as a distant, highly star-forming galaxy with red UV continuum, moderate extinction, and no large hidden star-formation component.

quotes.quote: The refined XRT position Evans+2009 includes only source ``B'' of , which has therefore been identified as the probable host galaxy Hjorth+2012 , an association strengthened by its characterization as distant ( z=1.059 ) and highly star-forming Jakobsson+2012 . The galaxy is easily detected in every filter in which we have observed it from the near-UV to 4.5 m. Modeling of the SED shows a moderately red optical (rest-frame UV) continuum indicative of a luminous, moderately extinguished galaxy. We derive a best-fit extinction column of A_V 1.7 mag and a star-formation rate of 100 M_ yr ^ 1 .

The UV/optical star-formation rate is comparable to, and possibly even in excess of, what we infer from the radio observations ( 50 M_ yr ^ 1 ), indicating that this system is a luminous, star-forming galaxy but not an extreme SMG and it does not harbor large amounts of heavily-obscured star-formation.

gmw_00010600.name: GRB 060814
gmw_00010600.overview: The host galaxy of GRB 060814 is a dusty star-forming galaxy located at a redshift of z=1.92 [arXiv:1112.1700, arXiv:1407.4456]. It is in a starburst phase and exhibits a large, distorted morphology with an angular extent of 2.5 arcseconds [arXiv:1407.4456].

sources.summary: The paper describes the GRB 060814 host as a large, distorted, dusty star-forming galaxy whose radio and optical rates place it in the ULIRG/SMG range.

quotes.quote: The host galaxy of GRB 060814 is discussed in some detail in as well as in and . It is well-detected in every optical/NIR band and is also one of the physically largest GRB hosts known, showing a complex, distorted morphology with an angular extent of 2.5 arcsec (21 kpc at the spectroscopic redshift of z=1.923 ) that makes it easily resolved even in ground-based optical observations (Figure fig:images ; note that, as noted by Jakobsson+2012 , the southwest source visible in the VLT image is a foreground object at z=0.84 but the remainder of the emission is from the host). Our SED modeling indicates a very large star-formation rate of approximately 200 M_ yr ^ 1 after correction for dust extinction.

The 3 GHz flux density of 15 Jy measured for this source translates to a star-formation rate of 260 M_ yr ^ 1 at this redshift. This is clearly within the range of ULIRGs and SMGs---although, again, the fact that this is in agreement with the optical SFR indicates that the galaxy is not actually optically thick but simply at the high-luminosity end of the population of ordinary, moderately-obscured galaxies. However, this galaxy is clearly in a starburst phase, given its modest mass ( 1.5 10^ 10 M_ ) and highly distorted morphology.

sources.summary: The paper identifies an object at the GRB 060814 afterglow position as its host galaxy, notes VLT/X-shooter spectroscopy of the host, and uses [OII], [OIII], and H emission lines to set z=1.92.

quotes.quote: At the afterglow position we could identify an object, showing a continuum signature in the spectra, that we therefore consider to be the host galaxy of GRB 060814.
VLT/X-shooter spectroscopy of the host galaxy has been performed within the program 084.A-0303 (PI: J. Fynbo).
Thanks to the identification in the NIR arm of the [ OII ] doublet, [ OIII ] 5007 and H emission lines associated with the host galaxy, we can establish a redshift of z=1.92 for GRB 060814.

gmw_00010601.name: GRB 061121
gmw_00010601.overview: The host galaxy of GRB 061121 is a luminous, radio-detected galaxy [arXiv:1407.4456]. It exhibits fairly blue optical and near-infrared colors with modest optical extinction [arXiv:1407.4456], and it contains an optically-thick starburst component alongside relatively unobscured regions [arXiv:1407.4456].

sources.summary: The paper finds the GRB 061121 host is a luminous radio-detected galaxy with blue optical/NIR colors, modest extinction, and possible additional obscured star formation.

quotes.quote: Nevertheless, the event is hosted within a very luminous, radio-detected galaxy. The 17 Jy source detected in the VLA image indicates a star-formation rate of 160 M_ yr ^ 1 (though with a substantial statistical uncertainty, since the RMS noise level of 5.5 Jy is higher than average.) The host galaxy of this event is well-detected in all optical/NIR bands; the color is fairly blue and the inferred optical extinction is modest ( A_V 0.4 mag). The UV/optical star-formation rate is 30 M_ yr ^ 1 .

Nevertheless, taking our current measurement at face value indicates that a (modest) additional optically-thick starburst is present in the galaxy. However, as the GRB itself shows no evidence of significant obscuration, GRB \ 061121 must have occurred in the relatively unobscured regions of the galaxy seen in our optical observations and not from the optically-thick component.

sources.summary: The paper treats the GRB 061121 host as one of three hosts lacking prior SFR values, then reports VLT/X-shooter spectroscopy, emission-line fluxes, and derived SFR and metallicity.

quotes.quote: The SFR values were taken from with the exception of the host galaxies of GRB 061007, GRB 061121 and GRB 071117, not included in that work. We obtained the VLT/X-shooter spectroscopy of these three host galaxies (ESO programs 095.D-0560 and 085.A-0795, PI: S.D. Vergani and H. Flores, respectively). We processed the spectra using version 2.6.0 of the X-shooter data reduction pipeline Modigliani2010 , following the procedures described in .

The measured emission line fluxes are reported in Table tabflux . We determine the SFR from the fluxes (corrected by the extinction determined through the Balmer ratio), with the same prescriptions as . Following the same prescription as in , we determined the metallicity of the objects in the sample with the method on the strong emission line fluxes reported in the literature Piranomonte2015,Kruhler2015 or on those measured by us; in the relevant cases, the results are consistent within errors to those already reported in the literature.

gmw_00010602.name: 3C 002
gmw_00010602.overview: The object 3C 002 is a high-redshift quasar [arXiv:1501.01434]. Multiwavelength observations of the galaxy include data from SDSS, Spitzer, Herschel, and 2MASS to analyze its full spectral energy distribution [arXiv:1501.01434].

sources.summary: The paper uses 3C 002 as a second quasar SED test case, fitting its full multiwavelength SED after discarding contaminated bands and finding the IR conclusions unchanged.

quotes.quote: Secondly, we consider 3C 002 (lower panels of Fig.  figure:UVoptical ), for which in addition to SDSS, Spitzer and Herschel photometry, we have photometry from 2MASS. We fit the full SED of this object, after discarding data points potentially contaminated by strong emission lines. Once again we reach the same results as in the case for 3C 454.0, thus we conclude that a detailed treatment of the emission in the UV/visible part of the quasars' SEDs has a negligible effect on the results inferred from the IR part of the SED.

gmw_00010603.name: PG 0049+171
gmw_00010603.overview: PG 0049+171 is a quasar that appears compact in both uGMRT and VLA radio images [arXiv:2301.07929]. Its flat-spectrum core suggests the radio emission originates from the synchrotron self-absorbed base of a low-power or frustrated jet [arXiv:2301.07929].

sources.summary: The paper reports compact uGMRT and VLA morphology for PG 0049+171, with flat-spectrum core evidence favoring a low-power or frustrated jet rather than stellar or free-free emission.

quotes.quote: PG0003+199 and PG 0049+171 appear compact in both uGMRT and VLA images. The 2-frequency uGMRT-VLA spectral index image shows a steep spectrum We consider 0.5 to be flat spectrum and <-0.5 to be steep spectrum. uGMRT core in PG 0003+199. The in-band VLA spectral index image also reveals a steep spectrum core. The 1.5 GHz Very Long Baseline Array (VLBA) image of PG 0003+199 detects an extended radio structure of 40 mas along the north-south direction Yao21 . Wang22a detect a two-sided 30 mas clumpy jet along the north-east – south-west direction in the 5 GHz VLBA image of this source. Therefore, the steep spectrum of this source is consistent with the presence of sub-arcsec scale jet emission. On the other hand, the flat spectrum uGMRT core of PG 0049+171, as is evident from the 2-frequency uGMRT-VLA spectral index image, could suggest a synchrotron self-absorbed base of a low-power or `frustrated' jet on sub-arcsec scales. In our uGMRT study, this source was found to lie above the 3 limit of the radio-IR correlation, ruling out the stellar origin for the radio emission Silpa20 . We note that, even within errors, the flat spectrum in this source ( _ 2-freq = -0.5 0.1 ) cannot arise due to thermal free-free emission ( = -0.1 ) from the accretion disk wind or torus or HII regions. This interpretation is also supported by the detection of a flat spectrum core in its in-band VLA spectral index image ( _ in-band = -0.47 0.07 ).
The radio emission in PG 0049+171, PG 1211+143, PG 1244+026 and PG 1426+015 is likely to be arising from the synchrotron self-absorbed base of a low-power or ‘frustrated' jet.

gmw_00010604.name: new galaxy
gmw_00010604.overview: The new galaxy is a relatively bright post-starburst galaxy located in the local Universe [arXiv:astro-ph/0303308]. It lies 71 kiloparsecs from 3C 273 at the redshift of a strong neutral hydrogen absorber [arXiv:astro-ph/0303308].

sources.summary: The paper describes the newly found post-starburst galaxy near 3C 273 as lying 71 kpc from the strong absorber, with a past starburst capable of driving wind material that could explain the absorber.

quotes.quote: One of the five galaxies missed by morris:93 is a relatively bright (M _B = -14.5 ) galaxy 71 70 \ kpc from 3C 273 (360 70 \ kpc from RX J1230.8+0115) at the redshift of the strong H I absorber ( cz_ abs =1589 ) analyzed by T2002. This galaxy has the spectroscopic signatures of a ``post-starburst,'' a rare galaxy type in the local Universe. The starburst phase might be expected to create a substantial wind that could be responsible for a high-column density, low-ionization absorber like the one present in the 3C 273 sight line stocke:03 .
The galaxy Leda 139866 is at 2329 , just out of the velocity range discussed by penton:02 , and the ``new galaxy" is the post-starburst galaxy described briefly above.

gmw_00010605.name: REBELS-12
