gmw_00010605.overview: REBELS-12 is a galaxy located at a redshift of z = 7.3459 [arXiv:2301.09659]. It exhibits spatially resolved [CII] and [OIII] emission lines, as well as a complex dust continuum morphology in Band 6 [arXiv:2301.09659].

sources.summary: ALMA Band 8 does not detect REBELS-12 continuum, but its resolved [CII] and [OIII] emission and low line ratio imply a cold, less bursty z 7 galaxy.

quotes.quote: Three REBELS sources detected in both [ CII ] and Band 6 continuum emission ( _ obs 1.3 mm) were followed up with Band 8 ( _ obs 750 m) in ALMA Cycle 8. This sample includes the two brightest Band 6 continuum sources, REBELS-25 at z_ [ CII ] = 7.3065 and REBELS-38 at z_ [ CII ] = 6.5770 (2021.1.00318.S, PI: Inami), as well as REBELS-12 at z_ [ CII ] = 7.3459 (2021.1.01297.S, PI: Fudamoto). The Band 6 continuum observations of these REBELS targets are described in detail in inami2022 , while in this work we focus on the newly obtained Band 8 data. REBELS-25 and REBELS-38 are in the phase center of their respective Band 8 observations, while the primary beam sensitivity of the Band 8 map at the location of REBELS-12 is 0.70 .

For REBELS-12, both emission lines are spatially resolved, and we adopt r_ [ CII ] = 3.8 1.1 kpc and r_ [ OIII ] = 1.5 0.4 kpc. The Band 6 continuum emission is additionally resolved, but shows a complex morphology (Section sec:results_OIII and Figure fig:moment0 ). In particular, at present it remains unclear if both dust components are physically associated to REBELS-12, or whether the brighter one corresponds to a foreground object.

gmw_00010606.name: REBELS-38
gmw_00010606.overview: REBELS-38 is a high-redshift galaxy detected in dust continuum emission [arXiv:2301.09659]. Observations reveal a two-component dust morphology, a cold dust temperature, and a large dust mass that requires rapid dust build-up [arXiv:2301.09659].

sources.summary: REBELS-38 is detected in Band 8 continuum with a two-component dust morphology, cold dust temperature, large dust mass, and low-burstiness indicators.

quotes.quote: We detect the dust continuum emission of two of our targets in Band 8 (REBELS-25 and REBELS-38), while REBELS-12 remains undetected.

The new Band 8 observations of REBELS-38 highlight a two-component dust morphology that was not visible in the original Band 6 data. The two dust peaks are individually detected at a S/N of 4.3 and 4.1 . This interesting morphology of REBELS-38 is discussed further in Appendix app:rebels_38morphology .

Given that REBELS-25 and REBELS-38 have low inferred dust temperatures, yet are robustly detected in both ALMA Bands 6 and 8, they require large dust masses of M_ dust 10^ 8 M_ . Their dust-to-stellar mass ratios are 0.4 - 0.8 , which necessitate rapid dust build-up. However, the dust contents of REBELS-25 and REBELS-38 are consistent with those predicted for massive galaxies by simulations dayal2022,dicesare2022 .

gmw_00010607.name: G15v2.779
gmw_00010607.overview: G15v2.779 is a bright, strongly lensed submillimeter galaxy located at a redshift of z = 4.243 [arXiv:1207.2724]. It is a massive, highly star-forming system that closely resembles the unlensed galaxy GN20 in its gas mass and infrared luminosity [arXiv:1207.2724].

sources.summary: The paper identifies G15v2.779 as a bright H-ATLAS lensed submillimeter galaxy at z=4.243, describing its strong star formation, gas-related properties, and similarity to GN20.

quotes.quote: This paper focuses on one source of particular interest drawn from the Phase 1 catalog: H-ATLAS J142413.9+022304 this object is denoted ``ID 141'' in hereafter, we refer to it as G15v2.779 Cox:2011fk . SPIRE photometry of this source shows that it is one of the brightest detected so far in Herschel wide-field surveys and that its SED peaks at wavelengths greater than 500 m, suggesting that it lies at z > 3 . This source has been the target of significant follow-up efforts: the Plateau de Bure Interferometer (PdBI) has detected millimeter (mm) and sub-mm CO emission lines which imply that the redshift of this source is z=4.243 0.001 , while data from the Atacama Pathfinder Experiment (APEX) have shown that the dominant cooling line in this galaxy is [ CII ] emission Cox:2011fk . This makes this one of the few SMGs known at z > 4 Capak:2008lr, Schinnerer:2008uq, Coppin:2009lr, Daddi:2009kx, Daddi:2009qy .

G15v2.779 appears to be a very massive, highly star-forming galaxy at z = 4.243 . Table tab:smgproperties summarizes the properties of G15v2.779 and compares them with unlensed SMGs at z 4 . G15v2.779 bears a close resemblence to GN20, having similar L_ IR , M_ gas , _ gas , r_ s , M_ dyn , and _ IR values.

gmw_00010608.name: G1
gmw_00010608.overview: G1 is a low-mass early-type galaxy located in the galaxy cluster MACS J1115.9+0129 [arXiv:1602.02753]. Acting as a strong gravitational lens, it has an unusually dark-matter-dominated inner region with a luminous-to-total mass fraction of 0.11 within its half-light radius [arXiv:1602.02753].

sources.summary: The paper models G1 as the main low-mass cluster-member lens, measuring its half-light total mass, velocity dispersion, stellar mass fraction, and unusually dark-matter-dominated inner regions.

quotes.quote: We present a strong lensing system, composed of 4 multiple images of a source at z=2.387 , created by two lens galaxies, G1 and G2, belonging to the galaxy cluster MACS J1115.9 + 0129 at z=0.353 . We use observations taken as part of the Cluster Lensing and Supernova survey with Hubble, and its spectroscopic follow-up programme at the Very Large Telescope, to estimate the total mass distributions of the two galaxies and the cluster through strong gravitational lensing models. We find that the total projected mass values within the half-light radii, R_ e , of the two lens galaxies are M_ T,G1 (<R_ e, G1 )=(3.6 0.4) 10^ 10 M_ and M_ T,G2 (<R_ e, G2 )=(4.2 1.6) 10^ 10 M_ . The effective velocity dispersion values of G1 and G2 are (122 7) km s ^ 1 and (137 27) km s ^ 1 , respectively.

By fitting the spectral energy distributions of G1 and G2, we measure projected luminous over total mass fractions within R_ e of 0.11 0.03 , for G1, and 0.73 0.32 , for G2. The fact that the less massive galaxy, G1, is dark-matter dominated in its inner regions raises the question of whether the dark matter fraction in the core of early-type galaxies depends on their mass.

gmw_00010609.name: G2
gmw_00010609.overview: G2 is a lens galaxy located in the galaxy cluster MACS J1115.9+0129 at a redshift of z=0.353 [arXiv:1602.02753]. It has an effective velocity dispersion of 137 km/s and a projected luminous-to-total mass fraction of 0.73 within its half-light radius [arXiv:1602.02753].

sources.summary: The paper models G2 as the secondary cluster-member lens, measuring its half-light mass, velocity dispersion, and higher luminous-to-total mass fraction while including it in the strong-lensing mass model.

quotes.quote: We present a strong lensing system, composed of 4 multiple images of a source at z=2.387 , created by two lens galaxies, G1 and G2, belonging to the galaxy cluster MACS J1115.9 + 0129 at z=0.353 . We use observations taken as part of the Cluster Lensing and Supernova survey with Hubble, and its spectroscopic follow-up programme at the Very Large Telescope, to estimate the total mass distributions of the two galaxies and the cluster through strong gravitational lensing models. We find that the total projected mass values within the half-light radii, R_ e , of the two lens galaxies are M_ T,G1 (<R_ e, G1 )=(3.6 0.4) 10^ 10 M_ and M_ T,G2 (<R_ e, G2 )=(4.2 1.6) 10^ 10 M_ . The effective velocity dispersion values of G1 and G2 are (122 7) km s ^ 1 and (137 27) km s ^ 1 , respectively.

By fitting the spectral energy distributions of G1 and G2, we measure projected luminous over total mass fractions within R_ e of 0.11 0.03 , for G1, and 0.73 0.32 , for G2. The fact that the less massive galaxy, G1, is dark-matter dominated in its inner regions raises the question of whether the dark matter fraction in the core of early-type galaxies depends on their mass.

gmw_00010610.name: B2 0954+25A
gmw_00010610.overview: B2 0954+25A is a compact, radio-loud active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2305.07443] that hosts a wiggling radio jet ending at a hotspot [arXiv:1802.04714]. The source exhibits radio variability [arXiv:2305.07443] and has been observed in three distinct optical emission states ranging from disc-dominated to jet-dominated [arXiv:2305.07443].

sources.summary: The paper treats B2 0954+25A as a distant transition object between FSRQs and loud NLS1s, with changing optical states, flat-spectrum compact radio emission, superluminal motion, and radio variability.

quotes.quote: B2 0954+25A is the most distant source of our small sample (z = 0.712, ). This source is missing from general studies of the ray emitting NLS1 class galaxies (e.g. , ), certainly due to its habit of completely changing its emission spectrum. Indeed B2 0954 + 25A has been observed in the optical in (at least) three different emission states: partially jet dominated in 1987 Jackson_Browne_1991 , disc dominated in 2004 SDSS2009 , and totally jet dominated in 2006 SDSS2006 . Through a detailed reanalysis of the intensity and shape of the Balmer line in those spectra, Calderone2012 did find a likely value of FWHM(H ) 2800 km s^ 1 , just slightly above the commonly adopted classification criterion for NLS1 sources. They consequently revisited the estimation of the black-hole mass by means of virial method and mass - stellar dispersion relation ( M- ), and derived a small black-hole mass, M_ BH = (1-3) 10^8 . Calderone2012 propose this source to be a transition object between FSRQs and loud NLS1s, due to its characteristics of both classes, and the strong similarity of its SED (in the disc-dominated state) to that of PMN J0948 + 0022.

B2 0954+25A is a compact radio-loud AGN with a flat radio spectrum. While usually flat, the radio spectrum of the source becomes inverted during burst activity ( 2005Torniainen ). Apparent superluminal motions (12c, ) suggest a small viewing angle (3 - 6 , ). B2 0954+25A is not as intensively studied as 1H 0323+342 and PMN J0948+0022, but radio variability is also seen for this source in the MOJAVE data set at 15 GHz https://www.cv.nrao.edu/MOJAVE/ .

sources.summary: The note describes 0953 + 254 as a wiggling radio jet ending at a hotspot, with no X-ray counterpart and no clear HST knot associations.

quotes.quote: 0953 + 254 (B2 0954 + 25A) The radio jet starts out at a PA of -115 , as in VLBI images, and wiggles a few times before ending at a hotspot 15 \ from the core. No corresponding features are detected in the X-ray band. Similarly, there are no clear knot associations in the HST image.

gmw_00010611.overview: The source 2023 + 335 is a flat-spectrum radio quasar and gamma-ray blazar located at a redshift of z = 0.22 [arXiv:2107.09395, arXiv:2307.07327]. It exhibits a point-like morphology [arXiv:2107.09395], a flat or slightly inverted spectrum at gigahertz frequencies [arXiv:2307.07327], and variability characterized by major flaring events [arXiv:2006.15790].

sources.summary: The paper analyzes B2023+336 as a nearby gamma-ray blazar whose point-like, soft spectrum and variability likely explain reported V404 Cygni excesses through source confusion.

quotes.quote: However, the 4FGL does catalogue a luminous ray emitting flat spectrum radio quasar (FSRQ), B2023+336, approximately 0.3 away from the position of V404 Cygni, detected through the Galactic plane . This is problematic, as the resolution of the LAT varies from between an optimal 0.15 at >10 GeV down to a substantially poorer resolution of 3.5 at 100 MeV (see Figure 17 in atwood_large_2009 ). As a result, source confusion between B2023+336 and V404 Cygni becomes the primary issue in reliably detecting ray emission from the LMXB with Fermi LAT at any but the highest energies.

We do not expect to see any extended emission from B2023+336, as it is a blazar, all of which are point sources. We use Equation eqn:ts to fit a radial Gaussian model as an alternative hypothesis against the null hypothesis of a point source model. The TS of extension for B2023+336 is calculated to be -0.01, strongly favouring the point source model over an extended one, as we would expect.

B2023+336 is a notable ray blazar, as it is one of the few that is seen through the Galactic plane, which is itself a luminous ray emitter. As a result, although we expect some contamination of the photons from B2023+336 with those from the Galactic plane, which has a soft spectrum, use of the 8 year Galactic background model should minimise this.

sources.summary: The paper identifies J2025.3+3341 as nearby blazar QSO B2023+336, analyzes its LAT light curves, and finds a major flare capable of contaminating V404 Cygni flare searches without significant spectral change.

quotes.quote: The source J2025.3+3341, the closest one with a separation of 0 28 from , is a blazar (QSO B2023+336). Sets of data in time bins of 3-, 15-, and 60-days for this source were analyzed. In Figure fig:ts , we show a TS map we obtained during MJD 55076--55078, which was determined to be at the peak time of a major flare from the 3-day bin data. As can be seen, the source's flare could contaminate our search results for short flares from \ because of the proximity. Based on the light curve results obtained from the 15- and 60-day bin data, which are shown in the top two panels of Figure fig:3lc , the flare was determined to last approximately from the beginning of the LAT data to MJD 55103.

After this major flare, there were several occasional short flaring events in the rest of the time period. We performed binned analysis to the LAT data in the major flare and in the rest of the time period for this blazar, and no significant spectral changes were found (photon index is 2.75 0.08 and 2.77 0.04 in the former and latter time period respectively).

sources.summary: The paper describes 2023+335 as a flat-spectrum quasar/AGN whose 2009 extreme scattering event produced rare refractive multiple parsec-scale imaging, with secondary images stretched relative to its normal jet direction.

quotes.quote: We have investigated highly atypical morphological parsec-scale changes in the flat spectrum extragalactic radio source 2023+335 which are coincident with an extreme scattering event (ESE) seen at radio wavelengths during the first half of 2009. We used (i) 15.4 GHz Very Long Baseline Array (VLBA) observations of the quasar 2023+335 obtained at 14 epochs between July 2008 and Nov. 2012 as part of the Monitoring Of Jets in Active galactic nuclei with VLBA Experiments (MOJAVE) program, (ii) earlier archival VLBA observations of the source performed at 1.4, 2, 8, 15, 22, and 86 GHz to analyze the properties of the proposed turbulent screen toward 2023+335, and (iii) data sets from the Owens Valley Radio Observatory (OVRO) and University of Michigan Radio Astronomy Observatory (UMRAO) single-dish monitoring programs performed at 15 and 14.5 GHz, respectively, to study integrated flux density changes. We report on the first detection of the theoretically-predicted rare phenomenon of multiple parsec-scale imaging of an active galactic nucleus induced by refractive effects due to localized foreground electron density enhancements, e.g., in an AU-scale plasma lens(es) in the ionized component of the Galactic interstellar medium.

We detected multiple imaging in the low galactic latitude ( b=-2 4 ) quasar 2023+335 from the 15.4 GHz MOJAVE observations when the source was undergoing an ESE. While the parsec-scale jet of the source normally extends along PA 20 , in the 28 May 2009 and 23 July 2009 images a highly significant multi-component pattern of secondary images is stretched out nearly along the constant galactic latitude line with a local PA 40 , indicating that the direction of relative motion of the plasma lens is close to orbital. Weaker but still detectable imaging patterns at similar position angles are sporadically manifest at several other epochs.

sources.summary: The paper flags 2023 + 335 as the brightest >1 Jy VLBI blazar near Baikal-GVD event GVD210409CA, with z=0.22, flat or inverted GHz spectrum, and Fermi-LAT gamma-ray detection.

quotes.quote: There are four blazars with historical VLBI flux density at 8 GHz above 1 Jy that coincide with the Baikal-GVD high-energy cascade events ( tab:HE_events ). Three of them are close to event GVD210409CA. The brightest is 2023 + 335 ( z = 0.22 ): its average VLBI flux density is 2 Jy at 8 GHz, with a flat or slightly inverted spectrum at GHz frequencies. Moreover, 2023 + 335 is detected by Fermi LAT in gamma rays 2012ApJ...746..159K .

sources.summary: The paper describes 2023+335 as a low-galactic-latitude quasar whose radio structure is distorted by rare strong interstellar scattering, preventing robust kinematic feature identification.

quotes.quote: 2023+335 (B2 2023+33): This low galactic latitude quasar is strongly affected by interstellar scattering 2013AA...555A..80P . The radio structure was too complex to reliably cross-identify any robust features for kinematic analysis. Examples of this kind of strong scattering are extremely rare among bright, compact AGN .

gmw_00010612.name: host galaxy of 11
gmw_00010612.overview: The host galaxy of 11 is a faint persistent source with an R-band magnitude of 24.8 [arXiv:0907.0087]. It is located 6.3 arcseconds off-axis from a nearby, bright spiral galaxy [arXiv:0907.0087].

sources.summary: The paper identifies a faint R-band host aligned with the GRB afterglow and offset from a brighter nearby spiral galaxy.

quotes.quote: We detect a persistent source in the R band, consistent with the afterglow position of 11, with magnitude R=24.8 0.4 . The host is 6.3'' off-axis from a nearby, bright spiral galaxy. From the positions derived from the relative astrometry performed on both the UVOT and CFHT images, we found that the afterglow and the host galaxy have an offset of less than 0.2 arcseconds (Fig. fig:f9 ).

gmw_00010613.name: The bright nearby galaxy
aliases_external: bright nearby galaxy near GRB 060111B host
gmw_00010613.overview: The bright nearby galaxy is located at coordinates R.A.=19h 05m 43.06s, Dec.=+70d 22' 29.7'' [arXiv:0907.0087]. Photometric observations indicate that it is an early-type Sb spiral galaxy with a photometric redshift of approximately 0.22 [arXiv:0907.0087].

sources.summary: The paper describes a brighter galaxy 4.5 arcseconds from the GRB host, gives its coordinates, photometric redshift, and early-spiral Sb classification.

quotes.quote: A much brighter galaxy is present in our imaging as well, centered at an angular offset of only 4.5 arcseconds from the GRB host galaxy. Astrometry based on CrAO R- band observations taken 16 days after burst and USNO-A2.0 reduction located the bright galaxy at coordinates (J2000) R.A.=19 ^h 05 ^m 43.06 ^s , Dec.=+70d 22 ' 29.7 '' with uncertainty of 0.2'' (Fig. fig:f9 ). We performed first photometric and then spectroscopic observations of this galaxy in order to check whether any connection with the host (e.g. a satellite galaxy) and/or the burst itself (e.g. a halo burst as if it originated from an old binary system) could exist.

Photometric observations have been performed at the NOT telescope about four and eight months after the burst. U , B , V , R , I and J magnitude are quoted in Table photom-gal . The best photometric redshift obtained with HyperZ code Bolzonella2000 is z=0.22^ +0.06 _ 0.07 . Its type is compatible with an early spiral galaxy, Sb.

gmw_00010614.name: JADES-JEMS-13396
gmw_00010614.overview: The high-redshift galaxy JADES-JEMS-13396 features a central component with a classic-bulge-like morphology [arXiv:2305.18518]. While most of this central component's mass formed during a recent star-formation episode, its surrounding smooth component is currently forming stars at a higher rate [arXiv:2305.18518].

sources.summary: The paper finds JADES-JEMS-13396 has a classic-bulge-like C1 morphology and distinct SFHs: most C1 mass formed in a recent episode while the smooth component is currently more active.

quotes.quote: Therefore, from a purely morphological perspective (Figure fig:fpho ), the C1 of JADES-JEMS-13396 ( n>2 ) is more like a classic bulge, while in the other 5 galaxies C1 are consistent with pseudo-bulges, although we stress that without dynamical measures it remains difficult to definitively distinguish the two.

JADES-JEMS-13396. The top row of Figure fig:seds_specz shows the SED fitting result of this galaxy. Despite that the mass-weighted stellar age of C1 is consistent with that of the Smooth component, the shapes of their SFHs are different. For the Smooth component, its mass assembly was at a relatively low, but non-negligible rate in early times of t_ lookback >0.4 Gyr, followed by a rapid increase in SFR. For C1, however, its most mass formed through a recent star-formation episode during t_ lookback =0.2 0.8 Gyr. By the time of observation, the Smooth component is forming stars at a higher rate than C1, with the sSFR of the former being 1.6 times larger (Table tab:sed_info ).

gmw_00010615.name: JADES-JEMS-15157
gmw_00010615.overview: JADES-JEMS-15157 is a galaxy with a star formation history that peaked 0.3 Gyr ago and then rapidly declined [arXiv:2305.18518]. It is situated near a clump with a similar stellar population, though their photometry is blended due to a small spatial separation [arXiv:2305.18518].

sources.summary: The paper finds JADES-JEMS-15157 has a C1 SFH that peaked recently then declined, low current C1 sSFR, and a nearby C3 clump whose photometry is blended with C1.

quotes.quote: JADES-JEMS-15157. The middle row of Figure fig:seds_specz shows the SED fitting result of this galaxy. Unlike C2 having a monotonically rising SFH, the SFH of C1 gradually rises toward a relatively recent peak at 0.3 Gyr, followed by a rapid decline over the past 0.2 Gyr. By the time of observation, the sSFR of C1 is only 0.4 Gyr ^ 1 , 7.5 times smaller than that of C2 (Table tab:sed_info ). We note that C3 has a similar stellar population to that of C1, but this is because the spatial separation between C1 and C3 is so small -- about 0.1" that is smaller than the angular resolution of NIRCam/F444W imaging -- that the aperture photometry (Section sec:phot ) for one substructure is significantly affected by the other. Finally, while C1 and the Smooth component share a similar SFH in early times, they differentiate from each other at late times: unlike C1 whose SFR quickly declined after the 0.3 Gyr peak, the Smooth component retained that peak SFR toward a later time of <0.1 Gyr.

gmw_00010616.name: JADES-JEMS-6885
gmw_00010616.overview: JADES-JEMS-6885 is a high-redshift galaxy with structural components that exhibit distinct star formation histories [arXiv:2305.18518]. Its component C1 has an older, post-starburst-like history, whereas components C2 and C3 are undergoing active starbursts that began approximately 0.2 gigayears ago [arXiv:2305.18518].

sources.summary: The paper finds JADES-JEMS-6885 has component SFHs that diverged over the last gigayear, with older, poststarburst-like C1 and ongoing bursts in C2 and C3.

quotes.quote: JADES-JEMS-6885. The bottom row of Figure fig:seds_specz shows the SED fitting result of this galaxy. There is no significant mass assembly before >1 Gyr for all structural components, but their SFHs are different over the recent 1 Gyr. Compared to C2 and C3, C1 is older by 0.3 0.5 Gyr (Table tab:sed_info ). Moreover, C1 has a poststarburst-like SFH, namely, it experienced a burst of star formation during =0.2 0.6 Gyr, followed by a rapid decline of SFR. Differing from C1, C2 and C3 are experiencing on-going bursts of star formation started 0.2 Gyr ago.

gmw_00010617.name: JADES-JEMS-16296
gmw_00010617.overview: The high-redshift galaxy JADES-JEMS-16296 contains two components, C1 and C2, which are both experiencing bursts of star formation that began 0.1 Gyr ago [arXiv:2305.18518]. Additionally, the C1 component shows evidence of star-formation rejuvenation alongside an older stellar population [arXiv:2305.18518].

sources.summary: The paper singles out JADES-JEMS-16296 as having recent central star-formation rejuvenation, with C1 and C2 both bursting and older stellar populations also present in C1.

quotes.quote: Indeed, we see evidence of star-formation rejuvenation in the C1 of JADES-JEMS-16296 by the time of observation. This rejuvenation event happens at 0.3 Gyr after the peak of the galaxy's most recent major star-formation episode. Interestingly, this timescale is very similar to the model predictions for the oscillation timescale ( 0.2-0.5 t_H , corresponding to 0.3-0.8 Gyr at z=3.7 ) of star formation triggered by gas replenishment Tacchella2016 .

JADES-JEMS-16296. The top row of Figure fig:seds_photz shows the SED fitting result of this galaxy. Both C1 and C2 are experiencing bursts of star formation started 0.1 Gyr ago, and their instantaneous sSFRs are similar, 5 Gyr ^ 1 . While it is not seen from the delayed-tau SFH (Figure fig:compare_sfh_param ), the reconstructed nonparametric SFH (Figure fig:seds_photz and fig:compare_sfh_prior ) suggests that older ( 0.5 Gyr) stellar populations are also present in C1, leading to its larger mass-weighted stellar age than C2 (Table tab:sed_info ).

gmw_00010618.name: JADES-JEMS-14436
gmw_00010618.overview: JADES-JEMS-14436 is interpreted as a possible merging system [arXiv:2305.18518]. Its substructure, C2, exhibits a clear tidal feature and physical properties that are highly consistent with a minor-merger origin [arXiv:2305.18518].

sources.summary: The paper interprets JADES-JEMS-14436 as a possible merging system: C2 has a tidal feature and physical properties consistent with a minor-merger origin.

quotes.quote: Regarding the latter, i.e. the C2 of JADES-JEMS-14436, we see a clear tidal feature associated with this substructure in the NIRCam SW's filter images (Figure fig:poststamps_cont and Appendix app:ind ). Therefore, the physical origin of this substructure is very likely related to a minor merger with a mass ratio of 1:10 (Table tab:sed_info ). The minor mergers can have two broad effects on the clump formation.

First, minor mergers can disturb the gas distribution in galaxy disks, trigger local starbursts and hence induce clump formation Bournaud2014,Inoue2016 . Second, the clumps can be minor mergers themselves, i.e., the clumps have an ex-situ origin. Using cosmological hydrodynamical simulations, Mandelker2014 found that the ex-situ clumps have a typical stellar age of 0.5-3 Gyr and specific star formation rate of 0.1-2 Gyr ^ 1 , which are in quantitative agreement with the C2 of JADES-JEMS-14436 (Figure fig:seds_photz and Appendix app:ind ).

gmw_00010619.name: JADES-JEMS-11059
gmw_00010619.overview: JADES-JEMS-11059 is a high-redshift galaxy containing old stellar populations with ages exceeding 1 Gyr [arXiv:2305.18518]. The galaxy also features a component with a rising star formation history and another experiencing a starburst that began 0.3 Gyr ago [arXiv:2305.18518].

sources.summary: The paper finds JADES-JEMS-11059 contains old C1 stellar populations plus a recent burst, a rising C3 SFH, and a C2 starburst with photometric redshift consistent with the galaxy.

quotes.quote: JADES-JEMS-11059. The bottom row of Figure fig:seds_photz shows the SED fitting result of this galaxy. C1 contains old stellar populations with age >1 Gyr, and it also has a recent burst of star formation that started 0.2 Gyr ago, peaked at 0.1 Gyr and declined afterward. Meanwhile, C3 has an overall rising SFH. For C2, although it is not in the segmentation map of the galaxy (Figure fig:poststamps_cont ), it is still included to the analysis because its photometric redshift solution is consistent with that of the galaxy JADES-JEMS-11059. C2 is experiencing a starburst started 0.3 Gyr ago.

gmw_00010620.name: CID-252
gmw_00010620.overview: CID-252 is a dual active galactic nucleus candidate identified through imaging and nuclear spectral energy distribution analysis [arXiv:2405.14980]. The system contains two bright nuclei with very similar photometric redshifts, suggesting they reside at the same redshift [arXiv:2405.14980].

sources.summary: The paper identifies CID-252 as a dual AGN candidate whose image decomposition needs an added companion PSF; its two nuclei have similar photometric redshifts and a nonzero same-redshift probability.

quotes.quote: In Figure fig:decomp , we present the multiwavelength simultaneous AGN-host image decomposition results for the dual AGN candidate CID-252 (Section subsec:second ). This system requires an additional PSF model to fit the image of the companion, as otherwise, significant residuals remain in the central region, causing unphysically small . CID-252-0 is identified as the primary AGN due to its higher PSF luminosity.

The second pair, CID-252, is identified as a dual AGN through imaging and nuclear SED analysis (Sections subsec:galfitm and subsec:second ). Despite the presence of two bright point sources complicating the \ measurements, there is still a significant probability, N_z=0.33 , that the two nuclei reside at the same redshift. In fact, the two nuclei have very similar photo- z values ( =1.003 for CID-252-0 and =1.092 for CID-252-2) that are close to the =1.134 available for CID-252-0.

gmw_00010621.name: Bootes I
gmw_00010621.overview: Bootes I is a bright dwarf spheroidal galaxy that contains a rich population of RR Lyrae stars [arXiv:2312.07279, arXiv:2001.01107]. Several of these stars appear to be extra-tidal, while others in the outskirts may be contaminants from the Sagittarius stream [arXiv:2312.07279, arXiv:2001.01107].

sources.summary: The paper finds 27 RRL around Bootes I, including new outer candidates, but argues that many outskirts candidates may be Sagittarius stream contaminants.

quotes.quote: Bootes I has a large population of RRL with 15 known from and , and an additional one found in Gaia DR2 by vivas20 . Two of those previously known stars seem to be extra-tidal stars. Our search found a total of 27 RRL around Bootes I, including the 16 mentioned RRL already reported. The new stars range in distance between 2.47 and 17.80 R_h from the center of the galaxy.

With the current data we are unable to isolate the true members but we suspect that in this case, most of the stars in the outskirts of Bootes I are indeed Sgr stream stars. It is possible that the 4 new RRL on the northern side of Bootes I (to the other side of Sgr stream) may be true members of the galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper notes Boo I's rich RR Lyrae population, finds one additional Gaia member, and suggests two RR Lyrae lie beyond the tidal radius.

quotes.quote: Boo I is the brightest of the sample of UFDs closer to 100 kpc with M_V=-6.02 . It has a rich population of RRLs. siegel06 detected 15 RRLs in this galaxy see also dallora06 . siegel06 assigned a Oo II classification to this galaxy. The completeness of the Gaia DR2 catalog must be particularly low in this part of the sky since it only recovers two out of the 15 stars previously known. In this galaxy we used a search radius of 2 since this is a large galaxy, with a tidal radius of 38 munoz18 . With this extended area, we were able to detect one additional star which has magnitude and proper motions in agreement with other members (Figure fig:BooI ). This star (named V16 in Table tab:RRL ), as well as the previously known V11, seem to be extra-tidal stars. The new star is a type star, which brings the final census for this galaxy to seven and nine . Using proper motion information from the general Gaia DR2 catalog for the unrecovered RRLs in this galaxy, we confirm that all but one star are proper motion members of Boo I. Star V7 in siegel06 , a type star, seems discrepant, although error bars are large at the magnitude of the HB of this galaxy.

gmw_00010622.name: Tucana II
gmw_00010622.overview: Tucana II is an ultra-faint dwarf spheroidal galaxy located approximately 32 kpc from Earth [arXiv:2001.01107]. It is considered a likely satellite of the Large Magellanic Cloud [arXiv:1804.10230, arXiv:2001.01107] and contains several RR Lyrae stars, including distant members located far from its center [arXiv:2312.07279, arXiv:2001.01107].

sources.summary: Tucana II is discussed through proper-motion prediction tests, likely Magellanic association, M2FS and MIKE spectroscopy, and ten bright confirmed members.

quotes.quote: The results of the pawlowski13 and pawlowski15 predictions are mixed: several satellites are consistent with being co-rotating with the polar structure (Hor I, Ret II, Willman 1, and Tuc II), Segue 1 is counter-rotating, and four other dwarfs (UMa II, Com Ber, Boo II, and Boo I) are outside the expected proper motion ranges. For Tuc II, the opposite is the case: the proper motion in declination is marginally consistent with the prediction, but the proper motion in R.A. is not. Viewed in three dimensions and integrated backward in time, the orbits of Hor I, Hyi I, Car III, and Tuc II track that of the LMC quite closely, suggesting that these objects are probably associated with the Magellanic system. deason15 and sales17 both consider Hor I and Tuc II to be the most likely dwarfs of Magellanic origin, which is consistent with the results reported here.

Tucana II (Tuc II) was observed spectroscopically with Magellan/M2FS by walker16 , who identified 8 member stars. Four of these stars were followed up at higher spectral resolution with Magellan/MIKE to determine their chemical abundances by ji16d . chiti18 observed one additional walker16 member, as well as two new member stars selected with SkyMapper photometry, with Magellan/MIKE, so that chemical abundance patterns are now available for the majority of the known Tuc II members. I adopt a membership probability threshold of p 0.5 to separate members and non-members in the walker16 catalog, but the resulting classifications are not very sensitive to this value (e.g., p 0.1 produces identical lists of stars). The union of these data sets results in a total of 10 member stars and 49 confirmed non-members brighter than g=20 . simon17 obtained medium-resolution Magellan/IMACS spectroscopy of Tucana III (Tuc III), identifying 26 member stars within the core of the satellite. hansen17 studied the chemical abundances of the brightest Tuc III star with a high-resolution Magellan/MIKE spectrum.

sources.summary: The paper argues SMC contamination is weak for Tucana II, reports one new RRL, and finds two distant RRL at roughly 5 and 12 half-light radii.

quotes.quote: In the case of Tucana II, a possible contamination from the SMC is not that clear. Not only the galaxy is farther away from the SMC, at 18 5 , but out of its 4 RRL, 2 of them are located within 1.1 R_h . The most external ones (at 5 and 12 R_h ) are also the brightest ones in the group ( G = 19.07, 18.97 mag), which may be too bright for the SMC. Although confirmation via radial velocities is needed, we believe that the scenario of contamination from SMC is weak in the case of Tucana II.
For Tucana II there were 3 RRL previously known vivas20 and we report one new RRL.

We also found agreement with previous studies of Tucana II in which stars up to 9 R_h were identified in the external parts of the galaxy by Chiti2021 . Here we also found 2 distant RRL in Tucana II, at 5 and 12 R_h .

sources.summary: The paper describes Tucana II as a DES UFD and likely LMC satellite, reporting the first three RR Lyrae members, including one possible extra-tidal star.

quotes.quote: Tucana II (Figure fig:TucII ) htb Tucana II (Tuc II) is a UFD ( M_V = -3.8 mag) detected in DES by two independent groups koposov15,bechtol15 . Its large physical size classifies the system as a dwarf galaxy. It is located close to the LMC (at 19 kpc) and at 32 kpc from us. Due to its proximity to the LMC, it is considered a likely LMC satellite. The color-magnitude diagram of Tuc II (see e.g., Figure 6 in bechtol15 ) reveals some stars located at the HB. We report here for the first time the detection of three RRLs as members of Tuc II, one is a and the other two are . The three stars have mean G magnitudes between 19.0 and 19.5, and are located at 5 , 6 , and 39 . The last one is at 5 r_h . It is not clear if that would make this an extra-tidal star.

gmw_00010623.name: SDSS J1130+0058
gmw_00010623.overview: SDSS J1130+0058 is an obscured active galactic nucleus [arXiv:astro-ph/0702672]. It is the only known active galactic nucleus to exhibit both double-peaked low-ionization broad emission lines and an X-shaped radio structure [arXiv:astro-ph/0702672].

sources.summary: The paper describes SDSS J1130+0058 as an obscured AGN with double-peaked broad lines and X-shaped radio structure, favoring a binary black-hole explanation over backflow.

quotes.quote: In this paper we study the object SDSS J1130+0058 which is the only AGN known to have both double-peaked low-ionization broad emission lines, and also X-shaped radio structures. Emission from an accretion disk can reproduce the double-peaked line profile of broad H , but not the radio structure. Under the accretion disk model, the period of the inner emission line region is about 230 years. Using a new method to subtract the stellar component from the data of the SDSS DR4, we obtain an internal reddening factor which is less than previously found.

The implied smaller amount of dust disfavors the backflow model for the X-shaped radio structure. The presence of a Binary Black Hole (BBH) system is the most natural way to explain both the optical and radio properties of this AGN. Under the assumption of the BBH model, we can estimate the BBH system has a separation of less than 0.04 pc with a period less than 59 years, this may pose some problem to the BLRs sizes, still we conclude that the BBH model is favored on the basis of the present limited information.

gmw_00010624.name: IC 451
gmw_00010624.overview: IC 451 is a galaxy with an asymmetric, one-armed disk structure that may be related to a past tidal interaction [arXiv:1809.05950]. Although located near Mrk 6, its high velocity offset and filament kinematics suggest the two galaxies do not form a gravitationally bound pair [arXiv:1809.05950].

sources.summary: IC 451 is considered as a possible accretion source near Mrk 6, but its velocity offset and filament kinematics argue against a bound pair or recent collision.

quotes.quote: Only one possible source of accretion has appeared in our deep images -- the IC 451 galaxy which is similar in luminosity to Mrk 6 at a projected distance of 98 kpc. Moreover, the galaxy's disc has an asymmetric one-armed structure (the inset in Fig. fig_BAO ) that could be related to a tidal interaction in the past; the Mrk 6 gaseous filaments are located exactly along the line between these galaxies. Can we consider the kinematically decoupled gaseous system in Mrk 6 as the first stage of formation of a gaseous polar ring in agreement with a popular accretion scenario ReshetnikovSotnikova1997,BournaudCombes2003 ?

The IC 451 systemic velocity equal to 5123 53 (according to NED) is 500 smaller than that in Mrk 6; it is too high for a gravitationally bound pair of galaxies. Even if we observe the first passage of IC 451 near Mrk 6, it is difficult to understand how the accreted gas dramatically changed its own angular momentum to reach observed line-of-sight velocities of 6000 that by 700 differs from those of IC 451. The observed difference of the line-of-sight velocities in the system Mrk 6--filaments gas--IC451 also rules out the hypothesis that the elongation of the most extended filaments in the NE direction is caused by a recent collision between the galaxies.

gmw_00010625.name: SDSS J142025.18+533354.9
gmw_00010625.overview: SDSS J142025.18+533354.9 is a galaxy located at a redshift of z = 0.522 [arXiv:1210.0020]. Its spectrum exhibits a relatively red continuum with several absorption features and no detected emission lines, indicating that its radio emission is likely associated with a compact nucleus [arXiv:1210.0020].

sources.summary: The paper associates the RX J1420.4+5334 radio emission with SDSS J142025.18+533354.9, measures a redshift from absorption features, finds no significant emission lines, and treats the compact nucleus as the likely radio source.

quotes.quote: The radio emission of 114 24 \ Jy is associated with the galaxy SDSS J142025.18+533354.9 and is offset from the association with galaxy A identified by 2000A&A...362L..25G (2MASS J14202436+5334117). SDSS J142025.18+533354.9 is a galaxy with a g magnitude of 22.5 and no previously measured redshift.

The resulting spectrum of SDSS J1420 is plotted in Figure fig:sdssj1420 . Super-posed on a relatively red continuum, we identify a number of marginally resolved absorption features corresponding to the Balmer series (H , H , H , H , H , and H ), Ca 2 H+K, Na 1 D, and the G-band. All observed features are consistent with a common redshift of z = 0.522 0.002 . We find no evidence for any significant emission features over the range of our spectrum, which covers rest frame lines including [ O 2 ] , [ O 3 ] , H , and H .

The upper limit on the SFR in RX J1420.4+5334 translates to a radio synchrotron flux density from star formation of < 5 Jy 2009MNRAS.397.1101G . Thus, most of the flux is likely to be associated with a compact nucleus.

gmw_00010626.name: J 1320-1427
gmw_00010626.overview: J 1320-1427 is a dwarf irregular galaxy located in the NGC 5044 Group [arXiv:astro-ph/0405241]. It features a low-surface-brightness halo, neutral hydrogen emission, and blue optical knots that indicate active star formation [arXiv:astro-ph/0405241].

sources.summary: The paper identifies J 1320-1427 as a new NGC 5044 Group member, with H I emission, star-forming optical knots, a low-surface-brightness halo, and irregular-galaxy properties.

quotes.quote: Fig. fig:n5044spectra shows the Parkes spectrum of the previously uncatalogued source of emission, discovered 2.5 North-East of the NGC 5044 Group centre (see Table tab:group_params ), to which we refer as ``J 1320-1427 '' after its refined position. The spectrum was extracted using a spatial box width of 5 pixels and Hanning smoothing over 7 channels; a second-order polynomial was fitted to line-free channels and subtracted to correct the non-zero baseline. The line has a width, at 50 intensity, of W_ 50 =47 . The integrated flux is S d V =4.1 0.1 Jy km s ^ 1 which, assuming optically thin emission and a distance to J 1320-1427 of 33 Mpc (the adopted distance to the NGC 5044 Group), implies a total mass of (1.05 0.03) 10^9 . ATCA imaging of J 1320-1427 reveals emission from a region centred on 13 20 13 , 14 27 32 , with a deconvolved size of 65 125 and a velocity range of 2716--2773 .

Our follow-up B and R band images (see Fig. fig:n5044_opt ) reveal knots of optical emission; two spatially-extended sources straddle the position of maximum, while two further bright regions lie, one south and one west of the main peaks. The two main optical components (labelled 2 and 4 in Fig. fig:n5044_opt ) are very blue, with ( B R ) values of 0.32 and 0.16 magnitudes respectively, suggesting that star formation is occurring within these regions. Applying a filter to remove the high surface-brightness objects in the R band image and convolving the result with an exponential kernel, revealed a very low-surface brightness halo extending north-east of the two main optical components, towards the extended emission.

gmw_00010627.name: J 0249-0806
gmw_00010627.overview: J 0249-0806 is a low-surface-brightness, irregular dwarf galaxy associated with faint diffuse optical emission [arXiv:astro-ph/0405241]. It is a member of the NGC 1052 Group and is spatially and kinematically distinct from the nearby galaxy NGC 1110 [arXiv:astro-ph/0405241].

sources.summary: The paper presents J 0249-0806 as a new NGC 1052 Group member, spatially distinct from NGC 1110, H I-rich, low-surface-brightness, irregular, and possibly on first infall.

quotes.quote: The spectrum taken at this position, of the object hereafter named ``J 0249-0806 '', is shown in Fig. fig:n1052spectra . A box width of 3 pixels and Hanning smoothing over 5 channels were used to produce this spectrum, and a fourth-order polynomial was fitted and subtracted to correct the non-zero baseline. The intensity integrated over velocity of J 0249-0806 is S d V =5.7 0.2 Jy km s ^ 1 , implying an mass of =(5.4 0.1) 10^8 , assuming a distance to the source of 20 Mpc. The angular resolution of the Parkes data was not sufficient to determine the spatial relationship between NGC 1110 and the newly-discovered emission.

We therefore used ATCA to image the region with an improved angular resolution ( 420 50 ), which confirmed the new detection and established this emission as spatially and kinematically distinct from that of NGC 1110. Fig. fig:n1052_m0 shows the distribution of emission from the two galaxies, overlaid on our R band optical image. J 0249-0806 lies 17 arcmin ( 95 kpc) south of the edge-on galaxy NGC 1110, and is associated with faint diffuse optical emission. The is centred on 02 49 14 , 08 06 51 , and has a velocity range 1404--1490 .

gmw_00010628.name: DES J014132.4 542749.9
gmw_00010628.overview: DES J014132.4 542749.9 is a radio-luminous but X-ray weak blazar located at a high redshift of z=5 [arXiv:2301.04533]. High-resolution radio observations reveal a single, compact emitting feature with a flat radio spectrum [arXiv:2301.04533].

sources.summary: The paper confirms DES J014132.4 542749.9 as a z=5 blazar using LBA observations, detecting a compact flat-spectrum radio feature and deriving an accurate radio position.

quotes.quote: DES J014132.4 542749.9 is a radio-luminous but X-ray weak blazar candidate at z=5 . We conducted high-resolution radio interferometric observations of this source with the Australian Long Baseline Array at 1.7 and 8.5 GHz. A single, compact radio emitting feature was detected at both frequencies with a flat radio spectrum.

We derived the milliarcsecond-level accurate position of the object. The frequency dependence of its brightness temperature is similar to that of blazar sources observed at lower redshifts. Based on our observations, we can confirm its blazar nature.

gmw_00010629.overview: The high-redshift galaxy 850.028 is located at a redshift of approximately z=2.5 [arXiv:2210.04437]. It exhibits a disk-like morphology with a Sersic index of 1.23 and an adopted moderate radius of 4.8 kpc [arXiv:2210.04437].

sources.summary: The paper revises 850.028 structural parameters because it lies near a diffraction spike; the catalogue radius was large, so the authors adopt a smaller radius and disk-like index.

quotes.quote: We replaced the structural parameters of the outlier galaxies with those estimated by TesisKarla , who also fitted them with a single S ersic profile using GALFIT , after masking out any companion galaxies or nearby stars, and found reduced- ^2 values similar to those in vdWel2014 .

850.028 at z=2.5^ +0.4 _ 0.1 lies close to the diffraction spike of a field star. The catalogue shows a large radius of R_ e =23 15 kpc and disk-like morphology with index n=1.06 1.22 , and a good-fit flag=0. We adopt the more moderate radius R_ e =4.8 kpc and n=1.23 .

gmw_00010630.overview: The high-redshift galaxy 850.007 is located at a redshift of approximately z=3.0 [arXiv:2210.04437]. Structural analysis of the galaxy indicates a Sersic index of 0.68 and an effective radius of 1.97 kpc [arXiv:2210.04437].

sources.summary: The paper revises 850.007 structural parameters because the catalogue fit had a very large radius, high Sersic index, and bad flag; the authors adopt smaller values.

quotes.quote: We replaced the structural parameters of the outlier galaxies with those estimated by TesisKarla , who also fitted them with a single S ersic profile using GALFIT , after masking out any companion galaxies or nearby stars, and found reduced- ^2 values similar to those in vdWel2014 .
850.007 lies at z=3.0 0.1 and the catalogue includes a very large radius R_ e =31 17 kpc and S ersic index n=8 6 with a bad fitting flag. We adopt R_ e =1.97 kpc and n=0.68 .

gmw_00010631.overview: Located at a redshift of z=1.52, 850.030 is a galaxy system characterized by a perturbed morphology [arXiv:2210.04437]. It features a very close companion galaxy and has a Sersic index of 1.25 [arXiv:2210.04437].

sources.summary: The paper revises 850.030 as a perturbed close-companion system at z=1.52, replacing catalogue morphology with a more conservative radius and Sersic index.

quotes.quote: We replaced the structural parameters of the outlier galaxies with those estimated by TesisKarla , who also fitted them with a single S ersic profile using GALFIT , after masking out any companion galaxies or nearby stars, and found reduced- ^2 values similar to those in vdWel2014 .

850.030 and 850.069 are located at z=1.52 0.06 and z=0.560 0.001 , and have very close companions and perturbed morphologies. Their morphological parameters in the catalogue are R_ e =21.5 0.3 kpc , n=1.10 0.03 and R_ e =11.1 0.3 kpc , n=1.310 0.005 , respectively. We use instead the more conservative values R_ e =14.8 kpc , n=1.25 and R_ e =9.2 kpc , n=0.71 , respectively.

gmw_00010632.overview: Located at a redshift of z=0.560, 850.069 is a galaxy with a perturbed morphology [arXiv:2210.04437]. It exists within a close-companion system and has an estimated effective radius of 9.2 kpc with a Sersic index of 0.71 [arXiv:2210.04437].

sources.summary: The paper revises 850.069 as a perturbed close-companion system at z=0.560, replacing catalogue morphology with a more conservative radius and Sersic index.

quotes.quote: We replaced the structural parameters of the outlier galaxies with those estimated by TesisKarla , who also fitted them with a single S ersic profile using GALFIT , after masking out any companion galaxies or nearby stars, and found reduced- ^2 values similar to those in vdWel2014 .

850.030 and 850.069 are located at z=1.52 0.06 and z=0.560 0.001 , and have very close companions and perturbed morphologies. Their morphological parameters in the catalogue are R_ e =21.5 0.3 kpc , n=1.10 0.03 and R_ e =11.1 0.3 kpc , n=1.310 0.005 , respectively. We use instead the more conservative values R_ e =14.8 kpc , n=1.25 and R_ e =9.2 kpc , n=0.71 , respectively.

gmw_00010633.overview: 850.044 is a high-redshift galaxy located at a redshift of approximately z=3.17 [arXiv:2210.04437]. Structural modeling of the galaxy with a single Sersic profile yields a Sersic index of 0.45 and an effective radius of 2.48 kpc [arXiv:2210.04437].

sources.summary: The paper reports 850.044 at z=3.17 with a bad catalogue fitting flag and adopts revised GALFIT structural parameters for its radius and Sersic index.

quotes.quote: We replaced the structural parameters of the outlier galaxies with those estimated by TesisKarla , who also fitted them with a single S ersic profile using GALFIT , after masking out any companion galaxies or nearby stars, and found reduced- ^2 values similar to those in vdWel2014 .

850.044 ( z=3.17 0.14 ), 850.56 ( z=2.48 0.01 ) and 850.072 ( z=3.36 0.35 ) have bad fitting flags in the catalogue. We adopt R_ e =2.48 kpc , n=0.45 ; R_ e =8.46 kpc , n=0.49 ; and R_ e =1.83 kpc , n=0.32 , for these cases, respectively.

gmw_00010634.overview: The galaxy 850.56 is a high-redshift object located at a redshift of approximately z=2.48 [arXiv:2210.04437]. Structural modeling of the galaxy indicates a Sersic index of 0.49 and an effective radius of 8.46 kiloparsecs [arXiv:2210.04437].

sources.summary: The paper reports 850.56 at z=2.48 with a bad catalogue fitting flag and adopts revised GALFIT structural parameters for its radius and Sersic index.

quotes.quote: We replaced the structural parameters of the outlier galaxies with those estimated by TesisKarla , who also fitted them with a single S ersic profile using GALFIT , after masking out any companion galaxies or nearby stars, and found reduced- ^2 values similar to those in vdWel2014 .

850.044 ( z=3.17 0.14 ), 850.56 ( z=2.48 0.01 ) and 850.072 ( z=3.36 0.35 ) have bad fitting flags in the catalogue. We adopt R_ e =2.48 kpc , n=0.45 ; R_ e =8.46 kpc , n=0.49 ; and R_ e =1.83 kpc , n=0.32 , for these cases, respectively.

gmw_00010635.overview: The high-redshift galaxy 850.072 is located at a redshift of approximately z=3.36 [arXiv:2210.04437]. Structural modeling of the galaxy indicates a Sersic index of 0.32 and an effective radius of 1.83 kiloparsecs [arXiv:2210.04437].

sources.summary: The paper reports 850.072 at z=3.36 with a bad catalogue fitting flag and adopts revised GALFIT structural parameters for its radius and Sersic index.

quotes.quote: We replaced the structural parameters of the outlier galaxies with those estimated by TesisKarla , who also fitted them with a single S ersic profile using GALFIT , after masking out any companion galaxies or nearby stars, and found reduced- ^2 values similar to those in vdWel2014 .

850.044 ( z=3.17 0.14 ), 850.56 ( z=2.48 0.01 ) and 850.072 ( z=3.36 0.35 ) have bad fitting flags in the catalogue. We adopt R_ e =2.48 kpc , n=0.45 ; R_ e =8.46 kpc , n=0.49 ; and R_ e =1.83 kpc , n=0.32 , for these cases, respectively.

gmw_00010636.name: SDSS J1538+5817
gmw_00010636.overview: SDSS J1538+5817 is a luminous elliptical lens galaxy located at a redshift of z = 0.143 [arXiv:0912.0744]. It acts as a gravitational lens for two distinct background sources [arXiv:0912.0744], displaying an Einstein ring and debris from a recent tidal interaction or merger [arXiv:1512.00462].

sources.summary: SDSS J1538+5817 has an Einstein ring, a second doubly imaged source, and debris from a recent tidal interaction or merger, plus a difficult patchy light envelope.

quotes.quote: SDSS J1538+5817 shows an Einstein ring with four distinctly bright areas and a doubly imaged source at different redshifts. Recent tidal interaction, possibly a merger, seems to have left an extended arm of debris with several lumps of higher density. The closer environment ( < 30 arcsec) of the lens is populated with about 10 objects on the line of sight. The next brightest galaxy in the field is only 15 ^ South-East. The isophotes of the lens do not seem to be affected by it.

sources.summary: The paper establishes SDSS J1538+5817 as a rare elliptical lens galaxy at z_l=0.143 and uses lensing plus photometry to characterize its mass distribution and dark halo.

quotes.quote: By means of HST/ACS and WFPC2 imaging and NOT/ALFOSC spectroscopy, we have established that SDSS J1538+5817 is a rare lensing system composed of a luminous elliptical galaxy, located at redshift z_ l =0.143 , that acts as a lens on two distinct and equally-distant ( z_ s =0.531 ) sources. The two sources are lensed into a double and a quad (with an almost complete Einstein ring) system, covering rather large angular and radial scales on the lens plane. This exceptional configuration has allowed us to investigate in great detail the lens total mass distribution within the effective radius of the galaxy, through parametric and non-parametric point-like lensing programs and perform a complete statistical study of the errors and correlations on the lens model parameters. Then, by fitting the lens SED with CSP models, we have estimated the luminous mass of the galaxy and combined the lensing and photometric measurements to examine the characteristics of the galaxy dark-matter halo.

gmw_00010637.name: NGC 6063
gmw_00010637.overview: NGC 6063 is a spiral galaxy that lacks a central bar structure [arXiv:1710.10320]. Its disk features a two-armed logarithmic spiral pattern with a small oxygen abundance enhancement within the arms compared to the interarm regions [arXiv:1710.10320].

sources.summary: The paper maps NGC 6063's ionized-gas velocity, oxygen abundance, and extinction with CALIFA data, derives spiral-arm geometry, finds a small arm abundance enhancement, and measures kinematic parameters.

quotes.quote: We derive maps of the observed velocity of ionized gas, the oxygen abundance, and the extinction (Balmer decrement) across the area of the four spiral galaxies NGC 36, NGC 180, NGC 6063, and NGC 7653 from integral field spectroscopy obtained by the Calar Alto Legacy Integral Field Area (CALIFA) survey. We searched for spiral arms through Fourier analysis of the spatial distribution of three tracers (non-circular motion, enhancement of the oxygen abundance, and of the extinction) in the discs of our target galaxies. The spiral arms (two-armed logarithmic spirals in the deprojected map) are shown in each target galaxy for each tracer considered. The pitch angles of the spiral arms in a given galaxy obtained with the three different tracers are close to each other. The enhancement of the oxygen abundance in the spiral arms as compared to the abundance in the interarm regions at a given galactocentric distance is small; within a few per cent.

We identified a metallicity gradient in our target galaxies. Both barred galaxies in our sample show flatter gradients than the two galaxies without bars. Galactic inclination, position angle of the major axis, and the rotation curve were also obtained for each target galaxy using the Fourier analysis of the two-dimensional velocity map.

gmw_00010638.name: NGC 7653
gmw_00010638.overview: NGC 7653 is a spiral galaxy that lacks a central bar structure [arXiv:1710.10320]. Its disk features two-armed logarithmic spiral arms that exhibit a small oxygen abundance enhancement compared to the surrounding interarm regions [arXiv:1710.10320].

sources.summary: The paper maps NGC 7653's ionized-gas velocity, oxygen abundance, and extinction with CALIFA data, derives spiral-arm geometry, finds a small arm abundance enhancement, and measures kinematic parameters.

quotes.quote: We derive maps of the observed velocity of ionized gas, the oxygen abundance, and the extinction (Balmer decrement) across the area of the four spiral galaxies NGC 36, NGC 180, NGC 6063, and NGC 7653 from integral field spectroscopy obtained by the Calar Alto Legacy Integral Field Area (CALIFA) survey. We searched for spiral arms through Fourier analysis of the spatial distribution of three tracers (non-circular motion, enhancement of the oxygen abundance, and of the extinction) in the discs of our target galaxies. The spiral arms (two-armed logarithmic spirals in the deprojected map) are shown in each target galaxy for each tracer considered. The pitch angles of the spiral arms in a given galaxy obtained with the three different tracers are close to each other. The enhancement of the oxygen abundance in the spiral arms as compared to the abundance in the interarm regions at a given galactocentric distance is small; within a few per cent.

We identified a metallicity gradient in our target galaxies. Both barred galaxies in our sample show flatter gradients than the two galaxies without bars. Galactic inclination, position angle of the major axis, and the rotation curve were also obtained for each target galaxy using the Fourier analysis of the two-dimensional velocity map.

gmw_00010639.name: 3CR 403.1
gmw_00010639.overview: 3CR 403.1 is a nearby elliptical radio galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.055 and hosted in a small galaxy group [arXiv:2207.13711]. Classified as a classical Fanaroff-Riley II radio galaxy, it features two radio lobes extending approximately 0.5 Mpc in the north-west to south-east direction [arXiv:2207.13711].

sources.summary: The paper presents multifrequency SRT, archival radio, X-ray, and optical analysis of FR II radio galaxy 3CR 403.1, measuring lobe spectra, source age, ICM conditions, and Faraday-rotation asymmetry.

quotes.quote: We present multifrequency observations of the radio source 3CR 403.1, a nearby (z=0.055), extended ( 0.5 Mpc) radio galaxy hosted in a small galaxy group. Using new high frequency radio observations from the Sardinia Radio Telescope (SRT), augmented with archival low frequency radio observations, we investigated radio spectral and polarimetric properties of 3CR 403.1. From the MHz-to-GHz spectral analysis, we computed the equipartition magnetic field in the lobes to be B _ eq =2.4 G and the age of the source to be 100 Myr.

3CR 403.1 (a.k.a. 4C 01.51) is a classical FR II radio galaxy 1974MNRAS.167P..31F at z=0.055 . As reported in 1966AuJPh..19..559B the optical counterpart is elliptical, and it has a low-ionization galaxy-like optical spectrum 2010ApJ...725.2426B . H and [ OIII ] 5007 luminosities are in the range between 10^ 39 and 10^ 40 erg s ^ 1 2011A&A...525A..28B , while the radio luminosity at 178 MHz is log _ 10 ( P _ 178 )=32.98 1985PASP...97..932S . In radio archival images, 3CR 403.1 shows two prominent radio lobes, extending in the north-west to south-east direction on a scale of 0.5 Mpc and thus being the second most extended 3C source among the 37 sources at redshift z<0.1 .

gmw_00010640.name: MACS0744 667.0
gmw_00010640.overview: MACS0744 667.0 is a low-mass star-forming galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.997 [arXiv:1909.04295]. The galaxy is gravitationally lensed and magnified by a factor of 1.4 by the foreground galaxy cluster MACS0744 [arXiv:1909.04295].

sources.summary: The paper studies MACS0744 667.0 as a lensed, low-mass star-forming galaxy at z=0.997, deriving emission-line fluxes and improved strong-line metallicity constraints.

quotes.quote: In this contribution we have presented MOSFIRE J band spectroscopy of MACS0744 667.0, a low mass ( log (M_*/M_ )=8.62^ +0.10 _ 0.06 ) star-forming galaxy at redshift z=0.997 magnified 1.4 by CLASH cluster MACS0744 in which we observe detection of 6584 and 6717, 6731 alongside strong detection. Additionally, we derive H , 4959 and 5007 line fluxes from HST/WFC3 G102 grism spectroscopy from the GLASS data release. Access to this set of emission lines is quite unique for a galaxy of this mass at this redshift. We derive metallicity from N2 ( 12+ log ( O / H )=8.11 0.05 with statistical uncertainty; 12+ log ( O / H )=8.11^ +0.16 _ 0.34 including additional systematic uncertainty) as well as N2S2H ( 12+ log ( O / H )=7.99 0.13 ; 12+ log ( O / H )=7.99^ +0.13 _ 0.23 ). While the inclusion of the / ratio in N2S2H increases the measurement uncertainty, we find that even in this case where S/N _ N2S2 is small, the improved mitigation of the dependency on ionization parameter reduces the overall uncertainty on the metallicity measurement.

gmw_00010641.name: NGC 3413
gmw_00010641.overview: NGC 3413 is an isolated S0 galaxy that represents a relatively low-mass host system [arXiv:2303.00763]. It is not a Milky Way analog and has no detected gaseous satellites in its vicinity [arXiv:2303.00763].

sources.summary: NGC 3413 is presented as the least massive host in the sample, an isolated S0 galaxy that is not MW-like and has no detected gaseous satellites.

quotes.quote: NGC 3413 NGC 3413 is a relatively isolated S0 galaxy and the least massive host in our sample. With a derived halo mass M_ Halo 6.3 10^ 10 M_ and rotation velocity V_ rot 90 km s^ 1 (Table table:analog_properties ), it is clearly not a MW analog, but the isolated environment indicates it is not a group member or satellite to larger systems. We consistently find no gaseous satellites in its vicinity across the different satellite selection methods.

gmw_00010642.name: NGC 4310
gmw_00010642.overview: NGC 4310 is a relatively faint, low-mass spiral galaxy [arXiv:2303.00763] classified as intermediate between a lenticular and spiral galaxy [arXiv:2203.00694]. It belongs to the NGC 4274 group [arXiv:2303.00763], featuring a dust lane and well-behaved kinematics with carbon monoxide tracing its bright optical and near-infrared emission [arXiv:2203.00694].

sources.summary: NGC 4310 is described as an intermediate lenticular-spiral with a dust lane, CO tracing the bright optical/NIR extent, well-behaved kinematics, and negligible central residuals.

quotes.quote: NGC 4310 --- This galaxy is classified as intermediate between a lenticular and spiral galaxy. From the optical and 4.5 m data (Figure fig:maps ), there appears to be a dust lane. The CO traces the extent of the bright optical and NIR emission. Kinematically, this is a very well-behaved galaxy, with only a small component in the centre.
In NGC 4310, there are small residuals near the centre. Because these residuals are small and near the centre (which is excluded from the analysis), they have a negligible effect.

sources.summary: NGC 4310 is described as a faint, low-mass spiral in the NGC 4274 group; shared candidates are assigned to the more massive group primary.

quotes.quote: NGC 4310 is a relatively faint spiral galaxy with an HI FWHM W_ 50 =155 km s^ 1 , close to the low-mass end of our host selection limit. We obtained V_ rot =92 km s^ 1 from an updated inclination angle measurement truong_high-resolution_2017 . Being distinctively less massive than the group primary, satellite candidates shared between NGC 4310 and 4274 are consistently assigned to the 4274 based on our group assignment method (equation eqn:dimensionless-separation ).

gmw_00010643.name: SDSSJ1408
gmw_00010643.overview: SDSSJ1408 is a high-redshift weak-line quasar located at a redshift greater than four [arXiv:0904.2181]. It is a radio-detected source with strong X-ray emission and relatively high-temperature dust [arXiv:0904.2181].

sources.summary: The paper observes SDSSJ1408 as a z>4 weak-line quasar with FIRST radio and strong Chandra detections, better SED modeling, and multi-band data testing synchrotron emission.

quotes.quote: Sections sec:ir , sec:optical , and sec:synchrotron then present results from an extensive observational campaign focusing on the WLQs SDSSJ1302, SDSSJ1408, SDSSJ1442, and SDSSJ1532. These data include Spitzer 3.6--24 m mid-infrared (mid-IR) photometry and near-IR photometry in J , H , and K_ s bands that are compared to the spectral energy distributions (SEDs) of typical quasars (Section sec:ir ); multiple epochs of optical spectroscopy and i band photometry (in addition to the available SDSS data) that constrain the line and continuum variability of WLQs (Section sec:optical ); and optical imaging polarimetry along with Very Large Array (VLA) radio continuum observations at L and C bands that test for the presence of synchrotron emission from a relativistic jet (Section sec:synchrotron ).

These sources were selected early on in the survey to be representative of the handful of WLQs known at the time; their emission-line strengths range from undetectable (e.g., SDSSJ1532) to weak, but clearly detected (e.g., SDSSJ1442), and two are FIRST radio sources (SDSSJ1408, R=14 ; SDSSJ1442, R=26 ), while the other two have no radio detections. The FIRST-detected sources also have strong Chandra X-ray detections SDSSJ1408, _ ox =-1.54 ; SDSSJ1442, _ ox =-1.42 shem06 , while SDSSJ1302 and SDSSJ1532 are X-ray weak _ ox =-2.08 and _ ox <-1.90 , respectively; shem06,shem09 . This section presents Spitzer mid-IR photometry in the 3.6--24 m range (Section sec:spitzer ) and near-IR photometry in J , H , and K_ s band (Section sec:nir ), which we use to construct rest-frame 0.1--5 m SEDs of WLQs that can be compared to those of normal quasars (Section sec:seds ).

This model fits the SEDs of the radio-detected quasars better ( ^2/ dof =1.7 for SDSSJ1408 and 1.3 for SDSSJ1442) than it does the SEDs of the radio-undetected quasars ( ^2/ dof =4.7 for SDSSJ1302 and 5.6 for SDSSJ1532). The radio-detected sources are also both bluer and have higher temperature dust, which is consistent with their redder [ 5.8 ] [ 8.0 ] colors.

gmw_00010644.name: SDSSJ1442
gmw_00010644.overview: SDSSJ1442 is a high-redshift weak-line quasar [arXiv:0904.2181]. It is a radio-detected source with strong X-ray emission and a spectral energy distribution characterized by high-temperature dust [arXiv:0904.2181].

sources.summary: The paper observes SDSSJ1442 as a z>4 weak-line quasar with weak but detected lines, FIRST radio and strong Chandra detections, and a well-fit hot-dust SED.

quotes.quote: Sections sec:ir , sec:optical , and sec:synchrotron then present results from an extensive observational campaign focusing on the WLQs SDSSJ1302, SDSSJ1408, SDSSJ1442, and SDSSJ1532. These sources were selected early on in the survey to be representative of the handful of WLQs known at the time; their emission-line strengths range from undetectable (e.g., SDSSJ1532) to weak, but clearly detected (e.g., SDSSJ1442), and two are FIRST radio sources (SDSSJ1408, R=14 ; SDSSJ1442, R=26 ), while the other two have no radio detections. The FIRST-detected sources also have strong Chandra X-ray detections SDSSJ1408, _ ox =-1.54 ; SDSSJ1442, _ ox =-1.42 shem06 , while SDSSJ1302 and SDSSJ1532 are X-ray weak _ ox =-2.08 and _ ox <-1.90 , respectively; shem06,shem09 . This model fits the SEDs of the radio-detected quasars better ( ^2/ dof =1.7 for SDSSJ1408 and 1.3 for SDSSJ1442) than it does the SEDs of the radio-undetected quasars ( ^2/ dof =4.7 for SDSSJ1302 and 5.6 for SDSSJ1532). The radio-detected sources are also both bluer and have higher temperature dust, which is consistent with their redder [ 5.8 ] [ 8.0 ] colors.

gmw_00010645.name: SDSSJ1532
gmw_00010645.overview: SDSSJ1532 is a high-redshift quasar at z = 4.62 that exhibits undetectable or extremely weak emission lines [arXiv:0904.2181, arXiv:astro-ph/9910001]. The object shows no radio emission [arXiv:0904.2181, arXiv:astro-ph/9910001], weak X-ray emission [arXiv:0904.2181], and no detectable optical polarization [arXiv:astro-ph/9910001].

sources.summary: The paper observes SDSSJ1532 as a z>4 weak-line quasar with undetectable emission lines, no radio detection, weak X-rays, and challenging SED modeling.

quotes.quote: Sections sec:ir , sec:optical , and sec:synchrotron then present results from an extensive observational campaign focusing on the WLQs SDSSJ1302, SDSSJ1408, SDSSJ1442, and SDSSJ1532. These data include Spitzer 3.6--24 m mid-infrared (mid-IR) photometry and near-IR photometry in J , H , and K_ s bands that are compared to the spectral energy distributions (SEDs) of typical quasars (Section sec:ir ); multiple epochs of optical spectroscopy and i band photometry (in addition to the available SDSS data) that constrain the line and continuum variability of WLQs (Section sec:optical ); and optical imaging polarimetry along with Very Large Array (VLA) radio continuum observations at L and C bands that test for the presence of synchrotron emission from a relativistic jet (Section sec:synchrotron ).

These sources were selected early on in the survey to be representative of the handful of WLQs known at the time; their emission-line strengths range from undetectable (e.g., SDSSJ1532) to weak, but clearly detected (e.g., SDSSJ1442), and two are FIRST radio sources (SDSSJ1408, R=14 ; SDSSJ1442, R=26 ), while the other two have no radio detections. The FIRST-detected sources also have strong Chandra X-ray detections SDSSJ1408, _ ox =-1.54 ; SDSSJ1442, _ ox =-1.42 shem06 , while SDSSJ1302 and SDSSJ1532 are X-ray weak _ ox =-2.08 and _ ox <-1.90 , respectively; shem06,shem09 . This model fits the SEDs of the radio-detected quasars better ( ^2/ dof =1.7 for SDSSJ1408 and 1.3 for SDSSJ1442) than it does the SEDs of the radio-undetected quasars ( ^2/ dof =4.7 for SDSSJ1302 and 5.6 for SDSSJ1532). The radio-detected sources are also both bluer and have higher temperature dust, which is consistent with their redder [ 5.8 ] [ 8.0 ] colors.

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSSp J153259.96--003944.1 as a z=4.62 high-redshift quasar candidate with no emission lines, no radio detection, no measured optical polarization, and possible BL Lac or unusual unbeamed quasar nature.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we present the discovery of a luminous point source at z = 4.62 , which in contrast to ordinary quasars, shows no emission lines. The object SDSSp J153259.96--003944.1 (which we refer to hereafter as \ for brevity) was selected from SDSS Photometric Run 77 (observed on 27 June 1998) as a high-redshift quasar candidate.

It shows no radio emission (the 3 upper limit of its flux at 6 cm is 60 Jy), indicating an radio-to-optical flux ratio at least as small as that of the radio-weakest known BL Lacs. It is also not linearly polarized in the observed I band to a 3 upper limit of 4 . Therefore, it is either the most distant BL Lac object known to date, with very weak radio emission, or a new type of unbeamed quasar, whose broad emission-line region is very weak or absent.

gmw_00010646.name: S1
gmw_00010646.overview: S1 is a large spiral galaxy that is interacting with its neighbor, S2 [arXiv:1812.08302, arXiv:2011.01438]. It contains the majority of its group's neutral hydrogen gas and is surrounded by faint, blue stellar streams likely caused by a disrupted satellite galaxy [arXiv:1812.08302].

sources.summary: The paper describes J1250 S1 as a large spiral with most of the group HI gas, richer gas content than S2, comparable stellar mass, and blue stellar streams.

quotes.quote: Using the ATCA data we can separate the \ flux in S1 ( \ emission between 7630 and 7860 km s ^ 1 ) and S2 ( \ emission between 7685 and 7880 km s ^ 1 ) galaxies and we find that the HIPASS source is mistakenly assigned to S2 galaxy in the HyperLeda database. We show that the majority of the \ gas appears to be associated with S1 galaxy, moreover the S1 is an rich galaxy whilst S2 is an poor galaxy. The dwarf galaxies S3, S6 and S7 are within the \ envelope associated with S1 galaxy.

Both, S1 and S2 galaxies have comparable stellar masses, while the \ mass of S1 is 7.6 times larger than the \ mass of S2. This is the one of the groups where two galaxies have an equal probability of being the central galaxy (see next Section). The gas-mass fraction of S1 is -0.73 dex and it is within the 2 scatter from the fit to the isolated galaxies while the gas-mass fraction of S2 is -1.42 dex, and it is more than 2 lower from the fit to the isolated galaxies (see Figure fig:gasfraction ). Figure fig:stream shows the DECam g band image which we adaptively smoothed to enhance the faint stellar streams around the galaxies in J1250 (Illustrating the discussion in Section gasstream ). The stellar streams around S1 galaxy (bottom left side in Figure fig:stream ) have a blue colour and they are visible in the H emission (Figure B10 in Sweet2013 ), also S6 and S7 galaxies are detected in GALEX All Sky Survey far ultraviolet and near ultraviolet images. The stellar streams around S1 galaxy are possibly caused by a disrupted satellite galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper describes J1250-20:S1 as one of the two most luminous interacting group galaxies, with comparable stellar mass to S2 but very different H i mass and intergalactic H i nearby.

quotes.quote: HIPASS J1250-20 has seven known members, five of these are spectroscopically confirmed, others are thought to be the group members, as stated in Sweet2013, Dzudzar2019 . We detect the \ emission which we associated with S1 and S2 galaxies. The non-detected galaxies are small compact dwarfs and we show that one galaxy, S4, is an \ deficient galaxy. The likely reason for deficiency in S4 is the tidal interaction with S2, see Figure fig:j1250_multiw . The two most luminous galaxies in J1250-20, S1 and S2 are interacting, see Figure fig:j1250_multiw . These galaxies have a comparable stellar masses however, they have vastly different \ mass.

The \ contours that we associate with S2 galaxies overlap also on top of its smaller companion with which its interacting, S4. In this work we associate \ emission to S2, as S4 is physically smaller, has stellar mass 0.9 dex smaller, and has a lower specific star-formation. The \ mass extracted from the region between the S1 and S2 galaxies is 10 ^ 8.9 M _ . This is a lower limit of the \ mass between galaxies as there is uncertainty where to place galaxy `borders'. The \ velocity of the extracted region corresponds to the recessional group velocity (in velocity space between S1 and S2), and in this region we do not detect faint stellar streams.

gmw_00010647.name: S2
gmw_00010647.overview: S2 is a spiral galaxy that is interacting with its neighbor S1 [arXiv:1812.08302, arXiv:2011.01438]. It also exhibits tidal interactions with the smaller companion galaxy S4, which are associated with red stellar streams [arXiv:1812.08302, arXiv:2011.01438] and a low gas-mass fraction [arXiv:1812.08302].

sources.summary: The paper describes J1250 S2 as a large spiral interacting with S1, with much less HI than S1, low gas fraction, and red streams.

quotes.quote: The lowest shown \ column density, the white contour, is a 3 detection and the \ distribution reveals a possible interaction between S1 and S2 galaxy; we find similar interaction through \ distribution in the HIPASSJ1408-21 group. We must note that because of the large elongation of the synthesized beam (as the source is at 20 degrees in Declination), this could be a result of the beam smearing. We find no evidence of interaction between S1 and S2 galaxy in the optical or H imaging at the sensitivity with which they were imaged (while we do find interaction in optical and H in the HIPASSJ1059-09 and HIPASSJ1026-19 group).

A small \ component associated with S2 galaxy coincides also with S4 dwarf galaxy (top right part in Figure fig:groupHI ). Galaxy marked S5 is not detected within the ATCA primary beam. We do not have spectra of S3, S4, S5 and S6 galaxies however they are assumed to be the group members as they are detected in SINGG H imaging. This imaging used filters with a bandwidth of 30 \ which corresponds to 3000 km s ^ 1 Meurer2006, Sweet2013 . Both, S1 and S2 galaxies have comparable stellar masses, while the \ mass of S1 is 7.6 times larger than the \ mass of S2. This is the one of the groups where two galaxies have an equal probability of being the central galaxy (see next Section). The gas-mass fraction of S1 is -0.73 dex and it is within the 2 scatter from the fit to the isolated galaxies while the gas-mass fraction of S2 is -1.42 dex, and it is more than 2 lower from the fit to the isolated galaxies (see Figure fig:gasfraction ). Figure fig:stream shows the DECam g band image which we adaptively smoothed to enhance the faint stellar streams around the galaxies in J1250 (Illustrating the discussion in Section gasstream ). The stellar streams around S1 galaxy (bottom left side in Figure fig:stream ) have a blue colour and they are visible in the H emission (Figure B10 in Sweet2013 ), also S6 and S7 galaxies are detected in GALEX All Sky Survey far ultraviolet and near ultraviolet images. The stellar streams around S1 galaxy are possibly caused by a disrupted satellite galaxy. The streams around S2 galaxy (top right side in Figure fig:stream ) have a red colour, they do not show emission in the H imaging and they are possibly caused by a tidal interaction with S4 galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper associates H i emission with J1250-20:S2, describes its interaction with S1 and S4, and notes H i between S1 and S2 at the group velocity.

quotes.quote: HIPASS J1250-20 has seven known members, five of these are spectroscopically confirmed, others are thought to be the group members, as stated in Sweet2013, Dzudzar2019 .
