quotes.quote: AGC 111946: this galaxy shows a low surface brightness disk-like light distribution, elongated in an approximately North-South direction. It has F(H )= 3.33 10^ 15 erg/cm ^ 2 /s. No specific HII regions were detected, though the nH image shows a faint and elongated area of enhanced brightness. The region has F(H )=1.54 10^ 15 erg/cm ^ 2 /s, half the total line flux from this galaxy. The first SF burst took place 1.5 Gyr ago, contributing 50 10 of the present luminosity.

gmw_00010755.name: AGC 122834
gmw_00010755.overview: AGC 122834 is a tiny dwarf irregular galaxy and a candidate member of the NGC 784 group [arXiv:0808.1789]. It exhibits an oval, ring-like outer structure, weak H-alpha emission, and starlight dominated by an ancient starburst [arXiv:0808.1789].

sources.summary: The paper describes AGC 122834 as a tiny dwarf irregular candidate member of the NGC 784 group, with weak H emission and mostly old modeled starlight.

quotes.quote: AGC 122834: this dIrr galaxy has been measured by Saintonge et al. (2008) and, despite its very small recession velocity ( 50 km sec ^ 1 ), it is potentially associated with the NGC 784 group at an average recession velocity of 180 km sec ^ 1 . The optical image of this galaxy is very small ( 15 arcsec) and shows an oval ring-like outer structure. The colours we measured are weird and the galaxy has a weak H flux ( 10 ^ 16 erg/cm ^ 2 /s). The 10 model fits indicate a single SF burst more than 10 Gyr ago that accounts for at least 99 of the measured light.

gmw_00010756.name: UGC 1561
gmw_00010756.overview: UGC 1561 is an isolated dwarf irregular galaxy that exhibits a strong H-alpha flux [arXiv:0808.1789]. The galaxy features four bright H II regions arranged in a parallelogram, alongside faint condensations within its disk and a recent star-formation burst [arXiv:0808.1789].

sources.summary: The paper describes UGC 1561 as an isolated dwarf irregular with strong H flux, four bright HII regions, disk condensations, and a recent star-formation burst.

quotes.quote: UGC 1561 is located south of the two groups of galaxies, at 24 ^ . It is 2.4 Mpc away from the NGC 672 group and 5.7 Mpc from the NGC 784 group in 3D space and assuming only Hubble expansion. It was classified as an isolated dIrr (Braun Burton 2001) and shows a strong H flux. U1561 has four bright HII regions arranged at the corners of a parallelogram.

The blue image shows a number of other faint condensations within the disk. The models indicate a first SF burst more than 10 Gyr ago contributing 41 10 of the luminosity and a second burst only 8 Myr ago.

gmw_00010757.name: LEDA 169957
gmw_00010757.overview: LEDA 169957 is a low-mass dwarf irregular galaxy that features a disky light distribution with a brighter central condensation [arXiv:0808.1789]. The galaxy shows a hint of a faint northern spiral arm and has experienced recent star formation [arXiv:0808.1789].

sources.summary: The paper describes LEDA 169957 as a low-mass dwarf irregular with a disk, central condensation, possible spiral arm, and recent star formation.

quotes.quote: LEDA 169957 was first measured in the ``Arecibo Slice'' survey (Spitzak Schneider 1998) and was later classified as a low mass dIrr (see Braun Burton 2001). It shows a disky light distribution with a brighter central condensation and a hint of faint spiral arm in its Northern side. Being four degrees to the south-west of the NGC 672/IC 1727 group and 150 km sec ^ 1 higher in velocity, implies that its distance from the NGC 672 group is 1.8 Mpc assuming only Hubble expansion. It has not been associated with this group in the past, and we assumed it is possibly isolated, but it does line up well with the linear relation shown in Figure fig:gals_X_vs_Vel .

gmw_00010758.name: SDSS J171359.00+333625.5
gmw_00010758.overview: SDSS J171359.00+333625.5 is an edge-on Seyfert 2 galaxy that hosts an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1910.05464]. Its gas and stellar components are counter-rotating, and it exhibits X-shaped, biconical outflows of ionized gas [arXiv:1910.05464].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J171359.00+333625.5 as an edge-on Seyfert 2 with counter-rotating gas and stars, AGN-ionized biconical outflows, and unusual oxygen kinematics.

quotes.quote: Using the integral field unit (IFU) data from Mapping Nearby Galaxies at Apache Point Observatory (MaNGA) survey, we study the kinematics of gas and stellar components in an edge-on Seyfert 2 galaxy, SDSS J171359.00+333625.5, with X-shaped bi-conical outflows. The gas and stars therein are found to be counter-rotating, indicating that the collision between the inner and external gas might be an effective way to dissipate the angular momentum, which leads to remarkable gas accretion into the galaxy center. Large 5007 equivalent width and AGN-like line ratio in the large bi-conical region suggest that the gas is ionized by the central AGN. The gas velocity in the bi-cone region shows that ionized gas is receding relative to the galaxy center, which could be the joint effect of inflows, outflows and disk rotation.

gmw_00010759.name: NGC 4567
gmw_00010759.overview: NGC 4567 is a spiral galaxy in the Virgo cluster that forms a gravitationally interacting pair with NGC 4568 [arXiv:1304.1279, arXiv:2307.09154]. The galaxy is overall radio-dim [arXiv:2307.09154] and hosts active star formation along its spiral arms [arXiv:2201.02270].

sources.summary: The paper discusses NGC 4567 in the overlapping NGC 4567/68 pair, noting matched H I velocities, mild outer disturbance, detected disk emission, and a symmetric spiral magnetic field.

quotes.quote: NGC 4567/68 NGC 4567/68 is a close, overlapping pair of Sc galaxies in the Virgo cluster. Their H i line-of-sight velocities match where the galaxies overlap (Chung et al. 2009). Optically, neither shows significant disturbances in their inner disks, while both look mildly disturbed in their outer part. We detect radio continuum emission from both disks at 20 and 6 cm with a normal spectral index. Polarized emission at 20 cm is only detected along the minor axis of NGC 4567 east of the galaxy center.

sources.summary: The paper reports NGC 4567 has active star formation along its spiral arms, higher SFE in those arms, and high disk SFE not mainly triggered by ram pressure.

quotes.quote: The active star formation of NGC 4567 (top-right) takes place along its spiral arms.
The spiral arms in NGC 4567 show higher SFEs.
We conclude that ram pressure is not the main trigger of high SFE in the disc of NGC 4567.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 4567 as an overall radio-dim, gravitationally interacting disk with shallow spectral-index behavior in radio-dim regions.

quotes.quote: Three galaxies are overall radio-dim: NGC 4298 (Fig. fig:rcfir_spixx1c1_nice_1 ), NGC 4535, and NGC 4567 (Fig. fig:rcfir_spixx1c1_nice_4 ).

The SI distributions in the radio-dim regions of the overall radio-dim galaxies show different behaviors: whereas these regions show a steep SI in NGC 4298, they have shallow SI in NGC 4567 and a broad range of SI in NGC 4535.
The correlation strength is not linked to perturbations: M 51 and NGC 4567/68 are gravitationally interacting systems with very different SFR--PI Spearman correlation coefficients.

gmw_00010760.name: NGC 4568
gmw_00010760.overview: NGC 4568 is an Sc spiral galaxy in the Virgo cluster that forms an interacting pair with NGC 4567 [arXiv:1304.1279, arXiv:2201.02270]. It exhibits an asymmetric neutral hydrogen distribution from this ongoing tidal interaction [arXiv:1304.1279], while its most intense star-forming activity is concentrated at its center [arXiv:2201.02270].

sources.summary: The paper discusses NGC 4568 in the NGC 4567/68 pair, identifying asymmetric H I from tidal interaction, detected radio emission, and highly asymmetric polarized 6 cm emission.

quotes.quote: NGC 4567/68 NGC 4567/68 is a close, overlapping pair of Sc galaxies in the Virgo cluster. Their H i line-of-sight velocities match where the galaxies overlap (Chung et al. 2009). Optically, neither shows significant disturbances in their inner disks, while both look mildly disturbed in their outer part. The asymmetric H i distribution of NGC 4568 with a maximum of H i surface density to the west and a tail to the north are signs of an ongoing tidal interaction (Chung et al. 2009). We detect radio continuum emission from both disks at 20 and 6 cm with a normal spectral index.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4568 as the VV 219 component with centrally concentrated star formation, high central SFE, and mass-ratio context relative to NGC 4567.

quotes.quote: VV 219 As illustrated in figure fig_SFR (b), the most intense star-forming activity in NGC 4568 (bottom-left) is at its centre, and SFR gradually decreases as the radius increases.
Another region with high SFEs is seen near the centre of NGC 4568, as often seen in spiral galaxies.

Although the dynamical mass ratios of NGC 5395 and NGC 5394, and NGC 4568 and NGC 4567 are estimated as 100 and 10, respectively, according to Iono05 , they did not correct the inclination in deriving the dynamical mass.

gmw_00010761.name: NGC 4689
gmw_00010761.overview: NGC 4689 is a small, face-on spiral galaxy [arXiv:1304.1279]. It features a mildly truncated neutral hydrogen disk with a moderate hydrogen deficiency, as well as a symmetric, low-surface-brightness radio continuum disk [arXiv:1304.1279].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4689 as a small face-on spiral with mildly truncated H I, moderate H I deficiency, symmetric low-surface-brightness radio continuum, and intermediate polarization.

quotes.quote: NGC 4689 This small face-on spiral has a mildly truncated H i disk to within the stellar disk, with a moderate H i deficiency (Chung et al. 2009). NGC 4689 has a symmetric radio continuum disk at 20 and 6 cm with a low surface brightness ( 200 Jy/beam at 6 cm). The spectral index is shallower in starforming regions of the inner disk than in the outer disk. Polarized emission is marginally detected in the galactic disk at 20 and 6 cm. The degree of polarization is intermediate ( 10 ).

gmw_00010762.name: NGC 4808
gmw_00010762.overview: NGC 4808 is a highly inclined galaxy characterized by an extended neutral hydrogen envelope [arXiv:1304.1279]. It features strong, patchy, and asymmetric star formation throughout its stellar disk, as well as a vertically extended radio halo [arXiv:1304.1279].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4808 as an extended-H I-envelope galaxy with strong patchy star formation, a vertically extended radio halo, asymmetric 6 cm polarization, and perpendicular magnetic fields.

quotes.quote: NGC 4808 The third galaxy with an extended H i envelope is NGC 4808. It shares many H i properties with NGC 4713. There is strong star formation throughout the stellar disk, with an asymmetric and patchy distribution. Its radio continuum properties are comparable to those of NGC 4532. This highly inclined galaxy has a symmetric radio continuum disk at 20 and 6 cm with a vertically extended radio halo.

gmw_00010763.name: J1601+3113
gmw_00010763.overview: J1601+3113 is a nearby low-metallicity dwarf galaxy with a disk-like morphology and a stellar mass of approximately 10^8.56 solar masses [arXiv:2104.05689]. Although its optical spectrum suggests a normal star-forming galaxy, mid-infrared colors and coronal line emission reveal a hidden active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2104.05689].

sources.summary: The paper identifies J1601+3113 as a nearby low-metallicity dwarf galaxy whose infrared colors and [Si VI] coronal line reveal an AGN hidden from optical diagnostics.

quotes.quote: J160135.95+311353.7 (hereafter J1601+3113) is a nearby ( z=0.031 ) galaxy, with no broad lines, and optically normal line ratios in the star-forming region of the BPT diagram (Figure bpt ). Based on the Max Planck Institute f ur Astrophysik/Johns Hopkins University (MPA/JHU) catalog http://www.mpa-garching.mpg.de/SDSS/ of derived properties for the Sloan Digital Sky Survey (SDSS) data release 8, it has a galaxy mass of 10^ 8.56 M _ , roughly an order of magnitude lower mass than the LMC. The NASA-Sloan Atlas catalog lists a index of 1.87 , implying a disk-like morphology, as expected based on its low metallicity izotov2012 . While a detailed analysis of J1601+3113's metallicity is given in section 3.5, its log( [ N 2 ] /H ) of -1.17 suggests a sub-Solar metallicity groves2006 . While there is no evidence for an AGN in J1601+3113 based on its optical spectrum, its mid-infrared colors are suggestive of an AGN hainline2016 , using data from the Wide-field Infrared Survey Explorer (WISE) and the 3-band color cut from jarrett2011 (Figure jarrett ).

This, combined with its BPT star-forming designation, makes the AGN candidate in J1601+3113 invisible through traditional optical diagnostics. As J1601+3113 is an optically normal, seemingly star-forming galaxy, that only shows its AGN nature in near- and mid-infrared diagnostics, the precise radiation field, and therefore, metallicity estimate holds a great deal of uncertainty, with estimates ranging from 5-15 depending on the method used.

gmw_00010764.name: VGS 50
gmw_00010764.overview: VGS 50 is a galaxy featuring an extremely extended, edge-on neutral hydrogen disk with a physical extent of 32 kiloparsecs [arXiv:1204.5185]. This gas disk exhibits fairly regular kinematics with no indication of a warp, though its surface density profile shows evidence of a central hole [arXiv:1204.5185].

sources.summary: The paper identifies VGS 50 as having an extremely extended, edge-on H I disk with regular kinematics, large physical extent, and evidence for a central gas hole.

quotes.quote: VGS 50 is identified as having an extremely extended H i disk, with _ H i / _ 90 5 , though it appears that the _ 90 optical radius includes only the brighter, redder inner disk and excludes the faint blue, more extended disk observed in the combined color image. With an edge-on gas disk and fairly regular kinematics with no indication of a warp, its 32 kpc physical extent is one of the largest in this sample. The H i surface density profile shows some evidence for a central hole in the gas distribution.

gmw_00010765.name: VGS 51
gmw_00010765.overview: VGS 51 is a disk galaxy located deep within a cosmic void [arXiv:1204.5185]. It features a regular neutral hydrogen disk that may be slightly offset from its stellar disk, numerous blue star-forming knots, and a very faint dwarf companion [arXiv:1204.5185].

sources.summary: The paper says VGS 51 lies deepest in the void, has blue star-forming knots, a regular but slightly offset H I disk, and a faint dwarf companion.

quotes.quote: VGS 51 is located the deepest within the void, being at a density of less than a tenth the mean. Optically, it presents a disk morphology with many distinctive blue knots presumably related to areas of high star formation, and the galaxy as a whole does have a relatively high specific star formation rate. The HI distribution appears quite regular both morphologically and kinematically, though the gas disk may be slightly offset from the center of the stellar disk. This is difficult to judge as the optical disk center may be confused by the irregular morphology. It has a very faint (M _r = -14) dwarf companion at nearly coincident velocity and a sky separation of 133 kpc.

gmw_00010766.name: G1
gmw_00010766.overview: G1 is a spiral galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.48382 [arXiv:2106.12927]. It features a compact bulge, a perturbed morphology, and a single long spiral arm [arXiv:2106.12927].

sources.summary: The paper identifies G1 as a spiral galaxy near the absorber and uses its morphology, impact parameter, and CGM modeling to interpret the gas origin.

quotes.quote: There exists a galaxy (G1) associated with the absorption system that was first spectroscopically identified by chen+98 . Its properties were later refined by several authors kacprzak+10a,nielsen+13a,kacprzak+15 . The galaxy's most recently determined properties are listed in Table tab:galaxy_properties . To summarize, G1 is a spiral galaxy at z=0.48382 with one long spiral arm, a compact bulge, and a perturbed morphology. The galaxy was modeled using GIM2D Galaxy IMage 2D, simard+02 , which fits the galaxy with a two-component model of a S e rsic bulge and an exponential disc.

We have used our detailed modeling of the absorption lines and the detailed observations of the host galaxy to determine the sources of the CGM. The low- and high-ionization phases can be explained mostly via a cold-flow disc, with the absorption system lying high above the plane of the galaxy G1. The high metallicity of the low-ionization phase indicates that it is accreting material, most likely material that has been expelled and re-accreted multiple times.

gmw_00010767.name: 3XMM J005450.3--373849
gmw_00010767.overview: 3XMM J005450.3--373849 is a background Seyfert-2 active galactic nucleus located at a redshift of z=0.222 [arXiv:1604.07431]. It is associated with a spiral galaxy that exhibits a complex structure with a conspicuous outer pseudo-ring and displays long-term X-ray variability [arXiv:1604.07431].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 3XMM J005450.3--373849 as a background Seyfert-2 AGN galaxy with an extended ringed optical counterpart, redshift z=0.222, long-term X-ray variability, and Seyfert-like luminosity.

quotes.quote: The information gathered in the previous sections from the optical and X-ray analysis shows that 3XMM J005450.3 373849 poses typical characteristics of a Seyfert-2 AGN galaxy, located at z=0.222 0.001 behind NGC 300, as demonstrates the optical spectroscopic observations that we obtained with Gemini. The X-ray source is positionally coincident with the central region of an extended optical source detected with HST showing a complex structure, that clearly matches the morphology of a R '_1 type spiral galaxy with a conspicuous outer pseudo-rings structure. The positional correlation strongly suggests a physical association between both sources.

The X-ray spectra of the source are well-fitted by an absorbed power-law model. By tying N_ H between the six available spectra, we found a variable index running from 2 in 2000-2001 years, to 1.4--1.6 in the 2005-2014 period. Alternatively, by tying , we found variable absorption columns of N_ H 0.34 10^ 22  cm ^ 2 in 2000-2001 years, and 0.54-0.75 10^ 22  cm ^ 2 in the 2005-2014 period. Although we cannot distinguish between an spectral or absorption origin, from the derived unabsorbed X-ray fluxes, we are able to assure the presence of long-term X-ray variability. Furthermore, the unabsorbed X-ray luminosities of 0.8-2 10 ^ 43  erg s ^ 1 derived in the X-ray band are in agreement with a weakly obscured Seyfert-2 AGN at z 0.22 .

gmw_00010768.name: NGC 4389
gmw_00010768.overview: NGC 4389 is a barred spiral galaxy located in the Ursa Major cluster [arXiv:1605.01638]. Its neutral hydrogen and ionized gas distributions, as well as its slowly rising rotation curve, indicate that its morphology and kinematics are dominated by its bar [arXiv:1605.01638].

sources.summary: The paper reports that NGC 4389 is an Ursa Major SBbc galaxy whose H and HI morphology and slowly rising rotation curve are dominated by its bar.

quotes.quote: NGC 4389 NGC 4389 is an SBbc galaxy in the Ursa Major cluster. The distribution of the H emission and of the HI from new VLA observations shown in Fig.  fig:n4389sum suggests that the morphology is dominated by a bar. The rotation curves derived from the ionized and neutral gas suggest that the kinematics are also dominated by this bar (Fig.  fig:rc ). came to the same conclusion using WSRT observations of the HI. The observations presented here trace the neutral gas out to a larger radius than , but show a similar slowly rising rotation curve which only reaches maximum rotational velocities near 100 km s ^ 1 .

gmw_00010769.name: NGC 5608
gmw_00010769.overview: NGC 5608 is a dwarf irregular galaxy with a smooth, featureless neutral hydrogen disk that closely matches its stellar disk [arXiv:1605.01638]. The distribution of this neutral hydrogen gas is slightly asymmetric, with the receding eastern side extending farther than the approaching western side [arXiv:1605.01638].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 5608 as a dwarf irregular with smooth HI matching the stellar disc, asymmetric HI extent, a flat outer rotation curve, and dark-matter-dominated decomposition.

quotes.quote: NGC 5608 NGC 5608 is a dwarf irregular galaxy for which we acquired new VLA HI observations. The new data reveal an HI disc with a smooth, featureless morphology similar to the stellar disc at 3.6 m (Fig.  fig:n5608sum ). The highest density of HI gas additionally appears to coincide with location of the strongest H observed in the narrowband image. NGC 5608 was not targeted for ionized gas kinematics from SparsePak observations due to the limited spatial extent of the H emission. The distribution of HI is slightly asymmetric, which can best be seen in the velocity field where the receding (east) side of the galaxy appears to extend farther than the approaching (west) side.

gmw_00010770.name: BCG
gmw_00010770.overview: The brightest cluster galaxy (BCG) is a cD galaxy located near the core of the galaxy cluster RX J1347.5-1145 [arXiv:1812.01034, arXiv:0711.4850]. It is positioned near the peak of the cluster's X-ray surface brightness, where the dark matter and baryons are centered [arXiv:1812.01034, arXiv:0711.4850].

sources.summary: The paper defines wBCG as the BCG coincident with the cluster core, places it near the X-ray peak, and finds no strong SZ excess around it, favoring cool-core sloshing.

quotes.quote: This is also consistent with the results of the weak- and strong-lensing analyses of optical data Bradac2008,Koehlinger2014,Zitrin2015,Ueda2018 , which show that the projected mass density has a primary peak centred near the AGN embedded in the cool core, and an additional component elongated towards a secondary peak at or near the brightest cluster galaxy (BCG) to the east of the X-ray peak (hereafter `eBCG' to distinguish it from that coincident with the cluster core, which we refer to as `wBCG'; see the top panel of Fig. fig:figure01 , which includes the lensing contours from Zitrin2015 ). Furthermore, optical spectroscopic analysis constrains the dynamics of the merger to take place mainly in the plane of the sky Miranda2008,Lu2010 . This is corroborated by the small difference in the redshifts of the two dominant BCGs, measured to be of the order of 100 km s^ 1 Cohen2002 .

The numerous analyses of the X-ray emission from RX J1347.5--1145 have shown that the peak of the X-ray surface brightness is located at the position of the cool-core region, only a few kiloparsecs away from the wBCG. In the major merger scenario introduced before, this has been generally considered as the centre of the primary cluster component. Therefore, we first apply our fitting pipeline jointly to the ACA+ALMA, Bolocam, and Planck data for modelling the thermal SZ signal from the region corresponding to the cool core observed as a strong cusp in the X-ray surface brightness maps of RX J1347.5--1145. In particular, we fit a spherically symmetric gNW pressure profile whose centroid is set at the position of the peak in the observed X-ray brightness distribution, ( 13^h 47^m 30 593,-11^d 45^m 10 050 ) .

Conversely, it is not possible to observe any signature of strong local deviations from the smooth gas pressure distribution within the cool core of the cluster. Indeed, no excess in the model-subtracted ACA+ALMA maps of the SZ signal is significantly detected in the direction of the region around the wBCG. In agreement with Ueda2018 , this suggests the occurrence of subsonic sloshing motions within the cool core.

sources.summary: The paper defines the BCG as the brighter western cD galaxy and uses it as the center aligned with the cluster dark matter, baryons, and main mass component.

quotes.quote: Further evidence for a merger scenario comes also from the optical morphology: the cluster contains two cD galaxies (see e.g. Fig. fig:arcs throughout this paper we will refer to the brighter, western one as the BCG, and the other as the second cD).
We find that the dark matter and baryons are both centered on the BCG within the uncertainties (alignment is better than <10 kpc ).
We clearly detect the main cluster component, which is aligned with the BCG. The offset from the BCG is (-2 2 ,-1 3 ) and is also consistent with the peak of the X-ray surface brightness.

gmw_00010771.name: J0455 4456
gmw_00010771.overview: J0455 4456 is classified as a triple quasar candidate [arXiv:2108.01672]. The system exhibits an irregular tidal feature indicative of a merger, and its three sources display colors consistent with quasars [arXiv:2108.01672].

sources.summary: The paper classifies J0455 4456 as a triple quasar candidate, citing an irregular tidal feature and quasar-like colors for its three sources.

quotes.quote: In addition to the classifications defined above, we also add ``dual quasar'', which include the two confirmed dual quasars (J0749+2255 and J0841+4825) from Shen2021 and a dual quasar candidate (J0459 0714) showing a tidal feature, ``triple quasar'', which includes a triple quasar candidate (J0455 4456) showing a tidal feature, and ``quad lens", which includes a quadruply lensed quasar (J2218 3322) with the Einstein ring and the central lens galaxy.
J0455 4456 is classified as a triple quasar candidate, which shows an irregular tidal feature in the images and the F475W F814W colors of its three sources are consistent with quasar color.

Our \ optical imaging is too shallow and inefficient to rule in/out high-redshift lens in individual systems, except in rare cases where a potential lens is detected (e.g. J2218 3322) or a merger feature is seen (e.g. J0455 4456 and J0459 0714), supporting the dual quasar classification.

gmw_00010772.name: J0459 0714
gmw_00010772.overview: J0459 0714 is classified as a likely dual quasar [arXiv:2108.01672]. The system exhibits a visible tidal feature, which provides morphological support for this dual quasar classification [arXiv:2108.01672].

sources.summary: The paper classifies J0459 0714 as a likely dual quasar, using its tidal feature as morphological support for the dual quasar interpretation.

quotes.quote: In addition to the classifications defined above, we also add ``dual quasar'', which include the two confirmed dual quasars (J0749+2255 and J0841+4825) from Shen2021 and a dual quasar candidate (J0459 0714) showing a tidal feature, ``triple quasar'', which includes a triple quasar candidate (J0455 4456) showing a tidal feature, and ``quad lens", which includes a quadruply lensed quasar (J2218 3322) with the Einstein ring and the central lens galaxy.
Similar tidal features are seen in J0459 0714, which is likely a dual quasar.

Our \ optical imaging is too shallow and inefficient to rule in/out high-redshift lens in individual systems, except in rare cases where a potential lens is detected (e.g. J2218 3322) or a merger feature is seen (e.g. J0455 4456 and J0459 0714), supporting the dual quasar classification.

gmw_00010773.name: J2218 3322
gmw_00010773.overview: J2218 3322 is a quadruply lensed quasar system that features a central lens galaxy [arXiv:2108.01672]. The system displays a weak Einstein ring and is characterized by a very small lens separation [arXiv:2108.01672].

sources.summary: The paper identifies J2218 3322 as a quadruply lensed quasar with a central lens galaxy, weak Einstein ring, and very small lens separation.

quotes.quote: In addition to the classifications defined above, we also add ``dual quasar'', which include the two confirmed dual quasars (J0749+2255 and J0841+4825) from Shen2021 and a dual quasar candidate (J0459 0714) showing a tidal feature, ``triple quasar'', which includes a triple quasar candidate (J0455 4456) showing a tidal feature, and ``quad lens", which includes a quadruply lensed quasar (J2218 3322) with the Einstein ring and the central lens galaxy.
On the other hand, a lens galaxy and a weak Einstein ring in J2218 3322 confirm the lensing scenario. J2218 3322 is among the smallest-separation quadruply lensed quasars Blackburne2008 .

Our \ optical imaging is too shallow and inefficient to rule in/out high-redshift lens in individual systems, except in rare cases where a potential lens is detected (e.g. J2218 3322) or a merger feature is seen (e.g. J0455 4456 and J0459 0714), supporting the dual quasar classification.

gmw_00010774.name: SDSS J084205.57+075925.5
gmw_00010774.overview: SDSS J084205.57+075925.5 is an active galactic nucleus characterized by a double-peaked broad line spectrum [arXiv:0807.1787]. This spectrum uniquely exhibits a third peak, which can be modeled using a prograde warped-disc profile [arXiv:0807.1787].

sources.summary: The paper models SDSS J084205.57+075925.5 as a double-peaked AGN whose broad Balmer spectrum has a third peak, using a prograde warped-disc profile with specified inclination and warp parameters.

quotes.quote: The observed broad line spectrum of sources SDSS J084205.57+075925.5, SDSS J232721.96+152437.3 both have a third peak. The line profiles (red line) calculated by our code compared with observations are shown in Figs f327 and f409 . The disc zone is from R_ in =250r_ g to R_ out = 1000 r_ g , and n_2=3.0 for a prograde disc case. The other parameters are: i=13^ , n_1=3.0 , n_3 = 28^ , _0 = 95^ for SDSS J084205.57+075925.5, i = 10^ , n_1=2.6 , n_3 = 22^ , _0 = 88^ for SDSS J232721.96+152437.3, respectively.

gmw_00010775.name: SDSS J232721.96+152437.3
gmw_00010775.overview: SDSS J232721.96+152437.3 is an active galactic nucleus with a spectrum dominated by starlight from its host galaxy [arXiv:0807.1787]. The object exhibits a double-peaked profile with a third Balmer peak, which can be modeled by a prograde warped disc [arXiv:0807.1787].

sources.summary: The paper describes SDSS J232721.96+152437.3 as a double-peaked AGN with host-galaxy starlight contamination and a third Balmer peak fit qualitatively by a prograde warped-disc model.

quotes.quote: The SDSS spectrum of one object (SDSS J232721.96+152437.3) is dominated by starlight from the host galaxy; we subtract the starlight and the nuclear continuum with the method as described in detail in Zhou et al. (2006); The other three spectra have little starlight contamination, the nuclear continuum and the Fe II emission multiplets are modelled as described in detail in Dong et al. (2008).

The observed broad line spectrum of sources SDSS J084205.57+075925.5, SDSS J232721.96+152437.3 both have a third peak. The line profiles (red line) calculated by our code compared with observations are shown in Figs f327 and f409 . The disc zone is from R_ in =250r_ g to R_ out = 1000 r_ g , and n_2=3.0 for a prograde disc case. The other parameters are: i=13^ , n_1=3.0 , n_3 = 28^ , _0 = 95^ for SDSS J084205.57+075925.5, i = 10^ , n_1=2.6 , n_3 = 22^ , _0 = 88^ for SDSS J232721.96+152437.3, respectively.

gmw_00010776.name: SDSS J094321.97+042412.0
gmw_00010776.overview: SDSS J094321.97+042412.0 is an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:0807.1787]. Its observed broad-line profile qualitatively matches a model featuring a retrograde warped disc [arXiv:0807.1787].

sources.summary: The paper fits SDSS J094321.97+042412.0 with a retrograde warped-disc profile, giving model parameters and reporting an overall qualitative match to the observed broad-line profile.

quotes.quote: A retrograde disc is needed to match the line profiles with the observations for SDSS J094321.97+042412.0, J093653.84+533126.8. Fig. f454 shows the comparison between the line profiles and the observed profile for SDSS J094321.97+042412.0 with i=17^ , n_1 = 2.1 , n_3=24^ , _0 = 202^ . And the parameters in Fig. f473 for SDSS J093653.84+533126.8 are: i=8^ , n_1=2.1 , n_3=6^ , _0 = 117^ . The disc zone is still taken from R_ in =250r_ g to R_ out = 1000 r_ g and n_2=3.0 . We see that the overall match except that in the two wings between the line profiles and the observations is good qualitatively.

gmw_00010777.name: HATLAS090750
gmw_00010777.overview: HATLAS090750 is an ongoing galaxy merger with asymmetric K-band and Pa-alpha features that suggest tidal interactions or gas flows [arXiv:2009.08446]. These asymmetric structures host active star formation, while the galaxy's central region contains compact molecular gas characterized by highly turbulent motions [arXiv:2009.08446].

sources.summary: The paper finds HATLAS090750 has asymmetric K-band and Pa features suggesting an ongoing merger, star-forming tidal or flow structures, central CO emission, and compact turbulent molecular gas.

quotes.quote: In the particular case of the HATLAS090750 system, the K band and Pa intensity images show two asymmetric features that may be related to gas inflow/outflow or tidal interaction. These features suggest an on-going merging process. Both features account for 18 of the total Pa flux suggesting on-going star formation activity. One of the asymmetric features has a projected velocity blueshift of 300 km s ^ 1 compared to the system centre, while the other feature presents a velocity redshift of 80 km s ^ 1 suggesting that this system has a complex 3D shape. The ALMA observation just traces the CO(1-0) emission coming from the central part of this system, probably due to sensitivity limitations.

For HATLAS090750, we find a very compact CO light distribution as suggested by its half-light radius. In its central zone, this system shows a low rotational velocity value ( V_ rot,CO 70 km s ^ 1 ) and a high median velocity dispersion _ v, CO 35 km s ^ 1 , suggesting a molecular gas ISM phase with highly supersonic turbulent motions and a CO-traced kinematic ratio V_ rot,CO / _ v, CO 2 .

gmw_00010778.name: HATLAS114625
gmw_00010778.overview: HATLAS114625 is a starburst galaxy classified as a LINER and an active galactic nucleus host [arXiv:2009.08446]. It exhibits disc-like morpho-kinematics with a low molecular gas fraction of approximately 0.1 [arXiv:2009.08446].

sources.summary: The paper classifies HATLAS114625 as a LINER and WISE AGN host; its CO and Pa data show disc-like kinematics, compact gas, and low molecular gas fraction.

quotes.quote: The remaining target (HATLAS114625 014511) is located in the low ionization nuclear emission line region (LINER). The H , [ O iii ] , H and [ N ii ] flux measurements are presented in Appendix sec:AppendixA . By using the Wide-field Infrared Survey Explorer (WISE; Wright2010 ) mid-IR colour diagram (right panel in Fig. fig:fig1 ; Stern2012,Mateos2012 ), HATLAS114625 014511 would be classified as an AGN host galaxy, while the other two targets are classified as SFGs in agreement with the BPT-diagram analysis.

The other two starburst galaxies, HATLAS114625 and HATLAS121446, show disc-like morpho-kinematics, with rotation as the dominant component supporting self-gravity ( V_ rot / _v 7-8 ). For both systems, we model the CO and Pa galactic dynamics and we aid the kinematic modelling by using K band photometric models to constrain the inclination angle parameter. From those analyses, we find that the CO and Pa dynamics present a good agreement in both galaxies (Fig. fig:maps ), as suggested by similar kinematic position angles ( PA 4 deg.) and emission line spatial extensions ( R_ 1/2, Pa /R_ 1/2, CO 1 ).

By adopting the dynamically based _ CO values, we obtain molecular gas fractions of the order of 0.1 for both starbursts, far below our initial expectations and consistent with values measured in local star-forming galaxies.

gmw_00010779.name: HATLAS121446
gmw_00010779.overview: HATLAS121446 is a starburst galaxy that exhibits disc-like morpho-kinematics, where rotation is the dominant component supporting self-gravity [arXiv:2009.08446]. Its ionized and molecular gas components display matching dynamics and similar half-light radii, while the galaxy maintains a low molecular gas fraction [arXiv:2009.08446].

sources.summary: The paper describes HATLAS121446 as a starburst with disc-like CO and Pa morpho-kinematics, matching ionized and molecular gas dynamics, agreeing half-light radii, and low gas fraction.

quotes.quote: The other two starburst galaxies, HATLAS114625 and HATLAS121446, show disc-like morpho-kinematics, with rotation as the dominant component supporting self-gravity ( V_ rot / _v 7-8 ). For both systems, we model the CO and Pa galactic dynamics and we aid the kinematic modelling by using K band photometric models to constrain the inclination angle parameter. From those analyses, we find that the CO and Pa dynamics present a good agreement in both galaxies (Fig. fig:maps ), as suggested by similar kinematic position angles ( PA 4 deg.) and emission line spatial extensions ( R_ 1/2, Pa /R_ 1/2, CO 1 ). Our observations suggest that the ionized and molecular gas components display roughly the same galaxy morpho-kinematics.

For HATLAS121446, these half-light radii estimates also agree with R_ 1/2,K (see Table tab:table2 ). However, for HATLAS114625, we find that R_ 1/2,K / R_ 1/2, CO / Pa 1.3-1.6 kpc, suggesting that the ionized and molecular gas ISM phases are distributed in a more compact disc-like structure in this galaxy. For both starbursts, the velocity curves agree and we derive ionized to molecular gas rotation velocity ratios V_ rot,Pa / V_ rot,CO 1.04 0.04 and 0.94 0.14 , for HATLAS121446 and HATLAS114625, respectively.

By adopting the dynamically based _ CO values, we obtain molecular gas fractions of the order of 0.1 for both starbursts, far below our initial expectations and consistent with values measured in local star-forming galaxies.

gmw_00010780.name: SMMJ2217+0017
gmw_00010780.overview: SMMJ2217+0017 is a bright submillimeter galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 2 to 2.5 [arXiv:1205.5452]. It is classified as an active galactic nucleus candidate with a compact core surrounded by star formation [arXiv:1205.5452].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SMMJ2217+0017 as a bright SMG and reports compact nuclear [NII] excitation, surrounding star formation, and AGN-candidate velocity fields.

quotes.quote: For the ninth source, SMMJ2217+0017, we obtained the redshift directly with our IFU observations. SMMJ2217+0017 was identified as a bright SMG ( S_ 1100 m =4.9 0.7 mJy) in the SA22 field Tamura09 with a VLA counterpart Chapman04c , and an optical through mid-IR ( Spitzer- IRAC) photometric redshift constrained to z 2--2.5.

We search the off-nuclear regions of RGJ0332 2732 and SMMJ2217+0017 for gradients in the [ NII ] /H excitation, finding that SMMJ2217+0017 shows very concentrated [ NII ] ( [ NII ] /H =0.7 0.1), with low [ NII ] /H ( [ NII ] /H =0.3 0.1) in regions around the nucleus, likely indicating a compact AGN core surrounded by star formation.

However, since there is evidence for the velocity fields in RGJ0332 2732 and SMMJ2217+0017 to represent gas which is photoionized by an AGN, given the high [ NII ] /H values, we mark these sources as an `AGN candidates' in the analysis.

gmw_00010781.name: RGJ0332 2732
gmw_00010781.overview: RGJ0332 2732 is an active galactic nucleus candidate that exhibits strong off-nuclear [NII]/H-alpha line ratios [arXiv:1205.5452]. Its extended emission-line observations indicate photoionization by an active galactic nucleus, accompanied by unusually smooth velocity and dispersion fields [arXiv:1205.5452].

sources.summary: The paper marks RGJ0332 2732 as an AGN candidate with strong off-nuclear line ratios, photoionized gas, and unusually smooth velocity and dispersion fields.

quotes.quote: Two of our sources (RGJ0332 2732 and SMMJ2217+0017) have high ratios of [ NII ] /H in their integrated spectra (Fig. fig:spectra ), indicating that these sources may host an AGN (a limit of [ NII ] /H > 0.7 is typically adopted for AGN dominated systems Kewley06 ). Given the high SFRs, it is also possible that the high ratios are caused by shock ionization from a galactic wind, as opposed to an AGN ionization field, raising the observed value of [ NII ] /H vanDokkum05 . The detection of H extended over a large scales ( 10 kpc), with no obvious outflow kinematics, further suggests these systems may still be star formation dominated in their H kinematics. We expect the high [ NII ] /H ratio from AGN excitation would typically be concentrated to the inner few kpc.

RGJ0332 2732, by contrast, shows continued strong [ NII ] /H outside the central nucleus and observations of the [ OIII ] , H emission indicate photoionization by an AGN also (Harrison et al. \ in prep.).
Indeed only RGJ0332 2732 (an AGN candidate) exhibits a smooth velocity and dispersion field.

gmw_00010782.name: RGJ0331 2739
gmw_00010782.overview: RGJ0331 2739 is a radio-bright galaxy with a high radio flux that suggests the presence of a buried active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1205.5452]. However, its velocity fields indicate that merger kinematics dominate the system, and its nebular-line properties are consistent with a star-forming galaxy [arXiv:1205.5452].

sources.summary: The paper reports RGJ0331 2739 as radio-bright with possible buried AGN activity, while its velocity field indicates merger kinematics dominate its nebular-line properties.

quotes.quote: The high radio flux of RGJ0331 2739 (S _ 1.4GHz =965 16 Jy) suggests the presence of an AGN. Although it is luminous in the far-infrared as well, the radio luminosity exceeds that expected from the far-infrared-radio relation Ivison10 by a factor 29. We do not see evidence for any AGN in the H , [ NII ] fields, and the AGN-radio core could be very extincted, buried in the system. It is clear from the velocity fields of RGJ0331 2739 (Fig. fig:maps and Section sec:kinemetry ) that merger kinematics appear to dominate over any possible AGN-driven properties, and the [ NII ] /H ratio lies comfortably within that expected for a z 2 star forming galaxy Erb06 . We therefore do not consider this source as an AGN in the remaining analysis for purposes of analyzing its nebular line properties.

gmw_00010783.name: host galaxy of FRB 190611
gmw_00010783.overview: The host galaxy of FRB 190611 is a bright source located at a spectroscopic redshift of 0.3778 [arXiv:2009.10747]. It has an effective half-light radius of 0.40 arcseconds and lacks any detectable structure such as spiral arms [arXiv:2009.10747].

sources.summary: The paper selects the brighter offset galaxy as FRB 190611's host, measures its half-light radius, spectroscopic redshift, and 11 kpc projected FRB offset.

quotes.quote: We obtained deep Gemini-S/GMOS images in the and i bands (the latter shown in Figure  fig:images ) revealing a bright source ( = 22.65 mag) approximately 2 0 to the north-west at , = \ (J2000), identified as the host galaxy by Macquart20 . We do not detect any significant structure (e.g., spiral arms) and measure an effective half-light radius of R_ eff = 0 40 . We also tentatively detect a considerably fainter source coincident within the FRB error ellipse ( 26 mag; at ) at a smaller offset of 0 43 from the FRB position. We estimate chance probabilities for the two galaxies to be unrelated to the FRB host of = 0.017, 0.10 for the bright and faint galaxy, respectively. Given the only tentative detection of the faint source and that the bright source has 2 , we consider the more clearly offset, bright galaxy to be the host of FRB 190611 and place it in our primary Sample A.
This yields a spectroscopic redshift of = 0.3778 based on the , , and [ ] line features. At this redshift, the physical projected offset of the FRB from the bright galaxy centroid is 11 kpc.

gmw_00010784.name: host galaxy of FRB 190711
gmw_00010784.overview: The host galaxy of FRB 190711 is a faint galaxy located at a redshift of 0.5220 [arXiv:2009.10747]. It has an effective half-light radius of 0.46 arcseconds, lacks clear morphological structures, and shows no detectable H-alpha emission [arXiv:2009.10747].

sources.summary: The paper securely associates FRB 190711 with a faint galaxy, reporting no clear morphology, a half-light radius, redshift, 3 kpc offset, and no H detection.

quotes.quote: The FRB is coincident with an 23.5 mag galaxy at , = \ (see Figure fig:images ), with an offset of 0 49 . No clear morphological structures can be identified in the GMOS imaging, and we measure an effective half-light radius of R_ eff = 0 46 . We assert a secure association of FRB 190711 to this galaxy, given the low chance probability of = 0.011 , and include it in Sample A.

Based on the detection of \ and [ ] in this spectrum, we find = 0.5220 . At this redshift, the physical projected offset of the FRB from the galaxy centroid is 3 kpc. We do not detect H emission, but this feature lies at a lower throughput portion of the spectrograph where there is also significant telluric absorption.

gmw_00010785.name: host of FRB 190714
gmw_00010785.overview: The host of FRB 190714 is a relatively bright galaxy with a spectroscopic redshift of 0.2365 [arXiv:2009.10747]. It exhibits bright nebular emission lines, has an effective half-light radius of 1.02 arcseconds, and shows potential evidence of spiral arms [arXiv:2009.10747].

sources.summary: The paper associates FRB 190714 with a bright cataloged galaxy, notes possible spiral arms, measures its half-light radius, redshift, and 2 kpc projected offset.

quotes.quote: This localization places FRB 190714 0 5 from the galaxy \ (see Figure  fig:images ), which was previously cataloged by the Pan-STARRS Chambers16 and the VISTA Cross12 surveys. It is a relatively bright source ( = 20.85 mag), and we estimate a chance association of = 0.005 . We thus include this galaxy in Sample A. We do not detect any distinct morphology of the host galaxy in our FORS2 I band image, but there might be evidence of spiral arms based on preliminary results obtained from imaging with the Hubble Space Telescope in preparation Manningsprep . We measure an effective half-light radius of R_ eff = 1 02 .
The bright nebular emission lines of , [ ] , , and [ ] yield a spectroscopic redshift of = 0.2365 . This places FRB 190714 at a projected physical separation of 2 kpc from the galaxy center.

gmw_00010786.name: host of FRB 191001
gmw_00010786.overview: The host of FRB 191001 is a spiral galaxy located at a spectroscopic redshift of 0.2340 [arXiv:2009.10747]. It forms a physical pair with a neighboring emission-line galaxy 25 kpc away, and the two systems are currently in the process of merging [arXiv:2009.10747].

sources.summary: The paper places FRB 191001 in a spiral-armed host, measures its redshift and 11 kpc offset, and identifies a neighboring galaxy as a merging physical pair.

quotes.quote: We therefore include this galaxy in Sample A. The host galaxy of this FRB shows clear spiral-arm features, with the FRB occurring in the outskirts of the northern arm see for a more detailed study of this FRB Bhandari20b . The estimated effective half-light radius is R_ eff = 1 44 .

The detection of strong nebular emission lines from , [ ] , , and [ ] yield a spectroscopic redshift of = 0.2340 . This places FRB 191001 at a projected physical separation of 11 kpc from the galaxy center.

The longslit was oriented at PA=100 ^ to include the neighboring galaxy , which lies 7'' east of the identified host galaxy. Its spectrum also shows strong nebular emission yielding = 0.2339 , i.e., coincident with the host of FRB 191001, revealing a physical pair. At a projected separation of 25 kpc, we expect that these galaxies are in the process of merging.

gmw_00010787.name: host galaxy of FRB 200430
gmw_00010787.overview: The host galaxy of FRB 200430 is a galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.160 [arXiv:2009.10747]. Deep imaging reveals no distinct morphology for the galaxy, which has an effective half-light radius of 0.57 arcseconds [arXiv:2009.10747].

sources.summary: The paper assigns FRB 200430 to a Pan-STARRS galaxy, finding no distinct morphology, a half-light radius, redshift z=0.160, and 3 kpc projected offset.

quotes.quote: This is 1 0 north of the previously cataloged galaxy \ in the Pan-STARRS catalog Chambers16 with =21.51 mag. We obtained additional g and I band imaging with Keck/LRIS on UT 2020 June 21, the latter shown in Figure  fig:images . Based on the offset and the host-galaxy magnitude, we derive a chance coincidence probability of = 0.005 . We therefore include this galaxy in Sample A. We do not detect any distinct morphology of the host galaxy based on the deeper Keck images and measure an effective half-light radius of R_ eff = 0 57 .

We determine a redshift for the host galaxy of z=0.160 based on the detection of the , [ ] , and [ ] emission features and the H and K absorption lines from Ca. This places FRB 200430 at a projected physical separation of 3 kpc from the galaxy center.

gmw_00010788.name: Q1009 + 29
gmw_00010788.overview: Q1009 + 29 is a quasar at an emission redshift of 2.63 [arXiv:1706.09512] surrounded by a large Lyman-alpha nebula [arXiv:1810.10655]. The system features a spectroscopically detected companion galaxy with a diffuse morphology that is likely to merge with the quasar host [arXiv:1810.10655].

sources.summary: The paper finds a redshifted intrinsic absorber in Q1009 + 2956 with partial N V coverage, weak Lyman absorption, and uncertain blended metal-line constraints.

quotes.quote: Q1009 + 2956 ( z_ abs =2.649510 ; Fig. fig-q1009-sys ): This system is 450 \ redward of the quasar emission redshift. The \ doublet of this system exhibits partial coverage in each of the three blended kinematic (Voigt) components required to fit its asymmetric profile, with the strongest component having C_ f = 0.70 0.06 (Fig. fig-q1009-sys and Table tab-nvfit ). Ly and Ly are detected, but are unusually weak compared to N v . The ratio of Ly to Ly is reasonable, but differences in the shapes of these lines, particularly the presence of a wing on the red side of the Ly profile, suggest a possible blend with another line. We will thus weight the Ly line more heavily as a constraint.

sources.summary: The paper uses extensive Keck/HIRES spectra of the z_em=2.63 quasar Q1009 + 2956, also called J1011 + 2941, to remeasure its z_abs=2.504 Lyman-limit sightline with improved signal-to-noise.

quotes.quote: The spectrum of the z_ em = 2.63 quasar Q1009 + 2956 has been observed extensively on the Keck telescope.

Here we present a re-measurement of the z_ abs =2.504 metal-poor Lyman limit system (LLS) towards the quasar Q1009 + 2956 (aka J1011 + 2941). Since the last analysis of this system by (hereafter Burles1998b ) this quasar has been observed numerous times and the combined dataset yields an effective S/N of 147 in the continuum for Ly at z_ abs =2.504 , compared to about 60 for the Burles1998b spectrum.
The quasar Q1009 + 2956 ( z_ em =2.63 ) was observed with the Keck telescope using the High Resolution Echelle spectrograph HIRES, Vogt1994 during many independent programs.

sources.summary: The paper reports that Q1009 + 29 has a spectroscopically detected companion with diffuse morphology, a large Ly nebula, and a likely future merger with the QSO.

quotes.quote: Q1009 + 29 - This source has a spectroscopically-detected companion about 6 arcsec away. The companion galaxy has been identified at rest-frame optical, and has an unusual, diffuse morphology. This QSO also has a huge Ly nebula surrounding it, extending all the way out to the companion. Due to the proximity of this companion to the host QSO, it is likely that the pair will become a merger.

gmw_00010789.name: HS1549 + 1919
gmw_00010789.overview: HS1549 + 1919 is a hyperluminous quasar located at the center of a massive protocluster [arXiv:2302.12848, arXiv:1810.10655]. The system contains approximately 200 discovered galaxies at the quasar's redshift, including a millimeter-detected companion galaxy [arXiv:1810.10655].

sources.summary: The paper uses the hyperluminous QSO HS1549+1919 as the central radiation source in the target protocluster, testing whether its luminosity, projected distance, and radiative history affect surrounding Ly-alpha halos.

quotes.quote: The target protocluster contains a hyperluminous QSO, namely HS1549+1919 ( L_ 1450 =1.5 10^ 14 L_ , Trainor2012,Mostardi2013 ) at its center.

Binning in UV magnitude, Ly luminosity, and Ly equivalent width (as well as distance from the HLQSO) were done so that each subsample has approximately the same number of LAEs ( N_ LAE 700 ). The projected distance from the HLQSO is used to test whether QSO radiation affects the LAHs of surrounding LAEs. HS1549 is so luminous that the entire field covered by the HSC could experience a higher ionizing radiation field than the cosmic average at z 3 Haardt2012 if QSO radiation has had time to propagate, and additional ionization induced by the QSO could increase Ly luminosity of LAEs in the field (see Section sec:5distance for discussion).

Subsamples based on the projected distance from the HLQSO ( d_ Q ) show a significant variation with a minimum at d_ Q 10 pMpc (Figure fig:3scalelp , the second panel from right in the second row). This could just be due to the stochasticity discussed above, but if real, it could be related to the QSO's radiative history.

sources.summary: The paper describes HS1549 + 1919 as a QSO in a massive protocluster, with NOEMA detecting a galaxy at its redshift and an unresolved secondary spectral component.

quotes.quote: HS1549 + 1919 - This source has had the most extensive follow-up coverage of all the KBSS fields, and in particular about 200 galaxies have been discovered at the QSO redshift in the optical and IR within several arcminutes. One of these galaxies is seen in our NOEMA observations, while the other mm-detected galaxy does not have a counterpart; however, HS1549 + 1919 is the only QSO in this sample that clearly lies at the heart of a massive proto-cluster (Chapman et al. in preparation), so it is not surprising that we have found yet another galaxy. The spectrum of this source contains two asymmetrical peaks, like those of HS1603 + 3820 and SBS1217 + 499, although here the secondary peak is much more prominent. Despite the extensive follow up observations, the secondary component has not been resolved. More details can be found in Chapman et al. (in preparation).

gmw_00010790.name: Mrk 1434
gmw_00010790.overview: Mrk 1434 is a low-metallicity dwarf galaxy that contains two ultraluminous X-ray sources [arXiv:2301.01317]. The northern source is associated with both compact and extended radio emission, which may indicate either an active galactic nucleus jet or a radio bubble [arXiv:2301.01317].

sources.summary: The paper finds Mrk 1434 is a low-metallicity dwarf galaxy with two ULX-like X-ray sources; the northern source coincides with compact and extended radio emission.

quotes.quote: The third galaxy (Mrk 1434) contains two X-ray sources (each with L_ X 10^ 40 erg s ^ 1 ) separated by 2 8, has a low-metallicity (12 + log (O/H) = 7.8), and emits nebular He II 4686 line emission. The northern source has spatially coincident point-like radio emission at 9.0 GHz and extended radio emission at 5.5 GHz. We discuss X-ray binary interpretations (where an ultraluminous X-ray source blows a `radio bubble') and active galactic nucleus interpretations (where a 4 10^5 M_ black hole launches a jet).

Radio emission is detected only from the northern source, . At 5.5 GHz, the emission is extended with major and minor axis full width half maxima of 1 1 0 6 (160 pc 90 pc), respectively, covering 3.5 synthesised beams. The centroid of the 5.5 GHz emission is 0 16 from the X-ray position (for reference, the 95 Chandra error circle is 0 35), and the integrated luminosity is L_ 5.5,int = 1.2 0.2 10^ 36 erg s ^ 1 .

gmw_00010791.name: NGC 4623
gmw_00010791.overview: NGC 4623 is an early-type galaxy that was observed using the Near-Infrared Integral Field Spectrometer on the Gemini-North telescope [arXiv:2311.13747]. Its central velocity dispersion decreases toward the center, which yields no supermassive black hole detection and only provides an upper limit [arXiv:2311.13747].

sources.summary: The paper observes NGC 4623 with Gemini/NIFS but finds decreasing central velocity dispersion, so JAM modeling gives only an upper limit and no SMBH detection.

quotes.quote: We observed four of our galaxies, NGC4458, NGC4623, NGC4379 and NGC4262, using the Near-Infrared Integral Field Spectrometer NIFS, nifspaper on the Gemini-North telescope.
By contrast, the velocity dispersion of galaxy NGC 4623 decreases towards the centre of the galaxy. This is only consistent with the JAM model without a black hole.
The map for NGC4623 instead provides an upper limit with a no SMBH detection.

gmw_00010792.name: SMSS J164147.78-775029.8
gmw_00010792.overview: SMSS J164147.78-775029.8 is a newly discovered high-redshift quasar [arXiv:2105.12215]. The object has an inferred redshift of 4.13 and exhibits weak emission lines [arXiv:2105.12215].

sources.summary: The paper presents SMSS J164147.78-775029.8 as a newly discovered high-redshift QSO whose weak emission lines required additional spectroscopy to confirm a redshift near 4.13.

quotes.quote: In the Figures below, we present the WiFeS spectra for the newly discovered QSOs from the redshift range z=3.8-5.5 , ordered by RA. figure section The lack of prominent emission lines in SMSS J164147.78-775029.8 led us to acquire additional WiFeS spectroscopy with the RT480 beam-splitter on UT 2021-07-04, in an attempt to better examine the associated Ly forest absorption and confirm the redshift. We present the weighted average spectrum in Figure fig:J1641 , with a 3-pixel median smoothing applied to reduce the noise. The onset of absorption shortward of Ly and the lack of transmitted flux shortward of the Lyman limit lend confidence to the inferred redshift of 4.13.

gmw_00010793.name: NGC 7171
gmw_00010793.overview: NGC 7171 is a local disk galaxy located at an estimated distance of 34 Mpc [arXiv:astro-ph/0609090]. It exhibits a flat rotation curve with a rotation velocity of 189 km/s and displays mild spiral streaming motions within its velocity field [arXiv:astro-ph/0609090].

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 7171 as an observed local disk-galaxy velocity-field template, describing its Fabry-Perot kinematics, distance and magnitude, inclination, rotation speed, flat rotation curve, and Tully-Fisher consistency.

quotes.quote: NGC 7171 has a fairly typical disk velocity field with mild spiral streaming motions, and abundant \ emission, yielding a 2-d velocity field that samples the disk well with high resolution. It was observed in the \ line with the Rutgers Fabry-Perot at the CTIO 4-m Blanco telescope, on October 27 and 28, 1989 by T.B. Williams and R.A. Schommer Cerro Tololo Inter-American Observatory is operated by AURA under contract to the NSF. . The data were reduced into intensity, velocity, and dispersion fields by BJW using procedures described elsewhere (Palunas and Williams 2000; Weiner \ 2001) and have been previously studied by Palunas (1996).

NGC 7171 has a heliocentric velocity of 2740 \ and we assumed a distance of 34 Mpc; its magnitude is M_B=-20.0 . The resolution of these 2-d maps is 1.5 , the inclination of NGC 7171 is 55 , and its rotation velocity corrected for inclination is V_c=189 . It has a flat rotation curve and lies on the Tully-Fisher relation of Sakai \ (2000).

gmw_00010794.name: LAB18.b
gmw_00010794.overview: LAB18.b is a high-redshift galaxy located at a systemic redshift of z = 3.093 [arXiv:1807.07933]. It contains detected dust continuum emission and a molecular gas mass of approximately 4.4 billion solar masses, with a double-peaked CO spectrum that suggests either a rotating disk or a merger [arXiv:1807.07933].

sources.summary: The paper reports CO and millimeter continuum detections of LAB18.b at z=3.093, deriving its molecular gas mass and finding a double-peaked CO spectrum consistent with a rotating disk or merger.

quotes.quote: The CO J=4-3 emission from LAB18.b is detected at 11 (peak flux to map variance on the integrated map). The derived systemic redshift is z_ CO =3.093 0.001 by using single Gaussian fitting (Figure fig1 right).

The CO J=4-3 luminosity is used to derive the molecular gas mass with M_ gas =r_ 41 _ CO L'_ CO(4-3) , which gives a molecular gas mass of M_ gas =(4.4^ +0.9 _ 0.6 ) 10^ 9 M_ for LAB18.b (Table tab2 ). We adopted luminous submillimetre galaxies (SMGs) median CO J=4-3 luminosity to CO J=1-0 luminosity ratio of r_ 41 =0.41 0.07 bothwell13 and starbursts and mergers conversion factor of _ CO =0.8 M_ (K km s ^ 1 pc ^2 ) ^ 1 downes98 since LAB18.b has a comparable IR luminosity.

The CO J=4-3 spectrum for LAB18.b shows double-peaked structure (Figure fig1 right) and small spatial offset ( 0 4 or 3 kpc) between the red and blue components (Figure fig1 middle). These suggest that LAB18.b has a rotating disk or a merger, while the beam FWHM is significantly larger than the offset. Dust continuum We detected 2.7 mm and 3 mm dust continuum emission at the position of the LAB18.b (Figure fig1 left).

gmw_00010795.name: UGSC
gmw_00010795.overview: UGSC is an extragalactic candidate counterpart to the gamma-ray source 3FGL J0221.2+2518 [arXiv:1810.06312, arXiv:1904.04278]. It is classified as a hybrid starburst galaxy hosting an active galactic nucleus core [arXiv:1810.06312, arXiv:1904.04278] and exhibits optical variability [arXiv:1810.06312].

sources.summary: The paper identifies UGSC as a polarized, SDSS-listed candidate counterpart to 3FGL J0221.2+2518 and argues it is likely a composite starburst galaxy with an AGN core.

quotes.quote: There is a source with polarization degree 5.2 1.3 clearly deviating from the median polarization degree across the field ( p_ av = 0.91 0.07 ) that qualifies as a ray emitting candidate. The Unidentified Gamma-ray Source Candidate (UGSC) is located at RA=02h21m33.3s, Dec=+25 12 47.2 (J2000), and its position with respect to the Fermi source is presented in Fig. Field . It is listed in SDSS as J022133.31+251247.3 with =17.59 mag. This source is not presented in any known AGN catalogue.

The upper limit on the X-ray luminosity of UGSC ( 7.4 10^ 41 erg/s ) is consistent with a classification as a star-forming galaxy, but it cannot rule-out the possibility that it hosts a low-luminosity or a heavily obscured AGN. Finally, the optical light curve of the UGSC (Fig. UFO_lc ) suggests that it can be a variable source, which strengthens the AGN argument. We conclude that the UGSC is most likely a composite object, i.e., a starburst galaxy with an AGN core.

sources.summary: The paper describes the 3FGL J0221.2+2518 candidate counterpart as an extragalactic hybrid AGN-starburst galaxy absent from AGN catalogues; its random-forest framework again selects that source as the likely gamma-ray emitter.

quotes.quote: Their analysis yield a candidate source in the 3FGL J0221.2+2518 field at RA=02h21m33.3s , DEC=+25 12 47.3 . Follow up spectroscopic observations confirmed its extragalactic origin while the comparison of the line ratios suggest a hybrid AGN-starburst galaxy. The source had not been included in published AGN catalogues and showed atypical, for a ray emitting AGN, [ 3.4-4.6 ] m and [ 4.6-12 ] m colors.
Only one source is classified as a GL source in 3FGL J0221.2+2518 with a 66 probability marked with an arrow. The same source was proposed as the potential ray emitter in .
Applying our method to UFFs, our best candidate for 3FGL J0221.2+2518 is the same as the one proposed by .

gmw_00010796.name: NGC 2388
gmw_00010796.overview: NGC 2388 is a spiral galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 0.01379 [arXiv:1603.04866]. The galaxy exhibits a clumpy appearance with several dust lanes along its spiral arms [arXiv:1603.04866].

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 2388 as SN 2015U's dusty host, giving its redshift and distance, describing HST imaging of the explosion site, and estimating core and local reddening.

quotes.quote: NGC 2388 is at a redshift of z_ host = 0.013790 0.000017 NED; 1991rc3..book.....D , which (assuming cosmological parameters H _0 = 71 Mpc ^ 1 , _m = 0.27 , and _ = 0.73 ) translates into a luminosity distance of 58.9 Mpc 2006PASP..118.1711W and a distance modulus of 33.85 mag that we adopt for all absolute-magnitude corrections. NGC 2388 and the SN site (pre-explosion) were imaged on 2004 Sep. 10 with the Hubble Space Telescope (HST) and the Near-Infrared Camera and Multi-Object Spectrometer (NICMOS) with the NIC2 aperture (scale 0 076 pixel ^ 1 ) in bands F110W , F160W , F187N , and F190N .

We find that \ exploded on the trailing edge of NGC 2388's spiral arm, in a region with several probable dust lanes and a generally clumpy appearance. One of those clumps falls within the 3 position error circle in both the F555W and F814W images and is not detected in the pre-explosion NICMOS images. However, this clump appears to be extended and smoothly connected with other bright regions, not point-like, and we therefore attribute it to the host galaxy and not to .

We also examined modeled estimates for the amount of dust obscuring the bulk of stars in the host galaxy NGC 2388 2015A&A...577A..78P , and we determined the same value at the location of the SN (within 1'' on the sky) by measuring the Balmer ratio implied by the H and H galaxy lines present in our DEIMOS spectrum e.g., 1971MNRAS.153..471B . We estimate E(B-V) 0.56 and 1.1 mag, respectively, toward the average star in NGC 2388's core and the average star near the explosion site of . Although this does not provide a measure of the line-of-sight reddening toward \ specifically, it does provide valuable context about the dust content of NGC 2388.

gmw_00010797.name: GN2 19221
gmw_00010797.overview: GN2 19221 is a galaxy characterized by a knotty star-forming disk morphology [arXiv:2202.06974]. It features a well-constrained dust attenuation slope and normalization, with negligible sub-unity covering fraction effects [arXiv:2202.06974].

sources.summary: The paper highlights GN2 19221 as the sample galaxy with a well-constrained dust attenuation slope and normalization, manageable covering-fraction and slit-loss effects, and knotty star-forming disk morphology.

quotes.quote: Out of the sample, one galaxy (GN2 19221) has a well-constrained dust law slope ( n 1.3 ) and normalization ( _ V 1.0 ).

From the existing data on this sample, one galaxy (GN2 19221) is in that position, where the effects of sub-unity covering fraction are likely negligible, the slit losses are plausibly well-accounted for, and the observational uncertainties are small enough that galaxy is constrained to have n 1.3 and _ V 1 . This galaxy therefore provides a promising example of what will be possible in the near future.

The four galaxies most consistent with standard dust law slopes (GN2 19221, GN1 37683, GN2 18157, GN3 34368) show a range of morphologies with examples of knotty star-forming disks (GB2 19221, GB3 34368), one edge-on galaxy (GB2 18157), and one that resembles a ``chain'' galaxy (GN1 37683). The effective radii for this subset vary widely ( r_ eff =0.3-1.20 ; see Table 2).

gmw_00010798.name: HE0323--4204
gmw_00010798.overview: HE0323--4204 is an inclined, probable spiral Seyfert 1.5 galaxy that exhibits strong nuclear activity [arXiv:1511.00904]. The galaxy hosts moderate star formation and has a neighboring companion galaxy that shows signs of interaction [arXiv:1511.00904].

sources.summary: HE0323--4204 is described as an inclined probable spiral Seyfert 1.5 galaxy with strong nuclear activity, hot dust and power-law continuum, moderate star formation, and a neighboring companion.

quotes.quote: HE0323--4204 is classified as Seyfert 1.5 by . The prominent broad emission line components of Pa and Br , the significant power-law and hot dust contribution to the continuum flux, and the forbidden [ Fe ii ] line indicate strong nuclear activity. The IRAS colours are (f_ (12 )/f_ (25 )) 0.18 and (f_ (60 )/f_ (100 ))=-0.36 , i.e. between the typical colours of starburst galaxies and elliptical galaxies. In the FIR colour-colour diagrams (Fig. fig:firdiag ), they lie on the Extreme Mixing Line with a starburst contribution of 50 . The continuum fit yields a stellar contribution of 30 .
The galaxy at redshift z=0.058 ( D_L=259.3 ) is very inclined and probably a spiral galaxy. In direct neighbourhood, there is a companion galaxy that shows signs of interaction.

gmw_00010799.name: HE2204--3249
gmw_00010799.overview: HE2204--3249 is a Seyfert 1.2 active galaxy with a blue stellar continuum and deep stellar absorption lines [arXiv:1511.00904]. It exhibits a possible dust lane, high extinction, and is likely in tidal interaction with several neighboring objects [arXiv:1511.00904].

sources.summary: HE2204--3249 is the most distant object in the study, with a possible dust lane, high extinction, nearby tidal companions, blue stellar continuum, and broad Seyfert-like components.

quotes.quote: HE2204--3249, also known as ESO 404-29, has a redshift of z=0.0594 ( D_L=265.9 ) and is the most distant object observed in this study. In the imaging study 2014A&A...561A.140B , the decomposition revealed a non-axisymmetrical residuum which could be interpreted as dust lane. From the line ratio Pa /Br , we get a high extinction of A_V 50 mag.

Several objects located in direct neighbourhood are probably in a tidal interaction with the galaxy. The spectrum shows a very blue continuum shape and deep stellar absorption lines are seen. From the continuum fit we get a stellar contribution to the continuum of about 90 . Additionally, the hydrogen recombination lines are rather weak compared to the continuum flux. Nevertheless, we detect broad components ( FWHM _ BLR 4000 ), consistent with the classification as Seyfert 1.2 in .

gmw_00010800.name: HE2211--3903
gmw_00010800.overview: HE2211--3903 is a barred spiral Seyfert 1.5 galaxy and a luminous infrared galaxy [arXiv:1511.00904]. It features an inner ring, an additional spiral arm, and ongoing star formation driven by secular evolution rather than strong interactions [arXiv:1511.00904].

sources.summary: HE2211--3903 is described as a barred spiral Seyfert/LIRG with an additional arm and inner ring, hot-dust-dominated red continuum, molecular gas, and secular evolution.

quotes.quote: HE2211--3903 (also known as ESO 344-16) is a barred spiral galaxy, classified as Seyfert 1.5 galaxy by . The redshift is z=0.0398 , corresponding to D_L=175.6 . In the previous imaging study 2014A&A...561A.140B , we reveal an additional spiral arm and an inner ring that can also be found in emission line maps by . Confirming the results from 2006A&A...452..827F , the spectrum is very red, due to black body radiation coming from hot dust at a temperature of 1100 K which makes up about 60 of the continuum radiation.

Based on IRAS fluxes, the galaxy is a LIRG. The H _2 mass is M_ H _2 =9.3 10^9 M_ 2007A&A...470..571B , resulting in a SFE of 4 M_ L_ ^ 1 . We see that the IRAS data suggest ongoing star formation activity. Since there is no evidence for any strong interaction, HE2211--3903 is likely to be an example for a secularly evolving galaxy.

gmw_00010801.name: MG 1549+3047
gmw_00010801.overview: MG 1549+3047 is a distant radio galaxy located at a redshift of 1.17 [arXiv:2108.07293]. It functions as a gravitational lens system, featuring a lensed radio lobe that is imaged by a foreground galaxy at a redshift of 0.11 [arXiv:2108.07293].

sources.summary: MG 1549+3047 is described as a gravitational lens containing a lensed radio lobe in a radio galaxy, observed with LOFAR but not successfully calibrated onto the target.

quotes.quote: The gravitational lens system MG 1549+3047 ( 15^ h 49^ m 12.6^ s +30^ 47^ 15^ ) was also discovered as part of the MG survey of radio sources, and consists of a lensed radio lobe in a radio galaxy 1993AJ....105..847L at redshift 1.17 2003MNRAS.343L..29T imaged by a foreground galaxy at z=0.11 1996AJ....111.1812L . Figure lbcs1549 shows the LBCS map of the field. The source itself was observed with LBCS and was found to have significant correlated flux in the three minutes of integration used for LBCS only on the shortest international baselines (from the array centre to DE609 Norderstedt, with some correlated flux on the other German baselines).

Bandpass and TEC solutions were inspected and found to be satisfactory, with coherent phase solutions derived on the Dutch stations from initial calibration of the target field against the TGSS data. The field source L465494 was used to solve for the delays. In this case, and unlike the case of , satisfactory delay solutions could not be obtained unless short baselines (those which did not involve an international station) were excluded; in this case, smoothly varying TEC solutions were derived for all international stations. Smaller datasets, averaged in time and frequency were again derived for the target, for the field calibrator sources within 1 degree (L465494, L465498 and L465466, see Fig. lbcs1549 ), and for a brighter but more distant calibrator source (L465490). However, tests showed that in this case, it was not possible to transfer phase solutions satisfactorily from the calibrator to the target or to other calibrator sources.

gmw_00010802.name: J10332972+4404342
gmw_00010802.overview: J10332972+4404342 is a galaxy with a strongly enhanced pseudobulge that may be on a high-inclination orbit [arXiv:2403.19212]. It exhibits a bar-like appearance, which might be feeding its central starburst [arXiv:2403.19212].

sources.summary: The paper describes J10332972+4404342 as a strongly enhanced pseudobulge galaxy possibly on a high-inclination orbit; its bar-like appearance may be feeding its central starburst.

quotes.quote: In contrast, J10332972+4404342, J14005783+4251203, and J14005879+4250427 (panel d, f of Figure fig:pseudo ) may be in high-inclination orbits, and the latter two are members of the same pair (KPAIR J1400+4251) which has a high relative radial velocity ( v = 234 km s^ 1 ). These three galaxies all have pseudobulges and might be bar galaxies from the appearance though it is uncertain given the limited resolution of the images. If this is true then their central starbursts may be field by the bars.

gmw_00010803.name: J14005783+4251203
gmw_00010803.overview: J14005783+4251203 is a pseudobulge galaxy that is a member of the galaxy pair KPAIR J1400+4251 [arXiv:2403.19212]. It may be a barred galaxy on a high-inclination orbit, with a central starburst potentially fueled by its bar [arXiv:2403.19212].

sources.summary: The paper describes J14005783+4251203 as a strongly enhanced pseudobulge galaxy in KPAIR J1400+4251, possibly on a high-inclination, high-relative-velocity orbit with a bar-fed central starburst.

quotes.quote: In contrast, J10332972+4404342, J14005783+4251203, and J14005879+4250427 (panel d, f of Figure fig:pseudo ) may be in high-inclination orbits, and the latter two are members of the same pair (KPAIR J1400+4251) which has a high relative radial velocity ( v = 234 km s^ 1 ). These three galaxies all have pseudobulges and might be bar galaxies from the appearance though it is uncertain given the limited resolution of the images. If this is true then their central starbursts may be field by the bars.

gmw_00010804.name: J14005879+4250427
gmw_00010804.overview: J14005879+4250427 is a pseudobulge galaxy that is a member of the KPAIR J1400+4251 galaxy pair [arXiv:2403.19212]. It may be a barred galaxy on a high-inclination orbit, hosting a central starburst that is potentially fed by its bar [arXiv:2403.19212].

sources.summary: The paper describes J14005879+4250427 as a strongly enhanced pseudobulge galaxy in KPAIR J1400+4251, possibly on a high-inclination, high-relative-velocity orbit with a bar-fed central starburst.

quotes.quote: In contrast, J10332972+4404342, J14005783+4251203, and J14005879+4250427 (panel d, f of Figure fig:pseudo ) may be in high-inclination orbits, and the latter two are members of the same pair (KPAIR J1400+4251) which has a high relative radial velocity ( v = 234 km s^ 1 ). These three galaxies all have pseudobulges and might be bar galaxies from the appearance though it is uncertain given the limited resolution of the images. If this is true then their central starbursts may be field by the bars.

gmw_00010805.name: J03381222+0110088
gmw_00010805.overview: J03381222+0110088 is a star-forming galaxy that belongs to a close, star-forming-dominated triplet rather than a true spiral-and-elliptical pair [arXiv:2403.19212]. It exhibits a very high total gas content and a relatively low star formation efficiency [arXiv:2403.19212].

sources.summary: The paper argues J03381222+0110088 is not truly in an S+E pair but in an SFG-dominated triplet, with high sSFR mainly from high gas content rather than tides.

quotes.quote: Finally, J03381222+0110088 (panel a of Figure fig:classical ) is the only one that is in an S+E pair among the 13 SFGs with strong enhancement. However, as shown in the optical image, it appears to be interacting with another nearby SFG which is in the same group of the S+E pair. The three galaxies form a close triplet dominated by SFGs. Therefore J03381222+0110088 is not in a de facto S+E pair.

It has a very high total gas content with M_ H2+HI /M_ star = 0.56 and a relatively low star formation efficiency of SFE=SFR/M_ H2 = 10^ 9.10 yr^ 1 , which is below the average SFE of the H-KPAIR sample in 2019A&A...627A.107L . It appears that the high sSFR of J03381222+0110088 is mainly due to its very high gas content which may not be related to any tidal effect.

gmw_00010806.name: 2MFGC 2280
gmw_00010806.overview: 2MFGC 2280 is a local active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1502.07353]. It was selected for study due to its flat X-ray spectral shape, which led to its identification as a Compton-thick source [arXiv:1502.07353].

sources.summary: The paper presents NuSTAR fitting of 2MFGC 2280, identifying it as Compton-thick for the first time after selecting it by its flat X-ray spectrum.

quotes.quote: Our sample consists of 10 local ( z<0.1 ) CT AGN observed by : NGC 424, NGC 1068, 2MFGC 2280, NGC 1320, NGC 1386, NGC 3079, IC 2560, Mrk 34, NGC 4945 and the Circinus galaxy. With the exception of 2MFGC 2280, these sources were selected to be known CT AGN and to cover a wide range in X-ray luminosity.

The \ observations of NGC 1386, NGC 3079 and 2MFGC 2280 are presented here for the first time. No detailed X-ray spectral modelling has previously been published on 2MFGC 2280, and it was selected for study here based on its flat spectral shape in the \ band, which resembles that of the other obscured AGN considered in this work.
The Compton-thick nature of 2MFGC 2280 is shown here for the first time.

gmw_00010807.name: [ C 2 ] bright companion
gmw_00010807.overview: The [ C 2 ] bright companion is a high-redshift merging companion galaxy that is being tidally stripped by a nearby quasar host [arXiv:1909.08619]. It potentially hosts a heavily obscured active galactic nucleus, which would make the system a dual active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1909.08619].

sources.summary: The paper treats the [ C 2 ] bright companion as a merging high-redshift galaxy, possibly hosting a heavily obscured X-ray source and forming a likely dual AGN system with the quasar.
