quotes.quote: In addition, we detect three hard-energy photons 2 0 to the west of the main quasar, cospatial with the brightest UV emission of the merging companion. As no soft-energy photons are detected in the same area, this is potentially indicative of a highly-obscured source. With conservative assumptions, and accounting for both background fluctuations and the extended wings of the quasar's emission, these photons only have a probability P=0.021 of happening by chance. If confirmed by deeper observations, this system is the first high redshift quasar and companion individually detected in X-rays and is likely a dual AGN.

With shallow ( 10 minutes on-source) Atacama Large Millimeter Array (ALMA) observations, 2017Natur.545..457D found that the quasar has a [ C 2 ] bright companion. Follow-up deeper and higher-resolution observations with ALMA and the Hubble Space Telescope ( HST ) by 2019ApJ...880..157D showed that the companion is visible on both sides of the quasar, spanning over 4 0 (20 kpc), and its kinematics can be explained by a toy model of a satellite galaxy being tidally stripped as it passes close to the quasar host galaxy.

One of the main objectives of our study was to see if the [ C 2 ] bright companion could host a faint AGN; given that the first SMBHs likely grew through galaxy mergers, the chances of finding lower-luminosity AGN are enhanced around the most distant quasars 2016PASA...33...54R . We describe our observations in Section sect:Obs and report the X-ray properties of the optically selected quasar in Section sect:QSOprops . In Section sect:Companion we present a potential X-ray (heavily obscured) counterpart to the companion galaxy seen by ALMA and HST .

gmw_00010808.name: NRAO 512
gmw_00010808.overview: NRAO 512 is a high-redshift quasar characterized as a compact radio source with a flat radio spectrum [arXiv:1107.2926]. On kiloparsec scales, the galaxy exhibits a two-sided radio jet morphology [arXiv:1107.2926].

sources.summary: The paper identifies NRAO 512 as a high-redshift quasar whose late 2009 multiwavelength brightening produced a separate gamma-ray excess west of 3C 345.

quotes.quote: However, after November 2009 (15 months), a significant excess of photons from the nearby quasar NRAO 512 started to contribute and thereafter was detected with increasing ray activity, possibly adding flux to 1FGL J1642.5+3947. For the same time period and during the summer of 2010, an increase of radio, optical and X-ray activity of NRAO 512 was observed.
The high-redshift quasar NRAO 512 , 0.5 \ west of 3C 345, is known as a compact radio source with a two-sided jet morphology on kpc scales and with a flat radio spectrum 1999yCat..41390545K .

The ray emission from NRAO 512, a high-redshift quasar, was identified based on a correlated increase in multiwavelength emission from radio up to GeV energies between 2009 and 2010. At monthly timescales the source started to be significant around November 2009. This increase of ray flux led to the first detection of the now identified new ray counterpart west of 3C 345. The LAT flux quoted in this paper for the first 15 months was below the EGRET detection threshold, offering a plausible explanation of why EGRET did not see emission from NRAO 512, except possibly during a ray flare event.

gmw_00010809.name: B3 1640 + 396
gmw_00010809.overview: B3 1640 + 396 is a candidate quasar counterpart associated with low confidence to the gamma-ray source 1FGL J1642.5+3947 [arXiv:1107.2926]. Multiwavelength observations show it is fainter than the nearby source 3C 345, and no gamma-ray emission has been detected from it [arXiv:1107.2926].

sources.summary: The paper treats B3 1640 + 396 as a candidate quasar counterpart, but finds no significant gamma-ray emission and shows it is fainter than 3C 345.

quotes.quote: Multiwavelength observations from radio bands to X-rays (mainly GASP-WEBT and Swift ) of possible counterparts (3C 345, NRAO 512, B3 1640 + 396) were combined with 20 months of Fermi LAT monitoring data (August 2008 - April 2010) to associate and identify the dominating ray emitting counterpart of 1FGL J1642.5+3947. The source 3C 345 is identified as the main contributor for this ray emitting region. However, after November 2009 (15 months), a significant excess of photons from the nearby quasar NRAO 512 started to contribute and thereafter was detected with increasing ray activity, possibly adding flux to 1FGL J1642.5+3947. For the same time period and during the summer of 2010, an increase of radio, optical and X-ray activity of NRAO 512 was observed. No ray emission from B3 1640 + 396 was detected.
It was then associated with the quasar B3 1640 + 396 (CLASS J1641+3935) at low confidence 2009ApJ...700..597A .

The radio, optical and X-ray flux ratios between 3C 345, NRAO 512 and B3 1640 + 396 could be used to gauge their individual ray photon contributions and might be able to provide an indication of false associations for ray photons from this part of the sky. Multiwavelength monitoring, in addition to the continuous all-sky monitoring by Fermi LAT, will help to improve the localization of ray emission from this region and is necessary for a deeper variability study for any of these sources in the future. Ultimately this might lead to the detection of ray emission from B3 1640 + 396 as well.

gmw_00010810.name: W2246 0526
gmw_00010810.overview: W2246 0526 is an extremely compact hot dust-obscured galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 4.6 [arXiv:1802.01865]. It is a highly massive galaxy whose infrared luminosity is dominated by active galactic nucleus torus emission [arXiv:1802.01865].

sources.summary: The paper revises W2246’s SED, finding lower IR luminosity after foreground contamination removal, a compact massive host, dominant AGN torus emission, and likely molecular gas.

quotes.quote: W2246 0526 (thereafter, W2246), a Hot DOG at z 4.6 , has been taken as the most luminous galaxy known in the Universe. Revealed by the multiwavelength images, the previous SPIRE photometry of W2246 is contaminated by a foreground galaxy (W2246f), resulting in an overestimation of its total IR luminosity by a factor of about 2. We perform the rest-frame UV/optical-to-far-IR spectral energy distribution (SED) analysis with and re-estimate its physical properties. The derived stellar mass M_ = 4.3 10^ 11  M_ makes it be among the most massive galaxies with spectroscopic redshift z>4.5 . Its structure is extremely compact and requires an effective mechanism to puff-up.

Most of ( >95 ) its IR luminosity is from AGN torus emission, revealing the rapid growth of the central SMBH. We also predict that W2246 may have a significant molecular gas reservoir based on the dust mass estimation.

gmw_00010811.name: W2246f
gmw_00010811.overview: W2246f is a foreground galaxy located northeast of the galaxy W2246 [arXiv:1802.01865]. Photometric data from the Sloan Digital Sky Survey indicates that W2246f has a photometric redshift of approximately 0.047 [arXiv:1802.01865].

sources.summary: The paper identifies W2246f as a foreground galaxy northeast of W2246 whose SPIRE blending contaminated W2246 photometry; it measures W2246f photometry and fits its SED.

quotes.quote: Two red crosses mark the positions of W2246 (22h46m07.5s, 05 d26m35.3s) and W2246f (22h46m08.3s, 05 d26m24.5s) based on the H-band image. Five-SDSS-band photometry and photometric redshift of W2246f have been retrieved from Sloan Digital Sky Survey SkyServer http://skyserver.sdss.org/ . The photometric redshift of W2246f ( z_ ph = 0.047 ) suggests that it is a foreground galaxy. Instead of retrieving the W1 , W2 , W3 and W4 photometry from the ALLWISE Data Release cutri2013 , we do the aperture photometry of both W2246 and W2246f based on the unblurred coadded images https://unwise.me unWISE, lang2014 .
We use the same three-component code to fit the rest-frame UV/optical-to-far-IR SED of W2246f. In Figure fig:sedw2246f , solid line shows the best-fit result of W2246f using SED3FIT .

gmw_00010812.name: W2246-NE
gmw_00010812.overview: W2246-NE is a companion galaxy located to the northeast of W2246 [arXiv:1802.01865]. High-resolution H-band imaging reveals that W2246-NE has a disk-like structure with a flux density of 6.9 +/− 0.1 ΙJy [arXiv:1802.01865].

sources.summary: The paper resolves W2246-NE as a northeast companion with disk-like structure, derives its photometry by surface-brightness fitting, and reports its H-band flux density.

quotes.quote: High-resolution H-band image reveals two components: W2246 and its northeast companion (W2246-NE: 22h46m07.6s, 05 d26m33.7s). We derive their photometry by using the package peng2002,peng2010 to fit the surface brightness profiles of W2246 and W2246-NE, simultaneously. For W2246, we adopt a + PSF model to represent the host galaxy and AGN component, respectively. For W2246-NE, we only assume a model.

The best-fit models suggest that both W2246 and W2246-NE have disk-like structure, with n= 0.7 and 0.8 , respectively. The model and residual images of W2246 and W2246-NE have been shown in Figure fig:hstw2246 . The remaining flux on the residual image contributes less than 3 of the total flux of W2246. The pattern of the residual image of W2246 suggests that the system is possibly not relaxed yet after a recent merger. The flux densities of the host galaxy of W2246, its AGN component and W2246-NE are 5.2 0.2  Jy, 0.9 0.2  Jy and 6.9 0.1  Jy, respectively.

gmw_00010813.overview: The galaxy 181756 is a featureless disk galaxy with a central bulge [arXiv:1103.2910]. It has a major axis size of approximately 29 kpc and is accompanied by several nearby bluish diffuse features that may or may not be connected to it [arXiv:1103.2910].

sources.summary: The paper describes 181756 as a roughly 29 kpc S0-like, featureless disk with a central bulge and nearby bluish diffuse features in SDSS imaging.

quotes.quote: 181756 (9323 km s ^ 1 ): The SDSS image has isophotal radii a=24 .20 and b=10 .48 with a major axis position angle PA=59 ^ . With the NED plate scale of 1 36 kpc and the major axis size is 29 kpc. The SDSS image shows a featureless disk with a central bulge. A number of nearby bluish diffuse features may, or may not, be connected with the galaxy.

gmw_00010814.overview: 192281 is a bright disk galaxy with a major axis of 32 kpc [arXiv:1103.2910]. Its structure shows possible outer isophote twisting, and its spectrum exhibits strong emission lines [arXiv:1103.2910].

sources.summary: The paper describes 192281 as a bright disk galaxy with possible outer isophote twisting, a 32 kpc major axis, emission-line spectrum, and nearby galaxies.

quotes.quote: 192281 (10470 km s ^ 1 ): The SDSS image shows a disk with possible isophote twisting outside the brighter central part. With a cosmology-corrected plate scale of 42.34 kpc arcmin ^ 1 and isophotal radii of a =22 .86 and b =6 .95, the major axis is 32 kpc at position angle 6 ^ .5. With g =16.2 this is a fairly bright galaxy, but not the brightest in the neighbourhood. The SDSS spectrum exhibits strong emission lines, the strongest being H + [ NII ] and [ OII ] , and weak absorptions.

gmw_00010815.overview: The galaxy 205190 is a radio source with a physical width of approximately 50 kpc [arXiv:1103.2910]. It is classified as an elliptical or lenticular galaxy that features a strong, warped dark dust lane and luminous outer extensions [arXiv:1103.2910].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 205190 with MCG +03-28-009, describing a roughly 50 kpc elliptical or lenticular radio galaxy with a warped dark lane and merger-like appearance.

quotes.quote: 205190 (9683 km s ^ 1 ): This object is known also as MCG +03-28-009=2MASXi J1048021+142428 and is a NVSS radio source. The major axis of 2 a =77.4 arcsec (using the 2MASS Ks measure) and the plate scale of 38.50 kpc arcmin ^ 1 imply a 50 kpc wide galaxy. The SDSS image shows an elliptical or lenticular galaxy with a strong dark lane that has a pronounced warp at its outer half-radius. The extensions of the dark lane appear as luminous ansae on both sides of the galaxy.

gmw_00010816.overview: The galaxy 200589, also known as CGCG 066-023, is a spiral galaxy with a strong bulge that implies an Sa classification [arXiv:1103.2910]. Its disk features dust lanes that trace possible spiral arms in both the inner regions and the galaxy periphery [arXiv:1103.2910].

sources.summary: The paper describes 200589 as CGCG 066-023, a luminous large Sa-like spiral with a strong bulge, dust lanes, weak H emission, and a possible projected neighbor.

quotes.quote: 200589 (10768 km s ^ 1 ): This galaxy is also known as CGCG 066-023. Its SDSS image shows a spiral disk with a strong bulge, implying an Sa classification. Dust lanes trace possible spiral arms both near the galaxy periphery and in its inner parts. The cosmologically-corrected plate scale is 42.43 kpc arcmin ^ 1 .

gmw_00010817.overview: The galaxy 006042, also identified as NGC 3466, is a barred spiral galaxy spanning approximately 50 kiloparsecs in width [arXiv:1103.2910]. Images of the galaxy reveal a bluish spiral arm that winds directly from the location of a superposed companion [arXiv:1103.2910].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 006042 as NGC 3466/UGC 06042, a 50 kpc barred spiral whose SDSS image shows a bluish arm connected to a superposed companion.

quotes.quote: 006042 (9921 km s ^ 1 ): This galaxy, classified as SB(s)b:, is also NGC 3466=UGC 06042=PGC 32872. With a plate scale of 39.01 kpc arcmin ^ 1 and a major axis of 76 .59 at a position angle of 50 ^ , this is 50 kpc wide spiral. The minor axis isophotal radius is 19 .7. The SDSS image shows a bluish spiral arm in N3466 winding from the location of the superposed companion.

gmw_00010818.overview: The galaxy 201697 is a strongly distorted spiral galaxy with a diameter of approximately 36 kiloparsecs [arXiv:1103.2910]. It features a warped disk pointing a southward appendage toward a nearby lenticular galaxy [arXiv:1103.2910].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 201697 with CGCG 095-077 NED02 and describes a 36 kpc strongly distorted spiral with a warped disk and nearby possible companions.

quotes.quote: 201697 (10955 km s ^ 1 ): This object is also CGCG 095-077 NED02=MCG +03-28-040=PGC 032951. The plate scale of 43.09 kpc arcmin ^ 1 and the 50 major axis imply a 36 kpc galaxy. The SDSS finding chart shows a strongly distorted spiral with a warped disk pointing a southward appendage toward a nearby lenticular 20 to the south. The SDSS spectrum shows strong H + [ NII ] and [ SII ] emission, with weak emission components of H within an absorption profile, and [ OII ] .

gmw_00010819.overview: 006066, also known as UGC 06066, is a disk galaxy with a physical size of 81 kpc and tightly wound spiral arms [arXiv:1103.2910]. Its central region displays an absorption-line spectrum, and the galaxy shows no obvious signs of interaction [arXiv:1103.2910].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 006066 as UGC 06066, an 81 kpc disk galaxy with tightly wound spiral arms, an absorption-line central spectrum, and no immediate SDSS neighbors.

quotes.quote: 006066 (11807 km s ^ 1 ): This galaxy, classified in NED as SAab: sp, is also UGC 06066. The plate scale at its redshift is 46.25 kpc arcmin ^ 1 and the isophotal major axis a =105 .15 implies a physical size of 81 kpc at PA=41 ^ . The SDSS spectrum of the central region shows only absorption features, including fairly pronounced Balmer lines. The SDSS finding chart shows a disk galaxy with tightly wound spiral arms and with no obvious signs of interaction.

gmw_00010820.overview: Also known as CGCG 041-030, 222042 is a disk galaxy spanning approximately 56 kiloparsecs [arXiv:1103.2910]. It features a bright nucleus, a possible barred structure, and an inner dark ring encircled by a luminous outer ring [arXiv:1103.2910].

sources.summary: The paper describes 222042 as CGCG 041-030, a 56 kpc disk galaxy with inner and outer rings, possible barred structure, and no obvious interaction signs.

quotes.quote: 222042 (11569 km s ^ 1 ): This is CGCG 041-030. The SDSS finding chart shows a disk galaxy with an inner dark ring encircled by a luminous outer ring. The inner regions show that this might be a barred galaxy with the arms emerging from the ends of the bar, with a possible SBb morphological classification. A bright nucleus is visible at the center of the bar.

gmw_00010821.overview: 226077 is a spiral galaxy with a diameter of approximately 23 kpc [arXiv:1103.2910]. It features an inner ring structure and exhibits strong emission lines on a typical absorption spectrum, with a nearby companion galaxy located to its south [arXiv:1103.2910].

sources.summary: The paper describes 226077 as a 23 kpc spiral with an inner ring, emission-line spectrum, and a nearby southern companion with weaker emission lines.

quotes.quote: 226077 (9250 km s ^ 1 ): The plate scale at the redshift of this object is 36.53 kpc arcmin ^ 1 ; with a major axis of 36 .99 at a position angle of 46 ^ this is not a very big galaxy, only 23 kpc wide. The SDSS image shows a spiral galaxy with an inner ring, possibly warranting an Sa() classification. The spectrum shows strong H + [ NII ] and [ SII ] emissions on a typical absorption spectrum, with H and higher Balmer lines also in absorption. This galaxy has a nearby neighbour, SDSS J122512.55+160536.0 about 1.5 arcmin to the south at 9180 60 km s ^ 1 .

gmw_00010822.overview: The galaxy 226111 is a highly inclined disk galaxy with a possible Sc morphological classification [arXiv:1103.2910]. It features dark truncations of its inner disk, weak H-alpha and [S II] emission, and a physical companion galaxy located to its north-west [arXiv:1103.2910].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 226111 with CGCG 100-019/MCG +03-33-020 and describes a highly inclined Sc-like disk with physical companions in SDSS.

quotes.quote: 226111 (10678 km s ^ 1 ): This galaxy, also known as CGCG 100-019 and MCG +03-33-020, The plate scale of 41.98 kpc arcmin ^ 1 and the major axis of 59 .83 at PA=69 ^ imply a size of 44.2 kpc. The SDSS image shows a highly inclined disk with dark truncations of the inner disk with a possible morphological classification of Sc. A nearby physical neighbour, SDSS J125932.41+150413.5=2MASX J12593238+1504138 at 10920 60 km s ^ 1 is visible 1.2 arcmin to the north-west. The main object shows weak H and [ SII ] emission on a spectrum with strong absorption lines.

gmw_00010823.overview: The galaxy 233609 is a small disk galaxy with a diameter of approximately 15 kiloparsecs [arXiv:1103.2910]. It features a bluish, featureless disk that is possibly face-on and lacks obvious spiral arms [arXiv:1103.2910].

sources.summary: The paper describes 233609 as a small, possibly face-on bluish disk without obvious spiral arms, with one real neighbor and other background objects nearby.

quotes.quote: 233609 (8143 km s ^ 1 ): The SDSS finding chart shows a featureless bluish disk, possibly face-on and devoid of spiral arms, with a superposed star. The plate scale is 32.57 kpc arcmin ^ 1 . It, and the isophotal major axis of 28 .48 at PA=150 ^ , imply a smallish 15 kpc galaxy. No SDSS spectrum exists in the archives, but the object has as real neighbour SDSS J130717.44+133847.8 1.5 arcmin to the south-east as an amorphous disk at 8070 60 km s ^ 1 .

gmw_00010824.overview: 008375 is an edge-on disk galaxy classified as Sa with a strong central bulge [arXiv:1103.2910]. The galaxy features visible dust lanes in its disk and exhibits an absorption spectrum [arXiv:1103.2910].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 008375 as IC 0881/UGC 08375, an Sa edge-on disk with strong bulge, visible dust lanes, absorption spectrum, and physical neighbor IC 0882.

quotes.quote: 008375 (7007 km s ^ 1 ): This galaxy is IC 0881=UGC 08375=CGCG 101-025, classified Sa in NED, with an isophotal major axis of 2a=108 .33 at PA=11 ^ . With the plate scale of 28.17 kpc arcmin ^ 1 , this galaxy has a semi-major axis of 25 kpc. The SDSS finding chart shows an edge-on disk with a strong bulge, warranting the Sa classification. Dust lanes are visible in the disk.

gmw_00010825.overview: 008379 is an apparently isolated Sbc spiral galaxy with a radius of 33 kpc [arXiv:1103.2910]. Its spectrum exhibits weak H-alpha emission alongside higher Balmer lines and ionized sulfur in absorption [arXiv:1103.2910].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 008379 as UGC 08379/CGCG 044-071, an apparently isolated Sbc spiral with a 33 kpc radius and weak H emission.

quotes.quote: 008379 (11967 km s ^ 1 ): This object is also UGC 08379=CGCG 044-071, classified Sbc in NED. The plate scale is 46.49 kpc arcmin ^ 1 and the major axis being 84 .09 at PA=79 ^ implies that the galaxy has a 33 kpc radius. The SDSS spectrum shows weak H in emission along with [ SII ] and higher Balmer lines in absorption. The SDSS finding chart shows a two-arm spiral that appears to be fairly isolated.

gmw_00010826.overview: 008488, also known as NGC 5185, is a large Sb-type spiral galaxy [arXiv:1103.2910]. Its structure appears as a tilted, possibly barred spiral disk with an asymmetrical north-east side [arXiv:1103.2910].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 008488 as NGC 5185, a large Sb galaxy with a tilted possibly barred spiral form and several small emission-line companions.

quotes.quote: 008488 (7363 km s ^ 1 ): This object is NGC 5185=UGC 08488=CGCG 072-104=MCG +02-34-025, an Sb galaxy according to NED, with a major axis of 111 .70 (from RC3) at PA=58 ^ ; with the plate scale of 29.62 kpc arcmin ^ 1 , this implies a galaxy with a semi-major axis of 28 kpc. The SDSS finding chart shows a tilted spiral, possibly barred, where the north-east side is more open than the south-west side. SDSS J133001.26+131839.4 is a physical companion; this is a blue disky 10 arcsec wide object, =17.88 mag galaxy some five arcmin south and 7350 30 km s ^ 1 with a blueward-sloping continuum, H , [ NII ] , and [ SII ] emission lines. A more distant companion, some 10 arcmin to the south south-east, is SDSS J133025.61+131148.2 at 7380 60 km s ^ 1 with the appearance of a nucleated dE but whose spectrum shows weak H , strong [ NII ] , and even stronger [ OIII ] emission lines.

gmw_00010827.overview: NGC 5221 is a disk galaxy classified as an Sb type with a strong central bulge and a circumnuclear dark lane [arXiv:1103.2910]. The galaxy's outer disk is strongly warped with bright bluish features [arXiv:1103.2910], and it possesses a tidal tail [arXiv:1708.07124].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 008559 as NGC 5221/Arp 288, a warped Sb system with bluish outer features and several neighboring galaxies at similar redshifts.

quotes.quote: 008559 (7025 km s ^ 1 ): This galaxy is NGC 5221=UGC 08559=ARP 288=VV 315b=VIII Zw 325=CGCG 073-040=MCG +02-35-006 classified as Sb: in NED. The SDSS finding chart shows a disky galaxy with a strong bulge and a circum-nuclear dark lane. The outer parts of the object are strongly warped with bright bluish features to the west and south. To the north-east the warp appears softer and less bright and blue than the other parts of the warped disk.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 5221 as the host galaxy of iPTF16abc, placing the transient in its tidal tail and adopting its Tully-Fisher distance and 21 cm redshift.

quotes.quote: Host Galaxy \ is spatially coincident with a tidal tail of galaxy NGC 5221. 2007A&A...465...71T derived a distance modulus of 35.0 0.4 mag to NGC 5221 from the Tully-Fisher relation, consistent with our derivation from the SN light curve (see sec:classification ). Separately, 2015MNRAS.447.1531C observe the 21 cm line in NGC 5221 and measure a redshift of 0.0234 , which we adopt for the remaining analysis in this paper.

gmw_00010828.overview: IC 0944, also known as UGC 08766, is an Sa-type galaxy spanning approximately 65 kiloparsecs [arXiv:1103.2910]. Its spectrum exhibits hydrogen-alpha emission alongside higher Balmer lines in absorption [arXiv:1103.2910], and it has a nearby SBb-type physical companion galaxy [arXiv:1103.2910].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 008766 as IC 0944/UGC 08766, a 65 kpc Sa galaxy with emission-line spectrum and a nearby SBb physical companion.

quotes.quote: 008766 (6996 km s ^ 1 ): This galaxy is IC 0944=UGC 08766=CGCG 073-085=MCG+02-35-019, classified in NED as Sa, with an isophotal band major axis of 138 .55 at PA=106 ^ . Since the plate scale is 28.13 kpc arcmin ^ 1 , this implies a 65 kpc galaxy. The SDSS spectrum shows H in emission and higher Balmer lines in absorption on a continuum with shallow absorption lines. The SDSS finding chart shows the nearby physical companion, SDSS J135132.95+140639.0, which is an SBb (or SAB(s)b:, as listed in the UGC note) galaxy at 7380 30 km s ^ 1 about one arcmin away, with H , [ NII ] and [ SII ] in emission, H emission with narrow emission in a wide absorption trough and the higher Balmer lines in absorption, on a blueward-sloping continuum.

gmw_00010829.overview: 230914, also designated as CGCG 074-030, is a 65-kiloparsec Sb-type spiral galaxy [arXiv:1103.2910]. Sloan Digital Sky Survey data reveals that the galaxy exhibits an absorption spectrum and has two neighboring galaxies located within three arcminutes [arXiv:1103.2910].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 230914 as CGCG 074-030, a 65 kpc Sb galaxy with absorption spectrum and multiple nearby emission-line galaxies.

quotes.quote: 230914 (11848 km s ^ 1 ): This galaxy is also CGCG 074-030 and has an 85 .06 major axis at PA=115 ^ . With a plate scale of 45.96 kpc arcmin ^ 1 , this is a 65 kpc galaxy that can be classified as Sb. SDSS shows an absorption spectrum. Two galaxies are within three arcmin; one is SDSS J135955.08+120319.2, a 20 arcsec face-on spiral at 11670 30 km s ^ 1 with Balmer, [ NII ] , [ SII ] , [ OII ] and [ OIII ] emission lines on a blueward-sloping continuum.

gmw_00010830.overview: The galaxy 008943, also identified as NGC 5417, is an Sa-type galaxy with a physical diameter of 35 kiloparsecs [arXiv:1103.2910]. Its spectrum exhibits absorption features only, and it has a physical companion galaxy located two arcminutes to its south [arXiv:1103.2910].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 008943 as NGC 5417/UGC 08943, a 35 kpc Sa galaxy with an absorption spectrum and a physical southern companion.

quotes.quote: 008943 (4933 km s ^ 1 ): This object is NGC 5417=UGC 08943=CGCG 046-039, an Sa galaxy with 105 .81 major axis in PA=117. With a plate scale of 20.09 kpc arcmin ^ 1 , this is 35 kpc object. The SDSS spectrum shows absorptions only. A physical companion, SDSS J140215.25+080022.9, a featureless 10 blob two arcmin south of N5417 and at 5190 60 km s ^ 1 , shows Balmer lines in absorption on a blueward-sloping continuum.

gmw_00010831.overview: 009031 is an almost edge-on disk galaxy with a general appearance resembling an Sb-type spiral [arXiv:1103.2910]. The galaxy features an equatorial dark lane and spans a semi-major axis of 27 kiloparsecs [arXiv:1103.2910].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 009031 as UGC 09031, an edge-on Sb-like disk with a prominent dark lane, emission at H and [NII], and uncertain companions.

quotes.quote: 009031 (11864 km s ^ 1 ): This galaxy is UGC 09031=CGCG 103-076=MCG +03-36-053, classified as a possible spiral (S?) in NED. With an isophotal major axis of 69 .80 at PA=40 ^ , and a plate scale of 46.03 kpc arcmin ^ 1 , this is a 27 kpc (semi-major axis) disk. The SDSS finding chart shows an almost edge-on disk with a prominent equatorial dark lane. The general appearance is of an Sb galaxy.

gmw_00010832.overview: The galaxy 248977, also identified as SDSS J144112.34+145324.4, is a relatively small galaxy with a bright center resembling a nucleated dwarf elliptical galaxy [arXiv:1103.2910]. It exhibits an absorption spectrum and has two diffuse, nearby blobs that may represent confused neighboring systems [arXiv:1103.2910].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 248977 as SDSS J144112.34+145324.4, a relatively small nucleated-dE-like galaxy with an absorption spectrum and possible confused nearby blobs.

quotes.quote: 248977 (9153 km s ^ 1 ): The SDSS finding chart shows a 30 arcsec diffuse image with a very bright center reminiscent of a nucleated dE (ndE), significantly larger than the size given in SDSS. This object is SDSS J144112.34+145324.4 with an optical redshift of 9150 60 km s ^ 1 and with an absorption spectrum that includes also H and in absorption. At this redshift, the 10 .55 size from SDSS translates into some 6 kpc; this is a relatively small galaxy that would be 20 kpc if the size shown in the SDSS image would be adopted. Two diffuse 5 arcsec blobs are nearby: SDSS J144110.38+145331.5 with =15.6 and ( g- )=0.42 that might be a dark-lane galaxy, and SDSS J144110.79+145333.9 with =16.81 and ( g- )=0.30.

gmw_00010833.overview: NGC 5790, also known as 009624, is an S0/a galaxy featuring an inner yellowish spiral structure and an outer bluish ring with dust lanes [arXiv:1103.2910]. The galaxy exhibits an emission-line spectrum and has a nucleated dwarf companion located approximately six arcminutes to the north [arXiv:1103.2910].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 009624 as NGC 5790/UGC 09624, an S0/a galaxy with inner spiral, bluish ring, emission-line spectrum, and a dwarf companion.

quotes.quote: 009624 (11068 km s ^ 1 ): This object is NGC 5790=UGC 09624=CGCG 048-076=MCG +01-38-022. Classified in NED as (R)SA0/a and in UGC as S0/Sa, it shows an inner yellowish spiral with an outer bluish ring with dust lanes and spiral structure on the SDSS finding chart. The SDSS spectrum shows H , [ NII ] , [ SII ] , [ OI ] , [ OIII ] and [ OII ] in emission, while H and higher Balmer lines are in absorption. There are a number of nearby galaxies without redshifts, but a physical companion is SDSS J145730.93+082326.3, a 10 nucleated dE or dwarf S0 about six arcmin north at 11130 60 km s ^ 1 .

gmw_00010834.overview: 009788 is a disk galaxy classified as an Im-type system [arXiv:1103.2910]. It features several dust lanes meandering along its equatorial plane, a faint northern extension, and a bluish northwestern extension [arXiv:1103.2910].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 009788 as UGC 09788, an Im-like disk with equatorial dust lanes, extensions, and several physical companions at similar velocities.

quotes.quote: 009788 (10176 km s ^ 1 ): The galaxy is UGC 09788=CGCG 049-079, classified Im? in NED. The plate scale of 39.68 kpc arcmin ^ 1 and major axis of 54 .70 (RC3) imply a 36 kpc object. The SDSS finding chart shows a disky galaxy with a number of dust lanes meandering along the equatorial plane. A faint and fuzzy extension is visible to the north, extending some 15 arcsec from the nucleus, and a bluish extension is visible at the north-west end of the equatorial dark lane.

gmw_00010835.overview: 009794 is a large galaxy with a diameter of 78 kpc [arXiv:1103.2910]. It is classified as an extremely disturbed object with a warped structure, dust lanes, and a nearby physical neighbor [arXiv:1103.2910].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 009794 as UGC 09794/VIII Zw 461, an extremely disturbed large galaxy with warped structure, dust lanes, and a nearby physical neighbor.

quotes.quote: 009794 (6406 km s ^ 1 ): This extremely disturbed object is UGC 09794=VIII Zw 461=CGCG 077--54=MCG +02-39-009. NED classifies it as SBdm: whereas UGC notes it as SBc-IRR. The band isophotal major axis quoted in NED, 7 .30, is an obvious mistake; the corresponding value for the B isophotal major axis in RC3 is 181 .20 at PA=42 ^ , which we adopt. With a plate scale of 25.75 kpc arcmin ^ 1 , this is a 78 kpc galaxy.

gmw_00010836.overview: 009838, also known as UGC 09838, is classified as an Sab galaxy [arXiv:1103.2910]. It features an inner ring structure and has two nearby physical companion galaxies with emission-line spectra [arXiv:1103.2910].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 009838 as UGC 09838, an Sab galaxy with an inner ring and multiple nearby physical companions with emission-line spectra.

quotes.quote: 009838 (10267 km s ^ 1 ): This galaxy is known also as UGC 09838=CGCG 049-158 and is classified Sab in NED and in UGC. The RC3 isophotal major axis is 77 .30; this translates into 51 kpc at a plate scale of 39.75 kpc arcmin ^ 1 . The galaxy looks like an Sb with an inner ring about half-way from the center to the edge. SDSS J152502.75+071056.2 is a physical neighbour two arcmin to north-west at 10380 60 km s ^ 1 , with H , [ NII ] , [ SII ] and [ OII ] emission lines on a slightly blueward-sloping continuum, and SDSS J152506.35+070552.5, a 30" blue irregular galaxy three arcmin to the south-west at 10410 30 km s ^ 1 with H , H , [ NII ] , [ SII ] , [ OIII ] and [ OII ] emission lines.

gmw_00010837.overview: The galaxy 260110, also identified as CGCG 079-040, is a 28 kpc galaxy with a featureless, elliptical-looking body [arXiv:1103.2910]. Its spectrum displays weak emission lines on a general absorption spectrum, and it has a distant, physical companion to the north-east [arXiv:1103.2910].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 260110 as CGCG 079-040, a 28 kpc featureless elliptical-looking galaxy with weak emission lines and several projected or possible companions.

quotes.quote: 260110 (10160 km s ^ 1 ): This galaxy is also CGCG 079-040, whose SDSS finding chart shows an elliptical featureless body. With a major axis of 43 .13 at PA=56 ^ and a plate scale of 39.25 kpc arcmin ^ 1 this is a 28 kpc galaxy. The SDSS spectrum shows weak H , [ NII ] , [ SII ] and [ OII ] emission on a general absorption spectrum. A physical but distant companion is SDSS J160514.09+141320.1, an edge-on 30 disk seven arcmin to the north-east at 10410 30 km s ^ 1 .

gmw_00010838.overview: 010272 is a lenticular galaxy with a semi-major axis of approximately 16 kiloparsecs [arXiv:1103.2910]. Its spectrum displays emission lines, including hydrogen-alpha, nitrogen II, and sulfur II, superimposed on a general absorption spectrum [arXiv:1103.2910].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 010272 as NGC 6081/IC 1202, an S0 galaxy with emission lines and a blue irregular physical companion to the southeast.

quotes.quote: 010272 (5124 km s ^ 1 ): This galaxy is NGC 6081=IC 1202=UGC 10272=CGCG 079-078=MCG +02-41-019, classified S0 in NED, with an isophotal band major axis of 96 .37 at PA=129 ^ , which at a plate scale of 20.54 kpc arcmin ^ 1 implies a 16 kpc semi-major axis galaxy. The object shows an SDSS spectrum with H , [ NII ] , and [ SII ] in emission on a general absorption spectrum. A physical companion is SDSS J161311.43+094952.0, an elongated blue irregular four arcmin to the south-east at 4890 km s ^ 1 with strong H , H , [ NII ] , [ SII ] , and [ OIII ] emission on a blueward-sloping continuum.

gmw_00010839.name: CIG 203
gmw_00010839.overview: CIG 203 is an isolated galaxy that has no physically bound companions [arXiv:1402.5526]. It is situated in a sparse large-scale environment, showing only a mild association with large-scale structure through two nearby neighbors [arXiv:1402.5526].

sources.summary: The paper describes CIG 203 as having no physically bound companions, a sparse large-scale environment, and only a mild association with large-scale structure through two nearby LSS neighbours.

quotes.quote: CIG 203 has no physically bound companions.
There is a sparse population of galaxies in the LSS around CIG 203, while CIG 401 and CIG 771 show a much more crowded LSS within 3 Mpc.
On the other hand, the galaxy CIG 203 appears only mildly in relation with a LSS since only two LSS neighbours can be found in its environment.

gmw_00010840.name: CIG 401
gmw_00010840.overview: CIG 401 is locally linked to companion galaxies that are caught under its gravitational influence [arXiv:1402.5526]. The galaxy is situated within a crowded large-scale environment within 3 Mpc, appearing to lie near the edge of a filament or wall structure [arXiv:1402.5526].

sources.summary: CIG 401 is locally linked to gravitationally bound companions, lies in a crowded large-scale environment within 3 Mpc, and appears to sit near the edge of a filament or wall.

quotes.quote: On the other hand, CIG 401 and CIG 771 are linked locally with companions that are caught under their gravitational influence.
There is a sparse population of galaxies in the LSS around CIG 203, while CIG 401 and CIG 771 show a much more crowded LSS within 3 Mpc.
The galaxy CIG 401 seems to be located towards the edge of a LSS, such as a filament or a wall.

gmw_00010841.name: CIG 771
gmw_00010841.overview: CIG 771 is a galaxy that has three physically linked companion galaxies [arXiv:1402.5526]. Its closest satellite is located 12 kpc away and likely influences its evolution, while its larger-scale environment resembles the outskirts of a poor cluster [arXiv:1402.5526].

sources.summary: CIG 771 is one of the least isolated CIG galaxies, with three physical companions, a close satellite likely influencing its evolution, and a larger-scale environment resembling a poor-cluster outskirts.

quotes.quote: Among the 46 CIG galaxies with at least one physical companion within its escape speed boundary, 36 have one satellite, nine have two satellites, and one has three satellites (CIG 771).

For instance, in the case of CIG 771, none of its three physically linked galaxies would violate the CIG isolation criterion. Nevertheless, its closest satellite, at 12 kpc and with a velocity difference of 230 km s ^ 1 , tackled by the present study may have a considerable influence on the evolution of CIG 771.
Due to the large and roughly equivalent number of blue- and redshifted neighbour galaxies within the projected 3 Mpc, the galaxy CIG 771 may reside in the outskirts of a poor cluster.

gmw_00010842.name: PG 0923+201
gmw_00010842.overview: PG 0923+201 is a luminous quasar located at a redshift of z=0.1921 [arXiv:2106.05655]. Its spectrum displays emission lines, with a broad H-beta line profile that suggests a complex broad-line region structure where virialized motion and outflows coexist [arXiv:2106.05655].

sources.summary: PG 0923+201 is a luminous quasar with z=0.1921 and prominent emission lines; its H delay map suggests virialized motion plus outflow, and its H delay agrees with empirical size-luminosity relations.

quotes.quote: PG 0923+201 has a redshift of z=0.1921 , m_ g =15.6 mag , and V band extinction A_ V =0.116 mag Schlafly2011 . Its spectrum shows prominent emission lines, and weak 5007 line Boroson1992 . The ratios of the equivalent width of 5007 and between 4434 and 4684 to that of H are about 0.04 and 0.72, respectively. Such strong and weak \ emission lines are common features seen in narrow-line Seyfert 1 galaxies (NLS1s), which are generally believed to be accreting at a super-Eddington rate. However, the FWHM of the H line is as large as 7600 , much broader than NLS1s (usually narrower than 2000 ).

PG 0923+201 The result shows shorter lags at higher velocities on both red and blue wings of the line profile, as would be expected for virialized motions. However, the lags in the redshifted bins are overall longer than these in the blueshifted bins, which might indicate a signature of outflow Bentz2009,Du2018a . The virial envelopes in Figure fig:BLR clearly show the asymmetric lag structure. We note that the center wavelengths of the narrow lines in our spectra (see Figure fig:fit ) are correct, meaning that this asymmetry is real. A similar pattern can be seen in other objects, such as NGC 3227 Denney2009 and Mrk 79 Lu2019 . This complicated structure may imply the coexistence of virilization and outflow in the BLR of PG 0923+201.
The H time delay of PG 0923+201 is consistent with the empirical relations of both and , in consideration of their associated scatters.

gmw_00010843.name: J1621
gmw_00010843.overview: J1621 is a high-redshift Lyman-break galaxy located in the cluster J1621+0607 [arXiv:1304.7015]. It is gravitationally lensed with a magnification factor of 10, displaying a confirmed tangential arc system and a maximum gas outflow velocity of 300 km/s [arXiv:1304.7015].

sources.summary: The paper analyzes J1621 as the third lensed z~4 LBG, using its arc confirmation, continuum spectrum, nebular emission, and lower maximum outflow velocity.

quotes.quote: Two are independent sources lensed by the well-studied cluster Abell 2390 and the third was located in the cluster J1621+0607 in the Sloan Digital Sky Survey. A2390 H3 and H5 represent two distinct highly-elongated pairs of lensed images that were spectroscopically confirmed to be at different redshifts by, respectively, frye1998 and pello1999 . The tangential arc system in J1621+0607 was spectroscopically confirmed by bayliss2011 . The gravitational magnification factor is 10 in each case pello1999 .

The final spectra, shown in Figure fig:spectra , reach an average continuum S/N per 70 resolution element of 5 for J1621, 9 for A2390 H3, and 10 for A2390 H5 over the rest-frame wavelength range 1250--1650 . This is comparable to that in the composite spectrum in jones2012 which has S/N equivalent to 10 at the improved resolution of 70 of our new data. Systemic Redshift Accurate systemic redshifts are required in order to examine the kinematics of gas seen in absorption and the techniques for estimating these are discussed in detail in jones2012 . This is straightforward when nebular emission lines are visible, such as is the case in both J1621+0607 (O iii ] 1661,6) and A2390 H5 (O iii ] 1661,6, He ii 1640, C iv 1548,51).

The maximum outflow velocity in J1621 is 300 . The outflowing low-ionization gas attains a somewhat lower ( 30 on average) maximum velocity at higher redshift. This trend is not due to lower quality data as it remains evident in Figure fig:fcov if we consider alternative measures such as the FWHM or an absorption threshold at 25 of the continuum flux. However all galaxies except J1621 have similar maximum velocities ranging from 600-800 .

gmw_00010844.name: Cosmic Eye
gmw_00010844.overview: The Cosmic Eye is a gravitationally lensed galaxy located at a redshift of z=3.07 [arXiv:1304.7015]. It exhibits a high neutral hydrogen covering fraction of 95 percent, which constrains its escape fraction to less than 0.05 [arXiv:1304.7015].

sources.summary: The paper uses the Cosmic Eye as a lower-redshift lensed comparison galaxy, noting its z=3.07 spectrum, unusual redshifted absorption, covering-fraction artifact, and H I escape-fraction constraint.

quotes.quote: These include the `Horseshoe' ( z =2.38, quider2010 ), cB58 ( z =2.73, pettini2002 ) and the `Cosmic Eye' ( z =3.07, quider2009 ). Spectra were taken with the 1200 line mm ^ 1 DEIMOS grating during two runs in October 2011 and June 2012. This provides a resolution of 1.7 corresponding to a velocity resolution of 70 km s ^ 1 , considerably better than for the composite discussed by jones2012 . Spectra of each galaxy covered wavelengths corresponding to at least 1175 1675 \ in the rest frame.

Likewise the extent of redshifted absorption is approximately +200 \ for all sources, with the notable exception of the Cosmic Eye as discussed in detail by quider2010 ; this indicates little difference in line broadening from rotation or other internal kinematic structure. Therefore, in this limited sample, the low-ionization gas kinematics are similar with a somewhat lower average velocity extent at higher redshift. Covering Fraction The covering fraction of each galaxy as a function of gas velocity is estimated from the methods described in Section sec:analysis and shown in Figure fig:fcov . Both methods are generally in good agreement indicating that Equation eq:profile is a valid approximation. Si ii covering fractions derived for the Cosmic Eye are systematically higher than indicated by the average absorption profile; this is largely an artifact caused by additional absorption at the wavelength of Si ii 1260 from intervening gas at z=2.66 quider2010 .
quider2010 show that the Cosmic Eye has an H i covering fraction of 95 implying f_ esc < 0.05 based on damped \ absorption, more stringent than the f_ esc < 0.1 from our analysis.

gmw_00010845.name: J0309+2717
gmw_00010845.overview: J0309+2717 is a high-redshift blazar located at z=6.1 [arXiv:2309.03936]. Very Long Baseline Array observations of the source resolve a one-sided radio jet extending approximately 0.5 kiloparsecs [arXiv:2309.03936].

sources.summary: The paper treats J0309+2717 as a z=6.1 blazar with a resolved one-sided jet, low-frequency spectral flattening, and a likely turnover plus high-frequency aging break.

quotes.quote: J0309+2717 J0309+2717 is a blazar at z=6.1 discovered by and its radio, X-ray, and SMBH properties have been studied in detail previously (e.g. Belladitta2020A&A...635L...7B, Spingola2020A&A...643L..12S, Belladitta2022A&A...660A..74B , Spingola in prep.). The Very Long Baseline Array (VLBA) observations conducted by resolve the radio jet at milliarcsecond (mas) resolution, which reveals a one-sided jet with an extent of about 0.5 kpc and shows multiple sub-components. The radio spectrum presented by suggests a spectral index of =-0.98 0.05 between 1 and 40 GHz.

The homogeneous FFA with a break due to an ageing electron population gives the best fit to the spectrum with a reduced ^2 of 0.6 and BIC >6 with all other models (see Table tab:curve_parameters ). The best fitting model suggests the spectrum peaks at 110_ 20 ^ +10 MHz observed frame, which translates to 750_ 110 ^ +100 MHz rest frame. The high-frequency break is indicative of radiative losses (i.e. inverse Compton losses) from an ageing electron population in the radio lobes e.g. Jaffe1973A&A....26..423J, Murgia2003PASA...20...19M, callingham2015ApJ...809..168C .

gmw_00010846.name: J0803+3138
gmw_00010846.overview: J0803+3138 is a high-redshift quasar located at z=6.384 [arXiv:2309.03936]. It hosts a supermassive black hole with a mass of 1.40 billion solar masses and exhibits a steep radio spectrum [arXiv:2309.03936].

sources.summary: The paper describes J0803+3138 as a z=6.384 quasar with high black-hole mass and optical luminosity, and classifies its GMRT radio spectrum as steep.

quotes.quote: J0803+3138 This quasar at z=6.384 has been discovered by using the DESI Legacy Imaging survey and UKIRT Hemisphere survey. NIR spectroscopcic follow-up observations with Gemini/GNIRS by show that it also has a high SMBH mass of 1.40 0.18 10^9 M _ and M _ 1450 of 26.49 . According to our radio observations, this source can also be classified as steep spectrum with = -1.12^ +0.16 _ 0.12 .

gmw_00010847.name: J1037+4033
gmw_00010847.overview: J1037+4033 is a high-redshift radio-loud quasar [arXiv:2309.03936]. It exhibits a slightly positive radio spectral index, making it a potential young high-frequency peaker source [arXiv:2309.03936].

sources.summary: The paper identifies J1037+4033 as the only sample quasar with a slightly positive radio spectral index, making it a potential young high-frequency peaker needing follow-up.

quotes.quote: J1037+4033 J1037+4033 is the only quasar from our sample with a slightly positive (but consistent with zero) spectral index ( = 0.06^ +0.12 _ 0.13 ). Therefore, it could be turning over at >3 GHz (rest-frame >21 GHz) instead of below 144 MHz. This would make it a potential HFP source, which are generally thought to be young e.g. Dallacasa2000A&A...363..887D . More GHz-frequency observations (using for example the VLA or VLBA) or ultra-low frequency observations ( <50 MHz) are necessary to confirm this.

gmw_00010848.name: J2336+1842
gmw_00010848.overview: J2336+1842 is a high-redshift quasar located at z=6.6 [arXiv:2309.03936]. It is classified as a steep-spectrum radio source, though deeper observations are required to confirm its spectral slope [arXiv:2309.03936].

sources.summary: The paper describes J2336+1842 as the highest-redshift object in its GMRT campaign, near the detection limit and classified as a steep-spectrum radio quasar needing deeper observations.

quotes.quote: J2336+1842 This quasar at z=6.6 is part of the sample recently discovered by and is the highest redshift source in our GMRT observing campaign. The flux density of this quasar is close to the detection limit in GMRT band 4 and 5. According to our fitting routine it can be classified as a steep spectrum source with =-1.22^ +0.19 _ 0.09 , even though the band 5 integrated flux density is higher than expected. Deeper observations (RMS <0.02 mJy) in the GHz regime are needed to confirm the spectral slope.

gmw_00010849.name: IRAS 19354+4559
gmw_00010849.overview: IRAS 19354+4559 is a class IIIb merger system located at a distance of approximately 289 Mpc [arXiv:1211.1674]. The system consists of two interacting galaxies with edge-on disks and tidal features [arXiv:1211.1674].

sources.summary: The paper treats IRAS 19354+4559 as a class IIIb merger with tidal features and edge-on disks, measuring single-source X-ray luminosities below starburst-relation predictions.

quotes.quote: IRAS 19354+4559 is a class IIIb merger system with nuclear separations of approximately 8.^ 5 Arribas04 . In this case, the offsets in right ascension and declination are comparable, being 6.^ 7 and 5.^ 3 , respectively. Both galaxies have significant tidal features suggestive of mutual interaction and appear as edge-on disks, although the eastern component has several foreground stars contaminating the optical images, partially confusing the orientation. Using fluxes from the RBGS, we calculate a FIR luminosity (L_ FIR / ) = 11.06 and an upper limit IR luminosity of (L_ IR / ) = 11.85 based on the redshift of z=0.067, which corresponds to a distance of 289 Mpc Lawrence99 . We did not find any IR or optical line diagnostics for this system.

IRAS 19354+4559 R03 predicts L_ soft, R03 = 9.2_ 4.2 ^ +8.0 10^ 40 erg s ^ 1 and L_ hard, R03 = 1.1_ 0.5 ^ +1.0 10^ 41 erg s ^ 1 for IRAS 19354+4559. Our model luminosities give L_ soft, model = 4.5_ 2.0 ^ +2.0 10^ 40 erg s ^ 1 and L_ hard, model = 4.5_ 1.9 ^ +2.1 10^ 40 erg s ^ 1 . As with the soft band of IRAS 18329+5950, both the soft and hard bands of IRAS 19354+4559 are on the lower end of the predictions.

gmw_00010850.name: XID-536
gmw_00010850.overview: XID-536 is a luminous galaxy merger that features a double nucleus [arXiv:astro-ph/0504195]. The system appears to be a merger of two evolved, old stellar populations with little evidence of vigorous star formation [arXiv:astro-ph/0504195].

sources.summary: The paper describes XID-536 as a luminous merger with a double nucleus, two old stellar components, similar nuclear SEDs, and little evidence for vigorous star formation.

quotes.quote: Two are Deep Field South sources, XID-56 and XID-536, with complex morphologies suggestive of recent merger activity. Substructures seen in the NIR AO image (FWHM 0.1 ), including multiple tight knots in XID-56 and a double nucleus in XID-536, are confirmed in the optical \ images. These structures are unresolved in the best seeing-limited ( 0.5 FWHM) NIR images. Stellar population synthesis models of the substructures indicate that XID-56 is a gas rich merger with a recent burst of star formation and significant amounts of dust. XID-536 appears to be a merger of two evolved stellar populations.

Figure fig:DN shows the images for galaxy XID-536. The center of this galaxy is composed of two concentrations with visual sizes on the order of 2 kpc. Both are fairly red, indicative of either old or dusty stellar populations.

gmw_00010851.name: N2 25
gmw_00010851.overview: N2 25 is a high-redshift quasar located at a redshift of z=2.610 [arXiv:astro-ph/0210248]. It is an optically unresolved, heavily obscured active galactic nucleus that exhibits a very hard X-ray spectrum and a red optical-to-near-infrared color [arXiv:astro-ph/0210248].

sources.summary: The paper identifies N2 25 as a z=2.610 obscured, reddened quasar with narrow-to-intermediate emission lines, heavy X-ray absorption, and near-Eddington accretion.

quotes.quote: The X-ray source N2 25 is identified optically with an unresolved (in 0.8 arcsec seeing) object with R=23.07 . The corresponding K band counterpart is also spatially unresolved. N2 25 has an optical/near-infrared colour of R-K=4.0 , considerably redder than the colours of typical high-redshift quasars which have R-K 2.0 .

N2 25 has a very hard X-ray spectrum as indicated by its hardness ratio of 0.26 0.11 (Table tab:sample - see Manners et al. 2002 for the hardness ratio definition and spread in values for the whole survey). The best fit value is N_ H = (2.9 0.7) 10^ 23 cm ^ 2 (shown in Fig. \ fig:xspec25 ). Therefore for any reasonable power-law continuum slope there is very heavy absorption by a column with 3 10^ 23 cm ^ 2 .

For N2 25 with FWHM =2000 km s ^ 1 we find M_ BH =1.5 10^ 8 M_ . N2 25 shares several properties with the class of objects known as narrow-line Seyfert 1s (NLSy1s) such as narrow emission lines and very high metallicity (as indicated by the strength of N V ).

gmw_00010852.name: N2 21
gmw_00010852.overview: N2 21 is a compact radio galaxy with an extremely red optical-to-infrared color [arXiv:astro-ph/0210248]. Its steep continuum spectral index indicates substantial dust reddening, which strongly suggests that the galaxy is undergoing an intense starburst [arXiv:astro-ph/0210248].

sources.summary: The paper describes N2 21 as a compact radio galaxy with dusty, starburst-like host starlight, unresolved narrow lines, and puzzling X-ray emission.

quotes.quote: The X-ray source N2 21 coincides with the position of a bright radio source ( S_ 1.4 =119 mJy; Ciliegi et al. 1999). The optical counterpart to the X-ray and radio source is a faint galaxy ( R=25.02 ). The K band image also shows a resolved counterpart with K=18.60 giving an extremely red colour of R-K=6.4 .

N2 21 has properties such as redshift, R-K colour and radio luminosity similar to the extremely red radio galaxies in Willott et al. (2001b). The continuum spectral index in the J and H bands is =3.5 . This is considerably steeper than the 2 of unreddened, moderately old (a few Gyr) stellar populations at this redshift and suggests that dust reddening is important.

The large amount of reddening necessary to fit the SED of N2 21 indicates substantial quantities of dust distributed throughout the galaxy. The existence of such a large amount of dust strongly suggests that N2 21 is undergoing an intense starburst.

gmw_00010853.name: N2 28
gmw_00010853.overview: N2 28 is a radio-quiet galaxy with a spectral energy distribution consistent with an evolved stellar population [arXiv:astro-ph/0210248]. It hosts an obscured, Seyfert-like active galactic nucleus and is located near the center of a group of extremely red galaxies [arXiv:astro-ph/0210248].

sources.summary: The paper identifies N2 28 as an extremely red, radio-quiet hard X-ray host with an evolved-galaxy SED, possible cluster environment, and obscured Seyfert-like luminosity.

quotes.quote: The X-ray source N2 28 is identified with an extremely red galaxy which has K=19.7 and R=25.8 . This galaxy is near the centre of a group of 4 extremely red ( R-K>6 ) galaxies (Fig. \ fig:erocluster ). The surface density of objects with K<20 and R-K>6 in this region is 30 times higher than is typical (Thompson et al. 1999). This high overdensity strongly suggests that at least some of these objects are at the same redshift and physically associated.

The X-ray spectrum N2 28 is detected in both the hard and full band images. The hardness ratio of HR=-0.14 0.18 is 2 harder than the typical HR=-0.5 of unobscured quasars, indicating a substantial absorbing column. The hard spectrum and high luminosity indicate the presence of an obscured AGN, however this source is considerably less luminous than quasars and more similar to Seyfert galaxies.
In contrast, the sub- L_ host of the radio-quiet N2 28 has an SED consistent with an evolved stellar population at z 1 and may be located in a cluster.

gmw_00010854.name: GRB 161023A
gmw_00010854.overview: The host galaxy of GRB 161023A is characterized by an average metallicity for its redshift [arXiv:1806.07393] and has an estimated age of 0.15 Gyr [arXiv:1903.01353]. Chemical abundance ratios favor a dusty spiral-disk model for this galaxy [arXiv:1903.01353], which exhibits mild dust depletion [arXiv:1806.07393].

sources.summary: The paper uses GRB 161023A afterglow absorption to characterize its host galaxy, deriving average GRB-host metallicity, mild dust depletion, low extinction, component distances, and ALMA molecular non-detection constraints.

quotes.quote: Using these column densities we derive a metallicity of [ S/H ] =-1.15 0.04 and [ Zn/H ] =-1.11 0.07 for the host galaxy, which is an average metallicity for a GRB host at this redshift. Thanks to the good signal-to-noise of the spectrum and the abundance of resolved features of different species we can study the dust-depletion patterns of the three velocity components observed within the host galaxy of the GRB using recently established correlations based on [ Zn/Fe ] for QSO-DLAs by . We measure only a mild depletion for components I and III and the average of the host with [ Zn/Fe ] =0.5-0.7 . Component IV, which is the one closest to the GRB, might have a higher depletion, similar to MW-disc-like extinction, but we caution against over-interpreting our findings as Zn absorption is very weak in this component. The dust-corrected average metallicity of the host of GRB 161023A is 0.94 0.08 .

Combining the column densities of these species and the information on spectral and decay slopes of the afterglow to predict how the afterglow would have excited them, we estimate the physical distance to the three systems detected in the host galaxy: d_I=990 60 pc, d_ II = 820 90 pc, and d_ IV =700 140 pc. Hence, the closest system to the GRB afterglow is in this case the one that is blueshifted the furthest in the spectrum. The ALMA observations did not yield a detection of molecules but allow us to impose limits on the column density of CO and HCO+ along the GRB line of sight, and can be used as a guide for future observations.

sources.summary: The paper favors a dusty spiral-disk model for the GRB 161023A host, explaining its silicon and sulfur behavior and estimating an age of 0.15 Gyr.

quotes.quote: GRB 161023A In Figure f:161023 , the reference model-data comparison highlights different behaviours for what concerns the various [ /Fe ] ratios. [ S/Fe ] data is in good agreement with the spiral disk model, whereas Si and Mg tend to suggest an irregular galaxy. The behaviour found for different elements is quite well explained by the dusty (i.e. increased dust production by CC-SNe) spiral model (Sp + ), shown in Figure f:161023_1 . Indeed, the statistical test confirm our explantion, choosing the dusty spiral disk scenario as the favourite to explain this host. Concerning the host age, it was estimated to be 0.15 Gyr at the time of the GRB event.

gmw_00010855.name: BTC40 J2340 3949
gmw_00010855.overview: BTC40 J2340 3949 is a high-redshift quasar detected at 1.4 GHz [arXiv:astro-ph/0209055]. It lies near the boundary of being classified as radio-loud and may exhibit a weak broad absorption line trough [arXiv:astro-ph/0209055].

sources.summary: The paper describes BTC40 J2340 3949 as a high-redshift quasar detected at 1.4 GHz, lying near the radio-loud boundary and possibly showing a weak BAL or associated absorption trough.

quotes.quote: One of the quasars, BTC40 J2340 3949, was detected in the deep, 1.4GHz ATESP radio survey (Prandoni \ 2000) with a peak flux of 0.57 mJy. We can adapt the radio-optical flux ratio, R_ o , of Kellerman \ (1989) for a high-redshift source, substituting the I band (8000 ) magnitude for the B band (4400 ) and scaling the radio flux to the proportionally lower frequency (8000/4400) ^ 1 by assuming a power law of the form f ^ 0.5 . In this case, R_ o 10 , putting the quasar just on the border of being classified as radio-loud. BTC40 J2340 3949 may have a weak BAL trough (or strong associated absorption) seen in N 5 at 6945 \ and C 4 at 8680 \ (the absorption at 7600 \ is atmospheric), and may therefore be similar to the radio-moderate BAL quasars discovered in large numbers by the FIRST Bright Quasar Survey (Becker et al. 2000).

gmw_00010856.name: BTC J0949+0715
gmw_00010856.overview: BTC J0949+0715 is a compact narrow emission-line galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0209055]. Based on its emission line diagnostics and weak H-beta line relative to [O III] 5007, it is classified as a Seyfert 2 active galactic nucleus [arXiv:astro-ph/0209055].

sources.summary: The paper identifies BTC J0949+0715 as a compact narrow emission-line galaxy whose line ratios, including weak H relative to [O III] 5007, make it almost certainly a Seyfert 2 galaxy.

quotes.quote: The galaxies exhibit point-source profiles and [ O 3 ] 4959,5007 emission (Figure cnelgs ) shifted into the I filter at z 0.6 , giving them colors similar to our quasar candidates. All three galaxies have ( V-I ) 1.7, making them, along with the quasar BTC40 1429+0119 ( V-I =1.71), the bluest (V-I) candidates in the sample (see Figure colors ). Contamination by such galaxies has been encountered by other surveys for quasars at lower redshift (see, e.g. Hall \ 1996 and Kennefick \ 1997).

In addition to [ O 3 ] , the spectra show [ O 2 ] 3727 and probable [ Ne 3 ] 3869, as well as H in one case. BTC J0949+0715 is almost certainly a Seyfert 2 galaxy, based on the emission line diagnostics of Baldwin, Phillips, Terlevich (1981) and Rola, Terlevich, Terlevich (1997). Even though H is detected in this object, it is very weak relative to [ O 3 ] 5007, and such a ratio is a good sign of AGN activity.

gmw_00010857.name: WISEA J234402.95-352641.8
gmw_00010857.overview: WISEA J234402.95-352641.8 is a galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.1 [arXiv:2401.17286]. Its nucleus, which shows signatures of a weak active galactic nucleus, recently experienced a multi-wavelength flare likely caused by a tidal disruption event [arXiv:2401.17286].

sources.summary: The paper identifies WISEA J234402.95-352641.8 as the z=0.1 galaxy hosting J2344, whose nucleus flared across X-ray, optical, infrared, and radio bands in a likely TDE within a weak AGN.

quotes.quote: The nucleus of the galaxy WISEA J234402.95-352641.8, at a redshift of z=0.1 , brightened by a factor of at least 150 in the 0.2--2 keV X-ray band, 3 mag in optical, and 0.3 mag in the infra-red, with the first X-ray detection occurring 20 d after the optical peak Homan2023 . Follow-up optical spectra taken within weeks of the X-ray and optical flare show a blue continuum, broad Balmer emission lines and narrow [ OIII ] and [ NII ] emission lines. Homan2023 analysed the early-time X-ray and optical characteristics of this transient event and found the most likely explanation for the transient emission to be a TDE within a turned-off AGN.

There is no radio source detected in either the RACS-low (0.88 GHz) or RACS-mid (1.37 GHz) observations, with a 3 upper limit of <0.64 mJy at 0.88 GHz and <0.63 mJy at 1.36 GHz. These radio flux densities provide an upper limit for the host emission as well as the early time radio emission at 1.37 GHz, and indicate that there is no strong radio AGN activity in the host galaxy. We note that we cannot rule out low-luminosity AGN emission in the host, and indeed the optical spectrum of the galaxy shows strong narrow [ OIII ] emission lines Homan2023 ; a signature of AGN activity.

gmw_00010858.name: Galaxy with a redshift of z=0.065
aliases_local: Galaxy with a redshift of z=0.065
gmw_00010858.overview: This galaxy is located at a redshift of z=0.065 [arXiv:2307.05689]. It is identified as the most likely host galaxy of the gamma-ray burst GRB 230307A, which has a physical offset of 36.6 kiloparsecs from the host [arXiv:2307.05689].

sources.original_name: Galaxy with a redshift of z=0.065
sources.summary: The paper identifies the z=0.065 galaxy as the most likely host of GRB 230307A, citing its chance-coincidence estimate, physical consistency, and large burst offset.

quotes.quote: Galaxy with a redshift of z=0.065 : With the half-light radius and band magnitude from DESI Legacy Survey https://www.legacysurvey.org/dr10/catalogs/ , the chance coincidence probability for this galaxy was estimated to be 0.11 (Table tab:obs_prob ). Although not statistically significant, the redshift is more consistent with the physical properties of the GRB. Therefore, we consider this galaxy to be the most likely host. The offset of GRB 230307A from the host galaxy is 29.4", which is 36.6 kpc in redshift 0.065. As presented in Fig. fig:classification d, the offset is consistent with those of type I GRB, and larger than type II GRBs.

gmw_00010859.name: VCC 848
gmw_00010859.overview: VCC 848 is a blue compact dwarf galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0111178]. It exhibits resolved 170-micrometer emission, an extreme dust surface density, and a cold dust temperature upper limit of 9.9 K [arXiv:astro-ph/0111178].

sources.summary: The paper describes BCD VCC 848 as having the coldest two-wavelength dust-temperature upper limit, extreme BCD dust surface density, and resolved 170 m emission.

quotes.quote: Of the BCDs detected only at two wavelengths, VCC 848 has the coldest dust temperature, 9.9 K as an upper limit.
The BCDs VCC 655 and VCC 848 are the most extreme examples.
Furthermore, there is evidence that the 170 m emission is resolved in two BCDs, VCC 1 and VCC 848 (see Fig. 5 of Tuffs et al. 2002).

gmw_00010860.name: J1518+1403
gmw_00010860.overview: J1518+1403 is an active galactic nucleus host galaxy that is treated as a single system [arXiv:2310.10235]. It appears to be undergoing a merger with a secondary source located 14.7 arcseconds to the northeast [arXiv:2310.10235].

sources.summary: J1518+1403 is treated as a single apparent merger with a nearby secondary source and shows no high-J CO detection, with intermediate inferred gas properties.

quotes.quote: From the CO(1-0) data there are no signs of any companions that are spatially and spectrally aligned with our targets, such that they would impact upon the measured flux values, aside from the apparent mergers occurring in J1222-0007 and J1518+1403 (which are both treated as single systems in this work).

From available multi-wavelength data Jarvis21 we also note that J1518+1403 has a secondary source located 14.7 arcsec away to the north east and J1222-0007 has a secondary source 4.7 arcsec away which are both likely to be on-going mergers.
Likewise, J1518+1403 shows no signs of a detection but is between the excitation of the high- z and starburst CO SLED.

gmw_00010861.name: J1222-0007
gmw_00010861.overview: J1222-0007 is a galaxy system undergoing an ongoing merger with a secondary source located 4.7 arcseconds away [arXiv:2310.10235]. The system is also identified as hosting a dual active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2310.10235].

sources.summary: J1222-0007 is treated as a single apparent merger with a 4.7 arcsec secondary source and is identified as an ongoing merger or dual AGN.

quotes.quote: From the CO(1-0) data there are no signs of any companions that are spatially and spectrally aligned with our targets, such that they would impact upon the measured flux values, aside from the apparent mergers occurring in J1222-0007 and J1518+1403 (which are both treated as single systems in this work).

From available multi-wavelength data Jarvis21 we also note that J1518+1403 has a secondary source located 14.7 arcsec away to the north east and J1222-0007 has a secondary source 4.7 arcsec away which are both likely to be on-going mergers.
Interestingly two out of the four non-detections in the ACA CO(1-0) data are known to be ongoing mergers/dual AGN J1222-0007 and J1316+1753, see Jarvis21 .

gmw_00010862.name: J1116+2200
gmw_00010862.overview: J1116+2200 is a quasar host galaxy with detections in the first three transitions of carbon monoxide [arXiv:2310.10235]. Line ratio analyses indicate that the molecular gas in this active galaxy is consistent with being optically thicker [arXiv:2310.10235].

sources.summary: J1116+2200 has first-three-transition detections used for gas modeling, weak high-J constraints, and line ratios suggesting optically thicker molecular gas.

quotes.quote: The remaining two targets (J0945+1737, J1116+2200) can't be analysed in much detail since the upper limits are not very constraining.

To further investigate the temperature and density of gas in these quasars we analysed the 5 targets with detections in all of the first three transitions (J1000+1242, J1100+0846, J1108+0659, J1116+2200 and J1518+1403), which provide best opportunity to test these properties.

From analysing the line ratios in Figure fig:temp_dens we find that the molecular gas in J1116+2200 seems consistent with being optically thicker and J1100+0846 seems consistent with being optically thinner.

gmw_00010863.name: NGC 6070
gmw_00010863.overview: NGC 6070 is a spiral galaxy located at a distance of 29.8 Mpc [arXiv:1004.4636]. It features a bar structure and a normal arm distribution where two main arms break into several on each side of the central region [arXiv:1004.4636].

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 6070 as a spiral galaxy with a bar and normal multi-arm structure, then uses its redshift and H II regions for emission-line analysis.

quotes.quote: NGC 6070, at a distance of 29.8 Mpc 2002A&A...389...68G , is classified as SA(s)cd by although state that a bar with two main arms can be identified. further studied the bar structure classifying it as Type II.o.CT according to . The galaxy shows a normal arm distribution, with two arms that break into several on each side of the central region (see Figure fig6070chart ). The disk dynamics was studied by , and studied the distribution of \ regions in the galaxy.

gmw_00010864.name: J0215--0555
gmw_00010864.overview: J0215--0555 is a Lyman-break galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 5.754 [arXiv:1902.03880]. Observations of the galaxy have detected Lyman-alpha emission, optical continuum emission, and a spectroscopically resolved carbon doublet [arXiv:1902.03880].

sources.summary: The paper reports resolved carbon emission from the z≈5.75 Lyman-break galaxy J0215--0555, detects Ly and continuum emission, and models asymmetric outflow and possible ionizing escape.

quotes.quote: We report the discovery of the first spectroscopically resolved / 1334, 1335 doublet in the Lyman-break galaxy J0215--0555 at z_ Ly =5.754 . The separation of the resonant and fluorescent emission channels was possible thanks to the large redshift of the source and long integration time, as well as the small velocity width of the feature, 0.6 0.2 . We model this emission and find that at least two components are required to reproduce the combination of morphologies of emission, \ absorption and emission, and Lyman- emission from the object. We suggest that the close alignment between the fluorescence and Lyman- emission could indicate an ionisation escape channel within the object.

We detect the Ly emission line of galaxy J0215-0555 at 10 above noise (Figure 2, panel a). The full width at half maximum of the line is 10 \ (observed) corresponding to 360 km s ^ 1 velocity width and z_ Ly = 5.754 0.002 . Optical continuum emission from the object is detected over the wavelength range 8220< <9300 , with a median flux of (4.06 0.03) 10^ 20 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 ^ 1 .

gmw_00010865.name: J125856.3 031944
gmw_00010865.overview: J125856.3 031944 is a newly discovered quadruply lensed source with a kite-like image configuration [arXiv:2404.14256]. The source is classified as a low-luminosity type 2 active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2404.14256].

sources.summary: The paper reports J125856.3 031944 as a newly discovered quadruply lensed, low-luminosity type 2 AGN with a kite-like image configuration and high magnifications.

quotes.quote: A quadruply lensed source, J125856.3 031944, has been discovered using the DELVE survey and WISE W1 - W2 colors. Followup direct imaging carried out with the MPIA 2.2 m and the Baade 6.5 m telescopes is analyzed, as is spectroscopy from the 2.5 m Nordic Optical Telescope. The lensed image configuration is kite-like, with the faintest image 2 magnitudes fainter than the other three.

As the lensed image configuration is nearly circular, simple models give individual magnifications of at least 25 for the 3 brighter images. The source's narrow emission lines and low intrinsic luminosity qualify it as a type 2 AGN.

gmw_00010866.name: BCG-S
gmw_00010866.overview: BCG-S is one of two brightest cluster galaxies in its host cluster [arXiv:2207.09416]. It is situated in a close visual pair with another galaxy component [arXiv:0804.0299], and its surrounding diffuse stellar emission is bluer than its central core [arXiv:0804.0299].

sources.summary: The paper treats RXCJ 0014.3-3022 SE A as a main BCG component in a close paired system whose surrounding diffuse light is bluer than the BCG cores.

quotes.quote: The second visual pair of BCGs, to the south-east, (i.e., RXCJ 0014.3 3022 SE A and B) is actually made of two very close pairs of galaxies at consistent photometric redshifts. A main component (i.e., one of the two BCGs) can be clearly identified in each of these very close pairs (see also Fig. FigBR1b ). The two BCGs are separated by 120 kpc on the plane of the sky and by 4119 416 km s^ 1 (rest frame) along the line of sight (from Braglia et al. 2007). Hence, likely RXCJ 0014.3 3022 SE is not an interacting pair.

Conversely, we find that the B-R colour of the diffuse stellar emission detected around the two pairs of BCGs in the merging cluster RXCJ 0014.3 3022 is significantly bluer than the central regions of the four BCGs. If the contribution of intracluster stars to the diffuse stellar emission around BCGs is not negligible, this means that the intracluster light has multiple origins, possibly linked to the dynamical state of a cluster.

sources.summary: The lens model treats BCG-S as one of two brightest cluster galaxies whose mass and ellipticity are optimized separately because radial arcs lie near both BCGs.

quotes.quote: In addition, we perform a visual inspection of the imaging and the fitted spectra, resulting in the exclusion of three faint galaxies whose spectra are contaminated by the light from BCG-S and BCG-N.
The second term corresponds to the mass contribution of the two brightest cluster galaxies (BCGs), BCG-N and BCG-S in Figure fig:RGB , which are individually optimized in the lens model.

The cluster-scale component of our new lens models ( _i^ halo ) is parametrized by two not truncated elliptical dPIEs, which are centered on the BCGs, denominated BCG-N and BCG-S in Figure fig:RGB . The halos are left free to move within a small range around the BCG positions (see table:inout_lensing ). Due to the presence of radial arcs in the vicinity of both BCGs (namely systems 2 and 4, see Figure fig:RGB and Table tab:multiple_images ), and to improve their reconstruction, the parameters describing the mass contribution, and the ellipticity, of both BCGs are individually optimized (i.e., they are modeled outside of the scaling relations). This consists of 4 additional free parameters for each profile.

gmw_00010867.name: COSMOS 0050+4901
gmw_00010867.overview: COSMOS 0050+4901 is a high-redshift, quiescent galaxy located in the COSMOS/UltraVISTA field [arXiv:1602.00007]. It is a strongly lensed system that is quadruply imaged by a foreground lens galaxy [arXiv:1602.00007].

sources.summary: The paper presents X-Shooter spectroscopy and a velocity-dispersion measurement for COSMOS 0050+4901, describing it as a strongly lensed, quadruply imaged, high-redshift quiescent galaxy.

quotes.quote: In this paper we present X-Shooter spectroscopy, and a stellar velocity dispersion measurement of COSMOS 0050+4901 , a quiescent galaxy found by muzzin2012 in the COSMOS/UltraVISTA field mccracken2012 . With the current data, this is now the highest redshift quiescent galaxy with a stellar velocity dispersion measurement, as well as the least massive quiescent galaxy beyond redshift 2 with a rest-frame optical spectrum.

COSMOS 0050+4901 is a strongly-lensed system where the lens is a single galaxy at z=0.960 found serendipitously in the COSMOS/UltraVISTA field mccracken2012 as a group of exceptionally bright, red galaxies. The source is quadruply imaged, with photometric redshifts of z 2.3-2.4 (depending on which of the multiple images is analyzed; muzzin2012 , hereafter M12). The brightest 3 images are magnified by a factor of 5 (M12, Muzzin et al. in prep).

gmw_00010868.name: J142904+354425
gmw_00010868.overview: J142904+354425 is an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1604.00171] that was previously classified as a megahertz-peaked spectrum source [arXiv:1604.00171]. It is a non-variable radio source that was not detected by the European VLBI Network, likely because its emission was resolved out [arXiv:1604.00171].

sources.summary: The paper reports that J142904+354425 was not detected with EVN, was previously included as an MPS source, and is likely resolved out rather than absent.

quotes.quote: J142904+354425 is not detected in the naturally weighted, 5 5 arcsec EVN image with 23 Jy beam^ 1 noise and a 30 34 mas restoring beam. J142904+354425 was included in our MPS selection in coppejans2015 and was not flagged as being variable based on its FIRST and NVSS flux densities. No match could be found for it in the dVMR catalogue because it lies outside the area that was imaged.

Considering that J142904+354425's predicted flux density at 1.7 GHz is 4.9 0.4 mJy, that it is an AGN (Section subsec:agn star ) and non-variable, we conclude that the non-detection is because J142904+354425 was resolved out by the EVN observations. Finally, as is evident from the Fig. fig:spectra , we cannot say for certain that J142904+354425's spectrum turns over.

gmw_00010869.name: J143024+352438
gmw_00010869.overview: J143024+352438 is an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1604.00171]. It was not detected in European VLBI Network observations, suggesting it lacks a compact radio source [arXiv:1604.00171].

sources.summary: The paper reports no EVN detection of J143024+352438, argues a compact source should have been seen, and notes that catalogue flux differences make its spectrum uncertain.

quotes.quote: J143024+352438 is not detected in the naturally weighted, 5 5 arcsec EVN image with 11 Jy beam^ 1 noise and a restoring beam of 6 18 mas. Considering that J143024+352438 has a predicted 1.7 GHz flux density of 2.7 0.2 mJy, we should have easily detected it if it is a compact source. From Fig. fig:spectra it is clear that the flux density difference between the WIR and LOFAR-150 catalogues ( 7.0 3.0 and 14.6 1.5 mJy, respectively) can have a significant impact on the shape of J143024+352438's spectrum. It is worth pointing out that J143024+352438 has a signal-to-noise ratio (SNR; defined as the peak brightness divided by the local rms noise) of 49 and 5 in the LOFAR-150 and WIR images, respectively. It is therefore possible that the spectrum can be described by a single power law between 150 and 1400 MHz.

gmw_00010870.name: J142917+332626
gmw_00010870.overview: J142917+332626 is a radio source that can be unambiguously classified as a megahertz-peaked spectrum (MPS) turnover source [arXiv:1604.00171]. It is one of the few turnover sources with a detected X-ray counterpart, which was identified via the Chandra X-Boötes survey [arXiv:1604.00171].
